54c2c193323437f024003921_57.t: We're just as screwed as you are. Me and Izz, we were listed as your accomplices. And we can't afford for you to get caught. You'll lead 'em to us, one way or another. DNA samples, things we've told you, astral residue, I don't know... cop stuff.

54c2c193323437f024003921_58.t: The little decker looks from you to Wu, eyes narrowed, sizing you up. You can see her make a silent decision before she speaks. Her voice is flat, resigned.

54c2c193323437f024003921_63.t: It's very difficult. I can't do it. Some people can… organizations… expert systems.

54c2c193323437f024003921_66.t: We gotta ask for help. There's only one person we know who has the kind of pull it takes to burn a SIN and hide us from the cops. Kindly Cheng.

54c2c193323437f024003921_70.t: Is0bel's voice is hollow and flat.

54c2c193323437f024003921_73.t: Gobbet tries to make her voice light. Confident.

54c2c193323437f024003921_76.t: She leans in, serious as death.

54c696f3303031301d005190_9.t: The young shaman's eyes never leave the floor.

54c696f3303031301d005190_132.t: It's true, Auntie Cheng! A vengeful spirit rose up from a pile of dirty rags! *It* killed Bao's...

54c696f3303031301d005190_164.t: Gobbet reaches up to stroke the rat perched on her shoulder.

54c696f3303031301d005190_252.t: That's the guy we saw in the surveillance footage. The one who killed Raymond Black.

54c696f3303031301d005190_322.t: I know that *I* wanna see this.

54c696f3303031301d005190_378.t: Hope is not a viable shadowrunning tool, Izz. We can't expect to stay in cover for long. One way or another, things are gonna get hot.

54c696f3303031301d005190_379.t: Hope is not a viable shadowrunning tool, Izz. We can't expect to stay in cover for long. One way or another, things are gonna get hot.

54cfdf5b306364a06a008577_21.t: She lets loose a wistful sigh.

54cfdf5b306364a06a008577_24.t: Parties, breaking and entering, blackmail... sounds like a good time to me. Wonder if Neville has an open bar?

54cff281303031f808007fca_1.t: She holds up her hand, indicates the surrounding platform.

54cff281303031f808007fca_7.t: We'll kiss her ass until it shines.

54cff281303031f808007fca_9.t: One more thing. Kindly Cheng is a dangerous woman. She may seem friendly, but don't let your guard drop. And whatever you do, do *not* disrespect her.

54cff281303031f808007fca_10.t: She drops her hand. Nods to the three of you.

54cff281303031f808007fca_14.t: And you - you're not exactly ordinary either, are you?

54cff281303031f808007fca_15.t: She smiles for the first time.

54cff281303031f808007fca_18.t: Because we need your SINs burned as badly as you do. And we need to figure a way out of this shit. Or we're all dead.

54cff281303031f808007fca_22.t: We're helping ourselves as much as we're helping you, Seattle. We need your SINs burned as badly as you do. And we need to figure a way out of this shit. Or we're all dead.

54cff281303031f808007fca_24.t: She smiles for the first time.

54cff281303031f808007fca_25.t: She smiles for the first time.
Yeah, that's what I'm worried about.

54cff281303031f808007fca_26.t: She rolls her eyes and let's out a little grunt.

54cff281303031f808007fca_28.t: Gobbet smirks and shakes her head.

54d2941f32343734920026a7_3.t: You got me. As I said, the Walled City is the worst slum in the eastern hemisphere. Maybe in the world.

54d2941f32343734920026a7_5.t: The Lotus Den. That's what Bao calls his little corner of hell. I don't know where it is offhand, but I have a way of finding things.

54d530173234376036005d0a_3.t: I could coax it out of the cloth and talk with it if you want. Maybe it'll lead to something.

54d530173234376036005d0a_5.t: As the energies bound into the cloth begin to swirl, Gobbet cocks her head appreciatively.

54d530173234376036005d0a_6.t: Her eyes go beady, and her nose crinkles.

this thing is alive... I don't think it's intelligent, but it's definitely living, despite being mostly made of cancer and disease.

54d5495c306364bc7300017a_4.t: Gobbet leans closer to the tank, still whispering.

54d5495c306364bc7300017a_7.t: Gobbet glares at Gaichu, her nostrils flaring.

54d92ad03063645032003070_20.t: Gobbet bends over, squeezing some water out of her ropes of hair. When she stands, she's got an ear-to-ear grin.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_7.t: Getting worked up about that is like being angry with gravity. Doesn't matter how pissed off it makes you, it isn't going to change. Corporate CEOs will always get away with murder, Seattle. It's an unwritten law of the universe.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_10.t: Anyway. It's been a long day, and I'm beat... frankly, I'm amazed that you're still standing. I'll see you tomorrow, though, right? I mean, you're not going anywhere.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_12.t: Yeah. Right. Good. I'll see you then, then.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_16.t: As life goals go, I've heard worse.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_19.t: Not officially, no. But let's discuss this some other time, huh? I'm exhausted, and I really need some sleep. You should get some, too. To be frank, I don't even know how you're still standing.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_21.t: We both need sleep. It's night-night time. To be perfectly frank, I don't even know how you're still standing right now.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_24.t: So anyway. I'm sleeping now. End of discussion. But, uh... I'll see you tomorrow, though, right? I mean, you're not going anywhere.
It's phrased as a statement - firm and final.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_25.t: Well, that was a thing, right? We fought a Yama King, and we *won.*
I mean, I know that we haven't really talked much, or anything, but... that's something, yeah? Worthy of a high-five, at least.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_26.t: Yeah, that was pretty intense. The stuff of legends, even.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_30.t: She motions toward her cabin door.
Sleepy time. Maybe we can talk tomorrow, or something.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_33.t: Yeah, I know what you mean.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_43.t: You two can bunk here for a while.

You've each got a head to take care of your necessaries. Try to knock before you enter someone else's, okay?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_49.t: The ork turns to you. Raises an eyebrow.
Anything you need before I leave you to it...?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_51.t: Gobbet's mouth screws up a bit.
Now that Nightjar and Gutshot are gone, it's just us.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_54.t: Nothing to worry about. He mostly keeps to himself.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_58.t: Close enough. It was full of BTL junkies when we came across it. They were completely wigged out on some multiplayer cybergame... I'm not sure they *ever* chipped out of it.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_62.t: Nightjar ran 'em out. Not sure what happened to them after that. Anyway, it's ours now... Auntie Cheng says so.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_66.t: Whatever, we weren't using it. As long as our downstairs neighbor keeps to himself, he can have the lower level, especially if it keeps Auntie Cheng happy.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_74.t: She chews on the words along with a mouthful of cheap pasta. A corner of her mouth curls up into a half-smile.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_77.t: Kind of a mouthful, but hey. If that's what you want, then so be it. Seattle it is.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_80.t: She pops a noodle into her mouth and chews.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_81.t: Yeah, sure. I don't think she knew what it meant... she just thought that it sounded pretty. You should get used to that, by the way. People here in Hong Kong like to stand out, and choosing unique names is one of the ways that they do it.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_85.t: I'll make sure to thank her for you.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_86.t: Her jaw stops working mid-chew. Slowly, she nods.
Yeah... yeah, I have. We all have. Everyone in town.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_87.t: Let me guess. You saw the Walled City, and a black whirlpool, and teeth. Thousands and thousands of teeth.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_88.t: The dreams are coming from inside the Walled City. I'm sure of it. All of the negative energy pent up in there... all of that pain and anger and poisonous qi... it's leaking out. And while we sleep, it's getting into us.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_92.t: She tries on a smile. Fails. Does her best to laugh it off.
Don't worry, Seattle, we're fine here. But, uh... let's talk about something else, huh? I don't like thinking about the Walled City very much. Too many old fears and bad feelings.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_96.t: Sure does. But then, lots of odd stuff happens in Heoi.

...In case you hadn't gotten the memo, we aren't in the best part of town.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_98.t: Yeah? I think that your list is different from mine. The people of Heoi have learned put up with a lot worse than bad dreams. I'm not saying they shouldn't be freaked out. I'm just saying that when you live in a place like this, you'll usually have at least twenty other things to worry about... tangible things, the kind that can put a bullet in your head.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_100.t: ...Yeah, I figured you would. That was a hell of a thing, right?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_102.t: She mimes holding a rifle. Makes a show of peering down the scope.
Bang. Bang. Dead. Dead. No blaze of glory, no final speech. Just... extinguished. Smashed like bugs.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_103.t: She shakes her head from side to side.

No way. Not me. When I die, I wanna know what's coming. Just winking out like that is the worst thing I can imagine. Even a drawn-out, painful death would be better... at least I'd have time to say my good-byes.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_104.t: Sure. Scream 'em. Whatever. The point is, I'd know that I was on the way out, and I could at least try to do something about it. Earn myself an A for effort, right?
She plunges her spoon back into the bowl, sloshing ham-scented broth up over the rim.
Nightjar and Gutshot... they never got that chance. It's fucking depressing.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_106.t: Yeah. She seemed like a good woman... tough, skilled. Light on her feet. But in the end, it didn't matter, because some asshole with a rifle made a decision a half a mile away.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_107.t: She absentmindedly raises her bowl to her shoulder. A rat crawls over the slope of her back and lowers its head to lap at the broth.
It was more Rat than me. She's the one who grabbed me by the gut and led us to that sewer entrance. All that I did was follow.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_108.t: 'Course you can, because I'm right. You don't wanna go gentle into that good night any more than I do.
She plunges her spoon back into the bowl, sloshing ham-scented broth up over the rim.
Nightjar and Gutshot... they never got the opportunity to choose. It's fucking depressing.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_109.t: We'll agree to disagree. But if nothing else, you'd want a chance to decide whether to go out easy or not, right? You'd want the option to change your mind.
She plunges her spoon back into the bowl, sloshing ham-scented broth up over the rim.
Nightjar and Gutshot... they never got that choice. It was taken away from them, and that's a damned tragedy.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_110.t: Ooh, big words from the greenhorn runner. Look, I know that they knew the risks, Seattle. We all do. That doesn't change the fact that it sucks.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_111.t: She plunges her spoon back into the bowl, sloshing ham-scented broth up over the rim.
Nightjar and Gutshot... they were good guys, y'know? And now they're dead, and they didn't even get the chance to go down fighting. It's depressing, is all.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_112.t: Yeah. That it was. Nightjar and Gutshot were two of the strongest men I've ever known... quality shadowrunners, both of 'em. I've watched those two fight through situations that'd kill anyone and come out on top. And how do they go out?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_113.t: Yeah? Well, good for you. That doesn't change the fact that I lost friends back there, or that you lost that mage that you came here with. What was her name again?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_115.t: Well, whatever. She seemed like a good woman... tough, skilled. Light on her feet. But in the end, it didn't matter, because some asshole with a rifle made a decision a half a mile away.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_118.t: She slurps thoughtfully at her soup.

A clever one. She's gotten me out of more trouble than I care to mention... gotten me into a fair amount of it, too. But I can always count on her to lead me out of hot water when I need her to. I can feel it in my belly, y'know? Sort of a tugging sensation. I've long since learned to follow it.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_119.t: Don't feel bad about it. Me and Rat... well, we have a sort of a special bond. She takes care of me, and I've always done my best to take care of her.
Another pair of beady eyes blinks up at you from Gobbet's hip. A second rat scurries up her shoulder to join the first.
And to spoil and pamper her earthly children, like these two. Meet Madness and Folly.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_121.t: She brushes her fingers across the white rat's mottled coat.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_122.t: I like the way they sound. Madness and Folly. It has a nice ring to it, right?
She brushes her fingers across the white rat's mottled coat.
My girls remind me not to take myself too seriously. I wouldn't trust Folly, though... she bites.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_123.t: She brushes her fingers across the white rat's mottled coat.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_124.t: Usually they don't. Me and Rat... we have a sort of a special bond. She takes care of me, and I've always done my best to take care of her.
Another pair of beady eyes blinks up at you from Gobbet's hip. A second rat scurries up her shoulder to join the first.
And to pamper her earthly children, like these two. Meet Madness and Folly.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_125.t: Sure. Why not? We're all a part of the same nest.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_126.t: Probably not. But I've never gotten sick, so...
She raises an eyebrow, scoops a noodle out of the bowl, and pops it into her mouth with a satisfied smile.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_128.t: I looked outside...? I mean, they're rats. Hong Kong isn't experiencing a rodent shortage, or anything.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_130.t: Yeah, in retrospect, it might've been.
She continues to brush Folly's coat. The creature chirps happily at her touch.
Odds are about fifty-fifty that you'd get your hand chewed off. I'd probably feel bad about that.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_131.t: She continues to brush Folly's coat. The creature chirps happily at her touch.
Odds are about fifty-fifty that you'd get your hand chewed off. I'd probably feel bad about that.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_132.t: A long time. Started when I was just a kid.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_133.t: Something like that. Your guess is as good as mine. Doesn't change the fact that I've been doing this for years, and you started yesterday.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_136.t: Good thing we're not in Seattle, then. But my point is still valid... you need to learn the basics, and I'm offering.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_140.t: Y'know, Seattle... this has been a nice chat and all, but it's getting late, and I still have the rest of a hot pot to power through. So, without wanting this to get awkward...

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_142.t: Smirking, she brushes Folly's coat with the knuckles of her left hand.
You can go get your own food. Now would be a good time.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_145.t: You're not good at hints, are you? Taking them, I mean.
She glances toward the door again, more pointedly this time.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_146.t: See what I just did there? That meant "leave," but in a polite way. Look, I'll do it again.
Another glance, complete with a sweeping arm motion ushering you out.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_147.t: You're in? Good. Starting the next time you visit, I'm gonna teach you all about being a shadowrunner.
You're gonna benefit from my bountiful experience. Wait and see.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_148.t: Plenty. Been in more firefights than I can count.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_149.t: You're pretty new at this - you and Duncan both. It shows.
She takes a moment to study you. Slowly, her mouth tilts into a smirk.
What you need is a wizened mentor. An experienced runner from whose experience you can benefit. I'm thinkin' that might as well be me.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_150.t: Been lucky, I guess. 'Course, the odds of you surviving as long as I have are pretty low. You don't even have anyone to show you the ropes.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_153.t: She chuckles through another mouthful of soup.
Yeah... sure. "Boss." That's you, all right... I mean, you only just got here, and you don't know a thing about life in Hong Kong, and you only just started shadowrunning. Seems appropriate to me.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_154.t: Her eyes twinkle as she smiles at you from behind her bowl.
So, Seattle... you wanna tell me what you're doing in my bedroom? I'm assuming you're not here to admire the view.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_155.t: She chuckles through another mouthful of soup.
Yeah... sure. "Killer." Very imaginative, and not ridiculous at all. Let's go with it.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_157.t: Oh, everything's just ducky over here. My friends are dead, I'm on my boss's shit list because of it, and I can't even eat a bowl of soup in peace.
She skims an oily slab of faux ham off the top of her soup and pops it into her mouth. Chewing thoughtfully, she looks back up at you with a smile.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_159.t: That's the attitude! Very stoic, very strong. It's downright inspiring.
She skims an oily slab of faux ham off the top of her soup and pops it into her mouth.
Now, you wanna tell me what you're doing in my bedroom...? I'm assuming you're not here to admire the view.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_160.t: She salutes you with her spoon.
What can I do for you?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_163.t: She spears a gray lump of seafood with a fingernail. Extends it to you. It smells like low tide at a municipal pier.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_166.t: Half-repressed memories of dumpster diving in the Barrens dance a merry jig in your brain as the mangled oyster slides down your throat.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_167.t: I wouldn't go that far. But it's seafood, and it isn't made of soy. That makes it good in my book.
She tilts back the tin, drains the remaining juice, and then flicks it into the overflowing bin at her side.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_169.t: It's fine. Shaping up. Having new roomies is always nice.
How're you enjoying your cabin? Comfy enough for you?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_172.t: Yeah. Handy. Auntie Cheng is definitely that.
Don't know if I'd say that she's on our side, though.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_173.t: She pushes back. Her voice goes brisk.
Look. Let's change the subject, if you don't mind. I'd rather not think about the old woman right now.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_175.t: A lazy smile spreads across her face as her fingertips brush Madness's back. The rat squeals and darts back into the safe haven of Gobbet's hair.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_177.t: She cuts you off mid-sentence. Her voice is soft, but firm.
This is where you want to be, Seattle. This is where you're safe. If you quit moaning about it and make an effort to adapt, you'll see that for yourself in no time.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_178.t: Yeah, you will. It'll happen quick, too... quicker than you'd think. Trust me, I know.
She gestures at the peeling walls of her cabin.
It may not be much, but we're safe here. In the shadows, that's everything.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_179.t: No, you wouldn't. It's monsoon season. Believe me, you're lucky that you have a roof over your head. Sorry if the accommodations aren't up to your usual standards, but you're safe here. Try to be thankful for that.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_180.t: Yeah, sure... considered it a bunch of times. I never really cared enough to actually *do* it, though.
You wanna call 'er something else? I'm open to it.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_182.t: Aye aye, cap'n. The Defiance it is.
Yeah, that's really not bad... I think that the others will be happy with it.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_183.t: Yeah... yeah, I like it. Okay, Seattle... the Revenge it is.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_184.t: What the hell, let's roll with it. The Dowager Empress she is.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_185.t: Aye aye, cap'n! The Kraken it is.
Yeah, that's actually pretty good... I think that the others will be happy with it.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_186.t: She does her best to stifle a laugh.
Uhhh... yeah. Sure. That's... that's pretty good, Seattle. You get a gold star for that one.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_187.t: Sure. Why the hell not? The Big Texas. That's just what we'll call her. I'm sure that Izz will be thrilled.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_188.t: I think that it's absurd. But then, we live in a pretty ridiculous world, so why not roll with it?
The corner of her mouth curls up.
Yeah, the Big Texas is just what we'll call her. I'm sure that Izz will be thrilled.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_191.t: She lets out an exasperated sigh.
All right. We can change it again. Just do me a favor and make up your mind this time, okay?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_193.t: Um. Okay. How about this: I'll tell you a story about a run gone bad. You tell me what you'd've done in the runners' place, and then we'll compare notes on your answer. How does that sound?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_200.t: Good. You know what I'm talking about, then.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_206.t: ...Come to think of it, most of her plans involved kicking things. There's something a little sad about that.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_212.t: So, that's the situation. Our payday is smashed. My temporary teammates are all standing around with stupid looks on their faces. The cops are coming. I'm standing by the kitchen.
She plants her hands on her hips, smiling.
What would you have done in my place?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_215.t: Fair. That isn't what I did, but don't think that it didn't cross my mind.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_225.t: The laughter disappears from her voice, and her expression goes serious. It's like somebody flipped a switch - suddenly, the look in her eyes is shrewd. Calculating.
Tell me. What was the moral of this story? What lesson was I trying to convey?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_226.t: While that is *a* lesson... maybe even a valuable one... it isn't what I was trying to convey. Now pay attention, because this is important.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_227.t: What I was *trying* to teach you is that you should trust your impulses. When a run has gone south and you need to improvise, don't overthink it.
She scratches Folly behind the ears.
Once you've committed to doing something, you've gotta follow it through, though. No arguing or hand-wringing, just *do it.*

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_228.t: That's right. Trust your impulses, and don't be afraid to wing it. Almost any action is better than no action at all. Once you've committed to doing something, you've gotta follow it through, though… no arguing or hand-wringing, just *do it.*

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_230.t: Well, that's *a* lesson, I guess. I, uh, wouldn't say that I agree with it, but if that's your major takeaway, well...
I think that I've just learned an important lesson about you.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_232.t: Aside from being a masterfully crafted romp delivered by a world-class storyteller?
She glares at you, her eyes full of reproach.
Yes, of course it had a point. It was a lesson, dimwit. Now pay attention.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_233.t: She crosses her arms over her chest.

Look, they screwed *themselves* over when Sib kicked in that door. They were in a heap of trouble, and what I did would have gotten them out of it if they'd *listened* to me. Know how I know? Because it got *me* out. They could have followed me, but instead, they chose to argue until they exploded. That's on them, not me.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_239.t: Good thinking! As it happens, there was, and I knew just what to do with it. The thought popped into my head and I just went with it.
She taps her temple with a smile.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_242.t: Yeah. Unfortunately, he wound up a lot rougher than he was supposed to be.
That door really did a number on him. Ribs sticking out of the skin, that kind of thing. It was a bloody mess.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_252.t: Her eyes narrow in mock indignation.
You say that like I couldn't have been. When I was twelve, I could've *destroyed* you. And also, I was sixteen. Practically an adult. You're an ork, you know how fast we grow up.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_253.t: Her eyes narrow in mock indignation.
You say that like I couldn't have been. When I was twelve, I could've *destroyed* you. And also, I was sixteen. Practically an adult in ork years. We grow up fast.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_255.t: Not unless you can talk to dead people.
That was a spoiler, by the way. The run didn't end well.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_257.t: All right, here's what we're gonna do. I'll tell you a story about a run gone bad. You tell me what you'd've done in the runners' place, and then we'll compare notes on your answer. How does that sound?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_258.t: They weren't with me when I got evacuated with the rest of the moviegoers. I never saw them leave the building, and I haven't heard from 'em since.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_260.t: Seattle. I'm hurt. It all went down exactly like I said it did.
Well, except for a couple of embellishments here and there. But they make it a better story. Artistic license, and all that.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_263.t: Yeah, sounds good. I'll have your next lesson ready and waiting, too.
She makes a brushing motion with her fingers.
Scurry along now. Your mentor needs her rest.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_264.t: She makes a brushing motion with her fingers.
Scurry along now. Your mentor needs her rest.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_267.t: Exactly, right? It may not be fine dining, but it's seafood, and it isn't made of soy. That makes it good in my book.
She tilts back the tin, drains the remaining juice, and then flicks it into the overflowing bin at her side.
So... what can I do for you?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_268.t: C'mon, it isn't that bad... I mean, it's seafood, and it isn't made of soy. That's gotta count for something, right?
She tilts back the tin, drains the remaining juice, and then flicks it into the overflowing bin at her side.
Waste not, want not. What can I do for you?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_269.t: She shrugs, then pops the oyster into her own mouth.
Suit yourself. So, are you here for a reason...?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_270.t: Kill you? I'm offering you a gift! A gift of *friendship.*
Gobbet waggles her finger under your nose. The oyster clings to her nail like a wad of chewing gum.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_275.t: She puts her hands on her hips, sending Madness scurrying.
Ready for your next lesson? Or did you just want to chat?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_280.t: That's good. I was hoping that you'd want to keep going with this. It's good for you, and I'm kind of enjoying it, too.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_287.t: Yeah. I did. Thanks for rubbing it in.
The words are rueful, but her tone is soft.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_288.t: Don't worry about it, I'm fine. When you run the shadows, losing people is an occupational hazard.
She offers you a lopsided smile.
As shadowrunners, we're disposable assets. It shouldn't come as a surprise when we get used up and tossed away.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_291.t: Shadowrunners get killed all the time. There's no sense moping about it.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_293.t: Anyway. The truth is, I'm mostly over it. When you run the shadows, losing people is an occupational hazard.
She offers you a lopsided smile.
As shadowrunners, we're disposable assets. It shouldn't come as a surprise when we get used up and tossed away.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_294.t: Thanks, but it's all right. When you run the shadows, losing people is an occupational hazard.
She offers you a lopsided smile.
As shadowrunners, we're disposable assets. It shouldn't come as a surprise when we get used up and tossed away.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_298.t: She folds her arms over her chest.
And you're right, I'm the teacher here. So shut up and listen until I tell you not to.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_302.t: She extends a hand so that she can count off teammates on her fingers. Madness scurries down her sleeve and perches on her forearm.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_309.t: Yep. We called it "the Sinking Ship." It was like an enormous raft, all bolted together from old shipping containers. Wasn't the most comfortable place I've ever lived, but the price was right and the company was good.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_313.t: I know, I know. It's stupid, right? But that was how the Johnson described it to us. The Shiny Object. That was what he wanted us to get. He never gave it any other name.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_319.t: He had the whole "wise old sage" thing going on. He had the robes, the little hat, the kind smile. Oh, and the pièce de résistance - a long, wispy beard. He was mean as a snake, though. Had a rep to prove it.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_323.t: She begins counting off examples on her fingers.

Pickled reagents in mason jars. Hermetic scrolls and charms. Relics looted from Buddhist temples, crates of old Balinese Rangda masks, and fist-sized stones from a Great Dragon's kidney. Xiuyan sold it all. How he got his hands on it in the first place is anyone's guess.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_329.t: A dreamy smile spreads across her face.
I wish I could've been there to see it.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_347.t: If Egret weren't pinned down in the loading dock, she could probably get the vault door open. But in order to get to her, I'll have to leave Tsui alone in the vault. If Xiuyan's reinforcements find Tsui before I get back with Egret, well...
She uses her thumb to draw a line across her neck.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_349.t: Yeah, I guess I could've done that. Maybe it would've worked. More likely we'd both have gotten gunned down in the vault, but who knows? This is the Sixth World! Miracles happen every day!

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_352.t: That's the most stylish answer, isn't it? Just audacious enough to be fun. It popped into my head right then and there, same as it did yours. So that's what I did. No hesitation, I just made it happen.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_356.t: I, uh, spent most of the fight huddled up in the corner for my own safety. What Tsui let loose from the stone didn't seem terribly interested in discriminating between friend and foe.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_359.t: We waited for things to calm down in the vault, and for the things to go slithering away. I think that Tsui had some limited control over them, which is why they didn't eat us. After they were gone, Tsui gave me the Shiny Object back, I put it in my satchel, and we bailed.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_363.t: Anyway. That's it, lesson's over. If you've got any questions, go ahead.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_377.t: A dreamy smile spreads across her face.
Egret really outdid herself. I wish that I could've been there to see it.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_382.t: I'd agree with you if we were going in expecting a straight fight. But as a shadowrunner, you should almost never want to get into one of those. When you're on a job, you're always gonna be outnumbered and outgunned. Going quiet is inherently safer than going loud, and if splitting the group is what it takes to do that...

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_386.t: When you boil it down like that, it sounds kind of silly, doesn't it?
But hey... that's the world we live in. And like I said, Xiuyan wasn't just a wizard. He was an entrepreneur. A business-wizard. The guy had resources.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_389.t: That's right. When last we left our heroes, Tsui and I were beelining for the vault where Old Man Xiuyan kept his most valuable possessions. And lo and behold, it was there, just like we thought it'd be. The door was open and everything.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_397.t: The usual stuff. Explosives, pop turrets, that kind of thing. Cad and his sister Yasmin took care of most of it without too much trouble.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_399.t: Hey, like I said, it was free. There weren't really even any rules to follow, which suited me fine. Malvina was sort of the leader of our little nest, but we never really listened to her. She was always trying to play the mom card, instill a sense of responsibility into us. It was cute. She did have a propensity for getting things done, though. I have to give her that.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_401.t: I dunno. Maybe. Never really thought about it before, but... yeah, maybe.
A corner of her mouth curls upward.
I guess that I'm a water rat now.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_404.t: Damn straight. And the traps weren't even that big a deal with Cad and his sister there to work on them. They disarmed most of the really bad stuff, made the deck safe to lounge around on.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_420.t: She puts her hands on her hips, sending Madness scurrying.
Are you ready for your next lesson?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_425.t: She flips a tangled rope of hair back over her shoulder.
Go ahead, then. If you need to ask, ask.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_432.t: She takes in a breath. Lets it out slowly.
...You, ah, you remember the lesson from last time? The one about Tsui and the Shiny Object?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_438.t: No. No, it was pretty bad.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_446.t: She bites her lip, grimacing at the unpleasant memories stewing in her head.

Deep down in the pit of my gut, I could feel the thrumming vibration of the Shiny Object. That heartbeat feeling that I had in the warehouse - it was back, and stronger than ever. Whatever that rock was, it was awake.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_452.t: Yeah, well. They were under a lot of stress at the time, and I *had* just crashed through their door like a charging rhinoceros.
Adrenalin and abject terror can make a girl do some stupid things, I'll tell you what.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_453.t: The smile on her face dies. She looks away.
Anyway, it wasn't an attack. There were no hostile invaders on the Sinking Ship, and our client had nothing to do with what was going on.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_455.t: Yeah, that's a good note. Wish I'd had you there at the time to keep me from doing it.
Adrenalin and abject terror can make a girl do some stupid things, I'll tell you what.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_461.t: I don't know why he did it. Frankly, I don't care. The only thing that's important here is that he did.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_486.t: You know how it is. We may have washed the blood off of the decks, but the energy of the Sinking Ship had changed... it wasn’t the carefree haven that I'd loved anymore. And as sensible as Malvina’s rules were, I wasn’t up to living under them. I had to go.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_488.t: ...Some of the things that Tsui let loose actually came in handy, funny enough. He'd summoned so many of them that he couldn't hope to control them all, and that gave me an opening to use them to my advantage. I was able to steer some of Tsui's followers into a pack of the damned things. It worked. Wasn't pretty, though.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_496.t: Yeah. It's a lot like a panic attack, really... that full-body certainty that something is *wrong.*

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_500.t: No, Seattle. Not for no reason. Auntie Cheng and people like her serve a purpose. You get a job from her, and you can trust that she's vetted the client. That’s important. It protects the whole team.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_505.t: I'd have preferred it if she'd ditched the thing. But in the end, that wasn't my choice to make. From what I've heard, she's still in power over there, so I guess that it worked out okay.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_507.t: Her voice fills with grim satisfaction.
He didn’t even live long enough to watch his mutiny fail.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_511.t: Yeah. Me, too. Now get a move on. I've got Gobbet things to do.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_512.t: Yeah, yeah... say what you like, you keep coming back. Now get a move on. I have Gobbet things to do.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_514.t: Not by a long shot. But if you aren't in a learning mood, we can shoot the breeze about something else.
She puts her hands on her hips, sending Madness scurrying.
So. What did you want to talk about?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_518.t: She looks up from the saucepan, blinking. A corner of her mouth twitches upward, but the half-smile falls away almost immediately.
Oh... oh, hey, Seattle. Caught me at a bad time, I was just making dinner.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_519.t: She blinks down at the duck, and her mouth dips into a frown.
Yeah. That'd probably be smarter, wouldn't it? I'm, uh... I'm not really thinking clearly today.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_522.t: She rubs the bridge of her nose with a pair of greasy fingers, leaving smudges of duck fat behind.
It's weird. Ever since our last lesson, I've felt... I dunno. Preoccupied. With the Sinking Ship.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_524.t: She looks down at the pot full of mangled duck on the counter. Closes her eyes.

I guess I'm just worried about Cad and Mal. Which is weird, because why would I be? I mean, I haven't even spoken to them in years. Hell, if anything, *they* should be worried about *me.* I'm the one who gets shot at for a living.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_526.t: She smacks her thigh, leaving a hand print of duck fat behind on her trousers.
Tell you what... *that's* what we should be talking about right now. The damned Walled City. *It's* what's really doing this to all of us.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_537.t: Yeah! 'Course we can. So, uh... we'll do that then! Right now.
She stops talking. There's a long, awkward pause.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_542.t: Y'know what? I know what this is. It's my own personal version of the same thing that we're all feeling. The psychic sewage that's being dredged up in all of us by... whatever's happening in the Walled City.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_547.t: She blinks down at the duck, and her mouth dips into a frown.
Oh. Yeah, I guess that it is getting a little squished, isn't it?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_548.t: I could use a bigger pot, but I'd have to wash it first.
She glances down at the piles of dirty clothes and assorted detritus on the floor of her cabin.
...And to do that, I'd have to find it. But I'm hungry now, and I've got this duck, so...
She gives the bird another heave. Something gives way with an audible crunch.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_550.t: She blinks down at the duck, and her mouth dips into a frown.
Oh. It isn't winning, it's just being stubborn. I'll make it fit, don't you worry.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_552.t: Hmm. This'd be easier with a bigger pot. I'd have to wash it first, though.
She glances down at the piles of dirty clothes and assorted detritus on the floor of her cabin, frowning.
...And to do that, I'd have to find it.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_556.t: Her jaw works to manage the enormous quantity of crispy dough in her mouth, but she doesn't seem to be making much headway.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_557.t: She struggles to move her jaw, but her mouth is stuffed too full for her to make much headway. She gestures feebly toward a pitcher of water on the ground near your feet.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_559.t: ...Okay. Yeah. Note to self: the Pastry Terrace on Prince Edward Road makes 'em *dry.*
She takes another swig from the pitcher, grimacing.
"Moist green apple and jellied trotter" my ass. That thing was like a desert made of pig.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_560.t: I was challenging myself! For science! And it wasn't the *whole* pastry, it was just half of one.
She lifts the remaining pastry for your inspection. The pork floss that coats its exterior looks almost festive; it's more reminiscent of a beige, curly tinsel than it is a food.
You, uh... you want the rest?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_562.t: She drops the mangled pastry into a clean pot on the floor.
Suit yourself. I'll have it later.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_570.t: Anyway! Was there something that you wanted to talk about?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_576.t: She waves her hand, as if to brush off the question.
Nope. Nah. Don't worry about it. Everything's going just fine on the raft, I'm sure. And even if there were something going on out there, we've got bigger fish to fry.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_588.t: Nope. I'd already decided not to. Before you and Izz showed up, I'd pretty much made up my mind to scuttle the raft and send everyone on it to a watery grave. Probably wouldn't even have felt bad about it afterward. I mean, who would've been around to tell me I was wrong? Nobody, that's who - 'cause they'd be dead.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_590.t: ...This whole "introspection" thing kinda sucks, I've gotta say.
She looks up at you, scowling.
It's much more comforting to just assume that all of my past decisions were right.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_591.t: ...Yeah. Y'know, Seattle, you aren't helping my mood any. I'm kind of having a tough time over here.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_598.t: ...But yeah, I get it. There's probably even some value there. I'm never going to start second-guessing everything that I do, though. That just isn't me.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_601.t: Oh! Actually, there are *two* things that I can say for certain. I think that Rat is happy that the Shiny Object is gone.
She fishes in her pocket for a few seconds, then produces a chunk of stone the size of a marble. It appears to be made of the same marbled red jade that the Shiny Object was.
Check it out - she gave me a present.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_603.t: From Rat, like I said. She *literally* gave it to me. Put it right in the palm of my hand. I had a vision. It had colorful lights, dancing rodents, the works. And when the world came back into focus, I was holding this magic marble.
She tosses the stone into the air and catches it with her other hand.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_605.t: So you mark my words, Seattle: the next time we're in the field, something magical is going to happen. I mean, *literally* magical. Possibly with explosions, it's too early to tell.
It'll be a journey of discovery for both of us.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_608.t: 'Course I am! I had a vision. It had colorful lights, dancing rodents, the works. And when the world came back into focus, this magic marble was in the palm of my hand.
She tosses the stone into the air and catches it with her other hand.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_611.t: I think that Rat is happy that the Shiny Object is gone.
She pauses for a moment, considering.
...That, or maybe that stupid egg did something to me. I don't know for certain. Either way, something is... different. I can feel it in my gut.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_613.t: The corner of her mouth tilts downward.
Yes, O Wise One. You're teaching me. Now quit being smug about it and give me an answer.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_623.t: I think we kinda do. I mean, we didn't exactly bend over backward to help Cad with his mutiny, did we? And I think that he kind of needed us to.
I, uh... I feel really bad about how this all turned out.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_624.t: Yeah, I guess. But, I mean, none of them made it out... not anyone.
I, uh... I feel really bad about how this all turned out.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_625.t: I don't wanna argue about it. Let's just say that it's a good thing you were there.
She shuffles her feet, stirring a pile of dirty blankets on the floor.
What we walked into on that raft... well, it was pretty terrible. More so than I could've imagined.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_627.t: It's good! I mean... probably not, like, *morally* good, but I think that I can use it.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_632.t: Again, Rat gave it to me. I think that I'll be okay.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_637.t: Don't be. It came from Rat - I'm pretty sure of that. The dancing rodents in my vision were a pretty good indicator.
She lets the stone roll in the palm of her hand, smiling. Its polished exterior glitters as it moves.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_640.t: You're damn right we will. Oh, and Seattle...?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_643.t: Leave 'em off. The jingling might scare Folly. There was this terrible incident at a Christmas pageant once, and, well...
She gives you a sly smile.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_645.t: Still smiling, she waves you off with the back of her hand.
Scurry along now. Your former mentor needs her rest.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_647.t: She gives you a sly smile.
Now scurry along. Your former mentor needs her rest.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_648.t: She gives you a sly smile.
Now scurry along... your former mentor needs her rest.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_649.t: Yeah? Can't wait to see you prove it.
She does her best to stifle a yawn.
...Maybe later, though. It's getting late, and I could use some shut-eye.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_650.t: Smiling slyly, she waves you off with the back of her hand.
Scurry along now. Your former mentor needs her rest.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_651.t: And miss out on all of this fun? Never. Oh, and Seattle...?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_653.t: Really? Sure, then... it's all yours. Here, catch.
She tosses the remaining pastry to you. It sails through the air, glistening suggestively in the dim cabin light.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_657.t: She blinks at you in astonishment.
Huh. I can't believe you ate that. I'm impressed.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_661.t: She stares down at the mashed lump of dough, her eyebrows raised.
Well, that was lucky. Guess I'm eating it later.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_664.t: Hey... that was kind of a dickish thing to do. I might still have eaten that.
I mean, probably not, but there was a chance.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_666.t: Don't knock it until you've tried it, Seattle. It makes you sound ignorant.
She stabs a thumb into her chest.
Now, me, I *have* tried it, and I can tell you that it's *disgusting.* But that opinion carries authority, because it comes from *experience.*

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_667.t: So anyway. You, uh... you want the rest?
She lifts the remaining pastry for your inspection. The forest of pork floss that tops the roll flutters suggestively.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_668.t: Hey, if you want it, it's all yours. Drink lots of water, though.
She tosses the remaining pastry to you. As it sails through the air, the pork floss that clings to its outer layer flutters suggestively.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_669.t: Her eyes narrow. With her cheeks overstuffed as they are, the effect is less threatening than it is comical.
She raises her hands, fingers splayed, and holds them at shoulder height. A typical spellcasting posture.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_674.t: It's been a long day, and you're still in my room. Why are you still in my room?
Go away, Seattle. We can talk more later if you want, but right now you're cutting into my beauty rest.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_677.t: No time to talk now. Remember what I said about having Gobbet things to do? Yeah, that still applies.
G'bye. We can hang out tomorrow, or something.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_680.t: Ugh. Don't remind me. That place is every bit as foul and screwed up as I remember it.
Gobbet shudders, wrapping her arms tightly across her body.
If you ever want to drag me back there, you'd better have a damn good reason for it.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_681.t: After a moment, Gobbet gets a mischevious twinkle in her eye and she draws herself up straight.
I can't believe you set a ghost loose on Bao's men. Or that Kindly didn't believe me when I told her about it! Tsk. See if I help her out, if a ghost ever comes for her.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_689.t: Gobbet positively vibrates with excitement, clapping her hands together with a huge grin.

That. Was. AWESOME. Think about it: we broke into some rich jerk's manor house, stole his artifacts so they could go to a museum, and we fought *mummies*! If that's not the plot of a trid blockbuster, I don't know what is.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_706.t: It always surprises me how much corporations will pay to screw up a competitor's feng shui. Hell, they'll even take each other to court over architecture that blocks qi. It has real impact, though. A little shift here and there might not seem like much, but even fractions of a penny lost on their stock value is millions.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_708.t: I guess all that history with his old unit was really eating at Nibbles, huh?
Gobbet folds her arms, glancing off into the distance.

I wasn't too happy with him being here when you first invited him to join the team, you know. But I was wrong. He's a solid asset - maybe not the most talkative, but a good guy to have in your corner.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_709.t: But turning Ishida into a ghoul like that, just to make a point?
That's some cold revenge, right there. It's over the line. I'm glad he's finally free of them, but I wouldn't wish that kind of fate on anybody.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_710.t: I'm glad he killed Ishida, and didn't turn him into a ghoul. That would've been a colder fate than anybody deserves.
With Ishida dead, he's finally free. Maybe now he'll learn to lighten up a little... Or at least crack a smile now and again.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_711.t: Gobbet's expression darkens, and she flips some ropes of hair back over one shoulder.

You know, everybody says it's bad form to back out on a job. What about if the Johnson decides to lie and screw you over? Everybody seems to accept that as standard procedure. Drives me up the damn wall, every time.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_712.t: But I guess it's better to play it safe, count on your rep, and get the job done. I know why you made the call you did. I just wish I could've put a few rounds into Tigath as he was smugly sauntering off.
It just doesn't seem right, letting him get away with playing us like that.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_713.t: I'm glad we gave Hwang get the data rather than Tigath. He may be a psychopath in charge of a gang of murderous goblins, but he told us the truth, at least.
Frankly, it's not my problem any more. I got paid, and I'm done with it.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_714.t: Deciding to gun them both down wasn't a move I expected. Seems to have worked out, though - we sent a message not to mess with us, and also we're not the kind of people to take kindly to threats.
She flexes, as if to show you how big her arms are.
Don't mess with us, or we'll put you down. Boom! I like it.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_719.t: I think we're closing in on the end here, Seattle. With the data we got from the Plastic Faced Man, Josephine's number is just about up.
She grins, patting you gently on the arm.
Just think, you'll be able to get your dad back. That's gotta be worth something, right?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_721.t: I can't believe you told Kindly Cheng that we let the Plastic guy go, though. I mean, we still have our skin, and we aren't chained to a rock at the bottom of a lake, so I'm counting it a win. But still... next time, just lie, okay?
The gentle pats turn into slaps. Her smile looks forced.
I'm not joking, by the way. Don't do that again. Seriously.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_723.t: You didn't bring me on our last run. So no, I don't have much to say.
Nice to have a night off, I guess.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_727.t: Yeah, sure... why not? I'll share.
To start, I guess that you're gonna need to know what the Walled City was like when me and Izz were kids. She didn't tell you anything about that, did she?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_729.t: She reaches down. Absentmindedly strokes Madness's silky coat. The rat chirps happily at her caress.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_748.t: I decided to go inside one more time. If I found Is0bel, I'd offer to lead her out. If I didn't, well...

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_751.t: ...Anyway, I *did* bump into her. And she *did* follow me. And Rat got us both out safe.
She offers you a weak smile.
So there. Happy endings all around.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_753.t: Yeah, I knew that you'd get it. And I *did* bump into her, and she *did* follow me, and Rat got us both out safe. So there you have it. Happy endings all around.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_754.t: That's right. More accurately, Rat got her out of there. She came to me in a dream, showed me that whatever was going bad in the Walled City was getting worse. The whole place was rotting from the inside out, choking in bad qi and lost hope.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_764.t: Dead, I think. I'm not exactly sure.
They were gone, though. Out of the picture. I was pretty much free to do whatever I wanted.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_776.t: She reaches down. Absentmindedly strokes Madness's silky coat. The rat chirps happily at her caress.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_777.t: They went bad. Crazy. That Pig shaman that I knew wound up going full-on toxic. It was horrifying... he burned himself out in an orgy of blood.
Nasty business, and not for me.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_781.t: You think that I didn't want to?
She sweeps a loose strand of hair out of her eyes.
Look, it was going to be dangerous enough to get Is0bel out on her own. Dragging a whole family out with her would've been impossible.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_785.t: ...Besides which, Is0bel didn't *want* to bring her family. She wanted to get away from them as much as she did from the Walled City. Never could get her to tell me why.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_786.t: Good. You would've been right to.
...Besides which, Is0bel didn't *want* to bring her family. She wanted to get away from them as much as she did from the Walled City. Never could get her to tell me why.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_791.t: Her jaw stops working mid-chew. Slowly, she nods.
Yeah... yeah, I have. We all have. Everyone in town.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_792.t: Let me guess. You saw the Walled City, and a black whirlpool, and teeth. Thousands and thousands of teeth.
You don't need to answer. I already know that I'm right, because I just had the same dream. Creepy, I know.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_793.t: The dreams are coming from inside the Walled City. I'm sure of it. All of the negative energy pent up in there... all of that pain and anger and poisonous qi... it's leaking out. And while we sleep, it's getting into us.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_796.t: She tries on a smile. Fails. Does her best to laugh it off.
Don't worry, Seattle, we're fine here. But, uh... let's talk about something else, huh? I don't like thinking about the Walled City very much. Too many old fears and bad feelings.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_800.t: I've had a warning from Rat. She says that we should leave.

54da5dae306364a88e0035e1_15.t: Gobbet folds her arms over her chest, rocking back on her heels.
Ugh, what a crock of shit. Are we really gonna help this guy out? This isn't our problem.

54da5dc6306364a88e0035ea_1.t: Squeezing water out of her ropes of hair, Gobbet nods in satisfaction.

Nicely done. We're almost to the goods, and the rest of the ship's got no idea we're here. Fighting down here will definitely get their attention, though. Let's play it smart. We get the goods, radio our pickup, and get out.

54da5dc6306364a88e0035ea_20.t: Gobbet shifts back and forth on her feet, ropes of hair flying as she looks left and right.
Shit, shit, shit... This isn't my idea of a good time. We're too exposed on this run. Alarms make me nervous.

54dbef30653937cc15006dd1_17.t: At the very least it'll keep security's hands full.

54dcfec1303031bc0a006c6f_235.t: The dossier lists Gobbet's real name as Yuchun Gwei, and her place of birth as Xi'an, Shaanxi. According to the contents, she is 23 years old, and served with the Baihu corporate military until conviction for insubordination lead to her dishonorable discharge. While you can't be certain Gobbet doesn't speak Mandarin, the idea of her being in the military - any military - is downright laughable.

54e26a883063641415004409_6.t: One of Gobbet's rats scurries out from under her ropes of hair to peer at Hwang. She her head and addresses the rat in cooing tones, stroking it's head.
Yes, he's such a big strong man, isn't he? Pushing around us mean ol' shadowrunners! He doesn't know how dangerous we can be, does he? Nooooo, he doesn't!

54e26a883063641415004409_31.t: Gobbet regards Hwang with indifference. She folds her arms over her chest and rocks back on her heels.
So. If you're some kind of magical hacker and you know Tigath's double-crossed us, where's the proof? Give us something we can look at.

54e26a883063641415004409_42.t: Cut and dry, nothing! That's our guy, and he's loaded for bear.
Gobbet shakes her head, scowling deeply.
Oh, Mr. Johnson. You should have played it straight with us.

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_8.t: Gobbet laughs, holding her hands up to her face. Her rats crawl out from her sleeves, one sitting on each palm.
Look here, babies. It's a man who's going to get himself shot if he pulls that gun, because he's got no idea how to use it.

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_16.t: You hide, dumbass. What the hell do you think?

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_40.t: Yeah... You know something? I don't trust you.
Gobbet watches the elf as he fidgets, shaking her head slowly.
If you cared about your family, you'd want the data out of Eastern Tiger's hands. If they're on to you, they can just kill you can take it.

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_52.t: Gobbet has an evil glint in her eye as she leans toward Tigath.
No sudden moves. I'm feeling awfully nervous. You move too quickly, accidents can happen.

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_84.t: Don't got much choice, do you?
Gobbet pulls a handful of peanuts out of her pocket, feeding one to each of her rats before popping the rest in her mouth.
You want the data and samples. We want some answers. The glory of the free market, neh?

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_95.t: Gobbet tosses another handful of peanuts down her gullet.
That's funny. I mean, not ha-ha funny, but... They should be making threats or issuing demands to you, if they know.

54e3cf8e653937dc01000fd2_3.t: Sheesh, put me on the spot, why don't'cha? Okay, okay... I see a two, four, five, and something that feels vaguely like a nine, but I don't know their order. This might take a sec. Cover me, alright?
Gobbet hunches over the keypad and hastily mashes its buttons, testing different combinations.

54e6169d306364441400703d_6.t: Glancing in your direction, Gobbet nods in satisfaction.
This shit we dealt with tonight? That's why I cut and run. If Auntie Cheng can't find a buyer for that data, we're out a payday.

54e6531030636444140071ff_13.t: Gobbet glances around nervously. She strokes the neck of one of her rats as it peeks out from under her jacket, obviously as worried as she is.
I don't like this - not one bit. That guy that called us is gonna make a play before we're done here, and we're gonna be the ones under the gun. By all rights, we should cut and run.

54e6531030636444140071ff_31.t: Gobbet looks up from picking her teeth with one fingernail.
What are the odds on Hwang coming after us? He doesn't seem like the type of guy to deal with disappointment in a calm way. More of a "smash everything with a hammer" kind of guy.

54e6531030636444140071ff_47.t: Gobbet hacks a few times and spits a wad off phlegm onto the MTR tracks.
So who do you think the joker who called us is? And more importantly, do you think he's got anything cool we can steal if he's an asshole?

54e6531030636444140071ff_48.t: Gobbet pushes her ropes of hair back over her shoulders, swelling up with glee.
Sweet! That means I can take his stuff. C'mon, I wanna see if we can get a double payday!

54e6531030636444140071ff_49.t: Gobbet deflates a little, shoulders slumping.
Well. I guess that's okay... I reserve the right to lift some stuff from his pockets if he's a jerk, though.

54e6531030636444140071ff_63.t: Man, that Hwang character sure was buttering you up.
Gobbet straightens her hair, and cackles quietly.
I bet he thinks he's got us all fooled. That murderous little bastard.

54e6531030636444140071ff_65.t: Hey, hey... Don't get me wrong, I'm all for changing it up if we get paid more and end up not getting shot at by as many people.
Gobbet sticks her hand out, rubbing her thumb and fingers together.
Believe me, I am all about the nuyen.

54eb9f4a3430617007000f3f_4.t: Pretty hard to not see that guy.

54ecfca8343061e01b00108f_5.t: You heard the man. If you know what's good for ya, you'll clap your trap, chicken man.
Can this schooner go any slower? I wanna get paid already. There's a cinnamon bun stall in Kai Tak with my name on it.

54ecfca8343061e01b00108f_13.t: Gobbet leans toward you and gives you a sideways look, her words just loud enough for Rooster to hear.

After all we've done for him, too. Maybe we haven't been accommodating enough. Maybe we should rough him up a bit - a broken jaw here, a punctured lung there - so it looks like he put up a fight. Because right now all he looks like is a coward.

54f4d9223430612012004418_10.t: Gobbet lets out a small whistle.
This lunk's got even more spice than Duncan.

54f8ce523430612c15004d70_3.t: I guess that the client said something about "other ways" to bypass the check-in system inside the building, but he didn't go into specifics.
Kindly had some choice words about that, but whatever. We'll find a way past security.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_8.t: Gobbet balls up her fists, placing them against her hips. Widening her stands, she puffs out her chest, beaming proudly.
Awesome! Now we can be friends!

54ff5820306364a01b004921_27.t: Gobbet rolls her eyes, throwing up her hands in defeat.
We can stand around here and compare dick sizes, or we can leave as one group. Then sort this out when K-E isn't sending armed goons to fill us with holes.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_35.t: Gobbet gives you a significant look. She speaks quietly, so that only you can hear.
Boss, I got good money on that keycard being the one that'll open the private safe Racter mentioned. We should ask them for it.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_56.t: Sure, whatever. Let's just get the hell out of here.

552f062b323431e41b0069fa_32.t: She breathes a sigh of relief.

552f062b323431e41b0069fa_34.t: Her voice is heavy with disappointment.

552f062b323431e41b0069fa_35.t: Her voice is heavy with disappointment.

552fd5cf306364d01000702b_8.t: Gobbet grins broadly, tilting her head back.

54d96b813030316814006f66_55.t: She chuckles to herself as she eases a card into an empty expansion slot.
Most deckers are early adopters. They buy whatever's newest... shell out huge amounts of nuyen in hopes of riding the bleeding edge. Stupid.

54d96b813030316814006f66_58.t: Do we look like we're hurting for space to you?

I've got a whole boat to work with - your cabin included, if I told Gobbet I needed it.

54d96b813030316814006f66_60.t: ...Huh. Well, I was joking. But if you're really okay with it, I might someday. We'll see.

54d96b813030316814006f66_61.t: Good. That's a good sign. Shows that you're thinking clearly.
Frowning, she yanks a stick of memory from the old deck.

54d96b813030316814006f66_62.t: Got a couple years to sit here and learn?
Frowning, she yanks a stick of memory from the old deck.
I can give you tips here and there. But really learning how to do this takes time that we don't have. Time, and desperation, and a need to make do with what you've got.

54d96b813030316814006f66_64.t: Of course I do. That isn't what this is about. I built the Octopus myself. She's *mine.* And no one else in the world has one.

54d96b813030316814006f66_65.t: She yanks a fried chip from the deck's motherboard and neatly sets it aside, then fumbles in a nearby bin for a replacement.

54d96b813030316814006f66_66.t: You wanna pay me for it? Actually, scratch that - I don't have the time.

54d96b813030316814006f66_67.t: If I were to build a machine for you, I'd have to neglect the Octopus.
She yanks a fried chip from the deck's motherboard and neatly sets it aside, then fumbles in a nearby bin for a replacement.

54d96b813030316814006f66_70.t: Was she your friend? You didn't seem that close.

54d96b813030316814006f66_71.t: I was. I may not show a lot of emotions, that doesn't mean that I don't have any.
She returns her attention to the Octopus, turning her back on you.

54d96b813030316814006f66_72.t: You don't have to apologize. Just change the subject.

54d96b813030316814006f66_73.t: Okay. I don't like small talk. Does that count?

54d96b813030316814006f66_74.t: Look, it's nothing personal. I just don't think that I'm a very interesting person.

54d96b813030316814006f66_77.t: There is a long pause before she responds. When she does, she sounds vaguely hurt.

54d96b813030316814006f66_78.t: Yes. You're still a mystery to me.

54d96b813030316814006f66_79.t: She turns. Gives you her full attention.

54d96b813030316814006f66_82.t: We'll start off easy. Tell me what you really think about Duncan.

54d96b813030316814006f66_83.t: I've heard how you abandoned him back in Seattle. If that's love, you've got a funny way of showing it.

54d96b813030316814006f66_87.t: And that would make it my turn.

54d96b813030316814006f66_94.t: She gestures at the cabin walls around you.
You enjoy it, don't you? Living like this. Working the shadows.

54d96b813030316814006f66_95.t: I knew it. I could read it all over you.

54d96b813030316814006f66_98.t: We may have more in common than I'd thought.

54d96b813030316814006f66_99.t: Her head tilts as she studies your face.
And, I think, my last question for you.

54d96b813030316814006f66_103.t: Shit. It's even later than I thought, and I've still got a long way to go before the Octopus is fixed.
She begins to turn away, her attention fixed on the stack of partially disassembled cyberdecks arrayed in front of her.

54d96b813030316814006f66_109.t: Her cheeks flush, just like they did the last time. She averts her eyes.
No. I'm sorry, but... no. Later, maybe. But not now.

54d96b813030316814006f66_111.t: Blinking, she turns away and buries herself in the Octopus's splayed innards.
Please excuse me. I have work to do.

54d96b813030316814006f66_112.t: Blinking, she turns away and buries herself in the Octopus's splayed innards.
Please excuse me. I have work to do.

54d96b813030316814006f66_113.t: If you've got another question, go ahead and ask. But be quick about it.

54d96b813030316814006f66_115.t: In that case, I'm sorry for you. You'll adapt, though.
She shifts her head in the direction of the Walled City.

54d96b813030316814006f66_116.t: Her brow furrows, and after a moment, she screws her eyes shut.
We met as kids. In the Walled City. My prison was her playground.

54d96b813030316814006f66_117.t: She shifts her gaze to the Octopus.
I just... have a hard time remembering those days. My childhood is kind of a blur.

54d96b813030316814006f66_119.t: Of course I'm okay. I'm fine.
She shifts her gaze to the Octopus.
I just... have a hard time remembering those days. My childhood is kind of a blur.

54d96b813030316814006f66_120.t: No, it isn't that. I'll be fine.
She shifts her gaze to the Octopus.
I just... have a hard time remembering those days. My childhood is kind of a blur.

54d96b813030316814006f66_122.t: I'd rather not talk about it. Ask me something else.

54d96b813030316814006f66_125.t: I'm done with this game. Please leave - I've got work to do.

54d96b813030316814006f66_128.t: Her eyes flit to the doorway of Gobbet's cabin. The corner of her mouth tilts upward into a slight smile.

54d96b813030316814006f66_130.t: But at least you're up-front about it. None of that passive-aggressive stuff.

54d96b813030316814006f66_131.t: That's kid of sad, actually. The only thing worse than hatred is indifference.

54d96b813030316814006f66_132.t: She turns back to the Octopus.
Fine. Hit me up if you change your mind. Until then, I have work to do, so if you don't mind...

54d96b813030316814006f66_134.t: She turns. Gives you her full attention.

Tell you what, though. I'm gonna make you a deal. If my being quiet is a problem for you, then we'll talk. I'll tell you all about myself, satisfy your curiosity. But in exchange, you need to satisfy mine.

54d96b813030316814006f66_137.t: She turns. Gives you her full attention.
...But I'll tell you what. I'm gonna make you a deal. You've pulled me away from what I was doing, so I'll satisfy your curiosity. And in exchange, you need to satisfy mine.

54d96b813030316814006f66_138.t: She turns to give you her attention.
It won't be twenty. But fine, let's play. I'll go first.

54d96b813030316814006f66_142.t: No, it's fine, I can multitask. I mean, if this bothers you, you can try coming back later... but I'll probably still be working.
From behind, you see her shoulders lift into a shrug.

54d96b813030316814006f66_145.t: She continues to study the cyberdeck's inner components, clucking her tongue. Skinny fingers dart in to label a stray wire with a strip of electrical tape.

54d96b813030316814006f66_149.t: Is0bel's avatar flickers onto the screen.
I saw that you accepted my offer. That was the right choice, you'll see. I'm ready to make our run on DeckCon whenever you are.

54d96b813030316814006f66_150.t: Is0bel's avatar flickers onto the screen.
You haven't accepted the job offer I sent you. Is there a problem?

54d96b813030316814006f66_152.t: A frown crosses the avatar's face.
Yes, it does. Why would you think that? It's a simple job for a valuable reward that'll help the entire team. We'd probably be back before anyone even realized that we were gone.

54d96b813030316814006f66_155.t: The avatar grinds its teeth. Sparks fly on the screen.
It's important to *me,* okay? It's something that I left behind when I left the Whampoa. Something personal.

54d96b813030316814006f66_156.t: If that isn't a good enough reason to spend an afternoon doing what we spend most of our time doing anyway, well, then...
I don't know what to say.

54d96b813030316814006f66_158.t: Hello down there! It's good to see you again.
She smiles down on you from the monitor, her outline scintillating with blue-white light. She looks peaceful. At ease.

54d96b813030316814006f66_162.t: Y'know... I've been thinking about the question you asked me a little while back. The one about the Walled City.
The, ah... the one that I dodged.

54d96b813030316814006f66_167.t: The avatar fidgets on the screen.
There were a variety of factors in play.

54d96b813030316814006f66_170.t: The avatar fidgets on the screen.
Okay. Fair enough. I'm pleasantly surprised that you aren't pushing me on this.

54d96b813030316814006f66_173.t: Please don't call me that. I let Gobbet get away with it because we go way back, but I've never liked it.
And no, you don't get it. The problem isn't that I'm tortured by bad memories, or anything like that. The truth of the matter is, I can't remember my childhood.

54d96b813030316814006f66_177.t: Out there in meatspace, I usually feel uncomfortable in my own skin.
The avatar offers you a lopsided smile.
I'd probably just live in here if I could.

54d96b813030316814006f66_181.t: I'd rather not go into it. It's personal. Suffice it to say that I've never missed them... at least, not until now.

54d96b813030316814006f66_182.t: Nothing that I didn't ask for. Now please, drop it.

54d96b813030316814006f66_183.t: Great. I'll keep that in mind.

54d96b813030316814006f66_185.t: The avatar licks its lips. Nods.

54d96b813030316814006f66_188.t: The scintillating figure turns to you on the screen.
Did you know that Kowloon Walled City is supposed to be cursed? That's what the locals believe. We had ghost stories and everything.

54d96b813030316814006f66_189.t: Our own homegrown legends about things that haunted the Walled City. Demons from another place. The Yama Kings.

54d96b813030316814006f66_193.t: "Don't go under that arch, or Qian Ya will catch you."
The avatar grabs at the air, its hands leaving glowing trails as they move.
"She'll rip out your teeth, tie your tongue in a knot, and make you her slave for eternity."

54d96b813030316814006f66_195.t: The avatar cocks its head, smirking. A dismissive gesture.
It's all bullshit, of course. But everybody in the Walled City believed it.

54d96b813030316814006f66_196.t: No. They can't be. If they were, the body count in the Walled City would be a lot higher. The Yama Kings are just a fiction that my parents and neighbors invented to excuse their own actions. "I can't get ahead because demons are keeping me down."

54d96b813030316814006f66_207.t: Everyone did. The locals were convinced that the slum had been poisoned forever by things that haunted it. Demons from another place. The Yama Kings.

54d96b813030316814006f66_216.t: The Is0bel avatar closes its eyes for a moment, then opens them with a smile.
Done. You'll find the pertinent documents waiting in your inbox. If you decide to accept the mission, just check the box in the message documentation and it'll ping my commlink automatically.

54d96b813030316814006f66_218.t: Fantastic! I can't wait to hear what you think.

54d96b813030316814006f66_220.t: All right. Your call. But if you change your mind, just say the word... I really think that this will be good for the team in the long run.

54d96b813030316814006f66_222.t: The avatar smiles down on you from the monitor.

Of course it does. This would be a small job - a side gig for me, outside of the scope of the work that Auntie Cheng brings you. As for the payday, I think I've got a line on some software that could help us. I can't get to it on my own, though... it's a two-person job at least.

54d96b813030316814006f66_223.t: Nope. Sorry. Jacking into the Octopus is how I relax after a long day of work. Some people go to sports bars or strip clubs. I do this. As a rule, I don't spoil my leisure time with shop talk.

54d96b813030316814006f66_224.t: There's a difference between *the job* and *our lives.* It's after hours, and I don't wanna talk about work. So if you wouldn't mind changing the subject...

54d96b813030316814006f66_225.t: It is. So if you wouldn't mind changing the subject...

54d96b813030316814006f66_227.t: Is0bel's avatar blossoms onto the screen at your approach.

54d96b813030316814006f66_230.t: You did good back there. The run went well.

We got the stick. The key to my old memories. All I have to do now is slot it.

54d96b813030316814006f66_236.t: That's about the shape of it. I'm feeling hesitant about this.

54d96b813030316814006f66_241.t: Yes. Much easier. Still not without risk, but--

54d96b813030316814006f66_242.t: ...Because I knew that one day, I might need them.

54d96b813030316814006f66_249.t: I... oh, *no.* No no no no no.

54d96b813030316814006f66_250.t: As you reach for the cable, Is0bel weakly slaps at your hand.
Nuh-n-no... No, don't do that. It's almost d-done.

54d96b813030316814006f66_252.t: She clutches her temples in the palms of her hands.
...Now I remember why I didn't want these anymore.

54d96b813030316814006f66_254.t: No, I'm not all right. I'm the *opposite* of all right.
She clutches at her skull like she's afraid it's going to crack open.
There's a lot of bad stuff in here. Things that happened to friends of mine, to relatives... things that I *watched* happen... no...

54d96b813030316814006f66_256.t: These memories are crystal clear. It's like all of this shit happened yesterday.

54d96b813030316814006f66_258.t: She screws her eyes shut in concentration. Beads of sweat hang heavy on her brow.
I... I think so, yes. It'll take time for me to sift through all of this.

54d96b813030316814006f66_259.t: She opens her eyes again. Looks up at you.
I'll hold onto these memories for as long as I can... hopefully long enough to reveal something useful. No promises beyond that, though.

54d96b813030316814006f66_260.t: Don't thank me yet. I could still crack in a half hour and lock away the files.

54d96b813030316814006f66_261.t: One thing that does jump out at me... Rhombus archived some software up here.
She taps her temple with a fingernail.

54d96b813030316814006f66_262.t: Some sort of basic attack modification. I'll have to study it to be sure, but... I think that I can use it.

54d96b813030316814006f66_264.t: Yeah. It's all very exciting. But for now, just... give me some space, okay?
Like I said, it's gonna take time for me to make sense of this stuff.

54d96b813030316814006f66_270.t: She gives you a weak half smile.

54d96b813030316814006f66_271.t: I *know,* damn it. That's why I suggested this in the first place.
She screws her eyes shut in concentration. Beads of sweat hang heavy on her brow.
I... I think that there's useful information buried in here, but it's hard to say. It'll take time for me to sift through all of this.

54d96b813030316814006f66_272.t: Yes. I can. But this is harder than you'd think.
These memories are crystal clear. It's like all of this shit happened yesterday.

54d96b813030316814006f66_273.t: These memories are crystal clear. It's like all of this shit happened yesterday.

54d96b813030316814006f66_280.t: Look, I'm going to do this. I have to, for the good of the team. I *know* that there could be something locked away in storage that could help us. That's why I suggested the run in the first place. I'm just dragging my feet a little because I'm afraid.

54d96b813030316814006f66_289.t: She shoves a box of assorted mounting screws aside with her foot.

54d96b813030316814006f66_303.t: She looks away. Rubs her eyes.

54d96b813030316814006f66_305.t: She runs the tip of her tongue over her lips and nods. A look of fierce determination seizes her.
Go on. Ask me a question. I'm ready.

54d96b813030316814006f66_313.t: The avatar frowns down on you, a look of supreme disillusionment on its face.
I'm decking... I'm in my safe space. And it doesn't help. The memories are just as painful here as they are in the real world.

54d96b813030316814006f66_314.t: No. I guess that it doesn't.

It fucking should, though. I can't handle not having a way to escape from this!

54d96b813030316814006f66_315.t: I don't know, okay? I just don't. Y'know what, hang on...

54d96b813030316814006f66_317.t: You're here with more questions, right?
She coughs into the crook of her arm. Wipes her nose with her sleeve.
I'm sure that you are. Why else would you be bothering me? So spit 'em out, and I'll go digging around in that cesspit that I call a memory. Who knows? Maybe we'll find a clue.

54d96b813030316814006f66_319.t: She takes a deep breath. Makes a visible effort to calm herself.
Yeah, I know... I'm sorry. This isn't the person that I want to be. I'll be okay. I can handle this. Just ask me your questions, okay? Let's get this ball rolling.

54d96b813030316814006f66_323.t: She takes a deep breath. Makes a visible effort to calm herself.
No... no, that wasn't like me. This isn't the person that I want to be. I'll be okay. I can handle this. Just ask me your questions, okay? Let's get this ball rolling.

54d96b813030316814006f66_324.t: No... I wish that I could, but I can't. Not considering what we're up against.
Her voice takes on a bitter edge.
