The avatar winks off of the screen, and the terminal powers down.

54d96b813030316814006f66_332.t: She squeezes her eyes shut. Rubs the bridge of her nose between thumb and forefinger.
Anyway. I guess that you're here with more questions, right? Let's get them answered, then.

54d96b813030316814006f66_336.t: The avatar puts her hands on her hips. A defiant gesture.
Yeah, that's right. And if you were in my shoes, you'd do the same.

54d96b813030316814006f66_342.t: Hell, yes. You're damned right I do.
The avatar puts her hands on her hips. A defiant gesture.
If you were in my shoes, you'd do the same.

54d96b813030316814006f66_369.t: Yes. It happened when I was a kid, too. Not often, but occasionally. People would have bad dreams, and then become obsessed with them. Consumed by their own dread.

54d96b813030316814006f66_374.t: She wasn't dead. What he found was much worse than that.
She pauses. Looks you in the eye.
Oral surgery was involved. You don't want to hear the rest.

54d96b813030316814006f66_393.t: She pauses. Looks you in the eye.
You probably don't want to hear the rest. Oral surgery was involved.

54d96b813030316814006f66_397.t: I didn't get pressed into service in a brothel. The organleggers didn't get me. I only had to watch one of my siblings die.
...So, yeah. It could have been worse. But that doesn't make an early life in the Walled City any easier to bear.

54d96b813030316814006f66_398.t: Yes. My older brother, Cabdalle. Heroin overdose. He was ninteen. Almost the same age that I am now.

54d96b813030316814006f66_403.t: I know. We all carry the same scars - you, and me, and Gun Show, and Gobbet. Some run bigger and deeper than others, but we've all got them.

54d96b813030316814006f66_404.t: ...And that's enough of that. Ask me something else. I don't wanna talk about this anymore.

54d96b813030316814006f66_405.t: Yeah. I know. But I'm having a hard time with that right now. We all carry the same scars - you, and me, and Gun Show, and Gobbet. Some run bigger and deeper than others, but we've all got them.

54d96b813030316814006f66_406.t: I don't want mine any more.

54d96b813030316814006f66_414.t: Go ahead and ask her - I'm sure that she'll be happy to brag about it. But I think that we have more pressing matters to discuss.

54d96b813030316814006f66_418.t: All right. Fine. If that's what you want.

54d96b813030316814006f66_441.t: And her connection to Rat, and her willingness to risk her own neck on a whim. We were different people then. Still are now. But the differences were even more pronounced when we were kids.

54d96b813030316814006f66_442.t: ...And you want me to answer them for you.
All right. Maybe I can satisfy your curiosity.

54d96b813030316814006f66_443.t: I suppose that I owe you an explanation. It's my story, and I didn't really give you one.

54d96b813030316814006f66_444.t: Yes. But not for anything that they'd done. My parents were good people... they fled Somalia to try and find a better life for their children. It wasn't their fault that they wound up in the Walled City. I wanted them left behind for me.

54d96b813030316814006f66_448.t: Before you ask, I checked. They're gone now. I can't go riding to their rescue. I made a decision when I was a kid. It felt like the right one at the time. And now I have to live with it.

54d96b813030316814006f66_455.t: Not particularly. But I get the feeling that you'll look at me differently until I do, so I will.
I wanted them left behind because of this.

54d96b813030316814006f66_458.t: Is0bel shivers, looking away from you.

I'm still mad at you for making me go back there. I'd say it hasn't changed, but it seems like it has. For the worse. It's just like I remembered it, but darker. Meaner. The perfect place for a thug like Strangler Bao and his goons.

54d96b813030316814006f66_492.t: Is0bel simply shrugs, taking a long time to think before replying.

What do you want me to say? We helped him kill his old unit. I know that was important to him, but as far as I'm concerned, I was only there for the drone. It's nice we could help him out, but I'm not a charity. I'm in it for the money.

54d96b813030316814006f66_500.t: Yeah, of course I've got thoughts. It was my run, right?
...And. Um. Thank you for coming with me.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_36.t: She turns her back and begins grabbing her gear.
Well. We'd better go and get her, hadn't we?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_40.t: She turns her back and begins grabbing her gear.
Well. Apparently she's changed her mind. So we'd better go and get her, hadn't we?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_41.t: She rubs her temples between thumb and forefinger.
Why is nothing ever as easy as it should be?...Don't answer that. It's a rhetorical question.

54da34c5306364a88e00320d_2.t: After a few moments of fiddling with the cables, Is0bel closes the panel up.
There we go. I've rebooted the cameras and patched them into our commlinks, and locked ship security out. Couldn't get into the cameras inside the ship, though. They must be on a different circuit.

54da5dae306364a88e0035e1_32.t: Is0bel curses, raising a hand as if to slap her cyberdeck. She slowly lowers her hand and takes a deep breath.
He hung up before I could complete a trace on him. Dammit.

54da5dae306364a88e0035e1_64.t: I think that punk kid just got in way over his head.

54da5dc6306364a88e0035ea_5.t: Is0bel plants a hand on her hip, consulting her commlink.

I'm not picking up any radio chatter. We should be in the clear as long as we can avoid getting into any fights. The storm's noise is muffled enough down here that they'll definitely notice if we start something.

54da5dc6306364a88e0035ea_6.t: Is0bel hefts her rifle and nods.
You're the boss. Lead the way.

54da5dc6306364a88e0035ea_16.t: Is0bel checks the chamber on her rifle, looking at you somberly as she closes the bolt.
Ready for more resistance? I think they're searching for us, but don't know which deck we're on. But they'll definitely be guarding the cargo.

54dcfec1303031bc0a006c6f_236.t: Is0bel, in turn, is listed as Fatima Abukar. Apparently, Is0bel was educated at an early age in terrorism and piracy by the revolutionaries of the Ethiomalian Territories, and is wanted in connection with the bombing 2052 bomb attack on the French embassy in Johannesburg. Given that you know that Is0bel grew up here in Hong Kong, this information appears to be as laughably inaccurate as your own 'dossier'.

54e26a883063641415004409_5.t: Is0bel glares at Hwang, placing a hand on the butt of her grenade launcher.
Don't you tell us what does and doesn't matter. We're only here because we want to be.

54e26a883063641415004409_34.t: Is0bel taps away at her commlink, and after a moment she powers up her cyberdeck in local mode.
You have proof that Wright isn't who he says he is. Give it to me so I can analyze it.

54e26a883063641415004409_43.t: Glancing between the photographs and her deck, Is0bel nods.
These haven't been tampered with as far as I can tell. They're legitimate.

54e26a883063641415004409_50.t: Is0bel looks at you and rolls her eyes.

Oh yeah, I'm sure this guy had a totally legitimate reason to be heavily armed and armored, and to meet with a bunch of shadowrunners who later ended up dead. Especially since he was so worried about his wife and kids in Seoul.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_226.t: Sleeping in dumpsters. Running from ghouls. Fighting over scraps.

Double-Trey was only thirteen when...

54d96ae73030316814006f45_272.t: Well, I gotta tell you - you're good at running this crew. But there's something I still don't completely understand.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_285.t: Been thinking about Josephine Tsang. What she did to our family. What she did to Carter.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_288.t: Wu's mouth becomes a straight line.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_300.t: Yeah, what? There's only one way this'll go...

54d96ae73030316814006f45_301.t: Yeah, my way. There's one way this is gonna go...

54d96ae73030316814006f45_302.t: What? My way? That's right. There's one way this is gonna go...

54d96ae73030316814006f45_306.t: Man, that was when Ray's authority finally got tested.

When I finally stood up to him.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_307.t: I thought that might have been part of it. But to be fair, I wasn't listening either. That was our pattern, wasn't it? I wouldn't listen, he wouldn't listen, then we butted heads. Rinse and repeat once every few weeks.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_308.t: Yeah, I know. I've been working on it, but I definitely still have my moments. Ray and I had our little pattern, didn't we? I wouldn't listen, he wouldn't listen, then we butted heads. Rinse and repeat once every few weeks.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_327.t: He drops his gaze to the floor again.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_332.t: You think something could make *this?* look bad?

54d96ae73030316814006f45_344.t: Wu shrugs his shoulders, searches your eyes.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_351.t: He glances up. His eyes are rimmed in red.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_368.t: Especially that last time. The week you left.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_369.t: He pauses for a moment, scowling. Then gives his head an agitated shake.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_376.t: He scrubs his sweaty head with the towel.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_381.t: He rubs the towel under his arms.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_383.t: More than ever. Raymond's alive. I'm sure of it.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_388.t: Good. We're brothers. I'd hate to have to mess you up.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_389.t: Like I said, you can take me with you on the run or not - it's your call. But I intend to be there for the fun part.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_391.t: He drops his towel on his bunk.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_392.t: Good. Let's go to town on him together.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_393.t: Knock off the "Easy, Duncan" crap. I'm not doing anything for "kicks."

54d96ae73030316814006f45_397.t: This is Raymond we're talking about.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_402.t: Wu pulls his left elbow across his chest and lets out a little groan.

Uh... so this was 2045 - years after the summer we moved in with Raymond. We hadn't seen DT or Lockjaw or any of the gang in forever. Raymond's rules. No contact with our hoodlum friends. No visiting the squat to check up on them.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_405.t: You don't sound anything like him.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_407.t: I know *I* didn't want to go back to the Barrens. We had it good at Raymond's.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_408.t: Isn't that weird? I *towered* over him! And it just didn't matter. The little man just had that *way* of looking at you. That *tone.*

54d96ae73030316814006f45_413.t: He was the only one, though.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_414.t: Anyway, all of that was just lead up.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_419.t: I never thought of it that way before.
He didn't just think we had broken his rules or that we'd backslid. He thought that we were still street hoods. That we were milking him - using him. No wonder he was so angry. He felt betrayed.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_420.t: He didn't just think we had broken his rules or that we'd backslid. He thought that we'd been playing him the whole year. That we were still street hoods. No wonder he was so furious. He felt betrayed.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_423.t: Yeah, that "listens badly" thing really got to him. He was really ashamed of himself for flying off the handle.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_424.t: "Listen to what you know instead of what you fear."

54d96ae73030316814006f45_425.t: Using his mother's words on him really did the trick. I wonder what kind of childhood *he* had.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_428.t: And who knows? Maybe I've got a few daddy issues to deal with, too. Guess I should try and keep an open mind... when we see him.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_429.t: Wu looks relieved. Seems like he's had enough self-reflection for a lifetime.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_442.t: His face goes white as a sheet.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_443.t: Yeah, but still. Nobody deserves to go out like *that.*
I dunno... I don't think I'll ever get comfortable with having a ghoul on our crew.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_447.t: Can't. I've been having nightmares about it since I was a kid. Still makes my skin crawl.
I dunno... I don't think I'll ever get comfortable with having a ghoul on our crew.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_449.t: That's the one. Completely freaked me out. I slept with the dumpster lid shut for a week. What a pleasure it was to go on a run that brought back such hideous memories.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_451.t: I still can't believe that you made a deal with that thing. I mean, it was a mummy. You don't make deals with mummies!

54d96ae73030316814006f45_455.t: What do I think about it? Well, hmm, let me ponder that for a second...

54d96ae73030316814006f45_457.t: And in the end, we didn't even kill the damned vampire. We got a starlet fired for money. That was our big accomplishment.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_459.t: I dunno. I guess that I just preferred the simplicity of being a cop.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_461.t: We killed the vampire, so that's something. But we didn't get the Wong girl fired, and that was kind of the point of the run.
I dunno. I guess that I just preferred the simplicity of being a cop.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_463.t: Actually, it wasn't bad. Reminded me a little of doing undercover work for the Star. We got to go to a fancy restaurant, do some legwork, blend in, and then take a bad son of a bitch into custody and motor off in a speedboat.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_464.t: "Overproduced crap"? Don't give me that, it was a great show! The back-and-forth between Chapel and Mia Suede was legendary! I swear, some people don't know art when they see it.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_466.t: ...Anyway. In all seriousness, I'm not sure that we should've let that Talon guy live. People like that tend to have long memories.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_468.t: The network didn't give it a chance. Kept airing the episodes out of order and moving the time slot around.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_469.t: That was a choice. 54d96ae73030316814006f45_473.t: I was young, and I had moves. It paid well for a summer.
He crosses his arms over his chest.
You're just jealous that they hired me over you.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_478.t: That was some real cloak-and-dagger shit, wasn't it? Hitting a high-tech weapons research lab, framing a triad for the run, dealing with other shadowrunning teams.
Oh, and to top it all off, there was a laser gun there. A *laser gun.*

54d96ae73030316814006f45_481.t: ...So I've gotta admit, I was a little sad when you gave the thing to that other team. I mean, I get why you did it - reputation is important, and all that - but that weapon was an asset, too.
I dunno. It was a tough call. Let's hope that you made the right decision.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_482.t: So yeah. I'm gonna go ahead and put in my request right now: the next run we go on, I wanna take the laser. This is me calling dibs.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_489.t: I don't think I've seen a bloodbath like that since the Barrens. Everyone dead but us. I'm not saying they didn't deserve it, but... *damn.* I mostly went legit after Raymond took us from the Barrens to stay *away* from massacres like that.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_493.t: Funny. I don't feel any different than I did before. Just kinda... empty.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_496.t: I dunno. I'm not sure what to think any more.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_497.t: That was the first convention I've ever been to. Heard about 'em, but I'd never been to one.
Some people have too much time on their hands.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_499.t: You mean extracting the Plastic-Faced Man?
Wu frowns and his answer comes out in a low rasp.
I knew we'd eventually find that guy but getting the information out of him before he brain-wiped was tense.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_500.t: He looks at his boots for a minute, making a decision.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_505.t: No. You're doing just fine. I'm just telling you what I think - privately. I'll get off it now.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_506.t: Yeah, I get it, and I have no trouble with you being in the lead. You're doing fine, I'm just telling you. I'll get off it now.
Just one more thing - that guy took Raymond. For that alone, I'd drill him.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_507.t: That's what we've *been* doing. Seems like all that we do.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_508.t: Wu eyes you for a moment, looking for sarcasm.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_509.t: At least we know that Raymond's alive now.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_511.t: Been a long time since we ran the streets together. Lived in a squat. I wanna say that if feels like old times, but it doesn't. This is different.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_512.t: He shrugs and checks his equipment.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_513.t: You don't like that? I like it.
He shrugs and checks his equipment.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_514.t: Did I mention I hate this country? Cuz I hate this country.
He shrugs and checks his equipment.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_516.t: Man, she sounds like a piece of work, doesn't she? Maybe we'll get to say Hi when we bust into her headquarters.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_517.t: We got the job done. Thanks for including me on the rescue team. Glad I was there when he woke up. It was a relief to see he had his memories intact.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_520.t: Wu drops a gnarly hand on your shoulder.
Well, regardless of Raymond or Edward or whoever he is, I'll follow your lead. Best I can commit to.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_521.t: Okay, if that's the way you feel, I'll support you.
Wu drops a gnarly hand on your shoulder.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_528.t: I know. I can see it.

This explains a lot - why he was so... distant for all those years. So secretive about his life before Seattle. I thought it was just a Chinese thing. But now I know. It was the guilt... the shame of it.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_529.t: Tell me about it. I was on the receiving end of most of his shit. But this... this explains a lot.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_530.t: Tell me about it. I had no idea...
The hand rubbing his head slows.
This explains a lot. A lot.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_531.t: I don't know. That look in his eyes... the pain. I don't know if I could live with that.
The hand rubbing his head slows.
This explains a lot. A lot.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_535.t: Wu's eyes bounce around a bit as he works through the scenarios.
Yeah, okay. Me too. I'll keep my eye open for anything suspicious.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_536.t: Wu's eyes bounce around a bit as he works through the scenarios.
Yeah, I guess that's possible. I didn't think of it. I'll keep my eye open for anything suspicious.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_537.t: Wu's eyes bounce around a bit as he works through the scenarios.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_538.t: Yeah, I will. No doubt. Hopefully, it'll never come up.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_540.t: Understood. I'll do what I gotta do. But if we've gotta do... something drastic... I hope we'll be able to talk about it first.

54d96ae73030316814006f45_548.t: Just stuck around the boat and waited to hear if you got killed or not.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_45.t: Stinks of fish, just like that place we squatted at on Leary Avenue back when we were kids. The one with that Aztlan family and their dog.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_46.t: I dunno. They managed to make that place pretty comfortable for a while. Until the 162s decided that they wanted it.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_47.t: He stretches, and his spine pops like a handful of firecrackers.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_48.t: And this has been one shit-stain of a day. Time to end it.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_57.t: He sweeps his eyes across the cabin. The doubt is plain on his face.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_69.t: He kept digging out my food stash. No matter where I hid it, he'd find it and scarf down a week's worth of grub.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_70.t: Yeah, until the 162s decided they wanted the place...

54be84343063641c1f00153f_8.t: Be careful. Her aura is streaked with corruption - I can practically taste it.

54be84343063641c1f00153f_10.t: Gaichu tightens his fingers on the hilt of his katana.

54bef3b9306364501c001580_15.t: His lip curls into a sneer.

54c007ae306364f4160019ce_6.t: His nose wrinkles as he tastes the air.

54c007ae306364f4160019ce_7.t: Not your vintage, hm? I'm not surprised. But *I* find the aroma quite enticing.

54c696f3303031301d005190_227.t: Yes. It is good to see you.

54cfdf5b306364a06a008577_13.t: It smells like quite a feast... sweet and unctuous, like duck fat and honeycomb.

54d00020306364a06a008a84_14.t: I'm essentially blind, so I'm afraid I cannot help.

54d5495c306364bc7300017a_6.t: Curious. This creature does not appear to be in pain, despite the rampant disease that runs through it.

54d5495c306364bc7300017a_13.t: Curious. This creature does not appear to be in pain, despite the rampant disease that runs through it.

54d92ad03063645032003070_1.t: Lifting his nose into the rain, Gaichu tastes the air with his tongue. He carefully turns his head, mouth open to catch the most noise.
I hear nothing but the storm, and I smell no adrenaline in the air. I believe we have arrived undetected.

54d92ad03063645032003070_3.t: I will not apologise for what I am, or what that means for my senses. It is a fact of my existence, as much as your height is a fact of yours.
Gaichu turns his unseeing, milky white eyes toward you.

54d92ad03063645032003070_8.t: Gaichu clicks his tongue a few times, and then bares his teeth in a rictus grin.
This storm is making it nearly impossible for me to hear. It will be much worse for the security on the ship. They most likely will not notice us if we stay out of sight.

54d92ad03063645032003070_9.t: If we are seen, they will be forced to use wired alarms, as the storm will make using their radios next to impossible.
Gaichu rests a hand on the hilt of his sword, and takes a hissed breath over clenched teeth.
We should hit them with all possible speed - before they can rouse the rest of the ship.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_1.t: It is not every day that one can say they fought an ancient god - and won.
The ghoul folds his arms across his chest as a thin smile graces his lips.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_2.t: I feel somewhat the same. Perhaps it is the supernatural nature of the Yama Kings, or the mind's own tendency to compartmentalize. Regardless, it *did* happen, and we survived. A challenge worthy of legend. How do you feel about being part of that?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_5.t: Given what I have seen, the world might not win. You have a singular level of drive and focus. The kind of intensity rarely seen.
Just let me know when you decide to topple the Corporate Court. I suspect I will need to prepare myself, if I am to keep up.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_6.t: Gaichu laughs, slapping a palm against his leg.
Well, that is a simple request, easily accommodated. I'm sure no one would begrudge you some rest and relaxation after all of this.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_7.t: Do you suppose that would have made a difference, in the long run? So long as there are are humans, there will be the powerful and those they exploit. I'm certain that Josephine's death would please you on a personal level, but someone else would simply fill the void she left.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_10.t: I'm afraid this cabin will need a lot of work because I can call it truly 'livable'. But I suppose I have to do it, or learn to love living in filth.
Gaichu suddenly looks toward you, as if realizing something.
That is, if I am still considered part of the team. Your affairs here are done, so I was unsure...

54d96be03030316814006f7f_18.t: Point taken. And it's better to be known as the ghoul who helped slay an ancient god of death. How do *you* feel, having been part of such a momentous occasion?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_21.t: Ah! A very zen way of looking at the world.
This is the nature of ronin - the name means 'wave man', you know.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_25.t: Gaichu cradles his chin one hand.
Push a man to the edge, and then you will see what he is truly capable of.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_27.t: Ahh. Well, take this as a request to speak with me more in the future, then. I would very much like to get to know you, and it has been a long time since I have had a friend. For now, however, I will need to keep cleaning this cabin, or I will need to learn to live in filth.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_32.t: Welcome to my sanctuary. I apologize for the lack of light, but as I did not need it, Gobbet took the bulbs for use elsewhere.
Gaichu puts down the box that he's in the process of moving and politely bows his head toward you.
How can I be of assistance?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_33.t: Gaichu rolls his shoulders in a diffident shrug.
I live. I am among those who can make use of my services and who are not attempting to claim the bounty on my head. Is that not enough, for now?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_35.t: Gaichu clicks his tongue and sharply dips his head.
Yes. I have indeed. I had thought that I was alone in this.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_36.t: I saw a vision of shadowy shapes, rows upon rows of sharp teeth, and the stench of sulphur. My initial assumption was that I was simply dreaming of my fear of becoming what so many assume of me: a monster without a mind.
Gaichu considers this, cradling his chin between two fingers.
Does this coincide with a dream you had?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_40.t: As you wish. You are my benefactor, and as such, I yield to you. Ask, then. I will do my best to answer.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_42.t: Because I give you my word that I will not harm you or anyone else on the team.
Gaichu squares his shoulders, expression stony.
I am a man with a disease, not a mindless beast. My word is my bond.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_45.t: Gaichu snorts harshly, shaking his head with a grimace.

If there is, it has eluded the best doctors in Shanghai and Hong Kong. The rest of the world has fared no better - if they had, there would not be substantial bounties upon ghouls in every major nation.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_48.t: If you think that to be necessary, I will acquiesce.
Gaichu gestures toward a small refrigerator in the corner of the room.
I have all of the foodstuffs that I require in here.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_50.t: Aside from my heightened senses of smell and hearing, I am stronger, tougher, and more resilient than any mundane human. I can achieve feats of physicality that are normally restricted to adepts and those with extensive enhancements.
Gaichu lifts one hand, fingers crooked to show off his elongated nails - claws, really.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_51.t: These, as well. As sharp as any small knife. I prefer my sword, but in a grapple they come in extremely handy.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_52.t: Gaichu chuckles, his laughter rasping out over his lips.
Unfortunately I do. I am not an undead creature of legend. If I manage to unlock the secret of rest without sleep, I will be certain to inform you.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_53.t: Gaichu cuts loose with a loud, rasping laugh. After a moment, the laughter turns to a cough, and he doubles over.
If I could, believe me, you would be the first to know. Unfortunately not... my condition has enhanced my body, but it has not granted me any mystical powers thus far.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_54.t: Most are. In the vast majority of cases, the infected become the crazed predators everyone sees on the nightly trid news.
Gaichu bares his jagged teeth, running his tongue across the tips of them as he tilts his head back.
I bear the physical signs, but not the mental ones. A few escape that fate.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_58.t: For now, it has. I will not suddenly become a ravenous beast lurking in the hold of your ship - at least, no more so than I already am.
Gaichu bears his teeth in a wry grin, dipping his head a little.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_60.t: Gaichu leans back against the boxes behind him.
Once I establish myself as a shadowrunner of quality, the name will *become* impressive. Until then, I am unconcerned with what common people think of it.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_62.t: Gaichu dips his head in agreement.
One does not defeat the enemy solely by strength of arms. One must turn one's own weaknesses into strengths. It is an old and valuable lesson, too often forgotten.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_63.t: That is correct. It is the armor worn by the elite soldiers of the Renraku corporate military - the Red Samurai.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_66.t: The corner of Gaichu's mouth curls up into the smallest of grins.
I did not steal it. It is mine.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_72.t: Cocking his head back, Gaichu smiles grimly.
Of course they are. But not for the reasons that you assume.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_75.t: Gaichu rasps out a coughing laugh that reverberates through the cramped room.
What other employment is appropriate for a cannibal whose only previous experience is in military operations? There are not many things I know how to do. This is one of them.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_77.t: Red Samurai teams do everything together - eat, sleep, train, play. We learn to think for each other, move as a single entity. When part of that team is broken, it affects everyone.
Gaichu runs his claws over his scalp, sighing.
The other members of the unit would be distracted, thinking about a wounded comrade, and would resent a new member for usurping the old member's place.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_79.t: Gaichu chuckles softly, shaking his head.
I believe that it is effective in most cases, but I also believe I am worth more alive than dead.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_85.t: They do, to an anachronistic degree. Political and ideological indoctrination is as much a part of the training as physical fitness and weapons practice. In order to use this...
Gaichu raps several knuckles on the hilt of his sword.
...They make certain you will only turn it on enemies of the company and the nation.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_86.t: Gaichu bares his teeth in a vicious grin.

When I was younger, I would have argued that you simply did not understand what it means to be Japanese. I think now, however, that you are correct. The Yamato Act is a convenient way to encourage nationalistic fervor, while avoiding the racial problems of other nations by simply removing non-humans.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_89.t: I was searching for a cure. I needed a place where I could disappear into the shadows, find work, and evade capture if a cure did not materialize. The Yamato Act meant that it was even more dangerous to be in Japan than anywhere else.
Gaichu smiles thinly, leaning back on his heels.
I went to China, primarily because I still hoped to be able to return home. It felt less final if I fled to somewhere nearby.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_90.t: I went first to Shanghai, but Renraku's presence there is significantly more robust than in Hong Kong. I learned that my previous unit had not stopped hunting me after I left Japan, so I decided to move further afield.
Gaichu unfurls his hands, claws pointing toward the ceiling.
Hence, Hong Kong. Where else can anyone arrive and disappear without the city caring one whit?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_91.t: If Renraku were not building that towering arcology to dominate the skyline, I would agree with you. Since it clearly has that kind of influence in the city, it seemed unwise to make my residence there.
Gaichu runs his nails over his chin.
Besides, I have always liked Hong Kong. It has more character than the UCAS.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_92.t: Since arriving here, I've drifted between neighborhoods, selling my services to those who are unafraid of my appearance and condition.
It has not been as profitable as I'd hoped.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_93.t: Definitely. I considered it, in fact - if for no other reason than its proximity to Asamando. But I was not yet ready to give up on a cure. Lagos lacks the infrastructure and medical care of better-maintained cities.
Perhaps when we part ways, I will relocate there.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_95.t: Yes, in Keihanshin. The Kyoto-Osaka-Kobe sprawl. It is less populated than Neo-Tokyo, but not by much. I grew up in east Kobe, where my father was posted in a Renraku complex.
Gaichu roots around in a nearby box, running his fingertips over several chips before deciding on one. He slots it into his PDA, and a trid photo flickers to life in the space between you.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_98.t: I used to cause a lot of trouble when I was in school. My father was constantly raging that I had no sense of responsibility, and that I needed to take life more seriously.
A cackle erupts from Gaichu, sudden mirth spreading across his face.
The final straw was when he bailed me out of jail.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_99.t: My friends and I stole a delivery van. It had a shipment of Shiawase simsense decks - brand new, very expensive. We were going to fence them and use the money to take a trip to Okinawa.
Are you familiar with what it's like to grow up in a corporate family?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_103.t: Gaichu clicks his tongue a few times. He seems reluctant to continue.

That is a story for another time, unfortunately. I still have a lot of arranging to do, and I doubt anyone else will be willing to be alone with me in a darkened room. Come back later, we can discuss it further.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_104.t: It is. And thank you again for your hospitality.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_112.t: Gaichu waves a hand around the room, fingers uncurling as if to offer the contents to you.

For everything that is not living, my hearing and sense of smell are more than enough to keep me aware of where things are. For instance, I can hear Gobbet frying oysters on a hot plate. They smell like she fished them out of a dumpster.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_113.t: Gaichu glances toward the ceiling. After a moment, he extends a hand, palm up. A single drop of leaking oil falls into it.

I am shocked that it has not sunk and drowned us all, to be perfectly honest. But it is as much of a home than anywhere else I have stayed in Hong Kong, and I am certain fewer people will stumble upon me and try to kill me. For now, it will suffice.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_114.t: Gaichu dips his head in greeting as you enter.
Hello again. How may I help you?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_117.t: Gaichu is seated in the lotus position, eyes closed. His fingertips rest on a braille reader that is plugged into his PDA; the device fills the room with a soft clicking from the pin combinations. As you approach, he lifts his hands from the reader's surface.
Hello again. Is there something I can assist you with?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_121.t: Gaichu draws himself up, folding his palms in his lap. With one toe, he pushes the braille reader away.

It is called "Hagakure" - "Hidden Leaves." Written by Yamamoto Tsunetomo, as a treatise on what it means to be a samurai, and how one should best serve one's lord. Required reading for the Red Samurai, unsurprisingly.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_122.t: Not so much old memories as old lessons.
Gaichu smiles sharply, running a claw across the line of his jaw.
I have many scars to remember my mistakes by, and each one seems to evoke a passage from the book.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_124.t: Gaichu ticks off the points by unfurling successive fingers.
It is partly a manual for proper behavior toward one's lord, partly a rumination on the mindset required to be victorious in combat, and partly a collection of stories.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_127.t: Spreading his palms, Gaichu bows his head in assent.

Some might take offense at that assessment, but I am inclined to agree. I do not plan with the same efficacy as I once did. But I would not curse the tiger for relying on its instincts - why should I not make use of mine?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_130.t: Gaichu smiles thinly, rapping a claw on the hilt of his sword.
More specifically, if a samurai has not been a ronin at least seven times, then he has not truly lived as a samurai.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_136.t: Gaichu's lips curl into a slight scowl, but he nods in agreement.

That is true. Tsunetomo never fought anyone, and some have criticized him for trying to speak of being a warrior without ever lifting a sword. But I believe that he has many things of value to say. He cared a great deal about the deeper meaning of being a samurai. If lived experience is the only path to truth, then all philosophers are fools.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_137.t: Barking out a sharp, solitary laugh, Gaichu smiles.
If that is your attitude, then I fear there is precious little I can do to change your mind on the subject.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_143.t: Gaichu considers this, leaning over to rest his arm on one knee.
I have fought a few shadowrunners in my time, and I admit, they have always proven more tenacious opponents than corporate soldiers have.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_148.t: Gaichu blinks, obviously surprised. A wide grin spreads across his face.
You know of him, then? Yes, that is Tsunetomo. He was eager to die and show his loyalty, but Lord Mitsushige had always found that custom to be wasteful.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_149.t: Gaichu cocks his head at you, his milky eyes narrowing mirthfully.
Preparing for the eventuality of killing me and collecting the bounty? Make sure you leave my face intact - I would prefer to be a well-preserved trophy.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_150.t: Gaichu bares his teeth in a rictus grin. You can hear a faint chuckle rumble in the back of his throat.

Now that would be an illustrious end to my career. A severed head, decorating a leaking wreck of a boat, home to extremely private individuals who will never show me off. Couldn't you sell it to a museum, or something of that sort?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_151.t: If you wish to know about what it is like to be me, ask away. I see no reason to conceal the details of my condition.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_153.t: Gaichu blinks a few times, and cocks his head as if to stare at the ceiling.

What is it like to eat anything else? Or for a diabetic to take insulin? It is like that: a fact of life. I have found that most people are revolted by the *concept* of eating the flesh of humans, rather than the act itself.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_155.t: Do I enjoy it? No, not especially. Some Japanese are fond of pork and chicken sashimi, but I have never been one of them.
But it is a fact I must accept. And if it is how things will be, I will make the best of it.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_156.t: No. But I was never a vegetarian, so why should I?
Gaichu seems puzzled by the question, as if he's missing nuances of meaning.
It does not matter where the meat comes from - someone who died in a car accident is as viable as someone I kill.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_159.t: Considering this, Gaichu leans over onto one elbow.

I have no idea. I do not believe there have been any organized studies of ghoul longevity. Most strains of the Human-Metahuman Vampiric Virus impart a semblance of extended life, but I do not believe that the Krieger strain is one. Somehow, I think it is more likely that I will die in combat than of old age - no matter how long I live.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_160.t: To what end? I live by the sword, and I will die by it. I have no illusions about my chances of growing old.
Gaichu shakes his head in dismissal.
How would I live out my days? In a rural country village, eating people who happened to expire on nearby roads? That will not be my fate.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_161.t: Gaichu gestures to some of the discolorations and nodules around his neck and scalp.
They look gruesome, but they seem to be as much a part of ghoul physiology as horns and dermal deposits are for trolls.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_165.t: Gaichu grins broadly, leaning toward you. He gestures at his face, drawing the backs of his claws down one cheek.
Surely you are joking. Look at this fearsome visage! Why, mount this over your front door, and you can be certain no unwanted guests will ever stop by. Probably not any wanted ones, either!

54d96be03030316814006f7f_166.t: Gaichu bursts out laughing, slapping his knee.
I can see it now! Eight thousand nuyen an ounce for powdered ghoul bones! Guaranteed to cure rheumatism and arthritis, and to balance the flow of blood in your liver.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_167.t: Ahh. Yes. I do recall you asking about that.
Gaichu's reticence is palpable in the long silence that follows. He does not continue.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_168.t: Gaichu purses his lips and sighs heavily.
No, everything is fine. I will tell you what you wish to know. I simply have not spoken of my family in a long time.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_174.t: Gaichu runs his fingers over his bald scalp, smiling distantly.
From then on, I tried to be the son he wanted, and he seemed glad of it. We grew much closer over the next few years. He took time off to visit me, when I was living in Kyoto.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_179.t: Perhaps ironically, hunting with my father is how I learned to prepare raw meat. Strange to think that such activities served as training for my current state of affairs.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_181.t: Sighing happily, Gaichu stares off into the distance. When he looks back, he smiles broadly at you.

Those were good days. I am sorry I cannot go back to those times. When one joins the Red Samurai, one must give up all attachments of one's previous life. The company, the code, and the unit become the entirety of your world.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_185.t: Gaichu laughs, slapping one knee in amusement.
I will not bore you with the details, if you are uninterested in how it is done.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_188.t: Gaichu smiles, waving an open hand in your direction.

To each their own. While I have lived in cities all my life, it is only because that is where work has forced me to be. Given a choice, I would much prefer to settle in a rural area. It gives one space to breathe.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_193.t: I have given you my word, and I intend to keep it. Do not be concerned. I simply have not spoken of my family in a long time.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_194.t: Ahh. A fair question, given my youthful indiscretions.
Gaichu leans back, his blind eyes turned toward the ceiling.
Are you familiar with the Kempeitai?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_200.t: We did as we were taught. Such is the way of authority on the young.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_202.t: No, why? It was a necessary step in my plans for my future, even if some of the behaviors that we were involved in were distasteful. The state of the nation was such that those kinds of behaviors were legal, and condoned.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_208.t: Gaichu inclines his head toward you.
This is my feeling as well. At the time, behaving in that way was appropriate. Now, it is not.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_212.t: These days, the megacorporations have begun to resist that kind of nationalism in favor of corporate pride. Youth Brigade service is becoming a black mark, rather than a positive.
Gaichu shifts, resting his elbows on his knees as he leans forward.
I do not know if the alternative is any better, though.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_215.t: Exactly what you would expect happened. I signed the contract, and became a soldier in Renraku's army. I was physically fit, at the top of my class, and it seemed inevitable I would see my desires fulfilled.
Gaichu raps two knuckles on his breastplate.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_217.t: Shaking his head wistfully, Gaichu snorts.

It seems ridiculous, in retrospect - we were all so young. None of us had any idea what we were doing, except trying to become the best soldiers in the world. And yet those strangers became my new family, in the absence of the old.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_218.t: On the whole? No. I do not know who I would have been, had I not joined. Perhaps there are things that I miss, but regret is perpetual. One is always tempted to ask "what if," but that is a trap.
Gaichu raps two knuckles on his breastplate.
This is who I became. The armor was my protection and my identity.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_219.t: Not at the time, but I was very close. I focused on my studies and my physical training to the exclusion of a social life. But I knew if I kept working, I would be good enough to get a post of my choosing.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_220.t: Every child who grows up in a Renraku family hears stories of the Red Samurai. They were the "heroic" cartoons and toy lines we were exposed to from childhood. They protected the families and interests of loyal Renraku citizens.
Gaichu grins crookedly, shaking his head.
Propaganda, of course. But effective propaganda.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_222.t: Of course. As I said before, the path of the elite warrior is not for everyone.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_226.t: Gaichu wrinkles his nose as if smelling something foul.
That chapter of my life is over and done with, and they are no longer part of my world. Why would you care about any of that?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_227.t: He stares at you for a long, silent moment. Finally, he grunts.
Very well. That is a fair point.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_228.t: Gaichu looks down at his hands, picking something out from beneath one claw. He continues to look at his hands while he speaks.
When I was younger, I would have more readily cut off my fingers than tell you what you ask. You must understand that secrecy is absolute, among the Red Samurai.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_230.t: Finally looking up from his hands, Gaichu leans back against the boxes.
Contact with the outside world is cut off completely. Even once training is over, your communications are restricted. Maintaining relationships outside of those you work with is frowned upon.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_237.t: I am unsurprised to hear it. You and Sasaki share similar attitudes: neither of you stops to consider *if* your goals are attainable. You simply know that they *are.*

54d96be03030316814006f7f_242.t: Of course I do! They were my family for eight years. Who wouldn't miss that? That sense of belonging and shared values?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_244.t: Gaichu closes his eyes, sighing through his nose.
When they ambushed me in Fukuoka, it was fight or die. I killed two of them before I escaped. I will not be returning to the unit, no matter what.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_245.t: He opens his eyes after a moment, shoulders slumping.
It is not an easy memory to grapple with.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_248.t: Of course it is! Do you think I do not know that?
I apologize. It is just that those feelings are still raw, sometimes.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_251.t: No, actually. Quite the opposite. She was excellent at encouraging us to go above and beyond what we thought ourselves capable of. I think part of her secret was that she never actually questioned if she could accomplish any of her goals. She simply assumed that it was inevitable.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_252.t: Gaichu nods, propping himself up on one knee.
It is. By rotating who has command duties, we were able to see very quickly who had the mind and personality to lead the team, and who was more comfortable following.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_255.t: I can see you will not be dissuaded.
Very well. I suppose it is a small thing to tell you.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_258.t: Gaichu folds his hands in his lap, nodding his head toward a nearby pile of boxes.
I would offer you a place to sit, but I fear I only have boxes.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_260.t: Gaichu has returned to reading, but plucks his fingers from the braille board as you enter.
Welcome back. What may I do for you?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_263.t: A single bead of sweat rolls down Gaichu's temple as he holds his position. After a few moments, he shifts to a relaxed stance, starting the form again.
Pardon me for continuing my exercises. I feel that I have been letting them slip, of late.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_265.t: Gaichu lifts his sword, offering it edge first toward you.

Do you see that edge? Forged by one of the finest swordsmiths in modern Japan. Diamond-coated edge, capable of cutting even the most hardened ceramic armors. But what good is a sharpened edge without the precision to apply it?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_266.t: When I was still a man, I could have cut a single pea in half with my eyes closed. Oddly, after becoming blind, I cannot replicate this feat.
Running the back of a claw along the blade's surface, Gaichu smiles thinly.
I think soon I will be able to perform this feat again. I simply have to train my body to ignore the senses that it no longer has, and pay attention to the new ones.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_269.t: It is not the easiest thing I have attempted, but neither is it the hardest.
Placing his sword off to one side, Gaichu turns to face you.

"Emptiness is form" is one of the great lessons of the "Hagakure." Train sufficiently, and both swordsmanship and obedience will come instinctively. That is the closest to perfection a man can attain.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_270.t: A lawless shadowrunner, have problems with authority? My goodness. I never would have guessed such a thing to be the case.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_277.t: Gaichu draws his nails over his scalp, sighing.

My time in Fukuoka was tense. Since leaving Keihanshin, I had been careful to stay out of sight by moving on foot or in the back of automated delivery vans. I was running out of food, however, and needed to be in a city for that.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_280.t: Gaichu closes his eyes, resting his face in one palm.

If I had taken my time, I could have made it to Kumamoto without incident. But I let caution slip when I got his email. I thought that if I disguised myself, I could take the train there - get out before my unit got any closer to finding me.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_283.t: I assumed that a train had derailed, honestly. But then I smelled the telltale acrid vapor of the explosives.
Gaichu wrinkles his nose at the memory.

Once you smell it, you never forget. The plastic explosive that we used has a particularly sharp odor, like old cheese. Something to do with an olfactory taggant added to help track it if it is stolen.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_285.t: Yes. That is so. Training took over - I knew I had to survive, and I was not thinking of anything but survival. So I charged Sasaki and Aomori.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_287.t: I caught Aomori in the throat with the tip of my sword. It was a maneuver I'd practiced hundreds of times before. There was no resistance as I cut through his trachea, and he fell as I rolled to the side. I could hear him choking for breath as he dropped.
Gaichu exhales a humorless laugh, and shakes his head.
It is strange to think how clear that memory is, even now.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_288.t: Gaichu snorts, waving a hand dismissively.
Spare me the two-nuyen psychiatry. Obviously, what you say is true... But the memory sticks with me so strongly for another reason. It was in that strike that I realized who I truly was.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_291.t: They thought they were fighting a beast. I would never had underestimated them as they did me. All the lessons I taught them about close combat, thrown out the window as soon as their preconceptions entered the battlefield. Disgraceful. As I killed Aomori, I realized that it was not that I was unworthy of the Red Samurai... but that they were unworthy of me.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_295.t: Gaichu taps himself on the forehead, just above his right eye.

It was there, on Sasaki. A downward stroke, from jodan stance. She had hesitated, just as Aomori had. I felt the breath go out of her, like someone had deflated a tire. She just slid down into a pile. I think she was trying to ask me how, when I ran.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_297.t: They did not follow me out into the city proper. From what I heard via the Matrix, the event was reported as a terrorist attack that was thwarted by brave Renraku soldiers.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_301.t: Gaichu leans toward you, expression intense.
Exactly. In that strike, I learned that lesson - and realized who I truly was, as well.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_305.t: Gaichu does not answer immediately. He seems uneasy with the question, fidgeting with his fingers.
I am unsure why that would make a difference. Can you explain what you are driving at?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_306.t: Ahh. I see. I think your mistake is in seeing this from a practical perspective. The problem is emotional, not mechanical.
Folding his arms over his chest, Gaichu continues to explain.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_309.t: Gaichu inclines his head, expression impassive.

I can see how it might seem that way from the outside. Part of what makes the Red Samurai fearsome is that they do not believe they can lose. Establishing a strong "esprit de corps" through this kind of unusual organization is part of the mystique.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_313.t: Gaichu's expression darkens, and he folds his arms over his chest.
You think our battles have not been in earnest? You believe that I should have been more efficient in defending myself?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_315.t: Oh, of course. Because combat is a binary - you are either capable of defeating all opponents, or none of them.
Gaichu sneers, baring his teeth in scorn.
Rank foolishness. I have done my utmost to end their lives.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_316.t: Gaichu looks about to retort in anger, but holds his tongue. With a deep breath, he purses his lips.
You are not incorrect about this. At the time, however, I thought it was safer to run.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_319.t: Hrm. What you say is true. They can track me down, but I always have the advantage of knowing the terrain. I can, in a general way, set the terms of the engagement.
Narrowing his eyes, Gaichu seems to consider this in earnest.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_320.t: Blinking a few times, Gaichu leans closer to you.
I am unsure that I clearly understand your meaning. Can you explain yourself more clearly?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_322.t: Drawing himself up and puffing out his chest, Gaichu glares at you.
You tell me. What do you see? I am the creature that stands in front of you - what is it that I am, in your opinion?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_329.t: Ahh, yes. Because I am such a weak-willed soldier of fortune that I cannot bear the idea of killing the last vestiges of my old life.
His tone is mocking as he spits his next words.
Certainly, that is more likely than the battles simply not going in my favor.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_330.t: Gaichu is halfway through pointing an accusatory finger at you, but stops short as you speak.
There may be... some truth to what you say. I had not considered that my instinct to flee may be subconscious.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_332.t: Gaichu narrows his eyes, inhaling sharply through his nose. There is a long pause before he speaks.
There is some truth to what you say. Were they members of an Ares Firewatch team, I would have attempted to eliminate them some time ago.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_333.t: Gaichu pulls back as if you've slapped him.
What do you suggest, that I hunt them down while they sleep and murder them in cold blood?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_334.t: What do you believe I have been doing?
Certainly, I have not been allowing them to attack me out of good will.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_337.t: Yes. That is the case. They cannot move forward and rebuild the core of the unit unless I die, both because of their own expectations and the stain on their honor. Even if they accepted my decision, the rest of the Red Samurai would not.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_338.t: Exactly. Until they can remove the stain on their honor, they are unable to move forward. I have trapped them in the past, and they feel that the only recourse is to take my life.
This is the trap of their duty.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_340.t: Because of this, I must learn to make my own way in this world. There is no older teaching for me to reach for.
Is this why you are concerned about my history with the Red Samurai?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_346.t: Gaichu squares himself, nodding several times as if agreeing with his own internal dialog.

Warriors must learn to push their body and soul to the utmost limit. So too it is with me, but my body and soul are no longer that of a man. I must learn to use these differences as an advantage, rather than a handicap.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_351.t: Gaichu blinks several times, his surprise evident.
That is remarkably kind of you. Were this a year or more ago, I would not believe you. After having seen how you handled the Whampoans, however, I do.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_356.t: Gaichu sighs, shoulders slumping a little.
I am unsure if that is the wisest course of action. I am going to need to think about this a great deal. Please, let me think on this. I will have an answer later.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_359.t: Gaichu pauses suddenly, turning to face you.
I have just realized you do not know my real name. Do you wish to?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_363.t: Please mind your step. I fear I have made a mess, and many of my possessions are quite delicate. I would be rather upset if you were to accidentally crush any under your feet.
After a moment, he places the one he holds - a small ceramic statuette of Daruma - to one side. Lifting his face, he touches his brow in salute.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_364.t: Gaichu gingerly begins collecting his things and arranging them back in the box.
I am simply thinking about the chain of events that have brought me to Hong Kong.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_367.t: I began searching the Shadowland BBS for any jobs against Renraku holdings here in Hong Kong.
Gaichu taps his braille reader with one toe.
It is surprisingly easy to use the Matrix, when you can read postings with your fingers. Slower than direct interface, but certainly possible.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_368.t: I also began to offer a reward for information on Red Samurai teams known to be in the city. A modest reward - nothing that would attract too much attention, but enough to keep people interested.
Gaichu grins thinly, cradling his chin in one hand.
This bore fruit. A Red Samurai team arrived here a short time ago, smaller than normal size.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_369.t: It is definitely them. My contact described Ishida and Takagawa perfectly, as well as a new heavy gunner - most likely from another disgraced squad. What's more, he described a woman who looked exactly like Sasaki, but with a scar here, on her forehead.
Gaichu draws a finger down across his brow, over his right eye.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_372.t: I have found a job I am certain will draw a Red Samurai response. Renraku is moving a prototype drone through Hong Kong, on the way to a research facility in Chiba. It is of a new design, implementing machine learning and pseudo-intelligence.
Something that advanced would certainly attract a Red Samurai team, should someone attempt to steal it.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_375.t: Gaichu inclines his head in agreement.
Precisely. I can rid myself of my pursuers, and he can be free of their yoke. While I owe this man no loyalty, I think I understand his sentiment. Besides, we will be receiving payment for a job.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_377.t: Gaichu leans back on his heels, lifting his chin.
What would you like to know?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_380.t: Gaichu gestures at a stack of chips laying nearby.

I did my own digging. The shipping schedules line up perfectly to place the drone in Hong Kong - and Red Samurai would not have the authority to fake such records, unless ordered to by their superiors. As I am the unit's private shame, this would not become a larger corporate matter. There is no profit in killing me, for the company. Only for the unit.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_383.t: Gaichu shifts, running a hand over his bald head. He looks away, embarrassed.
Well, I... Uh... I was hoping you would help me. You and the rest of the team.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_385.t: Gaichu shifts uneasily. He seems to be sniffing the air in anticipation.
What, may I ask, is your decision?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_387.t: Gaichu's head falls, but after a moment he nods.

I understand. I will forward the details to your mission computer, should you change your mind. This opportunity will not be present forever, though. I would caution haste, if you think you would like to pursue this run.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_389.t: Gaichu cocks his head, giving you a look of confused disdain.

Of course not. Why would I be interested in a fight where I did not stack the odds in my favor? I have no intention of allowing them even the slightest chance at victory. I will set an ambush, spring it, and eliminate them. All of them.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_390.t: Gaichu nods firmly, his expression grim.

Surprise is the husband of victory. This is an essential law of combat - there is no such thing as a "fair fight." Only a fight you win, or lose. I have no intention of allowing them to set the terms of the fight.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_391.t: Yes. My contact described Ishida and Takagawa perfectly, as well as a new heavy gunner - most likely from another disgraced squad. What's more, he described a woman who looked exactly like Sasaki, but with a scar here, on her forehead.
Gaichu draws a finger down across his brow, over his right eye.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_394.t: Gaichu gingerly begins collecting his things and arranging them back in the box.
I am simply thinking about the chain of events that brought me to Hong Kong.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_395.t: Gaichu gingerly begins collecting his things and arranging them back in the box.

No. I am remembering my home, and obviously I would like to return to it. Long ago, however, I came to terms with the impossibility of that desire. Today, I am simply thinking about the chain of events that brought me to Hong Kong.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_396.t: Gaichu is examining his armor and gear, looking for defects.
I am ready to go when you are - but it doesn't hurt to do a last minute inspection. Message me from the MTR station when you are ready.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_397.t: Gaichu runs his fingertips over his gear and armor, checking for defects and weak points.
Have you changed your mind about the run I proposed?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_401.t: Good evening. A fine evening, isn't it?
He puts the sword aside and smiles in your general direction.
What can I do for you?

54d96be03030316814006f7f_405.t: Gaichu grins thinly, cradling his chin in one hand.

There is some poetic justice in this, I feel. His claims of my weakness, mirrored by his own descent into feral madness - and yet here I am. One with enough willpower to survive and prosper despite my ill fortune.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_412.t: Regardless, Ishida's sword is now mine. It is a newer blade than my own, and appears to be made with some manner of enhance dikote process along the tungsten carbide egde.
He lifts the katana, running one claw down the back of the blade.
I have noticed a marked improvement in my speed when using it, particularly with follow-through moves. Should an enemy fall to this sword, I will be able to reposition much more rapidly.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_415.t: Gaichu cocks his head as he ponders this. After a long moment, he shakes his head.
No. I do not believe I do.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_418.t: Gaichu recoils, expression one of rank incredulity.
Surely you are joking with me. I will stay here, and aid you in your endeavors. I have no other life to turn to, nor anyone else to call friend. As you have aided me, so too will I aid you.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_423.t: Allow me to thank you again for interceding on Ishida's behalf.
Gaichu bows very low, holding it for an almost uncomfortable amount of time.
I had blinded myself to the possibility of ending his life without attempting to avenge my suffering upon him.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_428.t: And I stand by mine, as well. I do not mean in any stereotypical "bushido says my sword is yours" fashion, either. In the shadows, your allies are your life - and you have proven to be the strongest ally I could ask for. It is only right that I return the favor.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_429.t: Very well. Let me know when you would like to discuss it.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_433.t: Gaichu does not open his eyes as you approach, but simply continues to meditate.
Come back another time. I am still pondering our last conversation.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_434.t: Gaichu is busily polishing his sword. His fingers tap a small ball of oil along the length, and he proceeds to slide a smooth stone gingerly over the gleaming edge.
If you don't mind, I'd like to speak later. I have to buff out some nicks in my sword.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_435.t: Gaichu is rummaging through his box of mementos, searching for a particular object by touch alone.
Could we speak later? I am feeling a little distracted at the moment.

54d96be03030316814006f7f_440.t: Gaichu shakes his head, running a hand over his scalp.

Just one of many, I think. Waiting for us in the Walled City's blighted heart.

557f6564323437bc38000176_0.t: Ishida lies on the cold tile, gasping for breath. His armor is rent and tattered, but his eyes still burn with rage. He spits at Gaichu as you approach.
Gaichu! Honorless cur! I regret the day I ever began teaching you. You're truly depraved to kill your own brothers, rather than submit to what is right.

557f6564323437bc38000176_1.t: Gaichu. Yes. That is my name, as everything of my old life has been taken from me.
Gaichu lifts the tip of his sword, placing it squarely on Ishida's chest.
What is honor, if it is built on a lie? What is duty, if the dogma behind it is corrupt?

557f6564323437bc38000176_2.t: Narrowing his eyes, the Red Samurai presses his chest upwards into the blade so that it begins to gouge his armor.
What do you know of it, vermin? Every Red Samurai who has become...subhuman...has taken their life. Every one but you! You're a disgrace to the order, and a disgrace to your people!

557f6564323437bc38000176_3.t: Gaichu bears his teeth in a wicked grin.
For years, you were taught that to be pure was to be powerful. That metahumans, the infected, and the deformed were to be scorned for their weakness. Yet here I stand, and there you lie. Broken.

557f6564323437bc38000176_4.t: You think a victory over Sasaki, Takagawa and me makes you superior? Hah!
Ishida's mouth twists into a cruel, sorrowful smile.
You'll spend the rest of your useless days cowering in the darkness, eating the flesh of your betters. You're no better than an animal, skilled though you may be.

557f6564323437bc38000176_5.t: Snarling in fury, Gaichu hurls his katana to the floor. He reaches out to grab Ishida by his collar, yanking him up so that their faces are only an inch apart.
You said the Red Samurai were the *best.* The strongest. The most capable. Those who were nonhuman were unworthy of consideration! You told me that I would be better for having joined you!

557f6564323437bc38000176_6.t: But it's a lie. I was infected, and I became *better* than I was! Your own blindness allowed me to escape - blind to my skill, and blind to the power of the 'impure'.
Gaichu lifts one hand, claws glittering in the pale warehouse light. His fingers splay out like an array of knives.
I was not superior because I was Red Samurai. I was Red Samurai because I was superior. *I* choose my own fate. *I* make my own destiny.

557f6564323437bc38000176_7.t: The ghoul draws his hand back, jagged shark's teeth bared as he presses them to Ishida's ear.

I will show you the strength of my disease, Ishida. I will make you like me, and you will be cast out, just as I was. I survived and remained intelligent because I have the will to do so. Can you say as much?

557f6564323437bc38000176_8.t: Ishida's head falls back as he laughs, but the laughter turns to bloody coughing.
You think I'll let you infect me, monster? I'll do what you were too weak to. I'll take my own life, like the 47 ronin of old. Such a foolish boy.

557f6564323437bc38000176_9.t: Foolish? You underestimate me, old man.
Gaichu drops Ishida, his head bouncing off the tiled floor with a sharp crack.
After I cripple your arms, you will have no means to kill yourself. The disease will burn through you like a fire, consuming every inch of your humanity.

557f6564323437bc38000176_10.t: At this, Ishida stiffens. His eyes go cold with resolution.
Then we shall see, Gaichu. And if you are wrong, I shall meet you again in my next life, and try to teach you to do better.

557f6564323437bc38000176_11.t: Gaichu whirls to face you, snarling.

What do you know of it?! Do you know what it is to be hunted at every turn? Treated like an animal and despised by all who see you? I could have stayed with the unit! I could have been the best of them!

557f6564323437bc38000176_12.t: Gaichu stops as Ishida begins laughing. Slowly, the ghoul lowers his clawed hand and takes a deep, heavy breath.
This... This is beyond revenge, isn't it?

557f6564323437bc38000176_14.t: Considering this for a long, quiet moment, Gaichu nods. He bends and picks up his sword.
These are more incisive words than I expected to hear. Thank you. I am unsure if I deserve counsel as wise as yours, but... I will take it.

557f6564323437bc38000176_15.t: Gaichu turns back to Ishida, and bows shallowly - but not insultingly so.
I regret our friendship ends like this, Ishida. You taught me well, and you ran a fine unit. Perhaps in the next life, we can be comrades once more. Until then... Goodbye.

557f6564323437bc38000176_18.t: Gaichu nods to you, turning back to Ishida.
Come. I will show you your own inner weakness.

55808ef9323437782b003aa0_0.t: The terminal glows blankly, awaiting your command to unsuppress the alarm system and tap into the Red Samurai communications channel.

55808ef9323437782b003aa0_1.t: Gaichu licks his lips in anticipation.
Now they will learn what it is to face me in true combat. No more running. No more hiding. Tonight, we end this for good.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_2.t: Gaichu cocks his head, sniffing the air in your direction.

Ahh. I see you have found my box of personal effects. Please, feel free to examine whatever you like within it. Just take care to put them back when you are done. I do not own much, but the things I keep are very precious to me. They are all that remains of my former life.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_6.t: It is a mask from the historical Noh play, "Ikuta Atsumori." The face is that of a young samurai, Taira no Atsumori.
Gaichu faces your direction, gesturing toward the mask.
I had a collection of a dozen or so Noh masks, when I was younger. That is the last remaining one, and my personal favorite.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_8.t: Noh actors wear those masks to cover their faces. The angle they hold themselves at changes the expression on the mask, so the acting is highly rigid and controlled. The entire performance is highly ritualized - there is no measure of improvisation allowed, only refinement of the prescribed form.
Gaichu folds his arms over his chest, dipping his chin in thought.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_14.t: A measure of civic pride, I suppose. And I like the story. Taira no Atsumori died at the battle of Ichi-no-Tani, just outside Kobe. The play takes place primarily at Ikuta Shrine, which is located in downtown Kobe.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_15.t: In old cities in Japan - especially in Keihanshin - I have always felt there is a sense of history wherever one looks. The skyline may have skyscrapers and delivery drones, but the foundations are laid on the bones of the dead.
Gaichu shrugs, folding his arms across his chest.
I think it is important to remember their sacrifices and joys, sorrows and glories.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_16.t: Gaichu laughs suddenly, shaking his head.
This is why sometimes I worry that I am a sentimentalist at heart.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_18.t: Gaichu wrinkles his nose, his expression one of distaste.
Ugh. That thing. It's a beautiful set, but it is a shame that I am unable to make use of it.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_19.t: No. My handwriting has always been absolutely terrible. Whatever fanciful stories you may have heard of being able to tell a swordsman's ability by his handwriting, I assure you that they are wrong.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_21.t: I appreciated the gesture, but my attempts at fine calligraphy mostly resembled excited chickens prancing over paper, their feet soaked in ink.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_25.t: Gaichu pauses, turning an ear more fully toward you.
Ah. It is a statue of Jizo, the protector of children. He is also the guardian of travellers - statues of him are placed along almost every road in Japan. His name outside of Japan is "Ksitigarbha."

5589801e306364c8160054ee_27.t: Jizo-in is a tiny place. It is fairly close to the much larger and better-known sister temple of Saiho-ji, on the western edge of Kyoto. Unlike Saiho-ji, however, Jizo-in is almost unknown.
Gaichu smiles fondly, looking sightlessly into the distance.
To get there, you have to walk through twisting residential streets, past a deep green grove of bamboo.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_30.t: Smiling faintly, Gaichu dips his head.
I rather thought so. I never saw more than two other people in the temple, no matter what the time of year. It seemed, at the time, to be my own private sanctuary.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_32.t: Cradling his chin in one hand, Gaichu seems to ponder this.
I am not sure how to answer. It was, but in a positive sense.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_36.t: Gaichu grunts, not turning toward you.
The fan is from Senso-ji temple, in Asakusa. One of the districts of Neo-Tokyo. The image is one of the less famous works by Ando Hiroshige, a woodblock printer from before the Meiji Restoration.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_41.t: Gaichu bursts into unexpected laughter, almost dropping his sword as he doubles over. He manages to gasp out a sentence after a long moment.
Oh my goodness. I had forgotten those were in the box.
He takes a few deep breaths, composing himself.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_42.t: Raising his sword, Gaichu returns to his practice.
Tell me, have you ever heard of Tetsuya Nakamura?

5589801e306364c8160054ee_50.t: Nakamura broke and ran, and I pursued him. He had a small holdout pistol - a PB-120 or something similar - and I grabbed the nearest weapon I could find.
Gaichu gestures toward you with the tip of his sword.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_53.t: Gaichu chuckles, tilting his head up toward the ceiling as he recalls the memory.
Takagawa managed to punch through his barrier with a well-placed.408 caliber slug from across the street. It didn't kill him, but it broke his concentration. Sasaki finished the rest.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_57.t: Gaichu nods in agreement, and steps forward to deliver a crosscut to the air in front of him.
Yes. But that page is all that I wanted from the book.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_59.t: Gaichu pivots on the ball of his heel, thrusting the tip of his sword into the darkness of the room.
The poet Saigyo. He was a master of conveying a sense of solitude and emptiness. And I have always found that poem to echo what I feel inside myself.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_64.t: Gaichu grunts, not bothering to look at you.
Haemadipsa zeylanica. The Japanese mountain leech.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_65.t: At this, Gaichu turns to face you, and you see that he is grinning wickedly.
Have you heard the story of Izanami and Izanagi?

5589801e306364c8160054ee_72.t: Before you can even ask a question, Gaichu raises a hand to silence you.
His name is Tako-kun. My mother gave him to me. No, I am not ashamed of this.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_73.t: Gaichu's eyes narrow, and he sniffs at you as he tries to determine whether you are making fun of him.
There is almost nothing in my life I have to remind me of my family. This is the thing I kept to serve that purpose.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_74.t: Gaichu still seems uncertain whether he should be angry or not.
An octopus is a clever creature. It knows how to conceal itself, how to open jars, escape from tanks. There are worse things than to be reborn as an octopus.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_75.t: I've always admired them for their talents. No one knows what an octopus is thinking when they look into its eye. They only know that they are being studied, just as much as they are studying it.
With that, Gaichu reaches out to take the plush toy, placing it gingerly back in the box.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_81.t: I am fine. Do not concern yourself.
The rough edge in his voice, however, says otherwise.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_82.t: Gaichu takes a deep breath, and begins again.
That hairpin belonged to a friend. It was a gift I gave her, while we walked along the banks of the Katsura River, near Arashiyama.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_84.t: Gaichu finally turns toward you, his expression unreadable.
Her name was Sayuri. We met in a spring seminar - an elective on Heian Era court poetry. I was trying to eliminate one of the softer electives I needed to complete my degree.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_85.t: Cocking his head, Gaichu puts a hand to his chin.
Have you ever met someone, and known within seconds that you would be close?

5589801e306364c8160054ee_86.t: It was a first for me, as well.
Gaichu draws a claw down the length of his jaw.
Yet there I was, and I knew it. It was not a romantic understanding, but rather that I knew - somehow - that our lives were intertwined.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_87.t: Naturally, we saw more and more of each other over the following weeks. And I suppose friendship turned to something more, as these things do.
Gaichu wraps his arms around his torso, looking away.
I did not have many friends. I never have. But she was that, and more.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_88.t: She was studying chemistry, which is about as far from global security studies as one can imagine. We had little in common, but it was still more than I really felt with anyone else.
Looking back at you, Gaichu shrugs.
It was an unconscious relationship, because it was easy. I never needed to think about it.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_89.t: Gaichu shakes his head, pressing a palm to his brow.
I am unsure how I cannot know, but that is the state of it. I believe I did, but how can I be sure?

5589801e306364c8160054ee_93.t: Plucking the hairpin from your fingers, Gaichu turns it delicately around in his fingers as he feels the shape.

I keep this to remind myself of the things we have to give up, in pursuit of what we think is best. But few of us ever really know what it is that we want - not truly. We simply grasp at mirages that we think will fulfill us.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_95.t: So I feel that it would behoove me to never forget the things I have given up in pursuit of "the better option." I do not dwell in the past, nor am I haunted by it, but it would be doing Sayuri a disservice to forget what she wanted for me.
Gaichu places the hairpin back in the box, turning away.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_97.t: Of course. This is a lesson I have been taught time and again, and no doubt will continue to learn until I die.

5589801e306364c8160054ee_98.t: Then you know the feeling I am speaking of.
Gaichu draws a claw down the length of his jaw.
Yet there I was, and I knew it. It was not a romantic understanding, but rather that I knew - somehow - that our lives were intertwined.

558c6c4730303194090069d2_13.t: Gaichu sniffs the air, then lets out a grunt of approval.
