I believe that we have located Raymond Black.

5594b0843234312019001a4a_14.t: At this point, the only thing we can do is move forward. Lead the way.

559ec0526236669809002374_115.t: Gaichu's face remains impassive but his eyelids lower in anticipation.
I have the opportunity to kill a god. I cannot allow the opportunity to pass unanswered.

559ec0526236669809002374_121.t: Gaichu's face remains impassive but his eyelids lower in anticipation.
I have the opportunity to kill a god. I cannot allow the opportunity to pass unanswered.

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_25.t: A small sound rolls in the back of Gaichu's throat.

55a59409303031880a002643_10.t: Gaichu nods his head in agreement.

We must keep our objective in sight. Kindly said that we can find information on Raymond's location in the security stations. It may be difficult getting into them, but once we do, that's where we can cut the alarm system.

55a59409303031880a002643_11.t: I believe it was also mentioned that, in order to free Raymond, we will need a proficient decker. We mustn't forget that. And one more thing...
He motions toward the Tsang security passes that you are shuffling in your hand.
We should not rely solely on those IDs. They may help, but using them alongside our own wits will get us farther.

55a59409303031880a002643_24.t: Gaichu nods his head in agreement.

We must keep our objective in sight. Kindly said that we can find information on Raymond's location in the security stations. It may be difficult getting into them, but once we do, that's where we can cut the alarm system.

55a59409303031880a002643_25.t: He motions toward the Tsang security passes that you are shuffling in your hand.
We should not rely solely on those IDs. They may help, but using them alongside our own wits will get us farther.

55a94da3623666741900929c_6.t: You describe a living hell. What is the appeal of a life without challenge? Without struggle?

55a94da3623666741900929c_17.t: Gaichu stretches. From under the overlapping plates of his armor, you can hear his joints pop.
This has been quite a day. With your leave, I will spend the remainder of the evening in silent meditation. You should rest as well. You look as tired as I've ever seen you.

55a94da3623666741900929c_56.t: His sacrifice showed great courage. Now, he will be slowly tortured for thousands of years.
I believe that will satisfy his debt.

55a94da3623666741900929c_65.t: Gaichu does not eat. He stands sullen amid the group.
I did not wish to defeat a god. I wanted to kill a god. I must admit, I am... disappointed.

55b940633234373838002559_4.t: The machine lies ahead, yes... but so does our quarry. I can smell it - the stench of something from another place.
He loosens his blade in its saya. The red-lacquered scabbard shimmers eerily in the light.
Let us advance, and meet this Yama King head-on. I will paint the walls with her blood.

5600401d626632e015004520_2.t: Let me see here... maybe that will be better.

He lifts the flute to his lips and blows. It produces a drunken warble of the sort that might be uttered by a dying bird, assuming that it was underwater at the time. Gaichu narrows his milky white eyes at the flute, then hurls it into a corner of the room.

5600401d626632e015004520_6.t: Ahh, my friend. Come, join me!
Gaichu waves you over. He is seated in front of a small tea set, and many sampler bags of assorted teas are scattered nearby.
I am trying to learn about Chinese tea. Perhaps you can help me.

5600401d626632e015004520_9.t: Lifting the cup to his lips, Gaichi takes a quick sip and swishes the "tea" around in his mouth. He makes contemplative noises before swallowing.
Hmm. Earthy. Slight hints of leaves and vegetation. And overtones of bitterness. Is this... A pu-erh tea? Maybe from Xiaguan?

5600401d626632e015004520_10.t: Gaichu beams proudly, too pleased with himself to notice your deception.
I confess, pu-erh is not my favorite type of tea. Too earthy and pungent for me - and it tastes a bit like something you'd find buried in the ground. Now! What can I do for you?

5600401d626632e015004520_11.t: Ugh. That is most unfortunate. I should drink some more, to ensure I remember the flavor - even if I do find it a little disgusting.
Gaichu slumps in defeat. Apparently he's too disappointed to notice your lie. He pours another cup of tea and lifts it to his lips.
Now... what can I do for you?

5600401d626632e015004520_12.t: Now, now. I know that the Chinese like their fermented teas, and that they can be quite pungent. But I do not think that there is any call to insult them to *that* degree.

5600401d626632e015004520_13.t: It's impossible for Gaichu to get any paler, but he certainly seems to be trying. After a moment, he coughs delicately.
Let us not repeat this affair to anyone else, hmm? And let us talk of something else. What is it that I can do for you? Preferably something that has nothing to do with tea.

5600401d626632e015004520_16.t: The ghoul looks crestfallen, but nods in assent.
All right. Perhaps later, then. What can I do for you?

5600401d626632e015004520_27.t: Let me see here... maybe that will be better.

He lifts the flute to his lips and blows. It produces a drunken warble of the sort that might be uttered by a dying bird, assuming that it was underwater at the time. Gaichu narrows his milky white eyes at the flute, then hurls it into a corner of the room.

5600401d626632e015004520_31.t: Gaichu is polishing a sword, humming a tune to himself as he works. When you enter, he sheathes the blade and inclines his head toward you.
Hello, my friend. How may I be of service to you?

5600401d626632e015004520_34.t: Gaichu stands, arms crossed over his chest, almost as if he were waiting for you to arrive. He nods in greeting.
Strange times we live in, are they not? Beset by unknown enemies, rescued by police, and conspiracies afoot all around.

5600401d626632e015004520_40.t: Gaichu shrugs and waves a hand in the direction of the door.

Duplicitous corporate managers, triad gangsters, or police... what, really, is the difference? None can be trusted fully. Each one would sell us out in a heartbeat if it benefited them in the long run. Yet, I continue to be paid, and so I will remain.

5600401d626632e015004520_43.t: The ghoul lifts his chin, taking a deep breath in through his nose.
Where you go, I will follow. My only counsel at this time is to be cautious, watch your back, and be prepared to have a way out.

5600401d626632e015004520_56.t: This is a subject that I have been considering. I enjoy working with this team, and I think that life in the shadows is my fate, but still...
Gaichu bobs his head, lost in thought.
I have been considering a vacation.

5600401d626632e015004520_57.t: Gaichu folds his arms, expression indignant.

Well, there is the obvious choice of Asamando. But other locales stand out as well! Haiti, for instance - ghouls are feared yet honored there, as they have been known to prey upon the Corpse Cadavres that infest the countryside. From what I have read, my kind are regarded as something akin to corpse janitors there.

5600401d626632e015004520_58.t: I have also been considering Bali. Warm weather, a society very tolerant of Awakened creatures, and a healthy respect and fear for leyak - which are not unlike ghouls. I would not mind exploring a jungle island, diving in the coral reefs, and generally trying to avoid having my head hunted for a bounty.

5600401d626632e015004520_61.t: And, should anyone cross me, I will simply shout that I am a servant of Queen Rangda, and that I will keep them safe from her wrath if they venerate me. After all, I *do* wear her colors of red, black, and white. Who would risk the wrath of a demon of death, eh?

5600401d626632e015004520_62.t: Gaichu claps you on the shoulder, smiling.
Perhaps you will have to come with me. Together, we could explore the water around Padang Bai!

5600401d626632e015004520_64.t: Gaichu purses his lips, regarding you with no small amount of suspicion.
Have you actually ever been on a vacation? Had a chance to relax without worrying about work, or being shot at? It can be surprisingly refreshing, you know.

5600401d626632e015004520_66.t: Gaichu folds his arms and snorts.

I am indifferent, I must confess. The pirates seem competent, but they are afraid of me. I briefly stepped off of the Bolthole, and it was made very clear to me that it would be better if I went back inside.

5600401d626632e015004520_70.t: Gaichu exhales heavily, shaking his head in disbelief.

This goes far beyond Krait. We are ensnared in a power play of global proportions. If Ares is willing to use Krait and her team to directly undermine Mitsuhama's influence on the Executive Council, then they are making a serious commitment to dominance in the Free Enterprise Zone.

5600401d626632e015004520_74.t: A chance? Of course we have a chance!
Gaichu stands straighter and squares his shoulders proudly.
It is a small chance - miniscule, in fact! But that is why we will succeed.

5600401d626632e015004520_77.t: Precisely! If the fate of Hong Kong is to rest in the hands of a corrupt Chief Inspector and her subordinates, history could do worse than to have us oppose them. I only lament that we will likely not have the chance to face Krait in a public place...

5600401d626632e015004520_79.t: Gaichu squints, bringing a hand up to his chin.
Whatever Krait is up to, if she is being tasked with damaging Mitsuhama, it will be *very* important to powerful forces. We must tread very carefully.

5600401d626632e015004520_82.t: Ishida, sometimes. The rest... not especially. I have made a concerted effort to put it behind me and move on with my life.
Gaichu's lips twist back into a grim smile.
Ishida I think about, because I wonder if he yet lives as a ghoul, or if he has been executed for his "infirmity."

5600401d626632e015004520_84.t: Not especially. After our battle, I made the decision not to dwell on the earlier parts of my life. If killing my former comrades is what it took to free myself, it would do them a disservice to undermine their sacrifice.
Gaichu folds his arms over his chest.

5600401d626632e015004520_95.t: I have never been in an underwater secret laboratory before. That was novel, I must confess.
Beyond that, it was a job. I am unsure what more to say than that.

5600401d626632e015004520_100.t: This is very true. SARS III appears to only have infected the client, and it is no longer an issue. All told, that is a better result than almost any other outcome.

5600401d626632e015004520_101.t: The ghoul spreads his hands with a satisfied look.

That was the sort of mission that I live for, to be honest. A high risk insertion into a secure facility, a complicated extraction that doesn't go according to plan, and a final showdown with some of the better soldiers that the megacorporations have to offer.

5600401d626632e015004520_103.t: I quite agree! Even if we will not be known by anything other than our street names, it is our deeds that matter in the end.

5600401d626632e015004520_108.t: Gaichu sighs, lifting his hands helplessly.

I hope the best for her, I really do - but it seems like such a waste on all fronts. Lose your lifetime of service and your pride in order to survive, or die betrayed by the company that you gave those years to in the first place.

5600401d626632e015004520_110.t: He shrugs, mouth drawn into a tight line.
I wish the best for her. Who knows - perhaps she will live, and our paths will cross in the shadows some day in the future.

5600401d626632e015004520_113.t: Gaichu snorts and spits on the ground.
Betrayal. It was inevitable. I do admit, I suspected that it would be Qiu who was responsible. Lam... he seemed too high-principled and naive to me. I suppose that I was wrong.

560196f43063640011003b25_13.t: Gaichu considers what you just read aloud.
Hmm... It sounds like these "researchers" are doing some kind of chip-based mind control down here, creating sleeper agents. Essentially, they are turning people into puppets.

5601caa73063640011003c69_7.t: Indeed. What could possibly go wrong?

5602ecf73063643816003192_1.t: There is something disconcerting about that sound.
His hand drops to the hilt of his katana.

5602ecf73063643816003192_5.t: Bloodlust... I can smell it in the air. The thing that has awoken has no mind of its own - its only thoughts are of violence.

5602ee046266328c18001575_22.t: We cut down a Yama King, Duncan. Bikers will not be a problem.

5602ee046266328c18001575_29.t: If that is your wish, then so be it.
The ghoul let out a low growl.
We should keep moving. The riots have provided excellent cover for our advance, but they will not last forever.

5602ee046266328c18001575_37.t: That was never in question. Of course we will emerge victorious; it is what we do, after all.
The ghoul let out a low growl.
We should keep moving. The riots have provided excellent cover for our advance, but they will not last forever.

5602ee046266328c18001575_44.t: There is no shame in accomplishing a task, however menial you might think it to be. Someone needs to see to this errand. It may as well be us.
The ghoul let out a low growl.
We should keep moving. The riots have provided excellent cover for our advance, but they will not last forever.

5602ee046266328c18001575_50.t: Gaichu's mouth parted into a crooked smile.
We cut down a Yama King, Duncan. We have nothing to fear from bikers.

5602ee046266328c18001575_51.t: I can smell our destination already. The tastes of blood and motor oil on the wind. And in the distance, I hear the sounds of crunching bone and the rev of V-twin engines, even over the cacophony of the riots.
Gaichu nodded at you, his gray flesh tinted yellow by the glow of the streetlights.

560304c33063643816003264_14.t: If this researcher is truly worried, she is sure to have already sealed the truly dangerous pathogens away. In labs such as these, samples can be rapidly shuttled away to secure refrigeration units via remotely-controlled drones. I know because I guarded one, once upon a time.

5604464e3063648823004d0d_6.t: Gaichu nods as Tang and her researchers step through the door.

How shall we proceed? I'd like to remind you that we have a sterilization countdown in progress, Silver Dream subjects at our backs, and a potential disease outbreak in this facility. Whatever we do, we must move quickly.

5604464e3063648823004d0d_29.t: I suppose that we have no choice but to leave, and quickly. Their Sector Commanders will no doubt have localized overrides for the trams. We'll need to eliminate them to escape.
Unfortunate. I was hoping to clean and butcher some of the bodies that we've been dropping on our way out. Leaving them to molder feels like a terrible waste.

5604a7a9643661740e0013c2_6.t: Not prisoners? Ridiculous. They knocked us out and locked us in a pen.
They've even gone so far as to have the Bolthole impounded.

5604a7a9643661740e0013c2_11.t: A low growl escapes the ghoul's throat.
"Guests." Strange. Nothing about this makes sense. If we are guests, why have we been caged? And further, why did they come for us at all? The APB on you was lifted, was it not?

5604a7a9643661740e0013c2_47.t: Yes. A great inconvenience for the both of us.
A low growl escapes the ghoul's throat.
None of this makes any sense. They questioned you, yes... but they did not question me. Why? Any interrogator worth his salt would have collected information from both of us simultaneously.

5604a7a9643661740e0013c2_54.t: Gaichu's nostrils flare as he tastes the air.
You were wounded - I can smell it. The blood is old... dried, perhaps. Did they mistreat you?

5604a7a9643661740e0013c2_73.t: And those of us on board were taken along for the ride.
He lets out an irritated grunt.
No warning was given. They simply hooked us to a tug, cut away our moorings, and hauled us away from the harbor.

5604a7a9643661740e0013c2_82.t: Do you hear that? Footsteps. Someone is coming.
He lowers himself into a ready stance.
Prepare yourselves. We are about to have company.

5604a7a9643661740e0013c2_98.t: I was taken, just as you were.

560591ce643661d4160012fc_14.t: Gaichu's voice comes out in a low growl.
Go. Get us some answers. With more information, we can better plan our next move.

56098865643661c8160012df_43.t: Then there is no more to be said.
He turns away, his armor rustling as his body shifts.
You go back to Lam and Qiu and accept the job. I will ready my gear.

56098865643661c8160012df_46.t: I suppose that the matter is settled, then.

56098865643661c8160012df_71.t: Ah. Probably a reasonable move, considering our employer's temperament.
No time like the present, I suppose. Let's try to raise Cheng on the comm and apprise her of the situation.

56098865643661c8160012df_81.t: We didn't follow you through the Walled City and test ourselves against a Yama King to let the police scare us away. Wherever this road may lead, we will follow.

56098865643661c8160012df_87.t: Our willingness is only a part of the equation. Kindly Cheng needs to know what is happening here as well.
She must already know that the Bolthole has been impounded. It would be wise to inform her of the rest before proceeding any further.

56098865643661c8160012df_99.t: The corner of the ghoul's mouth twitches up into a smirk.
It is good to know that you care. Now tell me. What do you feel that our next step should be?

56098865643661c8160012df_112.t: A wry smile creeps onto the ghoul's face.
And so they did... to a point.

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_63.t: Yes. And if they do so, those security personnel could complicate the situation.

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_92.t: Petty thieves. These two are unworthy of our time.
His lips lift into a rueful smile.
I imagine that the situation will resolve itself soon enough. The building that they're attempting to rob probably has a security contract... perhaps even automated defenses.

560efedf643661880e003a1b_11.t: Gaichu leaned in to growl into your ear.

The police cannot see us through the smoke... at least, not yet. If we give them a wide berth, we could probably slip past unnoticed. Of course, the people that they have caught will suffer for it, but that is a fate that they have brought upon themselves.

561444d664366150150022f8_39.t: The corner of Gaichu's mouth curled into a smile.

561ef7da643661281b000d3d_18.t: He steps forward, the plates of his armor rustling as he moves.
Come. Let's finish this and be done with it.

56218ac8306364fc18005d11_18.t: Gaichu cocks his head and starts running his fingers along the tram's wall.
Something is wrong. Do you feel it? This tram made a wrong turn at the last fork. We are headed in the wrong direction.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_6.t: Gaichu reaches up to wipe a tear from one of his milky eyes.
I wanted to look like a trid star. Instead, they made me into the wretched creature that you see before you.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_38.t: This cannot be Sheng. He does not know what you're talking about.

5633e0113063644416006cf6_12.t: Gaichu lifts his chin, grinning slightly.

563905d43063649c0800054a_5.t: As the sound of the gas explosion fades, you hear the tromp of boots in the distance. Gaichu nods in satisfaction.
That sounded like five or six guards going to investigate the blast. It is possible that in their haste they have left the elevators unguarded.

563905d43063649c0800054a_12.t: As the sound of the gas explosion fades, you hear the tromp of boots in the distance. Gaichu cocks his head and listens.
Five - perhaps six people running to investigate. Keep your fingers crossed that we are lucky - we may have just diverted the guards on this floor to that stove.

563a46753063643417003e71_10.t: Yes... ah... we came to Fuchi to see if we could secure more funding. Mr. Okuda requested that I come in makeup.

563a46753063643417003e71_15.t: Yes... but only the difficult chefs.
It is... ah... something of a morality play... about cannibalism. And white, fluffy hats.

563a46753063643417003e71_19.t: Yes... that is correct. We're here trying to secure additional funding from Fuchi's entertainment division. Production costs will be higher than expected.

563fe3b7306364f02200541b_3.t: Those guards are well-trained. I can sense their suspicion. I suggest that we either give them a wide berth, or attack them before getting too close.

56415f426436615816003b2f_5.t: This machine thrums with power. Can you hear it, the low vibration at its core?
Gaichu inclines his head at the machine.
I cannot tell you what purpose this device might serve, but given its position, I would expect that it is quite important.

5643bf5f643661c0130063f0_6.t: Gaichu answers with a noncommittal grunt.
Lead on. I'll be right behind you.

5643bf5f643661c0130063f0_15.t: Gaichu drives an armored shoulder into the closing doors, wedging them into place.
There. That should keep reinforcements from flooding in behind us... for a time, at least.

5643bf5f643661c0130063f0_16.t: A temporary solution. It will not hold them for long.
He cocks his head, tilting it in the direction of the hallway.
The killing is far from over. We will shed blood to the scream of these blaring alarms, and together, we will cut our way to the evidence that we seek.

5643bf5f643661c0130063f0_17.t: Yes - a temporary one. It will not hold them for long.
He cocks his head, tilting it in the direction of the hallway.
The killing is far from over. We will shed blood to the scream of these blaring alarms, and together, we will cut our way to the evidence that we seek.

5643bf5f643661c0130063f0_28.t: Gaichu answers with a noncommittal grunt.
Lead on. I'll be right behind you.

5643bf5f643661c0130063f0_34.t: Gaichu answers with a noncommittal grunt.
Lead on. I'll be right behind you.

5643bf5f643661c0130063f0_41.t: That was... smoother than I had expected. Quite a feat to make it so far without bloodshed.
He loosens his blade in its scabbard.
Now that we are here, in the belly of the beast, combat is surely inevitable.

564528e4306364e01b008184_18.t: You are accusing *us* of kidnapping Lily?
Gaichu glances in Lily's direction, his face stern.
Where is this misinformation coming from?

565f3a8c62663208210043f1_51.t: I'm afraid that Gobbet is right.
If this... "payment"... goes through, your shadowrunning days are over. You could remain in Heoi, perhaps. But you would need to find a different line of work.

565f3a8c62663208210043f1_59.t: This presents us with something of a conundrum.
His voice is curiously soft, and an undertone of sorrow runs through it.
With your SIN restored, you could travel back to Seattle... but I could not go with you. And even if you remained in Heoi, our professional relationship would have to come to an end.

565f3a8c62663208210043f1_71.t: Gaichu lets out a grunt, an expression of disgust on his face.
Somehow I doubt that. We never should have trusted--

566213356436616013004496_11.t: Gaichu steps forward, shielding his face with one hand.
The storm will work to our advantage, yes. But the longer we stand around talking, the greater our risk of detection. We should strike now, while we still have the initiative.

566f0c4f643661780f0042a2_14.t: If that is your choice, then so be it. Our association ends here.

566f69ef6436613815007c49_26.t: Think of all the challenges that we have bested to date. If Qian Ya and Ares couldn't stop us, then what challenge could Seattle present?
Gaichu slaps a hand against his thigh and laughs.
We will prosper there, just as we did in Heoi! And the shadows will tremble at our approach!

566f69ef6436613815007c49_28.t: Probably a wise decision... it smells like kerosene and shoe leather.
But enough of this. Our future looks bright, don't you think? We will prosper in Seattle, just as we did in Heoi. And it was past time for a change of scenery.

566f69ef6436613815007c49_31.t: Gaichu walks over to the lounge table, pulling a bottle of Jomo's baiju off of it. He tosses it to you.
A toast, then, to where we are going. What will you drink to? Wealth? Good luck? Long life? I would toast *you,* but alcohol and my condition react poorly together.

566f69ef6436613815007c49_33.t: Gaichu leans back, putting a hand on either hip.

Well, then! Are you ready to show me the wonders of the UCAS? Find a whole new variety of troubles we can get into? Go from job to job like the hired warriors we are? I, for one, believe we will have interesting adventures wherever we go.

566f69ef6436613815007c49_52.t: You will have to pardon me if I pass, then. I doubt that they will have anything I am capable of digesting. Most restaurants don't.

566f69ef6436613815007c49_60.t: Gaichu makes a quiet rumbling in his throat, and dips his head.

People reinvent themselves constantly. This is true of everyone. Think of the arc of Duncan's life: from barrens ganger, to police officer, to Triad-affiliated shadowrunner. I think that I know more about this subject than most. Believe me when I say that leaving others behind is inevitable.

566f69ef6436613815007c49_64.t: He sounds like a leopard seal with a sinus infection.
Sometimes, this acute hearing of mine is a curse. Sixteen days of this will be... challenging to endure.

5695567e643661dc1600166d_23.t: Still, we were able to talk to speak with a desk sergeant without getting caught. I take that as a good sign.

569596123063640c1f006f01_11.t: Gaichu inhales sharply through his nose, baring his teeth.
I smell gun oil, and can hear the crackle of a radio. Guards nearby - likely in the next room. No alarm as of yet. We have the drop on them.

56ac0de5626632681d00a572_4.t: If that is how you wish to proceed, then so be it.

56ac0de5626632681d00a572_13.t: We've just seen what these patrols will do to anyone they catch wandering the streets.
He nodded toward the fresh corpses of the locals that the patrol had just gunned down.
If we get too much closer, they will fire on us, just as they did to those poor fools. We should try to find a way around.

56ad342b626632980d007eb8_73.t: The warehouse is burning quite well.
His face glows yellow-orange in the flickering light.

54b97f29303031641a004032_2.t: His craggy face flickers onto the screen.
My friend. There is something that I would like to discuss with you... something related to the Ares run that you recently accepted.

54b97f29303031641a004032_9.t: You must reconsider. This is very important to me - I cannot stress how important.

54b97f29303031641a004032_11.t: He gives you a terse nod, his jaw clenched. Koschei stands unnaturally still, but its sensor cluster irises in to focus on you.
That will be... acceptable. But see to it that you do, my friend. I *need* those circuits. And I will have them, one way or another.

54cfdf5b306364a06a008577_19.t: He strokes his chin, and Koschei flexes its articulated limbs in anticipation.

54cfdf5b306364a06a008577_26.t: What do you suppose our first order of business should be? I doubt that we'll be welcome at Ma's party, but it could be worth investigating.
Or we could head directly to his apartment and try the door. I'm open to either approach.

54d00020306364a06a008a84_16.t: Racter turns toward you, his dissatisfaction obvious.
That was less fruitful than I'd hoped. There were some interesting details, but nothing incriminating. Nothing conclusive.

54d4ef50306364544f00014a_5.t: Racter rapidly begins re-wiring the electronics panel, swapping plugs and wires across several boards. After a moment, the panel emits a soft beep.
There we are. You should be receiving the camera feeds on your commlink now.

54d5495c306364bc7300017a_8.t: I'm not surprised. Assuming these experiments dealt with the metagenes of the subject, severe mutations would have been acceptable byproducts of the research.
Racter taps the vat's glass with the back of an index finger.
Ethics often fall by the wayside in these projects.

54d5495c306364bc7300017a_9.t: Racter leans closer to the tank, tapping it with the back of one index finger.
Fascinating. Mutation and cancerous growths on a massive scale, yet the creature is still living - albeit with a great deal of technical assistance. I'm amazed they've managed it.

54d5495c306364bc7300017a_11.t: Racter is nonplussed, and simply shrugs.
Research requires sacrifice. This creature's life and death will be meaningless unless humanity learns from it - be it the data itself, or a caution against science without ethics.

54d92ad03063645032003070_21.t: Racter gives Gobbet a quizzical look, tilting his chin upward.
How easy, exactly, is a sunday afternoon?

54d92ad03063645032003070_23.t: Racter unclips Koschei from the harness he used to haul it aboard. In response, it whirrs and begins powering up weapons systems.
I wouldn't call it "smooth sailing", but we seem to have slipped by unnoticed. Let's keep it that way.

54d96c983030316814006fac_4.t: Yes, my friend. Yes, of course you will.

54d96c983030316814006fac_8.t: And you know what I am. What I can do.
You will be here beside me when the time comes, yes?

54d96c983030316814006fac_10.t: He nods, his pearl-white smile glinting in the dim light.
Be well, my friend. And rest while you can... there is more looming on the horizon than you could possibly dream of.

54d96c983030316814006fac_11.t: None of us truly know anything. But I haven't frightened you off yet, and I take that as a good sign.
He nods, his pearl-white smile glinting in the dim light.
Be well, my friend. And rest while you can... there is more looming on the horizon than you could possibly dream of.

54d96c983030316814006fac_12.t: Fair enough. Perhaps not the answer that I was hoping for, but so long as you don't stand in my way, there won't be a problem.
He nods, his pearl-white smile glinting in the dim light.
Be well, my friend. And rest while you can... there is more looming on the horizon than you could possibly dream of.

54d96c983030316814006fac_13.t: And that remains an open question. I suppose that we'll just have to wait and for ourselves.
He cocks his head, grinning. Koschei mimics the motion.

54d96c983030316814006fac_15.t: I would enjoy that, my friend.
And what do you think the likelihood of that is? Do you intend to stand beside me when the time comes?

54d96c983030316814006fac_21.t: Racter glances back toward his workshop. The automated tools that line the walls light up in response.
And with that, my friend, I should really get back to my machines. Our task in the Walled City may be over, but my work for the evening has only just begun.

54d96c983030316814006fac_22.t: He nods, his pearl-white smile glinting in the dim light.
And rest while you can... there is more looming on the horizon than you could possibly dream of.

54d96c983030316814006fac_24.t: No, my friend. No, of course you don't.

54d96c983030316814006fac_25.t: Yes, my friend. Of course it was.

54d96c983030316814006fac_26.t: Well, my friend. Quite an evening.
He takes a drag on his cigarette.

54d96c983030316814006fac_27.t: That said, I am quite tired, and after a brief rest I should get back to my work. Koschei is bound to be in need of repairs, after all.
He turns his back on you.
Do me a favor and see yourself out, won't you?

54d96c983030316814006fac_28.t: I bid you goodnight, my friend. My work for the evening has only just begun.

54d96c983030316814006fac_29.t: What more is there *to* say? We have done good work together, yes, but we aren't working now. And when I stand in this shop, my time is my own.
The industrial arms that hang from the ceiling spring to life, filling the air with the sound of whirring machinery.
Again, I bid you goodnight. My work for the evening has only just begun.

54d96c983030316814006fac_33.t: He tips his cigarette. A cone of burning embers tumbles to the ground.
I've been waiting for this for a long time.

54d96c983030316814006fac_35.t: He turns back to the whirring machines that fill his shop, watching their progress with hunger in his eyes.

54d96c983030316814006fac_36.t: And you will see it soon, my friend.
He turns back to the whirring machines that fill his shop, watching their progress with hunger in his eyes.

54d96c983030316814006fac_37.t: He tips his cigarette. A cone of burning embers tumbles to the ground.

54d96c983030316814006fac_38.t: He tips his cigarette. A cone of burning embers tumbles to the ground.

54d96c983030316814006fac_45.t: You must reconsider. This is very important to me - I cannot stress how important.

54d96c983030316814006fac_47.t: He gives you a terse nod, his jaw clenched. Koschei stands unnaturally still, but its sensor cluster irises in to focus on you.
That will be... acceptable. But see to it that you do, my friend. I *need* those circuits. And I will have them, one way or another.

54d96c983030316814006fac_54.t: Ah. I was wondering when I'd meet the new neighbor.
His voice is pleasant. Cultured. There's a hint of a Russian accent there, but it's buried under layers of nuance.

54d96c983030316814006fac_60.t: He turns toward you, smiling, and for the first time you can see his face. He has broadly handsome Slavic features and a chiseled jaw. His eyes are like flecks of ice.
So sorry to have kept you waiting...?

54d96c983030316814006fac_61.t: You're too kind. Now, tell me - what can I do for...
His voice trails off as a flash of motion catches his eye.

54d96c983030316814006fac_62.t: With alarming speed, a sinister-looking drone scuttles out from under the work table. Its movements are surprisingly agile and fluid. The machine rears back menacingly, spreading its forelegs in a clear sign of aggression.

54d96c983030316814006fac_63.t: The man's smile tilts, and his tone goes apologetic.
Please, don't mind the drone. He can be... territorial. But so long as you remain civil, he will not bite.

54d96c983030316814006fac_64.t: He extends a hand. Simultaneously, the drone relaxes into a neutral position, lowering its killing legs.
Racter. My mechanical counterpart here is called Koschei.

54d96c983030316814006fac_65.t: His hand is rough and abnormally warm to the touch. He shakes your hand with a solid grip.
I am very pleased to meet you, my friend. In a community such as Heoi, it's important to be on good terms with one's neighbors.

54d96c983030316814006fac_67.t: He glances at the bracer on his forearm. A technical display winks to life, then gutters out.
Very well. This morning's casting should still be cooling for a few minutes yet. That's time enough to talk.

54d96c983030316814006fac_69.t: Exactly what I said. A casting that I made of a new locomotive assembly for Koschei.
He gestures at the display above the work table.
A biomimetic design, as you can see. This one is inspired by the walking legs of a decapod crustacean - the mangrove crab, to be specific.

54d96c983030316814006fac_71.t: More training and experience than I care to mention.
He offers you a wry smile.
Drone architecture was once my profession, you see. Now it's more of a calling... one that I'm free to pursue, now that I've freed myself from the shackles of corporate servitude.

54d96c983030316814006fac_75.t: Oh? How marvelous! I hadn't thought to meet anyone versed in the Russian classics here in Heoi.

54d96c983030316814006fac_76.t: Indeed, he was those things. But it was not Koschei's misdeeds that drew me to the name.

54d96c983030316814006fac_83.t: The same could be said of many in Heoi, I'm sure. This *is* a smugglers' den, is it not? Our entire economy is based on people having things that they shouldn't. Is there a particular device that interests you, out of curiosity...?

54d96c983030316814006fac_84.t: His smile flashes brilliantly in the light.
Good guess! That's precisely what they are. They... "fell off a boat," you might say. They weren't cheap, but I acquired them and had them mounted to the walls of my shop - I simply had to have them.

54d96c983030316814006fac_85.t: The return on investment has been dramatic. Yes, they're cruder by far than the waldo devices that I used in my professional life, but they still do the job and they are *mine.*
He gazes lovingly at the industrial arms, still smiling.
They have increased my fabrication capabilities nearly tenfold. And that, to me, is worth any price.

54d96c983030316814006fac_87.t: No more so than anyone else who follows his passions and perfects his craft.

54d96c983030316814006fac_88.t: You have a good ear, I'm impressed. Yes, I grew up in Nizhny Novgorod... went to school there, started my career there in the industrial sector. A fairly common story, I'm sure. But I have also traveled a great deal, and in so doing, I have absorbed a number of other languages and dialects.

54d96c983030316814006fac_89.t: ...It shames me to admit that I'm only literate in twelve, however.

54d96c983030316814006fac_90.t: Perhaps, when compared to the common man. But I've known a great many polyglots who can - and do - put me to shame.
He gives you another half shrug, and Koschei mirrors the gesture.
Arabic has been a particular bugbear of mine - the unfamiliar characters and lack of vowels make it damned tricky to get a handle on. But I suppose that all men have their limits.

54d96c983030316814006fac_91.t: His brow furrows. Koschei takes a step forward.
I assure you, I am not bragging. In truth, I've never much cared what anyone else thought of my abilities or accomplishments - I am quite self-sufficient in that regard.

54d96c983030316814006fac_92.t: If I tell you that I feel ashamed, it is because I do. When knowledge eludes me, I take it poorly.
His shoulders relax, and Koschei adopts the same posture.
The curse of perfectionism, I suppose.

54d96c983030316814006fac_98.t: No. Not at all. This is just the way that I speak.
Would it make you more comfortable if I used the word "da" more often? Referred to you as "comrade," perhaps?

54d96c983030316814006fac_100.t: I'm afraid that I will have to disappoint you, then. I am not a caricature or a cartoon. I am a man who happens to have grown up in the Russian Republic. If you cannot grasp the distinction, then I pity you. But I won't change myself for your sake.

54d96c983030316814006fac_101.t: That is something of a sore subject. My departure was involuntary, you see... I did not part ways with my employer under the best of terms. I will tell you that I worked for Grishin-Aviakor, but you'll forgive me if I don't want to go into detail.

54d96c983030316814006fac_103.t: He pauses, and for a second something flashes in his eye. Koschei lowers its body into a crouch.
A rather... personal question, wouldn't you say?

54d96c983030316814006fac_106.t: He gives you a shallow nod.
I dislike the term, but yes. I run the shadows. What gave me away?

54d96c983030316814006fac_107.t: He shakes his head slightly, a sad look on his face.
Such a reductive assessment. I assure you, you're selling my little counterpart quite short. Koschei *is* combat-capable - very much so - but that is far from his only purpose.

54d96c983030316814006fac_110.t: Interesting. Well, your intuition is correct, as I said. Now I'm going to use mine: you're a shadowrunner as well, but you're new to the profession. Am I correct?

54d96c983030316814006fac_113.t: He wags his finger at you.
Come now. There's no sense in obfuscating the truth. We're neighbors, after all.

54d96c983030316814006fac_114.t: You are correct, of course. The residents of shadow communities are usually either too poor to live elsewhere, or engage in behaviors too illicit to risk it. I will admit to being in the latter camp.

54d96c983030316814006fac_115.t: Designing robotics is my passion, but it doesn't always pay the bills.
He runs a hand over Koschei's armored chassis, caressing the sculpted metal with his fingertips.
My status as a foreigner has proven something of an impediment in this regard.

54d96c983030316814006fac_117.t: He glances at the display on his bracer again.

54d96c983030316814006fac_120.t: A compelling offer. I'm doing quite well on my own, but I must admit that there are certain jobs for which I am unsuited.

54d96c983030316814006fac_131.t: Very good. I should focus my attention on this morning's casting anyway.
A pleasure meeting you, at any rate. I'll let you be on your way.

54d96c983030316814006fac_132.t: Your hand smacks into his with a hearty thwack. If he's taken aback, he doesn't show it.
Yes. Well. A pleasure. In a community such as Heoi, it's important to be on good terms with one's neighbors.

54d96c983030316814006fac_133.t: After an awkward moment, he withdraws his hand. If he's irritated, he doesn't show it.
I am very pleased to meet you. In a community such as Heoi, it's important to be on good terms with one's neighbors.

54d96c983030316814006fac_134.t: And you as well. Now, tell me - what can I do for...
His voice trails off as a flash of motion catches his eye.

54d96c983030316814006fac_135.t: His voice trails off as a flash of motion catches his eye.

54d96c983030316814006fac_142.t: Racter touch-types a series of commands into his bracer, and the arms relax into an idle posture. He smiles at you, a cigarette dangling from his lip.
There, that's better. Now, what can I do for you?

54d96c983030316814006fac_144.t: Perfectly well, thank you. They all seem competent enough, and there haven't been any major personality conflicts so far.
So long as they continue performing to an acceptable standard, I can see no reason not to continue our arrangement. It's good to run with a team again.

54d96c983030316814006fac_146.t: Gaichu is quite intriguing. I'm very pleased that the team is willing to overlook his unfortunate condition; many in the shadows wouldn't be.
There is a momentary pause. He shrugs.
...And that, I'm afraid, is all that I have to say. Apologies if you were looking for more.

54d96c983030316814006fac_147.t: There is a momentary pause. He shrugs.
...And that, I'm afraid, is all that I have to say. Apologies if you were looking for more.

54d96c983030316814006fac_148.t: You don't manage morale, my friend. You manage the team. So long as we continue to succeed, morale will take care of itself.

54d96c983030316814006fac_150.t: No, I don't mind. Not at all.
He takes a drag on his cigarette, then slowly releases the smoke.
I can't promise that my answers will satisfy your curiosity, of course. But you may certainly ask.

54d96c983030316814006fac_159.t: Koschei scuttles forward, allowing the rigger to rest his hand on the sloping metal of its chassis. Racter stares at his own deformed reflection in the metal of the drone's body, a sad smile on his face.

54d96c983030316814006fac_162.t: He wipes his hands, as if cleaning them. A wry smile spreads across his face.
...And then the AfA invaded Greece and Spain, sparking off Euro War II. We redeployed our troops to help repel them, and all was made right again.

54d96c983030316814006fac_165.t: Just a figure of speech, my friend. Nothing more. Now, shall we talk about something else? Surely there are more interesting - and relevant - things to discuss.

54d96c983030316814006fac_166.t: Indeed. There are lessons everywhere, if only we stop to look for them. But I have gone on for too long about this already... I must be boring you. Shall we talk about something else? Surely there are more interesting - and relevant - things to discuss.

54d96c983030316814006fac_169.t: No, my friend. The British Aerospace Nightwraith is a fighter-bomber with stealth capabilities. These were the machines that rained fire on our troops... or were purported to have, at any rate. Nobody has ever come forward to accept responsibility for the attack.

54d96c983030316814006fac_170.t: Yes, that's right. And the deaths of tens of thousands of people, and the destruction of the infrastructure and economy of nearly forty million others. These are impressive numbers, yes? And they represent a tragedy on a grand scale. But nations at war seldom concern themselves with such things.

54d96c983030316814006fac_173.t: I suppose that I can't fault you for it. Not many people outside of Europe care to remember. Suffice it to say that the Euro Wars were formative experiences for Russians of my generation. They weren't traumatic, per se... not as they were for the Germans, Finns, and Poles. But they were important, nonetheless.

54d96c983030316814006fac_174.t: He takes a drag on his cigarette.
Ah. Yes, I suppose that you wouldn't know. They were only active for the years surrounding the conflict, after all.

54d96c983030316814006fac_183.t: It was, to a point. They were disorganized, to be sure, but perfectly capable. We enjoyed a certain level of camaraderie while we ran together. Sadly, we are no longer on the best of terms.

54d96c983030316814006fac_187.t: He looks away, a wistful expression on his face. Koschei scrapes the ground with its forelegs, its sensors fixed firmly on you.

54d96c983030316814006fac_189.t: Life in Hong Kong is more structured than it is in Berlin. Things make sense here in a way that the Flux State didn't.
He taps a finger to his temple.

I have an orderly mind. Everything in its place, neatly labeled and filed away. This approach is what allows me to make sense of the world. To say that the people I knew in Berlin thought differently would be an understatement.

54d96c983030316814006fac_191.t: He taps a finger to his temple.

I have an orderly mind. Everything in its place, neatly labeled and filed away. This approach is what allows me to make sense of the world. To say that the people I knew in Berlin thought differently would be an understatement.

54d96c983030316814006fac_195.t: A good group of people. Disorganized, to be sure, but capable. Sadly, we are no longer on the best of terms.

54d96c983030316814006fac_196.t: About. I have spent the majority of my life as a legitimate roboticist, as I told you. Thankfully, my teammates in Berlin were good teachers. We even enjoyed a certain degree of camaraderie toward the end.
Sadly, we are no longer on the best of terms.

54d96c983030316814006fac_198.t: Why, I left Russia to hunt down the men who stole my research.
He pulls the cigarette from his mouth. Tips it toward you in salute.
And, thanks to you, I have recovered it.

54d96c983030316814006fac_199.t: The very same. And now it is mine again... mine to implement, to improve upon, and, ultimately, to perfect.

54d96c983030316814006fac_201.t: They're likely being apprehended, even as we speak. We *did* plant evidence that they colluded with the Red Dragons, after all.
A predatory smile spreads across his face.
Corporate espionage carries heavy penalties, you know. Very heavy. They can look forward to spending the rest of their days in a black-site prison.

54d96c983030316814006fac_203.t: They brought it upon themselves. If they have brains in their heads, they'll spend the rest of their miserable lives thanking their captors for protecting them from me.

54d96c983030316814006fac_205.t: Oh, yes, my friend. Quite a lot more. It is my life's work, and it is *important.*

54d96c983030316814006fac_206.t: He holds his tongue for a moment, considering.
...When we first met, I told you that I parted ways with Grishin-Aviakor under unfortunate circumstances, yes?

54d96c983030316814006fac_207.t: These same circumstances led me to Berlin. I was wronged, and I would not find recourse in Russia.
I suppose that there's no sense in being vague. I'll tell you the story if you want to hear it.

54d96c983030316814006fac_216.t: There is a squeal of tortured metal as Koschei drags its claws over the iron grating that covers the floor. Racter glances down at the drone and it goes calm. He takes a deep breath and continues.

54d96c983030316814006fac_218.t: I picked up and left the next day. My old team was upset, to be sure - Lucky Strike in particular howled for my blood. But I know where my priorities lie. And so, here I am. And here they are. And soon, I will be ready to act.

54d96c983030316814006fac_228.t: Very well. I'll share the story if you're ready to hear it.

54d96c983030316814006fac_230.t: Go ahead and ask, my friend. I'm not stopping you.

54d96c983030316814006fac_231.t: He speaks through clenched teeth. Koschei drags a claw across the ground, and you can hear the squeal of metal on metal.
Those aren't their real names, obviously. They shed their former identities when they left their old lives behind.

54d96c983030316814006fac_233.t: He idly brushes Koschei's armored chassis with the fingertips of his right hand.
Oh, how wonderful it will be to find them. What a reunion we will have.

54d96c983030316814006fac_234.t: Yes. Working for Ares Asia Holdings, ensconced away in one of their enclaves here. I am not certain which one yet, but I'm narrowing it down.
He idly brushes Koschei's armored chassis with the fingertips of his right hand.
Oh, how wonderful it will be to find them. What a reunion we will have.

54d96c983030316814006fac_235.t: Yes. Yes, much more. It was my life's work, and it was *important.* Reducing it to a bargaining chip was a deadly insult, both to me and to all those who would benefit from my work.

54d96c983030316814006fac_238.t: Religious? No. Religious superstition is a relic of the past. I welcome the *future*... a future that my research was intended to help usher in.

54d96c983030316814006fac_241.t: Yes, that's right. The seeds of the idea trace back even further, to the 1920s and J.B.S. Haldane. But it was Huxley who popularized it. And now, the transhumanist vision has progressed beyond philosophy. It is fact.

54d96c983030316814006fac_244.t: Yes, my friend. I will admit, my vision of the future may sound overly optimistic... but it is a future that I'm sure is coming, all the same.

54d96c983030316814006fac_245.t: None taken. I have long since grown accustomed to such responses. I will admit, my vision of the future may sound overly optimistic. But it's a future that I'm sure is coming, all the same.

54d96c983030316814006fac_248.t: ...And with that, I'm afraid that I'm going to have to bid you good night. I've taken enough of a break for one evening; there is still work to be done, and it would be lax of me to put it aside any longer.

54d96c983030316814006fac_251.t: None taken. I have long since grown accustomed to such responses. I will admit, my vision of the future may sound overly optimistic. But it's a future that I'm sure is coming, all the same.

54d96c983030316814006fac_253.t: And what would you suggest to be more important, more *impactful* to our species than the science of cybertechnology?

54d96c983030316814006fac_254.t: These are all fine things, of course. I wouldn't downplay the importance of any of them. But cybertechnology has fundamentally altered our people - indeed, our *species* - on a physiological level. Can you name me a novel, or a painting, or a piece of music that can claim the same?

54d96c983030316814006fac_255.t: Apples and oranges, my friend. Apples and oranges.

54d96c983030316814006fac_260.t: He wags his finger at you.

You're putting words in my mouth. I never dismissed magic as superstition. Magic is an observable, measurable phenomenon, as real as the laws of thermodynamics. What I said was that religion, in the classical model, is rooted in superstition. I stand by that analysis.

54d96c983030316814006fac_262.t: The corner of his mouth tilts upward.
I suppose that's probably true. This is a matter that's near to my heart. Indeed, it ties into the guiding philosophy of my life.

54d96c983030316814006fac_267.t: Sorry about the smell - it's long past time to purge the coolant sump.

If I had access to the proper equipment, this would not be a problem - I could clean the fluid and reuse it. But alas, halogen treatment chambers are rather hard to come by, even in a place like Heoi.

54d96c983030316814006fac_268.t: He shakes his head as if to clear it.
Anyway, my friend. You didn't come here to discuss the ins and outs of shop maintenance, I'm sure. So, what does bring you here?

54d96c983030316814006fac_270.t: He tilts his head in acquiescence.
Be my guest. I have no secrets to keep.

54d96c983030316814006fac_273.t: He shifts his cigarette to the corner of his mouth.
Very well. The long version it is.

54d96c983030316814006fac_277.t: Finally, the system had a magical component.
The corner of his mouth dips downward.
...I must confess a certain degree of ignorance when it comes to this portion of the project. I understand some of the theory behind what was done, but the specifics are beyond me.

54d96c983030316814006fac_286.t: The third and final element was a magical component that served to stave off entropy and render the first two systems self-perpetuating.
You can think of magic as the lubrication that kept the gears turning. An unfortunate, but necessary concession.

54d96c983030316814006fac_287.t: If that is what you'd prefer... it doesn't bother me either way. Just know that it is important to me that we recover my research, and the prototype that was stolen.
He claps you on the shoulder.
And we *will* recover them, my friend. Of that, I have no doubt.

54d96c983030316814006fac_288.t: He takes a long drag on his cigarette, sucking the smoke deep into his lungs. When he speaks, it erupts from his mouth in a plume.
The ultimate freedom to direct our own progress, and to steer ourselves as a people in whatever direction we wish.

54d96c983030316814006fac_292.t: He raises both hands in a placating gesture.
Just an example, my friend, just an example. Substitute in *my* parents instead - or any other pairing that you choose to imagine - if it makes you more comfortable.

54d96c983030316814006fac_299.t: What I desire is a truer fusion of man and machine.
His eyes flit to Koschei. The drone takes a step forward in response.

Modern cybernetic systems tend to be iterations on the biological components that they replace. There are exceptions, of course - there is no analog for the datajack in nature. But on the whole, our imagination has been depressingly limited in this regard.

54d96c983030316814006fac_301.t: The timely realization of such a future is what drives me forward. It is the cause to which I have devoted my life. And, thankfully, it is inevitable.
He tips his cigarette, sending ash spilling down onto the metal floor.
...Assuming, of course, that we don't destroy ourselves first.

54d96c983030316814006fac_302.t: Whenever we choose to define ourselves in that way. "Humanity" is an intrinsically human concept. If we decide to rebrand ourselves as a different species, that is our decision to make. I strongly suspect that nobody else would care.

54d96c983030316814006fac_304.t: Koschei raises itself up on its legs, stretching so that Racter can caress its chassis. The motion is alarmingly fluid, more like a living thing than a machine. Racter traces his fingertips over the drone's shell, his eyes focused on an imaginary point on the horizon.

54d96c983030316814006fac_308.t: Look around you. Everything *about* the world that we inhabit bears the stamp of metahuman tampering, from the food that we eat to the homes that we live in to the air we breathe. "Playing God" is a meaningless term. We've been reshaping our environment since time immemorial. All that I'm advocating is a further refinement of the process.

54d96c983030316814006fac_311.t: I see no shame in taking pride in my work.
Koschei steps forward, the tip of its leg digging into the metal grate that lines the shop floor.
I believe that the fruits of my labor speak for themselves.

54d96c983030316814006fac_313.t: You're speaking of the essence limit, and the problems associated with essence loss.
He releases a plume of smoke from the corner of his mouth.
Yes, of course, it will be a problem. But as with all problems, a workaround will present itself. Of this, I am certain.

54d96c983030316814006fac_314.t: No, my friend, I am not taking it on faith. My beliefs are rooted in my own observations and experiences. For the time being, let us leave the matter at that.

54d96c983030316814006fac_321.t: As if on cue, the drone scuttles forward, the hardened tips of its claws screeching across the metal floor of the shop. It comes up short just in front of you, a strange mechanical chitter rising from deep within its chassis.

54d96c983030316814006fac_323.t: Because I have work to do, and I'm sure that you can find better uses for your time than listening to me prattle on.
He gestures toward the hatch in the ceiling.
And with that, I'm afraid that I must ask you to show yourself out. We can continue our talk at a later time.

54d96c983030316814006fac_326.t: It is. I have work to do, and I'm sure that you can find better uses for your time than listening to me prattle on.
He gestures toward the hatch in the ceiling.
And with that, I'm afraid that I must ask you to show yourself out. We can continue our talk at a later time.

54d96c983030316814006fac_327.t: He turns back to his work.
Good-bye, my friend. We'll talk later.

54d96c983030316814006fac_331.t: Is there something that I can do for you...?

54d96c983030316814006fac_333.t: He takes a drag on his cigarette.
One best suited for contemplation, planning, and visions of the future.

54d96c983030316814006fac_334.t: His smile widens. Light gleams off of strong, sharp teeth, lending him a predatory appearance.
If you like. I must confess, I have rather enjoyed our talks... it is good to have a sounding board to bounce ideas off of.

54d96c983030316814006fac_336.t: Koschei scuttles forward, mechanical pedipalps weaving. Its motions are as alien and charged with menace as they've ever been. Racter glances at the machine, and it takes a step back.

54d96c983030316814006fac_337.t: He returns his attention to you with a smile.
But enough of this maudlin talk. You had questions for me - go ahead and ask them, please. I will answer whatever I can.

54d96c983030316814006fac_339.t: An idiosyncrasy of mine, I suppose. When I get lost in my own head, relating to others becomes... difficult.
It's nothing personal. Just a quirk that I occasionally struggle to hide.

54d96c983030316814006fac_342.t: Perhaps. But that has never stopped you from seeking me out for conversation.

54d96c983030316814006fac_344.t: Yes, a good observation. Koschei could be described as semi-autonomous, though for a different reason than you might think.

54d96c983030316814006fac_345.t: Yes, a good observation. Koschei's behavior could be described as animalistic, and for good reason.

54d96c983030316814006fac_347.t: The drone lowers itself, its killing legs splayed. Racter looks to the drone, his head cocked. Then he returns his gaze to you.

54d96c983030316814006fac_355.t: No, my friend. Of course not. Koschei is not capable of *storing* any part of a human mind - he simply doesn't have the capacity for it. If I were to die, he would fall dormant, the same as any other drone.

54d96c983030316814006fac_357.t: Well said, my friend. And the feeling is mutual - if I must associate with violent criminals, I suppose that I am happy to call them friends.

54d96c983030316814006fac_358.t: We live in dangerous times, my friend. Everybody on this boat is dangerous. But if my relationship with Koschei alarms you, we can discuss other matters... the weather, perhaps. The choice is yours.

54d96c983030316814006fac_364.t: That wasn't precisely what I meant, no. But I'll take your comment for what it is. What I was hinting at, my friend, is that Koschei's behavior is unlike that of other drones. And there is a good reason for this.

54d96c983030316814006fac_365.t: Ah. Fair enough, I suppose, fair enough. But there *is* something that sets Koschei apart from other drones. His behavior is unusual, and for good reason.

54d96c983030316814006fac_368.t: He studies your face, peering at you through the gloom.
But tell me... are you certain that you *want* to know? Some secrets are less pleasant than others. You might prefer to leave this stone unturned.

54d96c983030316814006fac_376.t: He uses the edge of his hand to trace a line across his hips.

Everything below the mid-pelvis is replacement material. That's where I was sheared in half, you see. It's a miracle that I survived the experience; the blood loss alone should have killed me. But here I am, alive and well. Greatly improved, in point of fact.

54d96c983030316814006fac_380.t: He taps his temple with a finger.

The doctors installed stimulus generators in my brain when they repaired me. With these, I can mimic the full range of sensations that the human body is capable of producing. Imagine the ramifications of such a thing. The things that you could do, the things that you could *experience.* The implications for operant conditioning alone are staggering.

54d96c983030316814006fac_382.t: Look at me. I have a shred of essence left in me, and only barely that. But I suffer none of the ill effects associated with traumatic essence loss.

54d96c983030316814006fac_389.t: The enhancements themselves are not the problem. The extent of the damage that they were meant to correct, however...

54d96c983030316814006fac_391.t: Yes. But not my datajack, or the headware that you can see. I am sporting a great deal of replacement material. You can't see it, but I assure you, it's there.

54d96c983030316814006fac_395.t: We can change the subject, if that is what you wish.

54d96c983030316814006fac_397.t: Glad to hear that we're on the same page.
He takes a drag on his cigarette.
I must confess, I sometimes forget how to interact with others... I am, by nature, a solitary creature. It is good to have a kindred spirit, someone whom I can understand, on the team.

54d96c983030316814006fac_398.t: Koschei scuttles forward, mechanical pedipalps weaving. Its motions are as alien and charged with menace as they've ever been. Racter glances at the machine, and it takes a step back.

54d96c983030316814006fac_399.t: He claps his hands together and rubs them briskly.
So, my friend! You had questions for me - go ahead and ask them, please. I will answer whatever I can.

54d96c983030316814006fac_404.t: I'm not surprised. They're Russian technology, government funded and quite experimental.
He sucks on his cigarette, a thoughtful expression on his face.
The project was canceled shortly after I received my implants. Something about graft and corruption on the administrative level... yet another example of good science derailed by bad politics.

54d96c983030316814006fac_406.t: I know of a handful of other recipients. From what I've heard, the others were all driven quite mad. To be handed control over the inner workings of one's own brain is a tremendous responsibility.
Perhaps the others were less well-equipped to handle it than I am.

54d96c983030316814006fac_414.t: The corner of his mouth curls into a wry smile.
Granted, I could be wrong about that. Perhaps I am mad. But then, what would that say about you, and your willingness to associate with me?

54d96c983030316814006fac_415.t: Sadly, no. As I said, the project folded... the devices themselves never made it to market. That's probably a good thing, truth be told... from what I've heard, all of the other recipients were driven quite mad.
Perhaps the others were less well-equipped to handle it than I am.

54d96c983030316814006fac_416.t: A little of this, a little of that. I have toyed with a number of my brain's basic neurochemical functions - simulating the experience of peer bonding by stimulating the flow of oxytocin, for example. But I must be careful not to overstep my bounds. Reprogramming the brain is a risky endeavor, however great the temptation might be.

54d96c983030316814006fac_423.t: He looks up at you, his face bathed in the blue light of the display.
Ah. Hello again, my friend. Back for more answers? I can take the time to talk if you can.

54d96c983030316814006fac_428.t: He turns his head slightly. Peers at you through the corner of his eye.
Tell me, my friend. What do you know about essence loss, and its effect on the metahuman psyche?

54d96c983030316814006fac_429.t: Mmm. Yes, that's right. And this detachment can be deeply traumatic for the sufferer... the literature is full of such cases.

54d96c983030316814006fac_430.t: He levels his gaze, focuses it in on your face. There's a smile on his face, but his eyes are devoid of warmth.
Now. What would you say if I told you that after my accident - and subsequent cybersurgery - I felt no different at all? That even in the beginning, I was completely unchanged?

54d96c983030316814006fac_434.t: Koschei scuttles forward, brushing up against Racter's leg. Metal on metal, separated by a thin layer of cloth. The drone's many eyes focus on you, irising wide open and bathing you in a dull red glare.

54d96c983030316814006fac_436.t: Like autism, psychopathy is a spectrum, not a singular disorder.
But I am definitely on that spectrum.

54d96c983030316814006fac_462.t: He wags his finger at you, scoffing.
Come, come, my friend. You know what we do for a living. We are violent criminals. We hurt people for money. That's what running the shadows *is.*

54d96c983030316814006fac_465.t: Can you quantify it for me?

54d96c983030316814006fac_466.t: And yet, you routinely choose to do what is wrong. In fact, you ignore your moral compass on a daily basis.

54d96c983030316814006fac_467.t: All right, my friend. I won't push you any further. Just tell me yes or no: am I still a part of this team? If not, I will return to my solitary ways. The choice is yours, but make it now. I have little patience for hand-wringing.

54d96c983030316814006fac_469.t: He purses his lips, but nods.
Very well. The decision is made. Show yourself out, please. You know where to find the door.

54d96c983030316814006fac_471.t: Ah, very good. A point for you, my friend - you had me going for a moment.

54d96c983030316814006fac_475.t: And now, if you don't mind, I would like you to leave.
He turns back to his work desk.
There is work to be done.

54d96c983030316814006fac_478.t: Thank you. The feeling is quite mutual, I assure you.
He salutes you with the stub of a cigarette.
Your acceptance is as appreciated as it was unexpected. I will not forget it.

54d96c983030316814006fac_485.t: I wasn't convinced that you'd want to continue our little chats after the last time.
From under his desk, a glint of light catches your eye. Koschei.
I am glad that I was wrong.

54d96c983030316814006fac_486.t: I'm pleased to hear you say so. I rather enjoy them myself.
He turns, the sound of metal on metal filling your ears.
So. What shall we talk about?

54d96c983030316814006fac_488.t: The replacements that I was fitted with, all those many years ago.
The many talon-like toes that splay out from his feet claw at the ground, scraping against the metal flooring.
They have been modified somewhat between then and now, as you can see. Improved.

54d96c983030316814006fac_490.t: If only I had the time and the resources, I would make them for you. Oh, the ways that I would improve you, my friend.
He shrugs, an apologetic smile on his face.
Perhaps later, after we've finished all of this. But for now, we have more pressing concerns.

54d96c983030316814006fac_491.t: The claws were inspired by the harpy eagle. Quite a majestic creature... a hunter of primates, you know.
The quantity was driven by artistic license. Impractical, perhaps, but they please me all the same.

54d96c983030316814006fac_493.t: The corner of his mouth lifts into a wry smile.
Yes. I imagine you do. Go on, then. Ask away.

54d96c983030316814006fac_496.t: Of course it does. You do not share our selective advantage. You see what is to come, and it repels you. For a man in your position, this is only natural. Revulsion is the expected response.

54d96c983030316814006fac_499.t: Of course it will. But you do not know, of course... you don't share our selective advantage. I suppose it stands to reason that you would put your head in the sand. It's a natural reaction, albeit a useless one.

54d96c983030316814006fac_500.t: Good. Very good. I am pleasantly surprised.

54d96c983030316814006fac_507.t: That remains to be seen. But they would give my people something to rally against.
The corner of his mouth tilts upward. The smile contains no warmth.
I, for one, am looking forward to seeing how it all plays out. Aren't you?

54d96c983030316814006fac_508.t: That remains to be seen. Ideally, we would take the cleanest, simplest approach to dealing with them. What that approach might be would depend as much on them as it would on us.
The corner of his mouth tilts upward. The smile contains no warmth.
I, for one, am looking forward to seeing how it all plays out. Aren't you?

54d96c983030316814006fac_509.t: You're joking, of course. But I wouldn't discount the idea... it is far more likely that intelligent alien life exists than that it doesn't. That said, we won't need to wait for first contact to contend with nonhuman intelligence. We're surrounded by them every day.

54d96c983030316814006fac_511.t: Some even say that we have already seen true AI. I had a teammate back in Berlin who insisted as much, though I had little reason to believe her.

54d96c983030316814006fac_513.t: Of course, my friend, of course.
He pauses for a moment. Thrusts his hands into his pockets.

Let us assume, for the sake of argument, that a group of people with my condition would prove... incompatible, in some way. I will concede that we would feel no compunctions about going to war with one another.

54d96c983030316814006fac_516.t: Very little. She blew my childhood behaviors out of proportion, I'm afraid... the woman had always been motivated by fear.
She had her reasons, I suppose.

54d96c983030316814006fac_519.t: After a brief pause, his lip tilts into a smirk.

...I suppose that she was right, in a way. My career in the shadows has led to more bloodshed than my my grandfather's mania could ever have hoped to. I was driven to it, though. Driven by theft and betrayal. Left to my own devices, I might never have taken a human life.

54d96c983030316814006fac_524.t: The corner of his mouth tilts upward.
Well, then. There you have it.

54d96c983030316814006fac_525.t: The corner of his mouth tilts upward into a smirk.
And yet, here you are again, spending your off hours in my shop. Conversing with me of your own free will.

54d96c983030316814006fac_528.t: A neighbor had a pet rooster. It would crow at strange hours and wake me up... it woke everybody up. We all hated that rooster.
So one day, I cut its throat with a hobby knife.

54d96c983030316814006fac_536.t: I learned what was expected of me. The doctor was very helpful in that regard; he offered false hope and placebos, and false promises that he could cure me if my family met his price. And to me, he offered an escape: all that I had to do was play along, and my problems would be solved.

54d96c983030316814006fac_541.t: Racter bends down and runs his fingers over Koschei's chassis. The drone stands motionless, the lenses of its many optical sensors focused on you.

54d96c983030316814006fac_547.t: ...I suppose that my mother had her reasons to be afraid. But she was a tiresome bore, all the same. I did very little to arouse such intense suspicion.

54d96c983030316814006fac_551.t: Silently, Koschei studies you, its articulated pedipalps weaving hypnotically.

54d96c983030316814006fac_555.t: You have nothing to fear, my friend. I am your friend - you can trust me.
And now, if you will excuse me...

54d96c983030316814006fac_556.t: No. I suppose not. And now, if you will excuse me...

54d96c983030316814006fac_558.t: You see? You know me so well, it's as if we share a single mind.

54d96c983030316814006fac_559.t: And I you. Goodbye, my friend - and goodnight.

54d96c983030316814006fac_560.t: Yes... I suppose that I would be. And I can appreciate the sentiment; I tend to discard things that bore me before long. And now, if you will excuse me...

54d96c983030316814006fac_565.t: He purses his lips, but nods.
Very well. The decision is made. Show yourself out, please. You know where to find the door.

54d96c983030316814006fac_567.t: He turns, the sound of metal on metal filling your ears.
So. What shall we talk about?

54d96c983030316814006fac_569.t: In any case, I do enjoy our talks. Sharing a spirited discourse makes me feel alive, in a way.
He turns, the sound of metal on metal filling your ears.
So. What shall we talk about?

54d96c983030316814006fac_584.t: A pity that our time with the creatures is at an end. But the job is done, and we have received our pay. And the blade that you recovered should prove quite useful.
A fine day's work, all things considered.

54d96c983030316814006fac_586.t: He takes a drag on his cigarette. Blows the smoke in a plume.
At least we had the encounter with the vampire to make things interesting.

54d96c983030316814006fac_587.t: A reasonably pleasant outing. We had a nice stroll, took in the night air, went to a gaudy restaurant, and took a man into custody against his will.
He takes a drag on his cigarette.
As jobs go, I've had worse.

54d96c983030316814006fac_589.t: Your choice to give away the laser prototype that we discovered confuses me, though. Why did you bother? We didn't owe that other team anything. We were there first, and *we* were the ones who found the device.
Ah, but it doesn't matter. A small thing, my friend, a trifle. The laser is nothing compared to my research. I am in your debt!

54d96c983030316814006fac_590.t: And in addition, you uncovered a prototype laser weapon! What an embarrassment of riches this job turned out to be!

I will be deeply pleased to see the weapon in action... I have heard that the Ares MP-series lasers cut through flesh, steel, and bone quite beautifully, like a scalpel made of light. A glorious thing, my friend!

54d96c983030316814006fac_595.t: ...Sit back and wait for better-suited researchers to carry the torch instead? It's bound to happen sooner or later. For myself, I will stick with my own research. It's more than enough work for one man, anyway.

54d96c983030316814006fac_596.t: Ah, betrayal. It never fails to sting, does it?
Koschei shifts its posture slightly, its articulated pedipalps weaving.
A subject that I am rather intimately familiar with, I'm afraid.

54d96c983030316814006fac_598.t: He offers you a shrug and a half smile.
...But then, that is why you're in charge, I suppose. I can be impulsive when it comes to matters such as these. Better that you use that level head of yours to keep our reputation untarnished.

54d96c983030316814006fac_603.t: The convention - DeckCon, was it? - was not to my liking, I'm afraid. Too much frivolity, too little substance. Gods, the Noodle Extruder had a longer line than any of the exhibits!
An absolutely shameful state of the affairs.

54d96c983030316814006fac_609.t: For my part, I have but one desire: to see the incredible machine that Tsang built. This... Fortune Engine.
He rubs his hands together greedily. Excitement glints in his eye.
There will be much that I can learn from this device, I'm sure. I confess, my friend... I'm practically salivating with anticipation.

54d96c983030316814006fac_611.t: No. I wasn't there, remember? I spent the evening here, in my shop, designing a new manipulator assembly for Koschei.
He gives you a satisfied nod.
A most productive evening, all things said and done.

54d96c983030316814006fac_614.t: He puts on an ingratiating smile.
You didn't see fit to bring me along on your last job, remember? Not that I minded the off-time... there is always more to do in the shop.

54d96c983030316814006fac_615.t: I was not there, my friend. I spent my evening enjoying a fine Ukrainian vodka laced with honey and peppers.
His lip curls into a wistful smile.
Good-quality stuff, that. A pity that it was my last bottle.

54da5dae306364a88e0035e1_30.t: What a common and base way to try and convince you to listen to him. I would enjoy putting a bullet in him.

54da5dc6306364a88e0035ea_7.t: Glancing at his bracer, Racter nods with satisfaction.

No alarms, no guards with guns, and no lockdown on the doors. Not bad. A word of warning, though: I doubt the storm's noise is loud enough down here to cover any fighting. If we get spotted, we'll have to dig in for a brawl.

54da5dc6306364a88e0035ea_12.t: Glancing at his bracer, Racter nods with satisfaction.
I don't think they know which deck we're on. We should be able to move freely about the ship until we're spotted. After that... Well. That's the nature of our profession, yes?

54da5dc6306364a88e0035ea_13.t: Koschei whirrs and flexes its legs.
We'll be ready. There's plenty of security, but they're not up to our standards. I expect no significant difficulty.

54e26a883063641415004409_7.t: Racter gives Hwang a long, cool stare. Slowly, he reaches over to his bracer and taps out a small set of commands. Koschei rises up, its weapons swivelling toward Hwang.

54e26a883063641415004409_32.t: You claim to have proof of Tigath's betrayal. I, for one, would like to see it.
Racter folds his arms over his chest and fixes Hwang with a skeptical look.
Without proof, we have no reason to trust you.

54e26a883063641415004409_44.t: Racter nods sagely, stroking his chin with one hand.
It seems that Mr. Hwang is correct. Wright has been keeping secrets from us... And I'm not pleased about it.

54e39226653937d419000f48_5.t: And they said this run would be simple. Who will join us next? The marching band?
He shoots you a glance, and the playfulness drops from his expression.
In any case, if the HPF knows about Rooster's extraction, so will the Talon. We need to prepare ourselves for a confrontation by both parties.

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_6.t: Racter curls his lips up in disgust.
Sir. Keep your hands in the open. Shaking as badly as you are, I think you'd only embarrass yourself if you tried to shoot something. Leave violence to the professionals.

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_15.t: I'd suggest paying us and getting out of Hong Kong, actually.
Racter's annoyance is palpable as he jabs instructions into his bracer.

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_38.t: Racter fixes Tigath with a suspicious glare.
Not just yet. None of this makes logical sense, you realize. What about your family? Publishing the data? Your concerns have changed to a remarkable degree.

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_53.t: Koschei whirrs menacingly, weapons focusing on Tigath.
I would suggest not making any sudden moves. Koschei's threat analysis software is prone to striking first.

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_85.t: You have your concerns. We have ours, too.
Racter consults his bracer momentarily, not deigning to look up at Tigath as he speaks.
We need to protect ourselves from possible reprisals, you see.

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_96.t: Racter utters a soft and thoughtful hum, cocking his head.
They should have contacted you by now. Making threats or issuing demands. That's standard corporate procedure.

54e3cf8e653937dc01000fd2_5.t: He calmly strokes his chin while he studies the door.
Old tech. I can bypass the locking mechanism manually. But this isn't a quick fix, so keep the goon squad off me.
Racter leans over the keypad and removes its cover, fingers reworking the dense wiring inside.

54e6169d306364441400703d_8.t: I hope Cheng can find someone to buy that data. I was counting on getting paid.

54e6531030636444140071ff_17.t: Racter glances up from running a diagnostic on Koschei with his bracer.
I expect our mystery caller will be paying us a visit tonight. I rather hope he puts up a good fight.

54e6531030636444140071ff_18.t: Racter shrugs. His indifference is as palpable as the smell of stale urine that permeates the MTR station.
As long as we finish the job and get paid, I will be happy.

54e6531030636444140071ff_19.t: Always, always. But I'm always eager to test Koschei in new and unexpected situations.
Koschei rises up on its legs, and Racter lays an affectionate hand on top of it.
Field testing, my friend. It's the only way to ensure progress.
