She takes another drag off her cigar, exhales the smoke through her nose.

55b3d7776236668819001370_107.t: She taps her ash on the floor.
Well, they knew the risks. They had a shot at moving up and they missed. Forget them.

55b3d7776236668819001370_108.t: I've got bigger problems. Like customers and tenants streaming out of the Walled City, screaming for their lives. Like the HKPF and those Tsang Security shitheels swarming the place.
She drops the stub of her cigar in an ashtray.
Now tell me what happened in the Walled City. Or do I have to get an acetylene torch and ask nicely?

55b3d7776236668819001370_109.t: Family matter, huh? That's it? That's all I get?
I'm down five soldiers. Vice numbers are gonna drop. Loans won't get repaid. I've taken losses, little bird.

55b3d7776236668819001370_112.t: We move on when I say we move on.
She eyes you closely, her mouth a sharp little slit. You can see her connecting the dots.

55b3d7776236668819001370_113.t: Cheng's mouth closes with a snap. She frowns.
Okay. We'll call it even. What killed him?

55b3d7776236668819001370_114.t: She eyes you closely, her mouth a hard little slit.

55b3d7776236668819001370_115.t: Kindly Cheng's eyes get big at that.

55b3d7776236668819001370_116.t: She picks up her bottle of foul spirits and studies the label. Her rusty-mean voice drops low.
But I know the Yama Kings. My grandmother used to try to scare me with stories about them. I guess that she thought she was teaching me something by scaring the shit out of me.

55b3d7776236668819001370_117.t: I remember one Yama specifically, Fu Mang - the Serpent of the Setting Sun. Oh yes. Fu Mang promised wealth and influence for those willing to do what was necessary to take it.
The story of Fu Mang spoke to me.

55b3d7776236668819001370_120.t: The triad boss crosses her arms across her chest.
Okay. We can leave it at that.

55b3d7776236668819001370_122.t: Cheng turns to you with an edge in her voice.

55b3d7776236668819001370_124.t: It is delicious, my darling. Wong Lun Fat was my superior in the Yellow Lotus. A 438.
Kindly Cheng's black button eyes sparkle with satisfaction born of malice.

55b3d7776236668819001370_125.t: It wipes the slate clean, my darling. Oh yes! You see, Wong Lun Fat was my superior in the Yellow Lotus. A 438.
Kindly Cheng black button eyes sparkle with satisfaction born of malice.

55b3d7776236668819001370_126.t: Oh yes, very important. It means I have verification from a trusted man. Wong Lun Fat was my superior in the Yellow Lotus. A 438.
Kindly Cheng black button eyes sparkle with satisfaction born of malice.

55b3d7776236668819001370_127.t: Tell our friend here what that means, Mr. Bao.
A wolfish smile spreads across her face, and her eyes squeeze into crescent-moons of pleasure.
And say it slow so I can savor it.

55b3d7776236668819001370_132.t: Don't be snide. You don't want to blow my good mood.
She raises her voice so the triads in the room can hear her.
The citizens of the Walled City need clarity and reassurance. The Yellow Lotus will provide that for them.

55b3d7776236668819001370_133.t: I may not have brought Josephine Tsang down but I've got my career going again.
She grabs her bottle and unscrews the cap with her thumb.
Your career too, Mr. Bao. You'll be my new Straw Sandal. Good partners support one another.

55b3d7776236668819001370_135.t: The soon-to-be-438 pours two shots and the stink of kerosene stabs your nose like switchblade. She hands a glass to Bao, touches it with her own, and holds it there.
Your loyalty and clear-headedness have proven immensely valuable, Mr. Bao. You are an effective ally and I wish you great wealth and good fortune.
They down the foul substance in one gulp.

55b3d7776236668819001370_137.t: At a cost, my sweet. At a cost.
Kindly catches your eye as she fingers the rim of her glass.
But we move forward, don't we?

55b3d7776236668819001370_138.t: Yes, yes. And it appears your fortune is changing too.
She pours herself another drink and leaves it sitting on the table.
Remember, you have a mysterious benefactor - one who lifted the APB on you.

55b3d7776236668819001370_142.t: I'll tap my network - find out if I can.
Kindly pulls out another thin black cigar.
But that's for later! I'm missing an entire piece of your story, my darling. What happened at Prosperity Tower? Did you rescue Raymond Black? Did his mother wipe his memory?

55b3d7776236668819001370_145.t: Got what you needed from him? My, but that sounds harsh.
She changes topics, apparently uninterested in hearing more.

55b3d7776236668819001370_146.t: She lights the cigar, a gleam of hunger in her shiny black eyes.
While you were in Prosperity Tower, did you find any incriminating data on Josephine Tsang? Dirt? Something I can use?

55b3d7776236668819001370_147.t: Kindly barks a laugh and the cigar dances in her mouth as she speaks.
Now I can take that dried up old bitch to the mat!
The triad boss pulls out a credstick and tosses it to you. The number on the data read-out is big - more than twice the payout of a single shadowrun.
With this, Josephine Tsang's days on the Executive Council are numbered.

55b3d7776236668819001370_148.t: Bao, it appears that my fortunes have truly changed!
She pours two more shots from her reeking bottle.
Our partnership has become quite enriching.

55b3d7776236668819001370_149.t: To my new number one shadowrunner. May our partnership continue to bear fruit.

55b3d7776236668819001370_151.t: Kindly Cheng watches you drink, smiling her serpentine smile. Then she downs her glass in one swallow and licks the rim.

55b3d7776236668819001370_152.t: Kindly Cheng smiles her serpentine smile.
She reaches over and downs your drink, too.

55b3d7776236668819001370_153.t: We'll talk more about that in the morning, my darling.
She reaches over and downs your drink, too.

55b3d7776236668819001370_155.t: The triad boss cocks her head, raises her cigar to her lips.
If the demon-summoning was accidental, what was his machine suppose to do in the first place?

55b3d7776236668819001370_156.t: Kindly's eyes narrow but then her mouth curls into a smile. She moves on with a shrug.

55b3d7776236668819001370_157.t: Your foster-father created a magical fortune machine?
The triad boss cocks her head, raises her cigar to her lips.
Please tell me you have control of this device. Please tell me you brought it to me.

55b3d7776236668819001370_159.t: Well this business has certainly proved lucrative for me.
Kindly Cheng studies the glowing tip of her black cigar.
I'm in a prime position to be the next Yellow Lotus 438, I only lost four men, *and* I finally got something I can use against Josephine Tsang.

55b3d7776236668819001370_160.t: Well this business has certainly proved lucrative for me.
Kindly Cheng studies the glowing tip of her black cigar.
I'm in prime position to be the next Yellow Lotus 438 *and* I only lost four men.

55b3d7776236668819001370_163.t: Did he? Maybe that's what he came here to do.
She lowers her eyes and her voice softens.
Everyone makes their choices, my sweet. And everyone pays the consequences. Even you some day.

55b3d7776236668819001370_164.t: Luck is when preparation meets opportunity.
She lowers her eyes, her voice softening.
It doesn't sound like your foster father was in a position to be lucky. But judging by the way you've run the shadows, I'd say your luck is about to change.

55b3d7776236668819001370_168.t: It could have been worse, I suppose. For some. For awhile.
The triad boss leans in and her voice becomes conspiratorial.
Listen to me, my sweet. When I was a girl there had never been a pandemic like VITAS, but in 2010, it happened. We'd never *seen* death like that.

55b3d7776236668819001370_169.t: The triad boss leans in, her voice conspiratorial.
Listen to me, my sweet. When I was a girl there had never been a pandemic like VITAS, but in 2010, it happened. We'd never *seen* death like that.

55b3d7776236668819001370_171.t: So if a demon goddess decided that the Walled City was her new living room and you didn't... whatever you did... what would have happened?
Perhaps the news would cover it - until the next sensation pulled their attention away. But after awhile, it would be drowned out by all the other crap competing for our attention.

55b3d7776236668819001370_177.t: There have been *many* Mr. Chengs, my darling. None have lasted long.

55b3d7776236668819001370_180.t: She stubs out her cigar. Screws the top back on her bottle.
Now get some sleep. You look like shit. Tomorrow, we'll talk about the future.

55b3d7776236668819001370_181.t: I'll work on that, my darling. But not today.
She dips her finger into her empty glass, finds the last drop of liquor, and brings it to her mouth.

55b3d7776236668819001370_182.t: Kindly Cheng doesn't respond. She's already moved on.
Bao, what's the status on those medical supplies?

55b3d7776236668819001370_183.t: We get to work, my darling! You heard Mr. Bao.
She dips her finger into her empty glass, finds the last drop of liquor, and brings it to her mouth.
The gods help those who seize opportunities.

55b3d7776236668819001370_184.t: Kindly Cheng turns away, dismissing you with a wave.
Bao, what's the status on those medical supplies?

55b3d7776236668819001370_185.t: Yes, we! This is your victory, too.
The thin black cigar bounces excitedly in Kindly's mouth as she speaks.
*We* will be the instruments of her destruction! We're gonna *hurt* that woman.

55b3d7776236668819001370_186.t: The triad boss pulls out a cred stick and tosses it to you. The number on the data read-out is big. More than twice the payout of a single shadowrun.
With this, Tsang's days on the Executive Council are numbered.

55b3d7776236668819001370_187.t: If you have what I'm looking for, you will be rewarded.

55b3d7776236668819001370_188.t: The triad boss pulls out a credstick and waggles it in front of you.
Data first. Then you get this. That's how it works, dear.

55b3d7776236668819001370_189.t: Then you'll need to manage your expectations better next time. There's a lot of nuyen on that credstick. You want it or not?

55b3d7776236668819001370_190.t: My fault for telling you how much I wanted it.
She pulls a second credstick from a fold in her robes and adds it to the first.
There. Negotiations are closed. Hand it over.

55b3d7776236668819001370_191.t: She tosses the credsticks at your feet and barks out a laugh. The cigar dances in her mouth as she speaks.
Now I can take that dried up old bitch to the mat!
You stoop to grab the credsticks. The numbers on their readouts are big - more than twice the payout of a single shadowrun.
With this, Josephine Tsang's days on the Executive Council are numbered.

55b3d7776236668819001370_192.t: Kindly barks a laugh and the cigar dances in her mouth as she speaks.
Now I can take that dried up old bitch to the mat!
The triad boss pulls out a credstick and tosses it to you. The number on the data read-out is big - more than twice the payout of a single shadowrun.
With this, Josephine Tsang's days on the Executive Council are numbered.

55b3d7776236668819001370_193.t: All this bullshit and I don't get *anything?*
She grabs a handful of mahjong tiles and drops them onto the table one by one.
Fine. No sense crying over that, I have business to do. I need to focus on bringing "order" back into the Walled City and prestige to the Yellow Lotus. That will cement me in my new position.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_1.t: Hey! I can't talk. I've got a whole buncha--
Ambrose falls silent, and stares at you.
For hell... you look like you've been through a train wreck.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_4.t: Are you out of your gourd? Right *now?*
Ambrose's jaw drops, flabbergasted. Then his brow wrinkles. and his jaw tightens.
You've got that look in your eye. I know that look. The *heavy work* look.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_8.t: Yeah, get out there and do something! I can't even drink, I've got so many people coming apart at the seams.
He nods to a couple of old men leaning up against the south wall, handing a bottle of rice whiskey back and forth.
Deshi and JP are self-medicating on my behalf.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_10.t: Me? Hell, I need everything I've got to watch the door!
Well, actually... I've got Matthew's drones in here, and the Death Machine up in the ceiling. I guess I do have something for Law...

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_14.t: What!? The Uzi III is solid gun, once you rebuild it. I mean, if it's stock, it's a piece of crud, but that's just because they stamp 'em out like cupcakes. But Raquel here... she's a *princess!*
Ambrose braces himself, as if getting ready to sign away his soul.
Go give Law my little princess.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_15.t: All right, I got no more time for this.
Ambrose fidgets, and stares around the clinic. His eyes are wide.
Go do your thing. I've got to do mine.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_19.t: Ambrose looks exhausted. He slouches like a sack of rice in his chair, gray bags under his eyes. Despite his bedraggled appearance, robot arms whiz around the clinic, reassembling it from its stint as a refugee bunker.
Hey... boy, it's good to see you...

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_21.t: What a crock. I don't know what that was, but it was *not* that. I'll tell you what it was...
A mechanical arm swings over, and points its gripper claw severely at you.
It was some dodgy, crazy-ass shit!

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_24.t: He looks you up and down.
You know what it was, don't you?

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_25.t: He purses his lips, and stares at you penetratingly.
Tell me about it later. I've got to take care of the neighborhood, right now... it's a mess around here.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_26.t: But once things are back settled, come around again. Drinks are on me. Storytelling's on you.
His habitual grin creeps back across his face.
I make a habit of knowing nothing about the drifter runners who move through... but in this case, I bet you've got one helluva tale to tell.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_29.t: The mechanical arm that pointed at you earlier pats you on the shoulder... affably. Almost tentatively.
Word is you worked off your favors to Kindly. Nice job. That's gotta be an all-time speed record.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_33.t: Ambrose grins widely at you. His living eye twinkles.

Sahara Combat League races, every Tuesday night. Desert Wars preseason starts next week on Thursday. Potluck style. I provide some wicked home brew that's also good for cleaning engine parts, but otherwise it's bring-your-own-beer.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_35.t: His signature grin grows even wider.
Laughing at the preshow corporate newsfeeds is the best part.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_39.t: His grin collapses into a winsome smile.
Shoot straight. Watch your back. Always keep a trauma patch and a few spare mags.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_40.t: If you can avoid it, don't deal with triads... but never, ever, on your mother's soul, cut a deal with a dragon.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_45.t: He shouts cheerfully in English, his words piling together in a loose Midwestern accent.
They call me "Ten-Arms." Welcome to the Kong, fellow UCAS-ian!

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_46.t: He chuckles, his chest shaking with mirth. A waldo arm springs to life and swings over to him. Its menacing metal pincer momentarily sets a cigarette between his lips - just long enough for him to inhale from it.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_49.t: Hey, my fellow countryman returns! You want some chips and dip? Help yourself, it's on the engine block.
A spider drone walks past you, carrying a biohazard bag with a severed arm in it.
There's a big Sahara Combat League race tonight. Everybody's coming over... you want to come? No, I bet Kindly's keeping you busy.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_51.t: Ambrose looks up from what appears to be a housework drone on the operating table.
Scrub up and get me the defibrillator paddles. I'm installing some headers on this guy's liver, and now we're going to shock him back to life!
But seriously, hand me the lube. What can I do you for, today?

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_53.t: Ambrose is staring at flat-panel monitor on the wall. A local news channel is zoomed in on an ultramodern aircraft carrier, moored in Victoria Harbour.

You following the IJN story? A Kensai fighter wing from the Masamune did an "honorary flyby" of the Executive Council meeting today. They're rattling the saber hard. Everybody's being courteous as hell, but *nobody's* blinking.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_55.t: Ambrose looks rougher and more tired than usual.
These dreams are getting out of hand. You've got 'em too, right? I *normally* wake up more tired than when I fell asleep, but this is nuts.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_57.t: Ambrose lies limp in his wheelchair while his spider drone tinkers delicately on a huge bionic lower leg... which appears to be attached to a dismembered troll thigh. Ambrose reanimates as you drawn near.
Hey... I'm doing some delicate work here. Just reaching a good point to pause, actually. Okay... what's up?

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_59.t: Ambrose looks exhausted. He's staring blankly, a glass of amber whiskey next to him. Engine parts litter the floor of the operating theater.
Hey. Big typhoon on the way in. Better batten down the hatches.
A robot arm swings over for him to take a weary drag from a cigarette.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_61.t: Ambrose is still cleaning up, cheerfully whistling along to muted doom metal music.
Hey. What can I do you for?

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_62.t: Ambrose is snoozing in his wheelchair, apparently saving up energy to watch the Sahara Combat League race.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_63.t: Ambrose is still tinkering away on a housework drone.
I don't know what they did to this poor cog. It's really something else. Remind me to never have toddlers, okay?

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_64.t: Ambrose is staring at footage of fighter planes on the wall-mounted trideo monitor. He looks sheepishly your way, then grins.
I can't help myself... they're just such beautiful machines! That IJN Masamune has one helluva flight deck...

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_65.t: Ambrose is clearly snoozing. As you approach, he startles into motion.
I'm awake! I'm awake! Just resting my eyes. What can I do you for?

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_66.t: Ambrose is still working on the giant leg. He sits limp in his wheelchair, while nearly a dozen mechanical armatures orchestrate a dance around the operating table.
The arms pause. He reanimates, blinking groggily.
Oh... hey. Ugh... what can I do you for?

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_67.t: Big storm coming in. I'm gonna bed down soon. You need something?

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_68.t: Muted retro-doom metal pervades Chrome Alley like an industrial spill of undead dreams. Ambrose whistles cheerfully along.
Hey! What can I do you for?

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_70.t: Sure I am! I'm a rigger. A rigger-doctor... A ROCTOR!
Ambrose bursts into laughter - then stops and clutches his head.
Oh man, I cannot laugh hard today. Too much partying last night... it hurts.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_72.t: Everybody in Heoi comes to me... 'cause I'm the only option!

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_75.t: How do you think? My meat’s not much to look at, but I’m called Ten-Arms because I can run ten armatures at once.
Ambrose giggles, jiggling in his wheelchair.
My question is: why don’t more surgeons do it this way?

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_79.t: Ambrose pats himself proudly on the chest with a robotic arm that swings over from a wall unit.
Makes me one-of-a-kind... except for all the rigger surgeons that *are* out there. Which is actually a lot, they're just not as good at the rigging as me.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_83.t: The full range of Chrome Alley's services is something that we like to keep beneath the HKPF's radar.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_84.t: Shoot... this is a friendly little neighborhood. I love it when people come by and bullshit. Hell, I host birthdays.
Ambrose's spider drone swings a bottle of coffee liqueur over to his nose. He sniffs it gingerly.
Nope... that's no good anymore. Full of spit and cigarette ash.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_86.t: It's really just a hobby and service to the community. I make my nut with the clinic.
He looks fondly over at the massive, partially assembled V8 engine occupying part of the operating theater.
That baby's gonna be a *goddess* when I'm done with her.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_87.t: Sure thing. You don't mind if I keep cleaning while we talk, do you? 'Cause I'm *gonna!*
Ambrose bursts into laughter at his own joke. The spindly robotic arms around you redouble their sweeping and wiping.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_91.t: I like you; you've got good taste... but that engine is family! Seriously, what do you want?

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_93.t: Lemme tell you what, though: someone busts in here, they're gonna be in a *world* of hurt.
There's a lot more than pipes and wires up in the false ceiling, and I've got Kindly's crew and the Club 88 boys on the panic button. They know which side their bread is chromed on.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_94.t: What, you thinking of trying to knock me over?
He laughs uproariously, but it's not clear if he's kidding.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_96.t: Ambrose drops his voice, and leans forward a little in his wheelchair.
Lemme tell you, though: I've been doing a *brisk* business in sleep aids.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_100.t: Hell, what do I know? I'm not a doctor, I'm just a guy who subscribes to "Psychological Modernista." If you want to know about dreams, ask Crafty. My formal diagnosis is that something fuckin' weird is going on.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_101.t: Hell no! If I had ketamine, I'd take it myself!
But seriously... it's not something that's good to self-prescribe. You eat a bunch of ketamine, it's not going to make you feel well rested.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_109.t: Just stay out of smuggler hulls. The IJN likes to go on sprees of blowing them up, to show how "well protected" HK would be under the yoke.
Those "honorary flyover" fighters had live ordnance loaded! Ridiculous!

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_110.t: Say what you will, though... that Kensai multirole fighter is a sweet bird.
Ambrose ogles a close-up of one of the sharklike jets, on the wall screen.
Twin Mitsubishi double-aught-delta turbofan engines, ramjets, thrust vectoring, generation four fight-by-wire... Hey gorgeous.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_111.t: Yeah: don't go into a bar full of drunk Japanese sailors with a "Free San Francisco" T-shirt on!

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_115.t: It will be. It's a vat-grown biological upper on a cybernetic lower. I'm merging the two together. It's going to give this poor guy the upper leg he wants back, with the lower leg he needs.
You're interested, huh? Okay, check this out... I'll be back.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_116.t: Ambrose goes limp. A half-dozen robotic arms suddenly pulse in a balletic surge of activity, working on the leg... bristling with needles, scalpels, forceps, microsolderers, glue, and various other tools. For several minutes, there's no sound but quiet clicking, and the hum of machinery. When the work is complete, the slender robotic arms draw back.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_117.t: They hover near the ceiling, around the operating area... like giant, attentive stick bugs.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_118.t: Ambrose's eyes flutter back open. He continues talking, hardly skipping a beat.
This guy works in the tunnels, with his feet in sewage all day. He got some kind of flesh-eating trench rot. It went right up his leg. Nearly killed him. They had to amputate at the hip.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_120.t: Yeah, it would. That's why I'm not charging him.
Ambrose shrugs, as if suddenly embarrassed.
Someone else grew the meat leg... I can't do that. I had this old prosthetic lying around already. It's a beater, but it'll last for years if he takes care of it.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_125.t: It's your first typhoon, isn't it? Typhoons grow out in the Pacific, then come crashing into the coast. The biggest ones hit Category 7... real monsters. Unreal.

This one's just a big wet huffer. Streets are gonna flood. Aircraft will be grounded. This one's Awakened, so get ready for a lot of communications interference... and get your galoshes. It's gonna be *wet.*

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_126.t: He gives you a sidelong glance, a sly look in his eyes.
Good time for running, in storms... all that wind and rain obscures surveillance. The curfew clears the streets. Cops and security contractors are all tied up.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_129.t: Nobody's in the mood for it. I'm not either. These dreams are driving everyone crazy.
He takes a big slug of his whiskey. A mechanical arm from the wheelchair absently fills the glass back up.
Fortunately, I have special training for dealing with dreams.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_130.t: Ambrose bites one of his nails, and regards you with a hint of suspicion.
Well... that's a long story. And kinda personal. It involved some jobs. A girl. A real good reason to leave Chicago. A whole shit ton of guns and money.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_132.t: Ambrose smiles. He seems to warm up, a little.
I was a hardcore vehicle rigger, back then... before Johnny Law cracked down on road rigs.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_133.t: Man, those were the cowboy days... freaky as hell, but great to remember.
Ambrose gets a wistful look on his face.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_136.t: Anyway, uh... I needed to get out of Chi-Town. Spread my wings.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_137.t: Yeah, I am. It's a terrible thing that's just happened there. My old 'hood is inside the Containment Zone. Lotta good peeps, locked behind that wall.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_140.t: VITAS plague, bug spirits... I'm not sure which is more scary.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_141.t: Yeah... some of the leaked material is pretty convincing.
Mega-VITAS plague, bug spirits... I'm not sure which is more scary.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_142.t: I know. I saw the leaked material, too. Some of the footage is pretty convincing.
Mega-VITAS plague, bug spirits... I'm not sure which is more scary.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_144.t: Ambrose gives a nervous shiver. The nearby robotic arms tremble briefly.
I'd take zombies over these "bug spirit" things, tell you what.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_147.t: Ambrose smiles, but there's a steely look in his eyes.
Sometimes, a guy just needs a change of scene.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_148.t: He shrugs awkwardly, as if to make light of it. His voice has a hint of strain in it.
That's a story for another day.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_151.t: He looks down at the remnant of his body, scarred and dismembered.
I sure don't look much like one now.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_152.t: Ambrose yawns and stretches, straining his leg stump and scarred arm in opposite directions.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_155.t: Listen: I'm circumspect about my past. And you're a new, active runner, so I don't want to know *any* of your story. It's only a liability to me.
Ambrose props himself up, painfully, and leans forward a little. His living eye has an intelligent gleam to it.
So let's make this conversation useful.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_160.t: If you're a short-timer, you can be as violent and splashy as you want. But...
A waldo arm swings over his shoulder, and points emphatically at you with its claw.
If you're in for the long haul, learn the local arts: discretion, restraint, respect. Get that long-term face, and *don't* embarrass people.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_164.t: So: this isn't Seattle. Decide who you want to be.
Ambrose shrugs - a lopsided gesture, with the stump of his missing arm bound in scar tissue.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_166.t: At some level, anything goes here... but, uh...
Ambrose takes a deep breath, and continues in a tone of low appreciation.
When in doubt, assume that old Hong Kong tradition rules.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_169.t: Ambrose takes a big breath, and sighs - contentedly.
I've found a nice little peace. If you survive, maybe you can, too - and you sure do seem like a survivor.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_171.t: Ambrose suddenly stops himself, as if he realizes what he's saying. He finishes lamely.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_174.t: Wait, what am I saying? My wisdom is you can never be too low profile, too lawyered up, or too well armed.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_177.t: Ambrose's voice is laden with irritated sarcasm.
I learned everything I just told you from the tai chi dude at the end of the dock... that's also where I got the overuse injury that put me in this chair.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_178.t: I kinda like you, in spite of your choice moments. Watch yourself, out there.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_185.t: Where the hell did Kindly dig you up? Wait, don't answer that...

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_187.t: The grapevine says it's your first rodeo, but I'm not sure I believe that.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_188.t: Ambrose gives you a completely level, calm gaze. No expression.
I'm... going to interpret that as wit.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_189.t: Hey, I'm not busted up, you insensitive jerk! I'm *economy size.*
Ambrose erupts into laugher, slapping the side of his wheelchair. He quickly devolves into shameless giggling.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_190.t: He stops giggling, and cocks a sober eye at you.
Seriously, though... that's some heavy shit. It'll take awhile to tell. Do you really want to know?

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_191.t: Well... you're doing good. I feel okay about telling you this.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_192.t: Ambrose spits on the floor, emphatically - in clear defiance of his own prominent "SANITARY SPACE - NO SPITTING" sign.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_199.t: Then, we had this one job.
Ambrose pauses. He takes a deep breath.
Hey, I can sum this up real quick. Otherwise, I tend to get long-winded. What do you want?

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_204.t: BAM! Something hit the van like a freight train.
Ambrose grits his teeth. His body visibly tenses at the memory.
The walls crushed in on me. There was a flash of light, then darkness... stabbing, everywhere. Pressure like a mountain falling on me. Feeling crushed to pulp... then I blacked out.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_205.t: Ambrose falls silent. He seems to be somewhere far away, in his mind. The seconds tick by.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_214.t: Ambrose gives a tense sigh. He's trembling.
It was our glove woman, Karen.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_217.t: Ambrose's eyes seem to clear, as if he's returning from far away.
We got barn-stomped right proper. Slaughtered.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_225.t: Ambrose taps the side of his head.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_228.t: Then she shot me up with enough adrenaline, PMO4, and morphine to reanimate a corpse.
Ambrose shivers a little, at the memory.
And she put *three* trauma patches on me in a row...

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_229.t: That's why this little hot rod has an artificial liver and a dialysis machine in it.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_230.t: Well... we thought it was a milk run. I, uh... our overwatch wasn't what it could be.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_231.t: Ambrose is silent for several seconds.
I'm not proud of all my choices. I was a different man, back then.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_232.t: A nearby robot arm equipped for heavy mechanical work emphatically snaps its vise-like claw.
That thing crushed the van like a beer can.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_235.t: Yeah. It shoved the van right into a plascrete wall.
A nearby robot arm equipped for heavy mechanical work emphatically snaps its vise-like claw.
Crushed it like a beer can.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_236.t: I learned something from that, tell you what: how to *seriously* slot someone's day.
Ambrose gestures with his chin towards the clinic's false ceiling.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_238.t: Afterwards... things were real hard for awhile.
Ambrose seems to take stock of himself. He breathes heavily. Tension drains from his body, and he slowly relaxes.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_240.t: Ambrose shakes himself, and rubs his eye anxiously.
Listen... this is a stressful subject for me. We can talk more about the past another day. For now, I'm spent.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_242.t: Oh man... you can really shake hot sauce in the eyes of a heavy moment. I like that about you... the world needs more of that. Soon your pain will end! Where was I? Oh yeah...

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_243.t: Ambrose wrinkles his face into a half snarl.
Hey, shithead... I don't have a *fucking job* for you. You're the one who's asking about my life. It doesn't matter to me.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_246.t: Fine by me. It's kinda personal, anyway. We can just leave the past in the past.
Ambrose gives a contented sigh, and looks around the clinic.
Today is inventory day. I love inventory day!

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_248.t: He smiles cheerfully, but looks slightly crestfallen.
Well... what can I do for you, today?

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_250.t: You want to keep going with this? All right...

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_259.t: Ambrose shakes his head in exasperation.
Karen was a walking contradiction. She had high aspirations, but she was terrible at all that "Thou shalt not kill or steal or throw people off subway platforms" stuff.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_260.t: Ambrose rolls his eyes and gives a strained grin.
She was like "Ambrose, if you smoke yourself, I will hunt you down in your next life and gut you."

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_261.t: I felt I owed it to her to live. That was the first time I ever felt like I owed anything to *anyone.*
His face clouds with anger and hurt.
And then, what she did to me... Un-fucking-believable.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_262.t: Ambrose's brows draw low. He glances with intense anger towards the operating table, for a moment.
Karen betrayed me. I don't want to talk about it.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_270.t: He breaks into a secretive, proud smile.
I figured out how to run it through my cyber.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_273.t: He looks proudly around at the medical clinic-cum-machine shop.
Maybe it's not a killing, but it's turned out to be a pretty good life.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_276.t: It's, uh... hard to explain if you're not a tech head. You wouldn't understand.
He breaks into an involuntary chuckle.
Okay, twist my arm! I'll tell you...

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_277.t: Ambrose smiles so delightedly that even his cybereye seems to twinkle. His voice is conspiratorial. He talks fast.

I use three external servers to hold it, over at my workstation. I run hardlines from the servers through two jacks: one to my cortex, one to my tactical computer. My other two jacks, I specially installed: one plugs into my encephalon, which has been repurposed.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_280.t: I'm not just Ambrose. I'm the chair, the servers, the machinery. I *am* Chrome Alley... and that's why my remaining essence is just a precious sliver.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_282.t: Ambrose folds his one arm tensely across this chest, and scowls.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_283.t: He nods towards a small framed picture over his impressive, home-built computer.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_286.t: Karen quit shadowrunning, too. Supposedly, she made a full recovery. She started delivering for smugglers. She was my main conduit for drugs and cyber.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_290.t: Ambrose rubs his mouth with the remainder of his hand. He pauses.
She couldn't sleep. She wouldn't take meds for it.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_292.t: One day, she was here. We were behind the curtain. I went out front, to talk to someone.
Ambrose presses one of his remaining fingers into the side of his neck.
She took her butterfly knife, and set the point right here.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_295.t: Ambrose stops. He holds his hand over this mouth, for a long moment. When he continues, his voice is thick.
She did it right there, on the operating table. There wasn't even a thump.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_297.t: Requiescat cum deis, Karen Santos... aum Kali, mater magna, aum Jesu Christe, swami noster.
He gives you a sidelong glance.
I'm not Trans-Catholic. Hell, I'm not into religion at all... but I try to honor her in that way.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_301.t: Ambrose looks silently at Karen's picture. Eventually, he turns back to you.
Ripshot's dead. My old 'hood in Chicago is in the Containment Zone. And Karen... well, she was my best friend. Ever. I'm the last man standing.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_304.t: Here... you want a drink? We should have a drink.
A robotic arm flings open a cabinet, clatters inside it, and swings across the room clutching a sapphire-blue bottle.
This is lambanog... it's a Filipino coconut vodka. Have a shot with me?

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_308.t: Ambrose pours himself a shot, and takes it in his ruined hand. He raises it towards the picture of Karen.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_309.t: Ambrose looks around in silence, then his gaze settles on you, lonely.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_312.t: Ambrose sighs, and clutches his shot glass against his chest - like a religious talisman. He looks thoughtfully at you.
That's more than I've told anyone in a long time. It does the heart good. Thanks.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_315.t: Ambrose chuckles, and shakes his head.
You're an ass-head, but I sure do like you.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_317.t: I'll always be angry at Karen. She abandoned me.
So what? She was my friend. I can be angry at her if I want to.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_320.t: Seems like you're doing okay... despite all the wild, crazy-ass shit it sounds like you've gotten yourself into.
But I didn't hear nuthin'. Far as I know, you sweep floors and wash dishes.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_321.t: Ambrose silently bows his head as you recite a string of words in Sanskrit and Latin, invoking Karen to rejoin the Trans-Catholic concept of universal oneness: the Atma Brahma of the Holy Ghost.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_322.t: Ambrose joins you in the final recitation.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_323.t: His eyes are shining when he looks back at you.
Thanks. I never learned it in full, even though she said it over me every day, when I was all messed up.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_324.t: Yeah? They'll say the same thing about you.
Ambrose's voice drops to an angry murmur.
I think I'm done talking about Karen.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_326.t: Ambrose gives you a hard look.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_327.t: Ambrose gives you a hard look.
Fine... you asked, you get the gory details.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_328.t: You're right... but hey, if I've got PTSD, a lot of runners have some PTSD. All the gunfights... survivor guilt...
He looks down at the cement floor, deep in thought.
It's rough, but at the end of day, it's a treatable neurological adaptation to extreme stress.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_330.t: Ambrose stops what he's doing. He regards you silently. His living eye glitters with intelligence.
I'm Ambrose Rokatansky. I'm the doctor around here.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_339.t: Maybe I crossed someone... the Mafia. Ares. The Russians. Stuffer Shack.
Ambrose winks - but with his cybereye. His living eye keeps watching you, relentless.
Maybe saying that my old 'hood is inside the Chicago Containment Zone is just a way to cover my tracks.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_341.t: Things went bad. We got ambushed.
Ambrose squints appraisingly at you. His expression is unreadable: a perfect poker face.
I must not have been doing a good job of overwatch.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_342.t: That ambush killed everyone else. Eventually, it even killed Karen.
With his three fingers, Ambrose points to the place tubes emerge from his side.
My *liver* and *kidneys* are machines in my wheelchair. I've lost about half my body. I'm in chronic pain. And every night, it's the same thing: nightmares. They never end.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_343.t: If I had any part in the end of the crew... well, I've paid with almost everything. It's still less than oth--
Ambrose is suddenly overcome with emotion. It seems to catch him by surprise. His poker face cracks in an agonal grimace, and tears well up in his eyes.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_344.t: After an instant, he masters himself - and his stony face returns.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_351.t: Ambrose's voice is flinty. His eyes are clear and unwavering.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_352.t: Ambrose gets very still. The robotic arms tinkering around you suddenly stop - and seem to sag. The only sound is the rumble and hum of the electro-furnaces. When he speaks, his voice is slow.
That's me when I was younger. Look at the time stamp. It was taken in 2034.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_353.t: I don't have a son. I don't have *anyone* back in the UCAS.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_355.t: There's a hint of real gratitude in his voice.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_356.t: Damn, that was a good car.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_357.t: I've got nothing to say about it.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_359.t: What? What are you implying? That... wait, that it's *me?*
Ambrose breaks into a smile that is sunny, though possibly a bit forced.
Hey, I'm flattered. I really am, but I stay away from the BBS. That world's behind me.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_360.t: Although I suppose if I ever found someone with whom I had unfinished business...
Ambrose's voice drops several octaves, for just a moment.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_361.t: Ambrose says it without a hint of irony or recognition.
I learn something new every day...

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_362.t: No, I don't know anything about that. I stay away from shadow boards.
I'm not part of that world anymore.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_363.t: I stay clear of the BBS. That's kid stuff.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_364.t: Ambrose gives a very slight nod.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_366.t: He smiles knowingly at you - but not in an unkindly way.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_368.t: Ambrose's brows rise in surprise for a moment.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_370.t: The arm gently sets the cylinder in your hand.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_384.t: Ambrose sighs. His expression suggests that he's had this conversation before.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_386.t: Why am I giving him BTLs?
So he doesn't have to get cheap shit that's going to fry his brain from a street dealer, that's why!

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_387.t: Those cool blues are *pharmaceutical grade.* Their tracks run clean and clear as live opera.
Patient confidentiality... I've already said too much. He's medicated. God knows he deserves it.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_389.t: Well, look who's all high and mighty on their moral horse!
Ambrose snorts in disgust, and continues mockingly.
Shooting things. Blowing people up. Lecturing on the dangers of BTL use.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_393.t: Ambrose shakes his head in exasperation.
That poor guy... he wouldn't harm a fly, but if there's someone who should have "failure to adapt" tattooed on their forehead, it's Matty.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_396.t: Matty doesn't want help to do something else, though. He wants to hole up with his little drone buddies, and be left alone.
Ambrose thrusts his chin out defiantly.
There ain't nothing wrong with that.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_400.t: Ambrose makes a mock toast with his arm.
To Reliable Matthew... hold true, little buddy. There's a rest for you at the end of the tunnel.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_405.t: Ambrose taps his temple with one of his only remaining fingers.
Because I'm already chromed to the gills.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_406.t: I don't look it, but I've got a full suite: quad jacks, knowsoft, radio, skillwire, tactical computer, whole buncha riggerware...
He lifts his dirty t-shirt, revealing a pair of tubes emerging from his side, running down into the wheelchair.
Check it out - I've got an artificial liver in my chair!

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_412.t: Seriously? You must live under a rock. Too much cyber bleaches your soul. I don't want that to happen to me.
It's a crying shame. There are such amazing things we could do with unlimited augmentation.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_414.t: Ambrose scratches himself ostentatiously with a robotic arm.
Listen, I like talking about my chrome more than the next guy, but not right now. Besides, it's kinda a personal subject. You looking for something, today?

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_417.t: Sure, what do you want to know?
Ambrose taps his wheelchair with his three fingers, eagerly.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_419.t: Ambrose gives an exaggerated shrug, pursing his lips.
Wellll... I'm not sure. It's life force. *Sort of.* Essence is like the ability of the spirit to recognize and stay connected to the body.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_426.t: No, no, no. You drop below zero essence, you DIE.
Ambrose shakes his head emphatically, face grim.
Every hardcore street soldier asks that. Trust me, there's no way to do it.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_428.t: Ambrose taps his three fingers on his paunch, thoughtfully.
As long as we're bullshitting, though... There are a lot of rumors about "cyberzombies": corporate experiments in deep augmentation, going into negative essence.

54d545e23030312c0c00300c_436.t: Ambrose gives you a crooked smile, eyes glittering.
Essence is cool stuff... cool stuff. At the end of the day, I don't know *what* is. I just know I'm almost out of it.

554693e9393936841900720a_0.t: Ermine's eyes are bleary. Blood from a recent nosebleed speckles her upper lip. You need armor? Ammunition? Painkillers?

554693e9393936841900720a_2.t: The older troll woman has a striking, amazonian quality. When she was younger, she must have really turned heads.

554693e9393936841900720a_12.t: Ermine speaks with the pride of a woman who bows politely to great powers, but never prostrates herself before them.
Families rebuilt this place when it was left to rot. Strong families, like mine. And we protect our own.

554693e9393936841900720a_19.t: It doesn’t take a shadowrunner’s eye to tell who runs Club 88. Even the roughest of patrons show deference to the troll woman in the back. It also helps that she tends a veritable arsenal of high-grade firearms.
Back again, I see. Didn’t catch your name before.

554693e9393936841900720a_33.t: Ermine haggles with a dwarf over the price of a customized shotgun, sealing the deal with the offer of a size-appropriate bandolier. The dwarf leaves happy, and Ermine slots another black market credstick into her stash.
Welcome back. How can I help you?

554693e9393936841900720a_50.t: It feels as though a pall hangs over Club 88. There are fewer patrons on the dance floor, and far more hushed conversations than raucous frivolity. Ermine scans the too-thin crowd, as if she could suss out the reason for the change.
Something’s not right. Not right at all.

554693e9393936841900720a_66.t: Ermine Ka Fai cleans and oils the disassembled parts of a half-dozen assault rifles spread across a nearby workbench. Her focus borders on obsessive, like a person rubbing her hands raw from excessive washing.
I knew it. I just knew it.

554693e9393936841900720a_67.t: She looks startled by the interruption, as if her words were not meant for other ears.

554693e9393936841900720a_69.t: She pauses in her work, staring at her hands a moment before finally turning her eyes upward.
It’s about Callum, and the man he has been in contact with. I know who he is now, and... it’s not right.

554693e9393936841900720a_74.t: She returns to her work, clearly trying to lose herself in it.

554693e9393936841900720a_77.t: She returns to her work, clearly trying to lose herself in it.

554693e9393936841900720a_80.t: The dread creeping through Heoi has pervaded even the induced euphoria of Club 88. Ermine herself is not immune, looking to be caught between the need for rest and the inability to find the peace necessary for it.

554693e9393936841900720a_98.t: The troll woman looks more weary than you have ever seen her. The club is virtually shut down, and she doesn’t seem to care. She has lost that presence which had once so defined her, and it has left her visibly smaller.
The world’s gone mad, hasn’t it?

554693e9393936841900720a_99.t: She looks at the nearby photo of her son Callum, hanging from metal shelf.
Callum thinks Henry isn't his father. He thinks I had an affair. He even threatened to tell Henry, if I don’t let him leave.

554693e9393936841900720a_106.t: Ermine fixes a level gaze at you.

Baihu has always frowned on metahumans. I was an exception. I thought that just because they accepted me, it wouldn't affect me anymore. In fact, I myself looked down on most metahumans as oversensitive and lazy.

554693e9393936841900720a_112.t: She reaches up to stroke her own horns, as if reassuring herself that they are still intact.
I realized I had *already* given them a trophy: my basic humanity. My principles.

554693e9393936841900720a_113.t: Ermine's voice cool and professional. Her expression is earnest.
For me, it wasn't about humans versus elves or orks or dwarves or "my kind." I want you to understand that. It was about common humanity. It's about how we treat *all* people.

554693e9393936841900720a_120.t: Ermine draws herself up to her full height. She restores herself to her usual regal bearing.
I'm sorry. I shouldn’t have troubled you with my problems. You're probably came here on business, and your time is valuable. Can I show you anything?

554693e9393936841900720a_128.t: The darkness infecting the people of Heoi has finally cleared and spirits are again bright. Nowhere is that more evident than with Ermine Ka Fai.
It is good to see you alive and well. Do we have you to thank for our good fortune?

554693e9393936841900720a_147.t: The darkness infecting the people of Heoi has finally cleared, but it has still taken a toll. Nowhere is that more evident than with Ermine Ka Fai. She looks like a broken woman, her once proud bearing crumpled
You... I thought you were lost to us, too.
Her eyes are bloodshot. She's clearly been crying.

554693e9393936841900720a_165.t: Ermine nods with slow determination. Her jaw is set with resolve.
I'll carry on. That's what I do. Henry and I had a good life together, and we raised two wonderful boys into men.

554693e9393936841900720a_182.t: Things could be worse, I suppose. I still have Henry and Freddy, and I believe you played a small part in that.
Her eyes glisten, as she speaks - but an unbowed moral strength also shines in them. Perhaps the quality that once made her a successful troll in a corporate world that punishes her kind.

554693e9393936841900720a_184.t: I will. And if you should ever find Callum... please...
She brings her hand up, over her mouth. Wipes her eyes.

554693e9393936841900720a_199.t: Things could be worse, I suppose. I still have Henry and Callum, and I believe you played a small part in that.
Her eyes glisten, as she speaks - but an unbowed moral strength also shines in them. Perhaps the quality that once made her a successful troll in a corporate world that punishes her kind.

554693e9393936841900720a_201.t: I will. And if you should ever find Freddy... please...
She brings her hand up, over her mouth. Wipes her eyes.

554693e9393936841900720a_217.t: Ermine gives a labored sigh, and seems to narrowly hold herself back from breaking down - then she pulls herself back together back to her full height.
Freddy and I will hold it together. We can do this.

554693e9393936841900720a_218.t: Her eyes glisten, as she speaks - but an unbowed moral strength also shines in them. Perhaps the quality that once made her a successful troll in a corporate world that punishes her kind.
No matter what it takes for him to be happy... that's what I'm going to do.

554693e9393936841900720a_219.t: I will. And if you should ever find them... please...
She brings her hand up, over her mouth. Wipes her eyes.

554693e9393936841900720a_234.t: Ermine gives a labored sigh, and seems to narrowly hold herself back from breaking down - then she pulls herself back together back to her full height.
Callum and I will hold it together. We can do this.

554693e9393936841900720a_235.t: Her eyes glisten, as she speaks - but an unbowed moral strength also shines in them. Perhaps the quality that once made her a successful troll in a corporate world that punishes her kind.
No matter what it takes for him to be happy... that's what I'm going to do.

554693e9393936841900720a_236.t: I will. And if you should ever find them... please...
She brings her hand up, over her mouth. Wipes her eyes.

554693e9393936841900720a_252.t: How do you think I am? It's all over. It's all over.

554693e9393936841900720a_253.t: Ermine gives a labored sigh, and seems to narrowly hold herself back from breaking down - then she pulls herself back together back to her full height.
I've still got Henry... and we know what our job is now: to hold things together, until maybe Callum or Freddy come back... or until we hear they're okay.

554693e9393936841900720a_254.t: Her eyes glisten, as she speaks - but an unbowed moral strength also shines in them. Perhaps the quality that once made her a successful troll in a corporate world that punishes her kind.

554693e9393936841900720a_257.t: She looks up at you. Her eyes seems dead. Like she's becoming a ghost.

554693e9393936841900720a_272.t: Ermine doesn't answer, but she looks as if she's already seen the end of the world.

554693e9393936841900720a_273.t: A ghost of smile plays across her face, then vanishes.
Stay strong? For what? None of it matters anymore.

554693e9393936841900720a_274.t: Ermine's voice is a hoarse, and strangely dead.
Soon... I think I'll be leaving all of this, too.

54d5743c3030314013003009_2.t: Maximum Law stands out in the rain, arms folded across his chest. His normal air of bravado and jitteriness has blown away like cobwebs. He exudes the aura of a granite block. Or an anchor, abandoned by its ship and about to be overwhelmed by the ocean.

54d5743c3030314013003009_4.t: I can't believe you're *shopping* right now.
He swings his goggled face around, gazing through the torrents of rain - taking in the chaos.

54d5743c3030314013003009_5.t: NO! I'm sick of people asking.
He juts his lower jaw. His VR goggles appear to be about to give up the ghost - but his feet are firmly planted.
I'm not going anywhere. This is my boat, and my barge, and my techno-palace! I'm staying!

54d5743c3030314013003009_6.t: "Maximum Law don't rabbit, yo! He is HERE to STAY!"
He raises his clenched fists, then splays his fingers dramatically.

54d5743c3030314013003009_7.t: What? No... I always just rely on the neighborhood for protection.
Shit, I guess I can't rely on *nothing* now...

54d5743c3030314013003009_8.t: Law takes the submachine gun reverently from you.

54d5743c3030314013003009_11.t: Law dutifully mimes the reloading sequence.

54d5743c3030314013003009_12.t: Law sheepishly takes his finger of the trigger.
Oh... okay, I got it. So... more like in "Cold Kill: Warfighter" than "Wildfire Chronicles."

54d5743c3030314013003009_14.t: Thanks. Man, this is... okay, this is serious.
Law begins to casually drape Raquel over his chest, finger unconsciously back on the trigger - then pauses, removes his finger, and carefully slings the weapon.
All right, I'm good. Anything I can help you out on?

54d5743c3030314013003009_15.t: Law brandishes Raquel menacingly, and practices a few quick turns, his finger perilously quivering on the trigger. He hesitates anxiously for a moment, but then he deliberately flicks the safety off.

54d5743c3030314013003009_16.t: Law grips the sling, and casually flings Raquel over his shoulder.

54d5743c3030314013003009_17.t: Law grips the sling, and casually flings Raquel over his shoulder.

54d5743c3030314013003009_22.t: The puppet's eyes flicker. Emotion seems to break through for a moment.
I perceive that changes... have... occurred. The images within my mind have... quieted. They are no longer... intrusive. I...
The puppet seems to make a heroic effort - and to lose. Its face reverts back into deathly serenity.

54d5743c3030314013003009_24.t: His voice is haggard. He sways on his feet.

54d5743c3030314013003009_27.t: His voice has the dreamy quality of sleep deprivation.
I haven't moved since you last saw me, 'cept to pee over the side.

54d5743c3030314013003009_29.t: He stares forlornly around the half-ruined shop... then suddenly breaks into a huge, giddy grin.

54d5743c3030314013003009_31.t: He thrusts his clenched fist high in the air. Triumphant. Heroic.

54d5743c3030314013003009_34.t: He looks around at the mess.
I guess it's time to put this back together.

54d5743c3030314013003009_35.t: Yeah, uh... there was an accident with the gun...
He down at his gauze-swathed foot, embarrassed.
Ambrose gave me a whole bunch of lidocaine. I can't feel it now... but he says Zippy is gonna have to grow me a new foot.

54d5743c3030314013003009_37.t: Law pats the Uzi III, slung comfortably under his shoulder.
I kinda like her. Ambrose is gonna teach me how to shoot. He says he can't really use her with a robot arm, so somebody's got to represent Geeks-With-Guns around here.

54d5743c3030314013003009_39.t: See you later. Watch your back and shoot straight, and all...
Law seems to fumble for words.

54d5743c3030314013003009_40.t: His eyes fall on your PDA. He pulls himself up into an authoritative bearing.
You're still using that piece of crap? That thing uses Scarab chips, but the array-core Alphex-02s are much better. It's got a PCMA2 motherboard, which is a box of wet noodles...

54d5743c3030314013003009_42.t: Law's shrill voice slowly blends into the sounds of the docks: the patter of rain falling on the tarpaulins, the sizzle of cooking, the creak of old wood, and the rapid music of metahuman voices.

54d5743c3030314013003009_45.t: What? Are you crazy? First of all, Scarabs are built by Fuchi and the PCMA series is by Renraku, so they're not even the same ecosystem. Secondly...
He begins listing his dubious points, punching his finger against his palm. A grin seeps across his face as he sputters at you.

54d5743c3030314013003009_49.t: He doesn't even look at you.

54d5743c3030314013003009_53.t: A short human in his late twenties stands in the middle, hands on his hips, talking loudly into the air. Bulky virtual reality goggles cover his eyes. A datajack cable runs from his temple, down his stained T-shirt, to a beltload of clearly home-built computer hardware.

54d5743c3030314013003009_54.t: NOBODY uses KM3s… like, nobody. They’re shit. … I don’t care what Deng says, if Shiawase SRP uses KM3s, they’re stupid. Hey… flesh is rolling up, I gotta go.

54d5743c3030314013003009_55.t: He turns to face you, goggles blank and enigmatic... blinking with tiny lights and wrapped in duct tape. He points at your PDA.
I ripped that PDA. You work for Kindly.

54d5743c3030314013003009_57.t: Servos whine as two lenses on his goggles zoom in on you.
*Who am I?* You're new here, I get it. You don’t know about me yet, but you will. I’m one of the guys that keeps this neighborhood running.

54d5743c3030314013003009_58.t: This is Law’s Technology Palace, satellite territory of Whampoa. You step on the boat, you’re in a sovereign state. I represent Whampoa here. I'm Maximum Law.
He draws himself up proudly, setting his hands on his hips.

54d5743c3030314013003009_60.t: Maximum Law's mouth crinkles in disgust. He jabs a pudgy finger at you.
Junk? Hey, if you can't see what's in front of you, that's not my problem.

54d5743c3030314013003009_66.t: Maximum Law surveys the docks from beneath his meticulously patched tarpaulin: goggled and belted with electronics, his arms folded, his expression stern... a sweat-drenched little king. His boat rocks gently. As you approach, he looks sharply your way, and breaks into an awkward smile.

54d5743c3030314013003009_68.t: Law looks crestfallen. With his scuffed shoe, he nudges one of the bins of electronics at his feet.

54d5743c3030314013003009_69.t: Kindly had Whampoa burn your SIN. Word is you're doing work for her. Not normal Yellow Lotus stuff.
Law shifts from foot to foot. He fidgets with the hardware on his belt.

54d5743c3030314013003009_70.t: I get it. Well, I didn't hear anything about it.
Law smiles tightly, like a conspirator.

54d5743c3030314013003009_71.t: Law says it distinctly, in a hushed tone - then seems to catch himself. He draws himself up to his full height. His usual demeanor of self-assurance returns.
That's pretty iron-clad. Pretty vicious. If you do good, you'll be noticed.

54d5743c3030314013003009_74.t: Law looks out through the rain, across the rocking boats. He wipes his foggy goggle lenses with a rag from his pocket. His stomach gurgles loudly.
Augmented reality goggles aside, this gig gets really boring sometimes. You ever want to talk shop, I'm here.

54d5743c3030314013003009_75.t: Law speaks with authority. He thrusts his chest out, and puts his hands on his hips.
It's gathering information. The world *runs* on information. If you've got metadata for me, great. If not, see if I care.

54d5743c3030314013003009_77.t: Law’s VR goggles drip in the rain, and blink with what look like error lights. He sweats profusely in the steamy humidity.
Yo, you’re back. I’ve probably got what you need. Just remember: you’re in Whampoa here.

54d5743c3030314013003009_79.t: Law is speaking into the air as you approach. His bulky goggles flash with an unusually diverse array of conditions lights.
Yo, T-Zero... I gotta go, man. This is the cat I was telling you about.

54d5743c3030314013003009_80.t: The goggle servos whine softly as a set of lenses focus on you.
Yo! What's going on? What have you been up to?

54d5743c3030314013003009_84.t: Law's voice is gregarious, yet tinged with awe. He smiles widely.
How can I style out the carnivore? What d'you need?

54d5743c3030314013003009_85.t: Yo! Welcome back to the Palace. You got anything interesting to tell me today?
He looks at the heap of wearable computers, PDAs, and chipdecks on his nearby workbench.
'Cause I got nothing but fixing hair curlers and debugging old ladies' PDAs. I swear I'm gonna die of boredom.

54d5743c3030314013003009_87.t: Law raises his VR goggles as you approach. His eyes are deep brown, surrounded by premature wrinkles. He squints in the light, blinking. He seems at a loss for words.

54d5743c3030314013003009_88.t: I just wanted to say I'm sorry. I didn't mean to, uh... to put anyone in danger.

54d5743c3030314013003009_89.t: He pulls his VR goggles back down.
I guess shadowrunning's a darker business than I thought.

54d5743c3030314013003009_91.t: Law looks pensively around the docks.
I want to get out of here. I want to get on to something bigger.

54d5743c3030314013003009_92.t: Rain drizzles. Torn tarpaulins flap weakly, and docks creak. The odors of garlic, sizzling fish, ginger, onions, raw sewage, and industrial effluent mingle in the heat.
This place is kind of a shithole, isn't it?

54d5743c3030314013003009_94.t: I guess that makes my strategy clear...
Law breaks into a rascally grin.
Welcome to Law's Technology Palace. What useless junk can I sell you?

54d5743c3030314013003009_95.t: It's good to be honest about it.
Law stares out into the falling rain. His goggle lenses whine in shifting tones, as if struggling to focus.
For now, though, it's home... and dealing tech is gonna be my way out of here.

54d5743c3030314013003009_97.t: They do, don't they? Well, for now... it's home. And dealing tech is my way out of here.

54d5743c3030314013003009_101.t: Yo! Welcome back to the Palace. The Palace of BOOM!

54d5743c3030314013003009_102.t: Law is clearly worried. He's got a sick look, like he's just been punched in the stomach. He frets about the Technology Palace.
Yo. Thanks for talking to me.

54d5743c3030314013003009_105.t: You gotta go? Okay... watch your back and shoot straight and all.
Law grins at you, and dextrously flashes a hand sign that shadowrunners give each other in holovid shows.
I got things to do, too.

54d5743c3030314013003009_108.t: Law's voice is gregarious, yet tinged with concealed awe. He smiles widely at you.
Anything you need, let me know.

54d5743c3030314013003009_110.t: Yo, I am your man for that.
Law glances around with an almost comic furtiveness, bulky goggles weaving.
Here? I can't leave the Palace...

54d5743c3030314013003009_117.t: For real? Come on, man... I mean, it's the BBS!

54d5743c3030314013003009_118.t: And I guess we shoot the shit while playing games sometimes too… but I play with great guys… We're all Whampoan, they're totally watching my back. Seriously, if there's a problem, it's totally from somewhere else...

54d5743c3030314013003009_119.t: The blood drains from Law's face.
You're... you're serious, aren't you? Oh no... oh, fuck, man... what've I done?

54d5743c3030314013003009_120.t: He swallows hard. Sweat runs in streams down his face, from under his VR goggles. He breathes heavily.

54d5743c3030314013003009_123.t: Law nods, and swallows hard again.

54d5743c3030314013003009_125.t: Maximum Law looks like he's about to be sick. His voice is faint.

54d5743c3030314013003009_126.t: Law nods, and swallows hard again.

54d5743c3030314013003009_131.t: Law is deeply preoccupied. He absently takes the cable from you and plugs it into his datajack.

54d5743c3030314013003009_132.t: You toggle the deck's illegal antitamper system. The internal capacitors electrify the cable with a split-second pulse of alternating current. Law stiffens for moment, muscles spasming.

54d5743c3030314013003009_133.t: He goes down like a rock, crashing loudly onto the deck. His heart will now be fibrillating. A few moments' wait, and he's still lying unconscious on the deck, which indicates he doesn't have an automated defibrillator in his heart. Death is assured.

54d5743c3030314013003009_145.t: With a punch of your fingers, you break his windpipe. Law gasps. Clutching his neck, he seems to lose all sense of the outside world.

54d5743c3030314013003009_150.t: You sure? If you've got some business you need help with, I'm the guy. You've got the power of Whampoa at your back, when you work with us.
Disappointment is poorly concealed in his voice.
Well, you know where to find me.

54d5743c3030314013003009_152.t: Maximum Law looks up at the gigantic blue-white letters with deep satisfaction.

54d5743c3030314013003009_153.t: He gives you a virile nod.
You're someone who appreciates the good stuff. I like that.

54d5743c3030314013003009_155.t: Half of Heoi gets its juice from a pirate tap on a big Mitsuhama DC cable under the river. Mitsuhama's like...
His voice winds up into a mocking whine.
"Oh, let's run two million volts from the cold fusion plant to the island, under the river... those slum-monkeys can't tap an underwater cable."

54d5743c3030314013003009_157.t: You know who built the tap that pulls electricity off that cable? Whampoa! You know whose barge holds the transformer? Mine!
Maximum Law thrusts his fist high in the air, in silent victory.
Cheap power for all, and a whole lotta extra for me. Behold the lighthouse of BOOM!

54d5743c3030314013003009_158.t: It shows everybody on this river who THE man is to come to, for tech.

54d5743c3030314013003009_160.t: I’ve got a command shell open right now. I’m networked with five guys.

54d5743c3030314013003009_161.t: Hey, if you don't understand it, that's not my problem. This is the new world. You're the one who's going to get left behind.
Law says it with supreme confidence. He holds his fist high in victory.

54d5743c3030314013003009_162.t: Don't give me that woo-woo villain-chaser bullshit. All that astral stuff? It doesn't do anything tech can't do better, ultimately. It's just a shortcut.
Law says it with supreme confidence: a statement of perfect fact. He hooks his thumbs in his bulky electronics belt.

54d5743c3030314013003009_163.t: Yeah... Shiawase, Fuchi, Renraku, a couple others.
Some of us took a look at what they’re doing. Their whole approach is flawed… they won’t get anywhere.

54d5743c3030314013003009_164.t: Yup. Corps can't think like us. They're paper tigers. Inside all that money and organization, they're systems run by idiots. Their coders lack perspective... no complexity of thinking. The system prevents it.
Law says it with supreme confidence: a statement of perfect fact.

54d5743c3030314013003009_165.t: Law says it with supreme confidence: a statement of perfect fact.

54d5743c3030314013003009_167.t: Law says it with supreme confidence: a statement of perfect fact.

54d5743c3030314013003009_168.t: Yo, what are you... my mother?
Law regards you with skeptical disgust.
Those wires are fine where they are - high and dry. They're only a problem for someone who does something STUPID.

54d5743c3030314013003009_169.t: I'm running a high-bandwidth tech operation. Do I look like I have time to tape copper and sip tea? No. I don't.
Law raps his knuckles fiercely on a clear-topped case full of buses, cables, and cable harnesses.
These are the only wires you need to think about.

54d5743c3030314013003009_173.t: Law says it with breathless awe.
You're serious, aren't you? Wow... okay. That's, like... *way* better than metadata. I'm gonna invoice this as full-on paydata.

54d5743c3030314013003009_175.t: Okay... here's one of our paydata preloaded credsticks.
Law hands you a nondescript certified credstick, and then produces a small chipcase with a flourish.
...And this is something special: a game avatar for "Inferno Souls," built by my friend Shockstat. Fully loaded and farmed.

54d5743c3030314013003009_177.t: She is. She's an awesome lady.
*Art galleries* show exhibits of her avatars.

54d5743c3030314013003009_179.t: Law holds his own fist high, as if in victory.
You're gonna love it. It's truly beautiful. *Art galleries* show exhibits of her work.

54d5743c3030314013003009_182.t: Law reigns himself in, clearly with great effort.
You know what? I can't talk about this right now. "Inferno Souls" is like ancient wisdom. "Master Felon" is pop-sauce.

54d5743c3030314013003009_185.t: WHOA! Are you serious? That's nuts!
Law slaps his thigh for emphasis.
BOOM! More proof for my theory: the Kong is *done.* I give it two years, tops, before demons or something eat everybody.

54d5743c3030314013003009_187.t: Grab a program. Here's my surplus stack.
Law grabs a nearby bin and slams it down in front of you.

54d5743c3030314013003009_189.t: It's a pleasure handling meta with you.
A little meta is like a fresh memory defragment... it makes my whole day better.

54d5743c3030314013003009_192.t: Law waves his finger around his ear, as if to signal, "Are you crazy?"

54d5743c3030314013003009_193.t: All that geomancy feng shui ancient sorceror stuff... it's a bunch of drivel. It's all...
"Oooo, we proactively boosted our quarterly outcomes by mindfully on-lining our beige cushions into a little circle and mooning each other."

54d5743c3030314013003009_195.t: BOOM! That's what I'm talking about! Stone-cold thrashfest!
He breathes a big sigh of relief and exhilaration.
Why didn't you just tell me that? I guess you just had to build up to it...

54d5743c3030314013003009_196.t: He holds up his palm, forcefully.
I don't want to hear it. I'm done talking about this. Done! Game over!

54d5743c3030314013003009_197.t: Maximum Law's insert-your-blather comments box is *closed* for the day!

54d5743c3030314013003009_198.t: *Of course.* Those guys are a maze of labyrinths. That's why I like to keep an eye on them.

54d5743c3030314013003009_201.t: Uh... no one's gonna believe that...

54d5743c3030314013003009_205.t: Law's jaw drops, as if in shock.

54d5743c3030314013003009_206.t: Do I look like a fucking tabloid?
He stretches his arms wide, in exasperation.
Come on. You're a stone-cold, steely-eyed killing machine!

54d5743c3030314013003009_210.t: Whampoa? Screw those clowns. I'm an independent contractor now. Real clear arrangement: I deal goods for them. They want something? They pay for it... and Law's Technology Palace is an independent territory.
Law kicks a bin of spare parts forcefully, emphasizing his point.

54d5743c3030314013003009_211.t: I'm starting my own tribe... the tribe of BOOM! You got a tech problem? We solve it... BOOM! You got garbage in your head? We tell you how it is. BOOM!
Whampoans are a bunch of backstabbing losers. You need anything BOOM right now?

54d5743c3030314013003009_212.t: Yeah, we're good... we're a tech tribe. If you don't mess up every so often, you aren't trying... right? If your code never breaks, it's not pushing the limit.

54d5743c3030314013003009_213.t: He purses his lips in thought.

I've got to pay my dues... but I'll rise in the tribe. It just takes time. No offense, but that's all the tribe business I want to talk. I'm taking this "information hygiene" thing seriously. Anything else on your mind?

54d5743c3030314013003009_214.t: We're pretty much the best, yo. What do you want to know?
Law folds his arms impatiently, as if he's irritated by your request - but he can't quite stifle a proud grin.

54d5743c3030314013003009_216.t: You really are new to the Kong, aren't you? We're the neo-Matrix collective. We run the Hong Kong data haven, and the Kong shadow-nets. We *are* the Kong shadow-nets.

54d5743c3030314013003009_218.t: Law gives a curt, virile nod.
My buddy R3dSt0rm wrote that. Pretty wicked, huh?

54d5743c3030314013003009_220.t: I am more outspoken, yo. I don't need the meatspace rituals. Modesty is *inefficient.*
Is that a Whampoan thing? For those of us who are most *dialed,* maybe. We're post-cultural.

54d5743c3030314013003009_221.t: Law sweeps his hand around, gesturing at the surroundings.
I hardly see any of this meatspace. Our meatspace headquarters is a big 20th century ship that's totally *awesome.* Whatever. It's just meatspace.

54d5743c3030314013003009_222.t: Whampoa is bleeding-edge on the path to becoming the singularity... and in the singularity, there's gonna be *nothing* but straight-up truth.
Law punches his fist down at the ground, striking a majestic pose like a trideo barbarian.

54d5743c3030314013003009_223.t: Law seems shocked at the question.
I'm an ambassador. I broker Whampoa's services to Heoi. Emissaries like me are all over the Kong, tying it together in an invisible network.

54d5743c3030314013003009_227.t: I'm an important part of an ascendant entity! If you're ignorant... yo, that's not my problem!

54d5743c3030314013003009_228.t: Whatever, I know who I am. You don't need to. More important, I'm Whampoan, and I know what *we* are.

54d5743c3030314013003009_231.t: We do tech for anyone in the Kong, outside the corporate umbrellas. Anyone can use our networks, if they pass our vetting and can pay for what we bring.
Law speaks without a shred of irony.
It's mostly the extralegal element that needs our skill.

54d5743c3030314013003009_236.t: Wow... really? We're pulling in outsiders?
He shakes his head in disbelief.
Well, I should've seen that coming.

54d5743c3030314013003009_238.t: I don't know why you're asking or how you know, but it's Whampoa business... you feel me? When we find out who's responsible, it's gonna be *nuclear ugly.*
He jabs his finger emphatically down, as if slamming an invisible missile-launch button.

54d5743c3030314013003009_239.t: Hey, what do I look like... a rumor-a-tron? It's my tribe, and shit's going ugly!
You here to dis my people, or what? Buy something or buzz off.

54d5743c3030314013003009_242.t: Law kicks one of the bins at his feet. The sea of electronic parts within it rattle loudly.

54d5743c3030314013003009_243.t: Law erupts into a torrent of livid, awkward profanity, cursing furiously in some sort of pidgin of Cantonese and tech-speak.

54d5743c3030314013003009_244.t: Eventually, he slows down, and his swearing slackens.

...When Magpie gets back, the shit is going *down.* We're gonna spike those bastards with some much black IC that their brains are gonna turn into pudding. And we're gonna feed that pudding to fucking dogs!

54d5743c3030314013003009_245.t: He folds his arms, and almost-comical snarl on his face.
I'm not surprised the other Elders got themselves killed. Figures. Well, Magpie will know what to do. We'll take care of this.

54d5743c3030314013003009_253.t: A lot of people are like, "Aaaah, uuuugh, respect your Elders, kiss their servos." Hey, that's good if you're in the old meat world, but we're *Whampoa.*

54d5743c3030314013003009_256.t: Every actuating lens on Law's goggles zooms in on you. A fresh set of status lights begins blinking. One appears to indicate that he is recording.
You were investigating the killings, right? Word is you were asking around...

54d5743c3030314013003009_258.t: Law's visible lower face goes ashen.

54d5743c3030314013003009_265.t: Law seems taken aback. His jaw drops a little bit, and his voice is hushed.
A ghoul? No way... there was all sorts of fighting in the mall. But I guess the first ones... huh. What happened? How did it go down?

54d5743c3030314013003009_266.t: For real? Man... this is going to go down in tribal lore.

54d5743c3030314013003009_271.t: Riot armor? Figures! The HKPF was around recently... some fishy business in one of the squats. For some reason, we stood off. They've got the firepower to pull it off... so they scouted us and came back for the Elders... Those gutter-slotted hairy--
Law slams his fist down on a nearby display case.

54d5743c3030314013003009_272.t: He grabs his hand, cradling it.
That hurts! I, uh... anyway... I'll spread the meta to the tribe. If the HKPF thinks we'll let this slide, they're as stupid as they look.

54d5743c3030314013003009_276.t: Law erupts into a torrent of livid, awkward profanity... some sort of pidgin of Cantonese and tech-speak.

54d579a43030314013003045_17.t: I'm glad we understand each other.

54d579a43030314013003045_20.t: I'm glad we understand each other.

54d579a43030314013003045_39.t: She turns her head to the side, candlelight glinting in her eyes. You get the feeling she knows more than she's letting on, but her body language tells you she's not interested in continuing the subject.

54d579a43030314013003045_42.t: A quick glance around the shop reveals an unusual level of untidiness. 54d579a43030314013003045_59.t: Sure, it's a little weird that *we're* having them, but these types of dreams are common in the Walled City. Nothing to get all worked up about... probably.

54d579a43030314013003045_60.t: Common as the cold. My mother devoted her life to researching the Walled City and the magical phenomena within it, so I grew up with stories about the city. 54d579a43030314013003045_61.t: This sides of her mouth dip into a shallow frown.

54d579a43030314013003045_62.t: Mom was... interesting. But not praiseworthy.

54d579a43030314013003045_82.t: The corner of her mouth dips downward.

54d579a43030314013003045_83.t: An embarrassed smile creeps into her features. She scratches her shoulder.

54d579a43030314013003045_131.t: Crafty shifts her gaze to a stack of books on a nearby table.
