559ec0526236669809002374_103.t: When the machine... malfunctioned... when the circulator became... stuck in Astral Space... it lodged in another... d-domain.
He sways and his lips begin to tremble.

559ec0526236669809002374_105.t: The old man begins to drift.
After twenty years of c-constant force... the circulator finally began t-t-tearing... Astral Space... created a r-rupture in *her* d-domain...
And now she's pushing through. And now she's pushing through. And now she's pushing through.

559ec0526236669809002374_108.t: Raymond stops his rocking. Becomes fully lucid. He snaps back at Wu.
I *understand* that, Mr. Wu! That's why I came BACK! I believe she is... only *partially* here! She hasn't pushed all the way through yet!

559ec0526236669809002374_109.t: She's probing her new h-hunting g-ground... must...
The emotional strain of his snap at Wu rattles him again.
I know what to do now... how to e-e-extract the circulator... shut the machine down... before she can......f-fully... manifest.

559ec0526236669809002374_128.t: He grabs his head with both hands, squeezes his eyes shut.
The mechanism I created to...to... to... circulate negative qi in the Walled City... got stuck.

559ec0526236669809002374_129.t: It got stuck. The mechanism I created to...to... to... circulate negative qi in the Walled City... got stuck.

559ec0526236669809002374_130.t: I am. I also have several... a-advanced degrees... doctorates in Physics... Taoist Alchemy.
My m-mother pushed me... hard... to reach my p-potential.

559ec0526236669809002374_133.t: Shouldn't have waited. Shouldn't have hid.
He looks up at you with bloodshot eyes.

55a94da3623666741900929c_50.t: No. My l-life is here now, I think. Helping the p-people of the Walled City... to recover. Finding a way to undo some of the damage I have done... w-with the Fortune Engine.

55a94da3623666741900929c_51.t: Forgive myself? Th-that will n-n-never happen. Never.
The old man stares into his soup.
And I won't just m-move on while thousands continue to suffer... because of me.

55a94da3623666741900929c_68.t: Love is the... the...the s-soul of genius. I was inspired by the p-potential to do good... on a g-grand scale.
And by a quiet p-profound arrogance that... that... I could do it.

55a94da3623666741900929c_77.t: The hint of a smile crosses Raymond's weathered face.
It will be... interesting to s-see what will happen to her company, now that the Fortune Engine is shut down.

55c8fe94626632c40f003d02_3.t: I b-believe that you are c-correct.
He points at a dingy terminal just in front of the towering machine.
The control c-console is just over there. We must move q-quickly.

55c8fe94626632c40f003d02_4.t: I h-have been ready for more that t-two decades.
He points at a dingy terminal just in front of the towering machine.
The control c-console is just over there. We must move q-quickly.

55ca7566643661a816000a25_1.t: Although his breathing is a bit erratic, Raymond seems steady enough on his feet.
Lead on. I'll be right behind you.

54d56f243030316013003007_1.t: Matthew seems folded in on himself. He has a shell-shocked look. His head hangs forward, and he doesn't appear to see anyone, including you... despite the brilliant, toothy smile plastered across his face.

54d56f243030316013003007_3.t: Sure... sure... I brought over the security ones. In case Ambrose needs to pilot them.
He blinks at you, eyes clearing for just a moment. A tsunami of abject terror floods them.
I had to leave the others. Keung Ha is going to *kill* me.

54d56f243030316013003007_5.t: He screams in surprise. It's a weird amalgam of terror and gusto - then he immediately goes back to his eerie, cheerful obliviousness. A red welt spreads across his face. The slap does not appear to have snapped him out of anything.

54d56f243030316013003007_6.t: Huh... what, beautiful? Oh yeah... doing good. Great! Real good. Real...

54d56f243030316013003007_7.t: He looks across the room, at two old men leaned up against the wall, sharing a bottle of whiskey.
I wonder if I can get some of that.

54d56f243030316013003007_8.t: Reliable Matthew stands in the middle of his drone barge. The downpour has scoured the barge, but otherwise everything appears surprisingly intact. Most of his drones are still present, and even his balloons still float... sluggishly.

54d56f243030316013003007_9.t: As always, he's grinning, languid, and frayed around the edges. Like a primped debutante sweating out a quart of alcohol from the night before.
Good morning, beautiful. Welcome back to robot paradise!

54d56f243030316013003007_11.t: He holds a single index finger up in the air emphatically.

54d56f243030316013003007_12.t: Picking up a loose washer off the deck, he flicks it at a balloon. It bounces off the balloon with a low *thunk.* The balloon hardly moves.
They're full of aerogel. Mmmmmmm, love those lighter-than-air solids.

54d56f243030316013003007_13.t: I'm a lucky sorta guy. What can I say? Guess people were just trying to take care of themselves, not take another man's stuff.

54d56f243030316013003007_14.t: He shakes his head emphatically several times.
Something happened? Oh, it was just a wild night. Real wild night. What a time!

54d56f243030316013003007_15.t: He looks around intensely. Looks up at the Walled City for a moment, shudders visibly, and turns away.

54d56f243030316013003007_16.t: He gives you the shooter sign.
Here's looking at you. Come around any time.

54d56f243030316013003007_18.t: I've decided to branch out. I'm telling Ambrose I'm not just gonna be *one* person anymore. I'm gonna be a lot of people.
He thrusts his chest out proudly.

54d56f243030316013003007_19.t: He gives you the shooter sign.

54d56f243030316013003007_20.t: He smiles balefully at you, eyes bright and cold.

54d56f243030316013003007_23.t: He lazily waves you off with his cigarillo.
No deals for outsiders. Move it along, beautiful... move it along.

54d56f243030316013003007_25.t: He lazily waves you off with his cigarillo.
Sorry, beautiful, you're not from around here... move it along.

54d56f243030316013003007_29.t: The salesman smiles indulgently at you: a tall, lanky human of clear Cantonese ancestry. He's wearing a cheap suit, and limply clutching a smelly cigarillo. He adjusts his tie with exaggerated nonchalance... Languid. Ostentatious. Like an American casino promoter.

54d56f243030316013003007_31.t: He taps ash over a bulky cleaning drone beside him. Not waiting for an answer, he continues.
Perhaps you're looking for a hexacopter as a guide to Hong Kong... or a little handheld tarantular beauty, to brew your tea?

54d56f243030316013003007_33.t: That's right. Reliable Matthew Shun, at your service.
He extends his hand, to shake. The odors of peach-scented moisturizer and stale tobacco smoke wash over you.

54d56f243030316013003007_34.t: Matthew hastily pulls his hand back, and bows - a little awkwardly.
I, uh... well, hey, beautiful, I didn't, uh... expect such formality, especially from a newcomer to our lovely shores. You've got some class, friend.

54d56f243030316013003007_39.t: Reliable Matthew looks over his shoulder at the security drones, some heavily cannibalized, in the corner of the yard. He turns back to you, with a nervous smile.

54d56f243030316013003007_43.t: Are you implying that they're stolen? If so, you're mistaken.
He smiles indulgently, as if you've just said something in an alien language.
This merchandise is provided *only* by trusted suppliers, who certify the authenticity and workability of their goods.

54d56f243030316013003007_47.t: Where do Fan's prawns and soychips come from? Or Lucy's lovely textiles? Or the electricity for the lights?
Reliable Matthew sweeps his arm expansively at the heavens, trailing a stream of blue cigarillo smoke.
Where do we get this GLORIOUS BLUEBIRD SKY!?

54d56f243030316013003007_48.t: Matthew takes a big breath, staring rapturously at the gray clouds. He looks back down at you. His expression goes flat. He shrugs limply, and speaks in monotone for a moment.
They're smugglers and fences. I don't know where they get their drones.

54d56f243030316013003007_49.t: He proudly taps the Aztechnology logo emblazoned on a housework model beside him.
The majority of them are genuine knockoffs... any IDs are stripped, but most were never registered in the first place!

54d56f243030316013003007_50.t: Well, it's a very exclusive process... you need to be a member of the community in good standing, have good references, and pass a background check.
He smiles widely. His eyes seem to twitch over you, nervously.
Or... if all you have is the drone... I can *work with you.*

54d56f243030316013003007_59.t: Reliable Matthew greets you with an expansive grin, sweeping his arms wide.
beautiful! To what do I owe this unexpected pleasure...? Why, you're not here for some robotics, are you? Well, shucks!

54d56f243030316013003007_60.t: He rolls his shoulders ostentatiously, and sticks his cigarillo casually between his teeth. His eyes have a strained, bloodshot look.

54d56f243030316013003007_63.t: Matthew's cigarillo is dead, drenched by rain. He stands with his signature nonchalance, but the edges of his face are twitching. His eyes are wide with fear. You could listen in, but you've already got the gist: Matthew is being chewed out.

54d56f243030316013003007_65.t: Matthew shakes himself off. His cheery demeanor surges back, with striking resilience. He turns towards you, a little wild-eyed but otherwise restored to his usual self.

54d56f243030316013003007_67.t: A sharp voice emits from the hover drone, interspersed with static crackles.
...Don't pick up the sales, you're going to be digging dog shit out of tire treads for a living. You got me?

54d56f243030316013003007_68.t: H-hey, Keung Ha... Come on, beautiful. I've got a half-dozen hot leads coming in just today...

54d56f243030316013003007_72.t: Matthew's voice is barely a whisper.

54d56f243030316013003007_77.t: Reliable Matthew stands quietly in his drone lot, arms hanging limply at his sides. He stares silently at the ground. This isn't the soft-spoken style of many Hongkongers. He looks sad.

54d56f243030316013003007_78.t: He looks up briefly as you approach, but quickly looks back at the ground.

54d56f243030316013003007_80.t: Yeah, okay... I've got this, uh... new mortar bot from Keung Ha. It's really...
He makes a halfhearted effort at salesmanship, then his shoulders slump.

54d56f243030316013003007_81.t: Matthew looks uncertainly at his own drones.
Those were all the ones that are any good. I guess.

54d56f243030316013003007_82.t: Matthew mumbles something under his breath. After a moment, he looks up suddenly.

54d56f243030316013003007_89.t: Matthew is still looking at the ground. He glances up briefly as you approach.
Uh... hi. Did you get my medicine?

54d56f243030316013003007_92.t: Matthew hastily takes the cylinder from you. His hands shake as he dispenses a single chip from it. He pulls back his hair, and slots the chip into a hidden datajack.

54d56f243030316013003007_94.t: His eyelids flutter. A ripple moves through his body. His eyes snap open. He flashes you a vacant grin.

54d56f243030316013003007_95.t: Boy, am I glad to be back! The day was a little dreary there, for awhile... but now I'm back, and I'm in fighting shape!
He pulls a fresh cigarillo from his pocket, and twists off the auto-lighter tip with gusto. It ignites with a faint crackle.

54d56f243030316013003007_96.t: It's my medicine. It makes me feel better.
He waves weakly at the drone lot.
It makes all this okay. Here, give it to me.

54d56f243030316013003007_99.t: Matthew gives you a ghost of a smile.
Sure, okay... buy me one, too, if you think of it.

54d56f243030316013003007_100.t: He seems to resist, but a wave of trembling overtakes him. He hastily counts out a crumpled assortment of bills, and shoves them at you.
Here, it's all there. Okay? Now give me the chips...

54d56f243030316013003007_101.t: Three hundre-- what? I... I can't pay that. I already paid Ambrose. I don't have that much...
He withdraws a handful of crumpled bills and a disposable credstick from his pocket, and looks down at them.
This is all I've got until I make another sale.

54d56f243030316013003007_102.t: He looks like he's about to cry, but he gives you the handful of money and the credstick.

54d56f243030316013003007_104.t: Wai-- What? No! It's medicine, I'm not well without it.
Matthew looks like he's getting agitated.

54d56f243030316013003007_105.t: He seems to fold in on himself. He trembles a little, and looks doggedly down at his feet. He seems to be on the verge of crying.

54d56f243030316013003007_108.t: Reliable Matthew flashes a winning smile as you approach.
Well, that was a close one! Thanks to you, we're back in business... and just for you, I've got a red-hot deal on solar chargers, just in time for the sun!
He stares up blissfully at the cloudy gray sky.

54d56f243030316013003007_109.t: Reliable Matthew flashes a sharklike smile as you approach.
We're back in business... but our pockets seem to be lighter. Noted, beautiful. Noted.

54d56f243030316013003007_110.t: Reliable Matthew won't look up. He stares at his feet, a look of angry sorrow on his face.
Just get your stuff and leave.

54d56f243030316013003007_113.t: Matthew has returned to his languid, slightly wild-eyed state of bliss. He flashes you a winning smile.
Hey, beautiful. *Whew.* Don't you worry... I managed to sneak off to Ambrose and get my medicine. I'm back!

54d56f243030316013003007_115.t: Reliable Matthew gives you a big wink, and shooter sign. His eyes don't seem to reflect the emotion, as usual.

54d56f243030316013003007_116.t: You in the market for high-powered Grade-A autonomous helpers? Or are you just here to hang out? Because if you're just here to hang out...
Matthew's synthetic personality seems to flicker for a moment. His expression pulses back and forth, between smarmy cheer and something like shy happiness.

54d56f243030316013003007_119.t: Hey, hey! It is SO good to see you! Now, what can we do? Let's set you up!
Matthew grins through the rain, suit soaked.

54d56f243030316013003007_120.t: Reliable Matthew appears to have gotten his "medicine" again. He regards you with steely eyes and a big sharklike grin.
Well, well. Look who's here. How may I help you, beautiful? Let's just get our business over with.

54d56f243030316013003007_121.t: Reliable Matthew regards you with steely eyes and a wide, homicidal grin.
How may I help you, beautiful? Let's just get our business over with.

54d56f243030316013003007_122.t: Reliable Matthew stands quietly. Cheap suit wet with the rain. Tapping his foot absently to the smooth jazz pervading the lot. A halo of cigarillo smoke surrounds him.
Hey, beautiful, good to see you. Let's set you up, shall we?

54d56f243030316013003007_127.t: *Whew!* Wow! Smell that fresh air! Feel that victory!
Some lucky soul is gonna get a GREAT deal on a drone today. All thanks to you!

54d56f243030316013003007_128.t: Can't talk now, beautiful... gotta make sales. I feel too good to slow down! Catch me later, why don't you?

54d56f243030316013003007_129.t: Boom boom, ba-doo-ba-boo-ba-doom! Catch you later, hot stuff!

54d56f243030316013003007_130.t: You've got an extended warranty coming to you on your next purchase.

54d56f243030316013003007_132.t: Matthew playfully blows the smoke of his cigarillo.
My medicine? I can't think of a thing to talk about - except how nice it is to see you. Nothing but blue skies over here, beautiful!

54d56f243030316013003007_134.t: He sighs heavily, through his plastic grin.
Clutching his cigarillo tensely between his fingers, he reaches up into his hair. With a faint click, he removes a chip.

54d56f243030316013003007_137.t: He looks around disconsolately at the drone-scattered deck.

54d56f243030316013003007_139.t: He lifts his head and looks you in the eye - tentatively, but with a certain conviction.
I know what people think. Some people like Ambrose don't look down on me. But everyone else... I just want them to leave me alone. I want to be left alone, okay?

54d56f243030316013003007_140.t: He gives you a shy nod. There's a deep recognition in his eyes.
I know what I'm doing. I'm going back now.

54d56f243030316013003007_142.t: That was real... I mean real, beautiful! You've got some chops on you. Whew!
He adjusts his tie with exaggerated gusto.
You know, something I think about... may I tell you? It's gonna blow your socks!

54d56f243030316013003007_144.t: So let's get real... really real!
Matthew takes a big, virile breath. He looks around in satisfaction, hands on his hips.
I'm not Matthew Shun, and I'm not Cool Blue Jazz. I'm both.

54d56f243030316013003007_145.t: He cocks an eye at you.
I think a lot about where I'm from.

54d56f243030316013003007_149.t: Reliable Matthew taps himself forcefully on the chest.

I'm proud because I'm more than the sum of my parts. Whatever aimless tweaker my feelings come from, they merge with a guy who never had a chance... and they alleviate his pain. I live a life of honor.

54d56f243030316013003007_150.t: He looks around - at the looming Walled City, the polluted river, the dilapidated drone yard.
I'm like a knight-errant. A youxia. I'm like *The Swordsman* of Jia Dao. I fight for humanity.

54d56f243030316013003007_151.t: Thanks for reaching out to Matthew. Don't you worry: I'm watching out for him.
Reliable Matthew nods in slow satisfaction, a wizened look on his face.
As long as there's chips, I won't get tired. I'll never leave.

54d56f243030316013003007_152.t: He claps his hands together, and breaks into a gigantic grin.
Now let's get back to it, BEAUTIFUL!

54d56f243030316013003007_154.t: He breaks out a fresh cigarillo, and rips off the auto-igniter with gusto. It bursts to life, in a puff of rank blue smoke. He regards you with a sly, exaggerated smile.
Now, my honorable brethren-in-arms... how can I brighten your day?

54d56f243030316013003007_156.t: Oh, yes I am. And this...
Matthew waves around his now-dead cigarillo.
...Is my frosty blade! Hail to the hero, beautiful. Injustice... watch out!

54d56f243030316013003007_159.t: I watch out for Matthew. My personality might look fake to you, but I'm his friend. I make his life better.
Reliable Matthew nods in slow satisfaction, a wizened look on his face.
It's a good gig. I'm proud.

54d56f243030316013003007_160.t: He looks around with regal authority - at the looming Walled City, the polluted river, the dilapidated drone yard.
I fight for humanity. I'm like a knight-errant. I'm like the Monkey King.

54d56f243030316013003007_162.t: He looks back down at the ground.
It's okay that you don't understand. Nobody does.

54d56f243030316013003007_164.t: Matthew just stares at his feet, for a long time... then mumbles quietly, still looking down.
I don't want to be here. I don't want any of this.

54d56f243030316013003007_167.t: He shakes himself briefly and stretches his shoulders.

54d56f243030316013003007_168.t: Matthew's eyes have a hard glitter. He speaks with a big smile that's cold like Antarctica.
You want to talk about my medicine, beautiful? Well... I have another idea, a *fantastic* idea. Let's talk about... you!

54d56f243030316013003007_169.t: Matthew points at your chest with a cigarillo.

54d56f243030316013003007_171.t: He takes a lusty, vigorous breath through his nose. His smile grows even brighter.

People think I'm dumb. But I'm not. I think. I think a *lot.* About who I am. Where I am from. I've come to know certain things, about this world. About people like you and my main supplier, Keung Ha.

54d56f243030316013003007_172.t: Matthew throws his cigarillo into the river, and vigorously adjusts his lapels.
I recognize an asshole when I see one.

54d56f243030316013003007_173.t: Are you saying something important? I don't think so.
He looks over his shoulder at the cigarillo, floating in the fetid brew of the river, awash in flotsam, oily effluent, and dilute sewage.
Look, its in its natural environment now: shit. Same as yours.

54d56f243030316013003007_174.t: He turns back to you, and shrugs.
There's more where that came from. Just like you.

54d56f243030316013003007_175.t: He gingerly fishes another cigarillo out from inside the breast of his coat.
Now... you here to buy a drone? Or are you just a little cloud passing through my glorious blue sky?

54d56f243030316013003007_178.t: Matthew's eyes widen for a moment. He looks intently at the credstick.
He waffles, as if torn, and looks appraising at you - sidelong.

54d56f243030316013003007_179.t: He seems absolutely baffled, but his eyes are starting to shine.
Maybe you can un-asshole yourself! I - I gotta push some tin. Gotta go! I mean stay! Err... you need a drone? It's work time.

54d56f243030316013003007_180.t: Matthew hastily extracts a fresh cigarillo from his breast pocket. Drops it. Pulls out another.
Gotta work now! How can I help you, on this lovely, lovely, LOVELY day?

54d56f243030316013003007_187.t: Oh, that's just Keung Ha... he's like a paper tiger!
Matthew sways back and forth on his feet, languid and jittery at the same time.
He's a big joker, always shouting, but I think he's a real softy at heart. Real big heart for the little people. Keeps me well stocked.

54d56f243030316013003007_189.t: Matthew's brows arch up in surprise.
Of course not... how could I afford them?

54d56f243030316013003007_191.t: Matthew gives you a long, awkward smile. The seconds stretch out. He blinks uncomfortably. When he speaks, his words sound stilted - like he's repeating a mantra.

54d56f243030316013003007_193.t: Matthew adjusts his suit lapels with mechanical gusto.
Whew! What a pep talker, that Keung Ha. I feel like I could take on the world right now!

54d56f243030316013003007_197.t: Matthew taps his temple with his finger, meaningfully.
Think about it, beautiful... all the boring, dangerous, painful jobs that people used to do. The jobs that drones do now!

54d56f243030316013003007_198.t: He glances around at Heoi - and up at the Walled City looming above. Despite his bright eyes and gleaming smile, his voice sounds almost thoughtful.

54d56f243030316013003007_200.t: He shakes off his passing air of seriousness. Clamps his cigarillo in his teeth. Thrusts his hands in his pockets.
They're really something else, AIN'T THEY?

54d56f243030316013003007_202.t: Reliable Matthew smiles widely, but the edges of his mouth twitch uncomfortably.
Drones free people to take skilled jobs. They make the economy more efficient. And drones give poor people services they couldn't ever afford otherwise.

54d56f243030316013003007_204.t: He waggles his cigarillo at you.

I know what you're thinking... I think it myself. "Drones take jobs." Well, medicine takes jobs from undertakers, and sewer drones take jobs from people who used to crawl through filth to feed their families.

54d56f243030316013003007_205.t: He holds his hand somberly over his heart, and his voice deepens.

When you see a drone messenger or maid, it's easy to think: "That used to be a person." Could be... could be. But that person can do another job now... and they can have a drone *themselves.* Everyone is better off!

54d56f243030316013003007_206.t: Matthew's voice rises. His smile wrinkles painfully around the edges as he carries on enthusiastically.
...The same people who call drone dealers "job stealers" often themselves come to me for--

54d56f243030316013003007_207.t: Matthew suddenly shuts up. His smile gets even more waxy.
Uh... hey, let's not get all wound up about this stuff... It's too cheery a day for running off in our heads!

54d56f243030316013003007_209.t: Mmmm-mmmmm! Wow, just smell that brisk spring air!
He inhales deeply through his nose, drawing in the fetid reek of the polluted river, and the sodden heat of monsoon season.
This topic is fascinating, really fascinating. But, uh... I've got to get back to work. Shall we walk the lot?

54d56f243030316013003007_210.t: Reliable Matthew smiles serenely at you.
Drones in the trailer? Oh, I think I have a couple in there. Probably ones I'm fixing.

54d56f243030316013003007_212.t: Funny thing about drones - when you see a few, they always look like so many more. Weirdest thing. Strange little fellahs. Let's not talk about maintenance, it's boring... I bet there's something you need today!

54d56f243030316013003007_214.t: He thumps his chest vigorously with his fist.
Never been better. Good air and good friends, it keeps a man healthy and wise.

54d56f243030316013003007_215.t: I must have some allergies. And I admit, I don't sleep as much as I'd like... too many friends! Too many demands on my time!

54d56f243030316013003007_217.t: I am, thank you! In a GOOD way!

54d56f243030316013003007_219.t: Reliable Matthew smiles blissfully, looking around at the docks. He admires the looming, oppressive mass of the Walled City.
Take your time. Make yourself at home. I'll be right here if you need anything.

54d56f243030316013003007_220.t: He flashes you a big smile... exaggerated and fake-looking, yet also somehow genuine.

54d56f243030316013003007_225.t: Are you implying that they're stolen? If so, you're mistaken.
He smiles indulgently, as if you've just said something in an alien language.
This merchandise is provided *only* by trusted suppliers, who certify the authenticity and workability of their goods.

54d56f243030316013003007_228.t: He twirls his cigarillo in his fingers.
Let's find you some quality little helpers!

54d56f243030316013003007_230.t: Where do Fan's prawns and soychips come from? Or Lucy's lovely textiles? Or the electricity for the lights?
Reliable Matthew sweeps his arm expansively at the heavens, trailing a stream of blue cigarillo smoke.
Where do we get this GLORIOUS BLUEBIRD SKY!?

54d56f243030316013003007_231.t: Matthew takes a big breath, staring rapturously at the gray clouds. He looks back down at you. His expression goes flat. He shrugs limply, and speaks in monotone for a moment.
They're smugglers and fences. I don't know where they get their drones.

54d56f243030316013003007_232.t: He proudly taps the Aztechnology logo emblazoned on a housework model beside him.
Most of them are genuine knockoffs... any IDs are stripped, but most were never registered in the first place!

5609dc5c643661e00e007ff7_78.t: She gives you a solemn nod.

5609dc5c643661e00e007ff7_82.t: No. That isn't what this is.

5609dc5c643661e00e007ff7_90.t: Qiu rubs the bridge of her nose between her thumb and forefinger.

565f332762663208210043d4_11.t: We'll to talk to her, then.

565f332762663208210043d4_14.t: Qiu rubs her temples, squinting. She looks exhausted.

565f3a6b62663208210043e7_1.t: The sentence trails to nothing as she squeezes her eyes shut, wincing. Her hands go up to rub her temples.

565f3a6b62663208210043e7_2.t: She rubs her temples with both hands.

565f3a6b62663208210043e7_4.t: She rubs her temples with both hands.

565f3a6b62663208210043e7_46.t: She nods mutely, her expression blank. Her eyes are fixed on the screen.

565f3a6b62663208210043e7_49.t: I don't know anything about this.

565f3a6b62663208210043e7_50.t: Yeah... maybe. Grouping up the people responsible for the Kowloon City massacre out of guilt, though?

565f3a6b62663208210043e7_85.t: She accepts the device, a grim expression on her face.

565f3a6b62663208210043e7_106.t: She sighs heavily. Rubs the bridge of her nose.

565f3a6b62663208210043e7_107.t: She sighs heavily. Rubs the bridge of her nose.

565f3a6b62663208210043e7_143.t: She stares at you, hard. After a few seconds, she averts her gaze.

565f3a6b62663208210043e7_147.t: That's one way of putting it. Feels like it's driving a railroad spike through my temples.

565f3a6b62663208210043e7_150.t: Truth be told, I think that the damned thing might’ve killed me if I'd held onto it for too much longer. People aren’t meant to use those kinds of foci nonstop for months on end.

565f3a6b62663208210043e7_151.t: Love to, but you're gonna have to give me a few seconds. This shit is excruciating.

565f3a8c62663208210043f1_15.t: She lets out a long sigh.

565f3a8c62663208210043f1_26.t: ...Anyway. I just wanted you to know what's on the line before we headed out to Tai Po.

565f3a8c62663208210043f1_27.t: ...I can't argue with the second part. Okay. You win. Go, do whatever work you need to do. Tie up loose ends, make your clients happy. 565f3a8c62663208210043f1_28.t: She begins to look away, then seems to think better of it. The camera refocuses on her face.

565f3a8c62663208210043f1_60.t: Okay... do whatever you need to do. It'll give me a chance to close my eyes for a few minutes, if nothing else. When you're finished, go to Jomo. He'll sneak us into Tai Po, and we can finish this.

565f3a8c62663208210043f1_78.t: Qiu lets out a long sigh.

55469435393936841900773d_9.t: He lifts an arm, flexing. His muscles jump out like a mass of serpents and steel cabling. His arm is easily as thick as most humans' waists.

55469435393936841900773d_12.t: The young troll bouncer manning the door is in the midst of patting down a prospective patron with enough force to dislocate a shoulder or hip, while other shady-looking patrons walk idly past him, into the club.

55469435393936841900773d_13.t: He waves the shaken man inside, propelling him forward with a final pat on the back.
People don’t realize that you gotta check for internal weapons as well as external ones.

55469435393936841900773d_42.t: Frederick Ka Fai stands stolidly by the club door, his arms folded across an impossibly large chest. He eyes those entering and leaving the club like a god might look down from Olympus, and he smiles while doing so.

55469435393936841900773d_47.t: He pulls up his taut T-shirt, revealing a thick layer of form-fitting body armor below it.

55469435393936841900773d_57.t: But to answer your question, when I do bench, I max out around 700 kilograms.

55469435393936841900773d_64.t: The younger of the Ka Fai sons mans his post at the club’s front door. He’s showing off his hulking arms to a couple of wan-looking girls from the docks. Their clearly self-interested fawning gets them inside with no cover charge.

55469435393936841900773d_79.t: Freddy Ka Fai is showing off some fresh ink to everyone who passes inside Club 88. On each forearm he has tattooed an anchor. He demonstrates how they look in various poses, from flexes to his standard arms folded across his chest.
Not bad, right? Someone told me that only real sailors get tats like these.

55469435393936841900773d_94.t: Freddy looks sullen at the club’s front entrance. And sullen on the young troll takes the form of careless indifference for the force he uses to pat down and propel forward the patrons entering the place.

55469435393936841900773d_110.t: The young troll bouncer is as far from alert as he’s likely ever been. Men and women pass through the front door without earning even a glance from Freddy. He just taps away at the screen of a tablet, oblivious to the world.

55469435393936841900773d_112.t: Oh, it’s you! Sorry about that. Just making some final arrangements.

55469435393936841900773d_130.t: Freddy Ka Fai greets all comers at the front entrance of Club 88. He performs his duty with renewed vigor, much to the dismay of anyone looking to enter with all their dental fillings intact.

55469435393936841900773d_141.t: Freddy Ka Fai greets all comers at the front entrance of Club 88. He performs his duty like a professional. No more fooling around, no more fantasies. The patrons note the difference and show him a new sort of respect.

55469435393936841900773d_149.t: Frederick watches the street with a stolid, sullen diligence. He shoots you a glance, clearly pre-occupied.

55469435393936841900773d_150.t: Frederick cheerfully stands watch, but barely goes through the motions of controlling the door.
Soon I'm gonna be in a different world.

5546930d3939368419006863_0.t: You need some drugs? Things are crazy outside our heads. Might as well be crazy inside your head, too.

5546930d3939368419006863_15.t: The eldest son of the Ka Fai family appears to be far less inebriated than before, though there is still a drink clutched in his massive hand. He’s parting ways with an uncharacteristically awkward elf who palms something as she goes.

5546930d3939368419006863_47.t: Callum sits with his eyes closed and his head back. One might assume he’s asleep or trying to shake a particularly nasty hangover. But when he stirs, his eyes are clear and uncharacteristically focused.

5546930d3939368419006863_64.t: Even though he has a drink in his hand, the red in Callum’s eyes is not that of drunken stupor, but of barely suppressed rage. An angry troll is a dangerous thing, and an angry drunk troll is a disaster waiting to happen.

5546930d3939368419006863_80.t: Callum has calmed greatly since last time. He has once again oscillated into his more somber brooding mode. He looks almost professional as he conducts a little business with some rail-thin gangers aching for their fix.

5546930d3939368419006863_99.t: Callum is in a rare state, steeped neither in melancholy nor in alcohol-fueled oblivion. His excitement can barely be held back by the corners of his mouth, and this comes in stark contrast to the dread hanging over Heoi.

5546930d3939368419006863_113.t: Callum's gaze is steely with determination.

5546930d3939368419006863_115.t: Thanks. I think you might be right... even though I wish you weren't.

5546930d3939368419006863_129.t: I had a long talk with my ma. She told me the truth about what happens at Baihu. It’s horrifying. I can’t even believe it’s possible.

5546930d3939368419006863_134.t: Let's just say I owe you a favor.

5546930d3939368419006863_135.t: Callum seems both unusually sober, and unusually thoughtful.

5546930d3939368419006863_136.t: Callum nods, very subtly, as you approach.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_0.t: His red-rimmed eyes glisten with anger.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_3.t: His sunken eyes swivel toward you. He wets his parched lips and addresses you.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_13.t: Go on. G-go away, and l-leave me to die in p-peace.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_16.t: He stares you in the eye, and his voice drops to a sharp whisper.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_17.t: He nods, though his tongue stumbles and stalls as he speaks.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_21.t: The junkie gives a weary smile.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_24.t: I was a b-bomb-maker. I designed c-c-custom ordinance. It was a d-dream-job... plus, it was very lucrative.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_26.t: A little, yeah. The p-power - the beauty - of explosions enthralled m-me. But I n-never wanted to h-hurt people.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_27.t: The junkie shrugs dismissively, but he avoids your gaze.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_30.t: The man starts digging his rough fingernails into his palms so hard that tiny crescents of blood stain his palms.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_35.t: The junkie's shoulders heave and shake, and he passes a hand over his face.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_36.t: He meets your gaze with red-rimmed eyes. He throws his arms wide.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_37.t: You're r-right, I did l-lie to myself. In fact, y-you've g-guessed the truth.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_38.t: The man barks with joyless laughter.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_40.t: The man shakes his head sorrowfully.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_41.t: The junkie chews his lip and throws you a sidelong glance.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_42.t: The junkie looks at you and snorts.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_44.t: The junkie begins to fidget and gnaw at his fingernails.
The b-bombs that I was s-so proud of were used against *c-civilian* t-t-targets.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_45.t: A gleam of light appears in the junkie's sunken, bloodshot eyes, and a shadow of a grin appears on his cracked lips.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_46.t: The spark of life in his gaze suddenly fades.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_48.t: The junkie's face assumes an ashen hue, and the corners of his mouth pull downward.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_50.t: The junkie's already sickly pallor turns ashen.
It's not even as simple as that.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_51.t: The man balls his hands into fists in his lap.

I thought my b-bombs were used in w-wars against m-militias, mercenaries, and armies - and I d-didn't think of them as *people.* But the w-w-weapons I developed w-were used against *civilian* t-targets.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_52.t: N-no... not so m-much. I was a b-bomb-maker. I designed c-c-custom ordinance. It was a d-dream-job... plus, it was very lucrative.
He rubs his thumb and forefinger together, then snaps.
Megacorps c-couldn't get e-enough of the devices I invented. I was t-too high on my own e-ego to w-wonder what they w-were up to.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_53.t: The junkie gives a weary smile.
Yeah. Yeah, they h-help. I *n-n-need* them. And they're better than the alternative.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_54.t: I g-guess that I must be feeling g-g-guilty about the b-bombs, huh? All of the b-blood on my hands. I can feel it all the t-time. I just like b-bombs, I didn't want anyone to get *h-hurt*...

559242c5303035cc1d004210_55.t: H-how am I ever going to make this g-go away? How will I *l-live* with myself? The d-drugs help me f-forget, but...

559242c5303035cc1d004210_57.t: The junkie sways mutely in place, gnawing on his chapped lower lip. A minute passes before he manages to speak again.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_59.t: A tight-lipped smile stretches across the man's haggard features as your suggestion registers in his brain.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_60.t: I'll t-tell you what. I'll th-think about it.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_62.t: His face fills up with something you hadn't seen there before. Determination.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_63.t: The man lowers himself into a squat and hugs his knees, his gaze somewhat absent and his cheeks glazed with tears.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_64.t: The man pauses, licking his lips, his mouth feeling for the right words.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_65.t: The junkie gives a crooked smile, his eyes a bit misty.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_66.t: The junkie sways mutely in place, gnawing on his chapped lower lip.
I w-wouldn't know where to b-begin... Need time to th-think...

559242c5303035cc1d004210_67.t: The man runs his unsteady hands through his hair repeatedly.
I c-can't just stop c-c-caring... What I was involved w-with... what I'm still c-complicit in... it's so t-t-terrible...

559242c5303035cc1d004210_68.t: The junkie relaxes his hands. His matted hair, where he was tugging, sticks out in ugly tufts. He frowns darkly.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_69.t: The junkie shoots you a dirty look.
I n-need help, not insults. What the h-hell do I *do?*

559242c5303035cc1d004210_70.t: Y-YES! Of *course* I d-do! T-tell me what to do!

559242c5303035cc1d004210_71.t: The junkie relaxes his hands. His matted hair, where he was tugging, sticks out in ugly tufts. Sobs wrack his wasted frame.
I j-just w-w-wish it were d-different.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_72.t: I thought y-you were going to h-h-help me!

559242c5303035cc1d004210_76.t: The junkie blubbers and whines. When he finally raises his reddened eyes to you, his gaze is lucid.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_80.t: The wretched man stares at the ground.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_81.t: Oh yes, let me do that r-right away. That s-sounds like a *great* idea. You don't want to h-help me. You're just here to make a quick b-buck.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_82.t: L-leave me alone, t-traitor. I never should've trusted you. I don't wanna t-talk to you any more.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_83.t: The junkie gives a weary smile.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_84.t: The junkie indicates the holes in his clothing and the bruises and scars on his body.
C-can't get any worse. Hand that packet over.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_85.t: It's p-purely circumstantial that I p-picked drugs as my escape. I'd just s-seek out another d-dependency. So, I m-might as well take Lee's free h-hit. Packet, please.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_86.t: The junkie's trembling hand shoots out, his fingers curling and uncurling.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_87.t: The man joins his palms in a beseeching manner.
I p-promise I'll start c-c-cutting back, starting t-tomorrow. Really!
The junkie's trembling hand shoots out, his fingers curling and uncurling.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_88.t: The junkie narrows his eyes at you. A hiss escapes his parched lips.
Why w-would I let you do that? That's Lee's gift to m-me.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_89.t: The junkie begins to scratch at his arms absentmindedly as he considers your words.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_90.t: The man crosses his arms across his chest, muttering to himself and shaking his head.
I r-really need that. J-just one h-hit to t-t-tide me over.
The junkie grabs your arm, ready to snatch the packet from your grasp.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_91.t: Oh, very kind of you, but no need.
The junkie extends a hand eagerly.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_92.t: The junkie shudders. His wide, staring eyes gaze into nothingness, and his jaw clenches.
The drugs d-didn't *cause* the n-nightmares. I acknowledge that. But I'd d-do anything t-to avoid the nightmares. Do d-drugs m-make it w-w-worse?

559242c5303035cc1d004210_94.t: The junkie draws himself up a little straighter.
Then k-keep the p-packet for yourself, as my thanks to y-you.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_95.t: Heard that one b-before. Don't c-care much for p-preaching. Packet, please.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_96.t: The junkie rolls his eyes and sighs. He points to the packet in your hand.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_97.t: The man snorts and lifts his chin high.
It's n-not a trick. I know exactly w-what Lee's offering. I'm not s-stupid.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_98.t: The man licks his lips and eyes the packet, but he doesn't reach for it.
D-do you think that'll m-make the n-n-nightmares worse?

559242c5303035cc1d004210_99.t: A wild look flickers in Stephen's eyes. He bares his teeth and snarls.
You j-just want to k-keep it for y-y-yourself! C-can't trust anybody here. Give me my present!
The junkie grabs your arm, ready to snatch the packet from your grasp.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_100.t: Fat tears begin to cascade down his dirty, ruined face.
I d-don't want to sleep because of these n-nightmares. I'm ravenous, so ravenous, but food makes me ill. I've got the s-shakes real bad, too. I just want this to end.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_101.t: The man frowns, eyes confused and fearful.
S-sorry, man, s-s-sorry. I was j-just afraid. I'm not d-d-doing so well. C-can't trust anyone.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_104.t: You h-have to help me, m-man!
His teeth grind as his grip tightens. His hand feels like a vise.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_106.t: I c-could use someone like you. I'd make it w-worth your w-while.
He peers up at you, eyes wide with expectation.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_108.t: He mutters in low, urgent tones.
I want you to t-take a m-message from me to Handsome Lee.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_109.t: Shaking slightly, the man stands, straightens - to the extent that he can - and places a hand on his heart.
Oh, d-don't let my appearance f-fool you.
The corner of his mouth curls bitterly.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_110.t: He leans in to speak, and as he does so, you glance down and notice that his tattered clothing bears designer logos.
I want you to t-take a m-message from me to Handsome Lee.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_111.t: The man stands gingerly and raises a trembling hand to prevent your departure.
N-naturally someone of your importance is m-much in d-demand. But my request is very s-simple.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_112.t: He leans in to speak, and as he does so, you glance down and notice that his tattered clothing bears designer logos.
I want you to t-take a m-message from me to Handsome Lee.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_113.t: The man attempts a winning grin, revealing a mouthful of rotting teeth.
A trivial t-task for someone l-like y-you, who g-gets around, knows everyone...

559242c5303035cc1d004210_114.t: The man ignores you. He stumbles, pulls himself up using you as a support. Under the layers of filth and grime you can see designer logos on his tattered clothing.
You need to get a m-m-message from me to Handsome Lee.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_116.t: He nods eagerly, desperation in his eyes. A sluggish tongue wipes itself over the cracked skin of his lips.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_119.t: The man gives a hollow laugh.
I know my w-way around substances well enough t-to know a bad trip when I have one. This is *s-something else.*

559242c5303035cc1d004210_121.t: Then tell Lee what he's done to me. That I need *r-relief.* T-tell him that I w-want...

559242c5303035cc1d004210_123.t: His reedy voice wavers, but he stops and says no more.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_124.t: His voice lowers to a mumble.
It's the pr-principle of the thing. J-just do it, okay? And p-please come back when you're done.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_126.t: The junkie stares into space. A faint trickle of saliva makes it way down his chin.
N-no... not exactly. More like n-nightmares. They feel very real. T-teeth b-b-biting. F-f-faces, f-f-f-faces I knew...

559242c5303035cc1d004210_128.t: Stephen Dynamite shoots you a sideways glance. His voice drops to a rough whisper.
I can pay you. I... I have m-means.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_130.t: Tell Lee what he's done to me. T-tell him that I need *relief.* These dreams, they're so... Tell him that I w-want...

559242c5303035cc1d004210_131.t: His eyes take on a glassy quality.
They still pay me, you know. For *what I d-did.*

559242c5303035cc1d004210_133.t: The junkie starts to scratch his neck and face. His eyes roll wildly.
I u-used to invent things. T-terrible things.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_134.t: Bombs. The most p-potent explosives. I s-sold them. Didn't c-care to whom... at least, not b-back then.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_136.t: A taut smile stretches across the junkie's features.
Too l-late. I know everything that h-h-happened. R-regrettably.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_138.t: A taut smile stretches across the junkie's features.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_139.t: A taut smile stretches across the junkie's features.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_140.t: I built b-b-bombs. The most p-potent explosives. I s-sold them. Didn't c-care to whom.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_141.t: The junkie just stands there, mute.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_142.t: A frown darkens the man's face.
Take your money, then. It's all stained in b-blood.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_144.t: Stephen Dynamite holds himself a little straighter and lifts his chin high.
I don't d-deny my problems. B-but I d-do have money. I've done w-worse than you'll ever do, shadowrunner. And they still p-pay me for it.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_145.t: The man's grip tightens further. He pulls you in closer, and you notice that his tattered clothing bears designer logos.
It'll be w-worth your while. Just t-talk to Handsome Lee for me.

559242c5303035cc1d004210_146.t: The man yanks you closer. Your eyes are watering from his pungent proximity. Still, you notice that his tattered clothing bears designer logos.
Please! Please! Take Handsome Lee a m-message from me!

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_1.t: From the chest up, the darkness swallows him. You can't see his face.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_3.t: The words roll in his throat like rocks in a tumbler.
How are you feeling today? Did you sleep well?

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_7.t: Me? Nothing. Our mage probed your mind as you slept.
He lifts the mug out of the light and toward his lips. You hear a long, loud slurp as he sucks the steaming fluid down.
Don't know the man myself, but I'm given to understand that he's good. Uses a light touch.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_9.t: Believe me, that nosebleed could've been a whole lot worse. We didn't have to put you on fluids, did we?
He sets the mug down on the table. Murky liquid sloshes over the rim.
He was even kind enough to give you a cloth to bleed into.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_11.t: What other mage? I don't know what you're talking about.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_13.t: Must be a figment of your imagination. It happens sometimes... the brain invents details to try and make sense of what's happening to it. Give it a few days, and the confusion should fade away. You'll see.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_14.t: We don't even have two mages on-site. There's no way that you got scanned twice.
But hey, believe what you want. Don't let it distract you, though... we've got work to do.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_15.t: Now, let's get to it, shall we?
He shuffles his papers. Flips open the notebook on the table with the tip of his pen.
Run me through what happened last night.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_16.t: A guffaw of laughter explodes out of him.

...Yeah. Figured you might say something like that. Look, you aren't gonna keep anything from us by refusing to cooperate. We've already pulled all the facts from your brain. They're just... disorganized. Jumbled up a bit.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_27.t: He lowers the notepad and focuses on you.

Y'see, talking to us isn't squealing. We already *have* all of the intel we need. But it'd be much more efficient if you would fill in the blanks. All that you're doing by clamming up is wasting time.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_28.t: I'm amenable to that. You answer my questions, and we'll answer yours.
He taps the desk with his pen.
Go on, pick up where I left off. You were headed to Combat Pang's. Cheng had sent you there to conduct some sort of shakedown.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_29.t: He lets out a heavy sigh.
...I did tell you that we picked up your friends, too, right? They're safe and sound in our hands... for now, at least. If you refuse to help us, I can't promise that they'll stay that way.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_33.t: He turns on the audio recorder with an audible click.
Now, we already know that your team went to Combat Pang’s, the biker bar. That your boss, Kindly Cheng, sent you out there to conduct a shakedown of some kind.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_35.t: Yeah. Sure you did. If there's one thing that Eddie Pang is known for, it's his good-natured willingness to compromise.
He glances at the notebook on the table.
Says here that the object he'd stolen was a neolithic artifact. A jade cong, from the Liangzhu period. Dated back to 3000 BC, or thereabouts?

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_37.t: Not the smartest of moves on Pang's part. There aren't a lot of fences who can move a thing like that. We've got it locked up for safekeeping, by the way. It's in good hands with us.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_38.t: Now, you said "we" earlier. That was you, and...

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_40.t: He taps the recorder with the tip of his pen.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_41.t: Gobbet. The ork girl with the rodent fixation.
He jots down a note on his pad.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_43.t: The rat-girl with the big mouth. Right.
All right, got it... and who was the last member of your team?

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_47.t: The rat-girl with the big mouth. Right.
The pen dances over the pad a final time, then stops. Your interrogator glances up at you.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_50.t: The dwarf. Right. Quiet little thing.
The pen dances over the pad a final time, then stops. Your interrogator glances up at you.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_51.t: The ex-cop. Nothing I hate more than a good guy gone bad.
The pen dances over the pad a final time, then stops. Your interrogator glances up at you.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_52.t: The rigger. Creepy son of a bitch.
The pen dances over the pad a final time, then stops. Your interrogator glances up at you.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_53.t: The ghoul. Y'know, I can't understand why anyone would willingly associate with a corpse-eater.
The pen dances over the pad a final time, then stops. Your interrogator glances up at you.
Guess it doesn't bother you, though.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_55.t: "Subtle." Funny. Don't think I've ever met a shadowrunner that I'd describe as subtle. You're like a bunch of cartoon characters with guns.
The pen dances over the pad a final time, then stops. Your interrogator glances up at you.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_57.t: The pen dances over the pad a final time, then stops. Your interrogator glances up at you.

All right, got it... and who was the last member of your team?

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_61.t: The rigger. Creepy son of a bitch.
All right, got it... and who was the last member of your team?

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_62.t: The ghoul. Y'know, I can't understand why anyone would willingly associate with a corpse-eater. Guess it doesn't bother you, though.
All right, got it... and who was the last member of your team?

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_63.t: The dwarf girl. Quiet little thing.
He jots down a note on his pad.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_64.t: The ex-cop. Nothing I hate more than a good guy gone bad.
He jots down a note on his pad.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_66.t: The Russian with the drone. Right.
He jots down a note on his pad.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_67.t: The Russian with the drone. Right.

All right. And the two of you were headed to the biker bar at the behest of your boss, Kindly Cheng. She sent you to recover an artifact that Pang had... appropriated... from one of her warehouses. Did I get that right?

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_69.t: He jots down a note on his pad.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_70.t: He jots down a note on his pad.

All right. And the two of you were headed to the biker bar at the behest of your boss, Kindly Cheng. She sent you to recover an artifact that Pang had... appropriated... from one of her warehouses. Did I get that right?

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_71.t: He jots down a note on his pad.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_76.t: He glances at the notebook on the table.
Says here that it was a jade cong, from the Liangzhu period. Dated back to 3000 BC, or thereabouts?

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_77.t: Yes. Your boss has quite the reputation. Not the sort of person that a low-level gang leader should steal from. Speaking of which...

Says here that you were after a jade cong, from the Liangzhu period. Dated back to 3000 BC, or thereabouts?

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_78.t: The guy's name is Wallace Koo. But like I said, I've never met him in person... they only bring him on-site when there's astral work that needs doing. If you were thinking of getting payback, you shouldn't get your hopes up.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_79.t: He turns on the audio recorder with an audible click.
We already know that your team went to Combat Pang’s, the biker bar. That your boss, Kindly Cheng, sent you out there to conduct a shakedown of some kind.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_80.t: There is a long pause before he answers.
That supposed to be a joke?

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_82.t: Now, let's get started, shall we?
He turns on his audio recorder with an audible click.
We already know that your team went to Combat Pang’s, the biker bar. That your boss, Kindly Cheng, sent you out there to conduct a shakedown of some kind.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_87.t: He crosses his arms over his chest.

You don't wanna go down this road. If we have to pull the mage back in here, it's gonna be painful, and there could be long-term side effects. Look. We already have the information that we need - it's right here, in this notebook. All that we're asking for is your help in sorting it.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_91.t: What other one? I don't know what you're talking about.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_92.t: He pauses a moment, considering, then shrugs.
Afraid you're wrong about that. There couldn't have been anyone else.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_93.t: That'd be a lot more intimidating if you knew who you were looking for.
He sets the mug down on the table. Murky liquid sloshes over the rim.
Besides, he was kind enough to give you a cloth to bleed into. He didn't have to do that.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_95.t: After you've learned why you're here, I expect you to thank me.

That's a discussion for another time, though. For now, let's focus on you, and what our man pulled from that memory of yours.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_97.t: Interrogators, plural? Afraid you're mistaken - we've only got the one.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_98.t: I'm confident that I won't stay on your bad side for long. Believe it or not, it's in your interest to be here. By picking you up, we did you a favor.

This is all a discussion for another time, though. For now, let's focus on you, and what our man pulled from that memory of yours.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_99.t: We aren't screwing with you. In fact, once you've learned why you're here, I suspect that you'll thank me.
He sets the mug down on the table. Murky liquid sloshes over the rim.
But that's a discussion for another time. For now, let's focus on you, and what our man pulled from that memory of yours.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_100.t: Yeah, that's right. Our mage probed your mind while you slept.
He lifts the mug out of the light and toward his lips. You hear a long, loud slurp as he sucks the steaming fluid down.
Don't know the man myself, but I'm given to understand that he's good. Uses a light touch.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_101.t: You're upset. I get that. But threatening me isn't going to help you.
He lifts the mug out of the light and toward his lips. You hear a long, loud slurp as he sucks the steaming fluid down.
For the record, the mage who worked on you did his best to be gentle. He's good at what he does... uses a light touch.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_102.t: We know a lot of things about you. One of the many benefits of magical interrogation - it cuts through the bullshit and gets you right to the facts.
He gestures at the bloody rag in your lap.
Sorry about the nosebleed, by the way.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_103.t: Yeah, I'll bet. Glad to hear that you're keeping your spirits up. Sorry about the nosebleed, by the way - it's a common side effect of magical interrogation. Shouldn't cause you any more trouble.

55e8b58a643661a812007a17_104.t: He lets out a low chuckle.
You'll what? Burst another blood vessel? Pass out again?

56049c1c643661740e00133d_4.t: The troll pulls an object out of a police evidence bag marked with the HKPF logo. A PDA with a cracked case, stippled with day-old blood.
We recovered this at the scene. Take a look, and see what we saved you and your team from.

56049c1c643661740e00133d_5.t: He sets the PDA down on the desk, activates it with the tip of his pen, and slides it toward you.
Just watch - don't touch. You don't want your fingerprints on this thing.

56049c1c643661740e00133d_12.t: The camera focuses in on a figure behind police lines. Her features are instantly familiar. Chief Inspector Krait. She looks straight into the camera. Then she reaches her hand to an ear and speaks into her commlink. Her voice is loud and clear enough that you can hear it over the PDA's tinny speakers.

56049c1c643661740e00133d_17.t: Officially, the Special Duties Unit suppressed the riot, just like they were supposed to. That's the story that all of the news outlets are running with. Unofficially... well, you be the judge.

56049c1c643661740e00133d_18.t: Officially, the Special Duties Unit suppressed the riot, just like they were supposed to. That's the story that all of the news outlets are running with.
Unofficially... well, you be the judge.

56049c1c643661740e00133d_33.t: My wife doesn't seem to mind. Why should I care what you think?
He takes a long slurp of soykaf out of his mug.
...Now. It's my job to tell you that you aren't under arrest. You and your team are here as guests of the HKPF. You'll remain that way until we tell you differently.

56049c1c643661740e00133d_36.t: He sets the PDA down on the desk, activates it with the tip of his pen, and slides it toward you.
But for now, keep your eyes on the screen. Oh, and don't touch that PDA - you don't want to get your fingerprints on it.

56049c1c643661740e00133d_37.t: Maybe you can. Only one way to find out.
He sets the PDA down on the desk, activates it with the tip of his pen, and slides it toward you.
Just watch - don't touch. You don't want your fingerprints on this thing.

56049c1c643661740e00133d_40.t: Go ahead. And you should probably know that we saved their lives by bringing them in. If they'd still been on the street when the crackdown started, well...
Just be thankful that they weren't.

56049c1c643661740e00133d_42.t: He pulls an object out of a police evidence bag marked with the HKPF logo. A PDA with a cracked case, stippled with day-old blood.
We recovered this at the scene. You'll find your details in here.

56049c1c643661740e00133d_43.t: You don't know how wrong you are.
He pulls an object out of a police evidence bag marked with the HKPF logo. A PDA with a cracked case, stippled with day-old blood.
You're about to find out, though.

56049c1c643661740e00133d_44.t: I don't doubt that. From what I've heard, you've been through a lot.
He pulls an object out of a police evidence bag marked with the HKPF logo. A PDA with a cracked case, stippled with day-old blood.
Experience doesn't make you bulletproof, though.

56049c1c643661740e00133d_47.t: And now that you've helped us, it's only fair that you learn something in return.
