54d3e906323437f05700370f_7.t: A message from Cheng? I can't wait to hear you mangle it.
You speak Cantonese so well. But before I hear it, I have a little message for her, too.

54d3e906323437f05700370f_12.t: I don't get to hear you butcher the message? I'm sad.
He grabs the small plastic drive from your hand. Slots it into his trid player.

54d3e906323437f05700370f_24.t: He pauses. Casts his eyes to the floor.
...And tell Mrs. Cheng to expect me at Swift Winds tomorrow.

54d3e906323437f05700370f_26.t: Strangler Bao reaches for his PDA. Taps a button on the side. Only a second later, you hear the sound of a door opening and footsteps running towards your position.

54d3e906323437f05700370f_27.t: Strangler Bao grins a sharklike grin.
You want to play? Let's play.

54d3e906323437f05700370f_30.t: His eyes get a stony look.

54d3e906323437f05700370f_31.t: So you have some sort of message from Cheng? I can't wait to hear you mangle it. You speak Cantonese so well.
But before I hear it, I have a little message for her, too.

54d3e906323437f05700370f_33.t: So you have a message from the old lady, huh? I can't wait to hear you mangle it.
You speak Cantonese so well. But before I hear it, I have a little message for her, too.

55455138656333641b003bab_0.t: Strangler Bao stands like a granite pillar in the smoky mahjong parlor, muscular arms folded across his chest. As you approach, his eyes light up with hatred. He stares at you, breathing heavily. Then he masters himself. His shoulders relax, and he settles into glowering coldness.

55455138656333641b003bab_1.t: Strangler Bao stands impassively, muscular arms folded across his chest. The low noise and stale smell of the mahjong parlor seem to glance off him, like rain off a granite statue. He acknowledges you with a nod. A quarter of an inch.

55455138656333641b003bab_2.t: His rumbling bass voice seems to vibrate the floorboards.

55455138656333641b003bab_3.t: Bao says it as if it explains everything.
He's been talking in the Matrix about your jobs. It's drawing attention. Kindly wants him shut up.

55455138656333641b003bab_5.t: Law isn't being discreet. He needs to be discreet.
Bao leans forward just a little, shifting onto the balls of his feet. Like a tilting slab of concrete.
Your job is to fix the situation.

55455138656333641b003bab_6.t: Don't bother finding me when you're done, unless there's something to clean up. I've got better things to do with my time.

55455138656333641b003bab_9.t: Did you forget? You're an instrument of the Yellow Lotus.

55455138656333641b003bab_12.t: I can't force you to do this... but if you don't handle this, I will. You may not like that.

55455138656333641b003bab_15.t: You better. This needs to be solved *immediately.*
Bao regards you with glacial animosity.
If you kill him, do it *quietly.* On the boat. No upsetting the neighborhood. If that's possible, for you.

55455138656333641b003bab_18.t: Bao stares at you with pure, patient malevolence.
Someday... we'll finish what we started.

55455138656333641b003bab_20.t: This needs to be handled soon.
Strangler Bao grunts and nods almost imperceptibly, as if to signal the end of the conversation.
Thank you. Your skill is appreciated.

55455138656333641b003bab_24.t: Bao stares at you with cold animosity.

55455138656333641b003bab_31.t: Bao gives you only the briefest glance, and grunts in dismissal.
I see you got my message. We had something to take care of, but you're late. Maximum Law was spreading information about your jobs in the Matrix.

55455138656333641b003bab_33.t: Bao doesn't reply. He appears to be lost in his thoughts.

55455138656333641b003bab_35.t: Bao's expression is inscrutable - a perfect poker face.
No. But you will not see him again.

55496bb3303031a8140055ca_4.t: Bao inclines his head. One quarter of an inch.

55496bb3303031a8140055ca_5.t: Strangler Bao's eyes widen until they're more white than color. There's raw hatred written there.

55496bb3303031a8140055ca_8.t: Bao doesn't move. Says nothing. But out of the corner of your eye, you can see him eyeballing you.

55496bb3303031a8140055ca_14.t: Bao inclines his head. One quarter of an inch.

55496bb3303031a8140055ca_15.t: Bao doesn't move but his jaw tightens and nostrils flare.

55496bb3303031a8140055ca_17.t: Bao inclines his head. One quarter of an inch.

55496bb3303031a8140055ca_18.t: The muscles in Strangler Bao's thick neck ripple and his eyes go "I will kill you" wide. He takes a half step forward before regaining control of himself.

55496bb3303031a8140055ca_21.t: Strangler Bao's eyes widen until they're more white than color. They glisten with raw hatred.

55496bb3303031a8140055ca_45.t: Bao fiddles with the PDA in his meaty hands.
He's telling the truth. No outgoing calls on his PDA.

555f608e6236667431006bac_49.t: Auntie Cheng's lost too many runners already. Besides, there are cleaner ways to end your life than jumping in front of a train.

555f608e6236667431006bac_50.t: He cuts you off with a grunt.
You were hallucinating. And you almost just got yourself killed. Now get up and get back to that floating wreck that you sleep in. You don't get to die in here unless Mrs. Cheng says you do.

555f608e6236667431006bac_51.t: You're right. Now get up and get back to that floating wreck that you sleep in. You don't get to die in here unless Mrs. Cheng says you do.

555f608e6236667431006bac_52.t: He grunts. Turns his back on you.
Whatever. Now get up and get back to that floating wreck that you sleep in.

55b3d7776236668819001370_2.t: Bao lifts a finger to his ear to transmit the orders. When he sees you waiting, his eyes narrow to sharp slits of malice but he continues his work.

55b3d7776236668819001370_3.t: Bao lifts a finger to his ear to transmit the orders. When he sees you waiting, he inclines his head a quarter of an inch.

55b3d7776236668819001370_37.t: Strangler Bao leans in, his finger to his ear.
My apologies, Mrs. Cheng, but one of the men you sent with the runners into the Walled City is still alive. He stumbled into Wong and Ho as they were pulling medical supplies off a lorry.

55b3d7776236668819001370_39.t: Strangler Bao lowers his head, listens to his earpiece again.
Our man says that Wong Lun Fat is dead. He got shot up by the HKPF inside the Walled City.
Bao lifts his head and makes eye-contact with the triad boss. He's almost salivating with avarice.
Our man saw it with his own eyes, Mrs. Cheng.

55b3d7776236668819001370_44.t: The death of Wong Lun Fat has opened a spot for you to move up, madam. *You* could be the next 438.
Bao smiles. The effect is chilling.
The gods help those who seize opportunities.

55b3d7776236668819001370_50.t: Bao lowers his head in respect.
You have proven that, Mrs. Cheng.

55b3d7776236668819001370_52.t: Bao stares at you over the rim of his glass as he drinks.

55b3d7776236668819001370_96.t: Bao lifts a finger to his ear to transmit the orders. When he sees you waiting, his eyes narrow to sharp slits of malice but he continues his work.

55b3d7776236668819001370_97.t: Bao lifts a finger to his ear to transmit the orders. When he sees you waiting, he inclines his head a quarter of an inch.

55b3d7776236668819001370_121.t: Strangler Bao leans in, his finger to his ear.
My apologies, Mrs. Cheng, but one of the men you sent with the runners into the Walled City is still alive. He stumbled into Wong and Ho as they were pulling medical supplies off a lorry.

55b3d7776236668819001370_123.t: Strangler Bao lowers his head, listens to his earpiece again.
Our man says that Wong Lun Fat is dead. He got shot up by the HKPF inside the Walled City.
Bao lifts his head and makes eye-contact with the triad boss. He's almost salivating with avarice.
Our man saw it with his own eyes, Mrs. Cheng.

55b3d7776236668819001370_128.t: The death of Wong Lun Fat has opened a spot for you to move up, madam. *You* could be the next 438.
Bao smiles. The effect is chilling.
The gods help those who seize opportunities.

55b3d7776236668819001370_134.t: Bao lowers his head in respect.
You have proven that, Mrs. Cheng.

55b3d7776236668819001370_136.t: Bao stares at you over the rim of his glass as he drinks.

55943ab8306364c82b0008b2_0.t: Captain Jomo is busily playing some kind of gambling game on his commlink. It beeps and flashes as he taps away.
Come on, Jomo. Win, not lose! Maybe buy yourself that new hat!
He doesn't appear to have noticed you at all.

55943ab8306364c82b0008b2_1.t: The game makes a sad, defeated noise. Jomo curses under his breath, nose wrinkling in disgust. Finally, he seems to notice you.
Stupid game. Always, it cheats me! Never a good hand... Now! Kindly Cheng sends you, lah? You need Captain Jomo to take you to play pirate?

55943ab8306364c82b0008b2_3.t: Jomo stands at the edge of the dock, smoking a hand-rolled cigarette as he stares out over the river. As you approach, he lifts a hand in greeting.
Ho there! Come to admire the river with Jomo? I have a bottle of baiju we can share.

55943ab8306364c82b0008b2_4.t: The ork pirate grins from ear to ear and produces a bottle half-full of clear liquid. There is no label, and the cork appears to be hand-carved.
It is the best my meager money can buy. Not many ships worth raiding, lately. God damned weather!

55943ab8306364c82b0008b2_5.t: A small sip was probably the better choice - the taste is like unto acetone, kerosene, and old tires. It burns in your mouth, a pile of tiny liquid coals, and you stifle a cough. Jomo claps you on the back, still grinning.
See? This is how Kindly stays looking so young! Chinese elixir of life!

55943ab8306364c82b0008b2_6.t: What favor can Jomo do for you? You need a body dumped at sea? I can do it fast, no one will see. Maybe you need to hide some cargo on a beach, make a map with 'X marks the spot'?
Captain Jomo bursts into hearty laughter, slapping his side in mirth.

55943ab8306364c82b0008b2_9.t: A smaller drink may have been a better idea. Your eyes begin to water as the baiju fills your mouth with the flavor of kerosene and old tires. Your throat feels like someone's poured molten glass down it. Stifling a cough, you hand the bottle back to Jomo. He's still grinning.
With this, you are ready to conquer the world!

55943ab8306364c82b0008b2_10.t: Raising the bottle to his lips, Jomo takes a long pull. When he finishes, he coughs a few times.

55943ab8306364c82b0008b2_11.t: Jomo raises a hand in greeting as you approach.
All done now? I take you to your ship now? Faster than you can blink, lah!

55943ab8306364c82b0008b2_12.t: Jomo raises a hand in greeting as you approach.
Welcome back, friend. Maybe now you tell me what favor you need from me? Jomo is a good friend to all in Heoi.

5600327e626632e01500440a_1.t: Jomo claps you on the back, grinning widely.
Ho, my friend! Finally they let you out of that ugly shed! I thought they'd be many more hours at least - they have no sense of urgency.

5600327e626632e01500440a_2.t: He puffs out his chest indignantly.

You insult me! Jomo is too slippery by far to be snared by a policeman's hook! I am here because they paid me for the privilege, the same as any other client. What does Jomo care if they wear badges and uniforms? Nuyen is nuyen, is it not?

5600327e626632e01500440a_5.t: So, my friend! How can Jomo assist you? Do you need a ride someplace? If so, say the word, and we will be off like a typhoon!
He lowers his voice to a conspiratorial whisper.
Or perhaps you're interested in contraband... goods of the sort that your uniformed allies will not provide. I have all manner of *unusual things* for sale, if you have the nuyen.

5600327e626632e01500440a_7.t: Drugs, illicit software... even one-of-a-kind sim chips! You name it, and Jomo can deliver.
The ork winks at you in a knowing fashion.

5600327e626632e01500440a_8.t: A single sim, truth be told. Jomo has no idea where it came from, but it seems like something that was custom made.
He pauses briefly to chew on his words.
It is a... shall we say... "specialty product." I haven't tried to open it myself, but the label on the chip says "RatParty.SIM."

5600327e626632e01500440a_11.t: Very good, very good indeed! You never know when you'll need one of... *those,* yes?
He nods decisively and stamps a foot.
Now let us speak of this no more.

5600327e626632e01500440a_12.t: The smile comes back, brilliant as ever.
How else can Jomo help you? Say the word, and I will accommodate.

5600327e626632e01500440a_19.t: I don't want to repeat the details here - the police don't need to know.
Jomo slips a PDA out of his pocket and taps at it.
Here. I've sent you the details - it should be on your computer already. Look it over, let me know if you're interested in the job.

5600327e626632e01500440a_20.t: He beams at you, his tusks glinting.
A thing of beauty, isn't she? With Jomo at the wheel, the Drunken Mistake can outrun anything on the South China Sea!

5600327e626632e01500440a_21.t: Ah, well, her former owner was a Fuchi executive. A bloated canker of a man, drunk on power and British gin!

He took this fine vessel on a one-man pleasure cruise, blundered into Loho-Jowah waters, and stalled her out. I came upon him, drifting helplessly, crying for help against the roar of an uncaring sea!

5600327e626632e01500440a_24.t: I named my prize the Drunken Mistake to commemorate that fateful day, and as a salute to her former owner.
Jomo places a hand on his heart.
May he live a long, besotted life, wherever the tides may have taken him! And if the waves dragged him down before he saw land again, let him drink forever in the afterlife!

5600327e626632e01500440a_25.t: Jomo raises his hands, an eyebrow arcing up at the same time.

I did not kill him, if that's what you're thinking. Jomo is not so heartless as to kill such a pitiful specimen as this! Naturally, I helped the poor soul onto my safe, seaworthy dinghy. Then I crossed over to the beautiful machine that you see, restarted her engines, and the rest is history!

5600327e626632e01500440a_26.t: No, never! Jomo is not so heartless as to kill so pitiful a specimen. Nay, if he can be said to have a fault, it is his bountiful compassion and generosity! I helped the poor soul into my sturdy, seaworthy dinghy. Then I crossed over to the beautiful machine that you see, restarted her engines, and the rest is history.

5600327e626632e01500440a_27.t: Not as such, no. Her former owner was a Fuchi executive. A bloated canker of a man, drunk on power and British gin!

He took this fine vessel on a one-man pleasure cruise, blundered into Loho-Jowah waters, and stalled her out. I came upon him, drifting helplessly, crying for help against the roar of an uncaring sea!

5600327e626632e01500440a_32.t: Jomo guffaws loudly, slapping a hand against his thigh.
This is why we are friends! No matter what the situation, you keep your sense of humor. It will be good to work together again, even if it's the police who are paying me this time.

5600327e626632e01500440a_33.t: Clicking his tongue scornfully, Jomo shakes his head.
Jomo has things to do, people to see, guns to run. Even if they're paying me to be your chauffeur, it's not the only business I have! Still, it will be good to work with you again.

5600327e626632e01500440a_36.t: He puffs out his chest indignantly.

You insult me! Jomo is too slippery by far to be snared by a policeman's hook! I am here because they paid me for the privilege, the same as any other client. What does Jomo care if they wear badges and uniforms? Nuyen is nuyen, is it not?

5600327e626632e01500440a_39.t: Ho, friend. It's time, isn't it?
Jomo stares at you sternly, his eyes narrowed.
Time for us to end this, yes?

5600327e626632e01500440a_40.t: Jomo is peering into his PDA, the flickering screen showing a trailer for some cut-rate Indonesian horror movie. Based on what you can see of the screen, it's titled "Ratu Adil Versus the Shadow Wizards." The ork flips the PDA off as he notices your presence.
Ahh! Just some light entertainment before work, you see. What is it you need, my friend?

5600327e626632e01500440a_41.t: Jomo nods at you with a mischevious grin.
Things all sorted out now? Utama happy? Qiu, too? How can Jomo help you today?

5600327e626632e01500440a_42.t: Jomo is hunched over the side of his boat, examining some deep scratches in the otherwise pristine hull. He curses under his breath, turning to face you.

You know, with all this work we're doing, I think that the Drunken Mistake will need a new paint job when we're done. She deserves a makeover after all of her hard work. So! What can Captain Jomo do for you today, eh?

5600327e626632e01500440a_43.t: Jomo is busy gulping down some manner of cheap Singaporean beer, the label marked with a golden merlion. He raises a hand in greeting.
Hello, hello! What can I do for you, my friend?

5600347d626632e015004458_1.t: It is my honor to introduce you to Captain Utama, our most gracious and benevolent host.
My Captain, I thank you for your generous hospitality. I remain your humble servant, and offer my deepest respect.

561ef5d7643661281b000cf9_0.t: The pirate beams a brilliant smile at you.
Ah, you return! And the lovely Senior Inspector Qiu is with you! Are you ready to go, my friend?

561ef5d7643661281b000cf9_1.t: He throws back his head and laughs.
Marine Region patrol boats wallow like pigs in shit! We will run circles around them!

561ef5d7643661281b000cf9_3.t: Jomo offers her a mocking half-bow, leans into the Drunken Mistake, and punches a button. The entire boat begins to vibrate as the craft's engines roar to life.
Best climb in and buckle yourselves down, then. This will be a bumpy ride!

561ef7da643661281b000d3d_2.t: Captain Jomo barks out a laugh.
Wouldn't recommend that, beautiful girl. Lots of sharks out there, both normal and Awakened. Nastier things, too. Any one of them would be all too happy to snap up a little thing like you.

561ef7da643661281b000d3d_4.t: I will wait here, with the boat. You come back to me when your business is done and I will take you home.
He gestures at a uniformed constable in the distance. She nods slightly in response.
You see her? That lady there - PC Cheung - is your contact. She can get you into the building.

561ef7da643661281b000d3d_5.t: Of course. You are my client. You pay me, I take you wherever you want to go. I help you, and you get out of this safe, then maybe you pay me again tomorrow to take you someplace else.

561ef7da643661281b000d3d_6.t: Go on, now. Raid your police station. I will wait here with the Drunken Mistake, and protect her from the advances of any swine who might try to impound her.
He casts a longing look back at his speedboat.
Don't leave us waiting too long, lah? Jomo is possessed of many fine qualities, but patience is not one of them.

561ef7da643661281b000d3d_8.t: Of course. Jomo knows how to play the getaway driver. He will do his part.
He waves you away with a gnarled hand.
Go on, raid your police station. I will wait here with the Drunken Mistake, and protect her from the advances of any swine who might try to impound her.

561ef7da643661281b000d3d_9.t: He casts a longing look back at his speedboat.
Don't leave us waiting too long, lah? Jomo is possessed of many fine qualities, but patience is not one of them.

561ef7da643661281b000d3d_10.t: Ask her yourself. Me, I just drive the boat.
He waves you away with a gnarled hand.
Go on, raid your police station. I will wait here with the Drunken Mistake, and protect her from the advances of any swine who might try to impound her.

5643aed1306364c00f006fe9_1.t: Jomo's face appears on your PDA.
Jomo here. I'm taking off, and I'll make a pass around the tower. You go down and get the girl, I'll swoop back in, and we'll all be off. Easy, lah? Ten, fifteen minutes, tops!

56537124626632d423006c6b_1.t: Aku seorang Kapiten! Mem-pun-yai pedang panjang!
He pumps his fist in the air, vigorously swinging some imaginary object overhead.
Kalau berjalan prok, prok, prok! Aku seorang Kapiten!

56537124626632d423006c6b_5.t: Jomo nods, a grave look on his face.
I know a place. Somewhere that the HKPF cannot approach by land, air, or sea. Jomo will take you there... and then, he will try very hard to talk his way out of suffering the consequences.

565f332762663208210043d4_1.t: Welcome aboard the Benteng, the jewel of the Loho-Jowah fleet!
He flashes you a brilliant smile.
You will be safe here - safer than you were in the impound yard, in Heoi, or at your mother's teat. Nothing enters these waters without Captain Utama's permission.

565f332762663208210043d4_2.t: Oh-ho? And this worries you? Take it from me... it shouldn't.
Jomo swings his arm out in a grand sweeping gesture.
Look around you. See for yourself.

565f332762663208210043d4_4.t: The seabed beneath your feet is littered with the carcasses of countless drones and aircraft.
He pumps his arms excitedly, miming the action of a cannon firing on an airborne target.
They all met their doom to the roar of the Benteng's mighty guns! Sheets of flying lead tore wings from fuselages, ripped through cockpits, shredded parachutes to tattered rags!

565f332762663208210043d4_9.t: Jomo favors the pirate with a nod.
Teugh speaks the truth, friends. Captain Utama is a delightful woman, possessed of many virtues... but patience is not one of them. We should be on our way.

565f332762663208210043d4_21.t: Qiu, you're with me. We have a captain to meet.

565f332762663208210043d4_28.t: Navy or no, they dare not approach.
Jomo sweeps his arm across the deck, smiling brilliantly.
Look around you. See for yourself.

565f332762663208210043d4_29.t: Oh, don't take my word for it, my friend.
Jomo sweeps his arm across the deck, smiling brilliantly.
Look around you. See for yourself.

566213356436616013004496_2.t: The pirate cups his hands to his mouth and barks out his reply.
You insult me! Krait's traitorous pigs were as blind to our approach as--

566213356436616013004496_16.t: Captain Jomo leans into the group.
Perhaps you should go exploring, lah? Do a little of the legwork that you shadowrunners are so fond of?

56ad342b626632980d007eb8_14.t: To the new home of the Yellow Lotus, beautiful girl!
Come, now - we must go. But trust Jomo... all will be made clear in time!

56ad342b626632980d007eb8_16.t: Why, to the new home of the Yellow Lotus, my friend!
Come, now - we must go. But trust Jomo... all will be made clear in time!

54da5dae306364a88e0035e1_4.t: The Elf on the screen has the bearing of someone with no time for pleasantries, and his tone is flat when he speaks.
This is Tigath. Is the task done?

54da5dae306364a88e0035e1_7.t: Tigath grimaces, but nods his head in agreement after a few seconds.
I'll pay whatever it takes to get them off my back. If I can't, my family's still dead.

54da5dae306364a88e0035e1_70.t: Tigath's mouth twists into an ugly scowl.
I know that, goddamit! But you have to understand, if I can't get clear of this hit team, my family is still dead even if you post that data!

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_0.t: Tigath fidgets nervously as you approach him. His entire body is tense, and his eyes flick rapidly around the market, as if searching for unseen threats. As he notices you, he relaxes somewhat - but only a small amount.

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_1.t: Oh, thank god you're here... I'm at my wit's end. I could swear that I'm being watched everywhere I go.
He continues to glance nervously around.
I don't like being out here. It's too exposed.

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_2.t: In the distance, a police siren wails. Tigath's hand dives under his jacket, but as the wailing recedes, he relaxes again.
Listen, did you bring the stuff? The faster I can get that stuff and leave Hong Kong, the safer I'll be.

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_4.t: Tigath stops fidgeting, takes a breath, and nods.
Okay, okay. I'm calm. Can we just get this over with?

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_11.t: What do you mean, somebody's after me? I told you that already! It's the Seoulpas!

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_12.t: Like, they were listening in on the conversation?! Holy shit, holy shit...
Tigath goes white and shifts back and forth on his feet, as if he's about to break and run.
What am I going to do?!

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_20.t: The elf runs a shaking hand through his hair and nods.
Yeah... Yeah, that makes sense. Okay. You ready to make the trade?

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_22.t: Tigath eyes you uneasily. He pulls at one fingernail as his eyes dart over the darkened market behind you.

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_23.t: Tigath closes his eyes and lets out a long, shuddering breath. He stands silently for a long moment, with his eyes closed. When he finally opens them again, his expression shifts from one of fear to that of cold professionalism.
Good. That shifts the balance of power considerably.

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_24.t: Before you can act, he puts one hand to his ear and utters a single sentence.
Move in and secure the area.
There is a sudden sound of running - boots on concrete. In the split second you glance over Tigath's shoulder, he's in motion.

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_25.t: Tigath fidgets a nervously, regarding you with suspicion.
What do you mean by that?

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_33.t: Tigath grits his teeth. He closes his eyes, takes a deep breath, and opens them again.
Right. I know I'm not paying you to care, but if you give me the data and tissue samples, you can get all that money you care so much about.

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_34.t: Tigath waves a hand in front of his face impatiently.
Yes, yes! Of course I've got to work on that, too. But what matters right now is me getting out of Hong Kong safely.

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_36.t: Tigath swears under his breath, wiping a hand over his damp forehead.
Shit, what!? That must be how the Seoulpas have been staying on me the whole time.

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_37.t: The elf gestures at you impatiently.
Well, hand over the data and samples, and you can get paid. Then we can all go home safely.

54e393aa3063640c0c0027dc_43.t: Tigath fidgets a nervously, regarding you with suspicion.

5693de1c3063641c0e0012f0_11.t: Lily gives you an incredulous look out of the corner of her eye, but begins smacking her lips together noisily.

You finally want 'em back, huh? The memories that we locked away. I always knew that you would one day. But you got so hostile after the procedure, I didn't think you'd come around this soon.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_2.t: Is0bel glowers at him, her cheeks darkening.
Oh, get bent, Rhombus. I got hostile with you because you got hostile with me.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_4.t: Is0bel cuts in, her normally small voice growing raucous.

I called you a patronizing bastard because *that's what you were being.* You wouldn't have taken that tone if I were a guy, or an ork, or a troll. You talked down to me like I was your little sister, or something. Well, guess what? I'M NOT!

557109403234316c1c0044d9_6.t: She shouts over him, cutting him short.
Also: "punch back"? That's a funny way of putting it. You TAILORED AN ESP to TROLL ALL OF MY POSTS ON SHADOWLAND! What kind of PRICK would do a thing like that?

557109403234316c1c0044d9_7.t: His face goes beet-red, and his chest puffs out.
The kind whose cat you had killed, you little monster! Captain Whiskers was just a *house cat!* He didn't have anything to do with this!

557109403234316c1c0044d9_8.t: That wasn't me, I would never hurt a cat! If I were going to have someone killed, it would have been you!

557109403234316c1c0044d9_10.t: You're the muscle, I take it. You and your jarhead friend over there.
She brought you along to beat the information out of me, huh?

557109403234316c1c0044d9_12.t: He scoffs at Is0bel. Wrinkles his nose in disgust.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_16.t: His eyes dart from side to side. Then he starts screaming.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_19.t: To hell with this. Let's hit him like a piñata until software comes out. Sound like a plan?

557109403234316c1c0044d9_21.t: Rhombus' eyes screw shut in concentration.

I know that I was trying to do her a favor. I don't remember what the advice I was trying to give her was about, though. I think... uh... maybe I was telling her what kind of RAM to pick up for a deck? Something like that.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_24.t: Yeah... I think that was it. But you were a real dick about it.
She stares up at him, fuming.
And the argument was nothing. It was what you did *after* the argument that was unforgivable.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_26.t: I'll give her what she wants! I'll give her everything!

557109403234316c1c0044d9_27.t: He keys in a series of rapid commands on his PDA, then turns to Is0bel, sobbing.
There. Just check your inbox. You have it! Now for the sake of whatever friendship we might have had, please... let me go!

557109403234316c1c0044d9_30.t: Up here, in headware storage. But I can't access it without the key. And thanks to Rhombus here, now I've got it.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_34.t: That's actually true. We were friends then.
She looks down at Rhombus, who lies whimpering on the floor.
Now, we're not. And that's why you're getting stuffed in a closet.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_38.t: Oh, that son of a bitch. Rhom must've called the cops... he's probably got a PanicButton, or something.
She casts a hateful glare at the closet door, then draws her slivergun.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_40.t: Not as much as those memories are going to hurt you, Izz.
We locked them away for a reason. And as much as I might dislike you now, I don't want to see you live through that again.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_42.t: Rhombus begins to back away, his eyes wide with terror.
No... no! You keep away! I can do things to you in the Matrix! I... I can drain your bank accounts, slander you, publish your intimate details! Stay back! Please!

557109403234316c1c0044d9_44.t: I'll give her what she wants! I'll give her everything!
He keys in a series of rapid commands on his PDA, then turns to Is0bel, sobbing.
There. Just check your inbox. You have it! Now for the sake of whatever friendship we might have had, please... let me go!

557109403234316c1c0044d9_45.t: His eyes dart from side to side. Then he starts screaming.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_46.t: I can't believe that this is happening to me. All over me trying to do you a favor.
He shakes his head at Is0bel.
That'll teach me to try to give a friend some advice, I guess.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_51.t: It wasn't what you said, Rhombus. It was how you said it.
She stares up at him, fuming.
And the argument was nothing. It was what you did *after* the argument that was unforgivable.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_57.t: Yes. I had Rhombus move the memories of my childhood into headware storage. That's why I couldn't give you any details about the Walled City - I didn't have access to them.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_60.t: But I... I thought that we were enemies now.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_61.t: We were. But there was no reason for us to be. Just take the key and go. Have a nice life.
He waves her off. He looks exhausted.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_63.t: I'm sorry, Rhombus. I don't know how things got as bad as they did, but... sorry.
I'm going to find out what happened to Captain Whiskers. I promise you that.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_64.t: That'd be nice of you, Izz. I'd appreciate it.
Almost bashfully, his gaze flits to the ground. He looks away, and color surges to his cheeks.
Oh, and Izz... I've, uh... I've got something of an admission to make.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_65.t: Look, I *get it,* Rhom. You've always liked me. I hate to break it to you, but it was never that big of a secret. And I *know* that's why you--

557109403234316c1c0044d9_66.t: His eyes go wide. He raises his hands, palms out, and shakes his head.
What? No, Izz - no, that's not it at all. I mean, you're a nice girl, and all, but... just... *no.*

557109403234316c1c0044d9_68.t: He flips his sleeve inside out, and you can see a blinking red LED embedded in the fabric.
I tripped it when you broke in. The cops are on their way. But wait, don't freak out - I'll call them off for you.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_69.t: She shuffles her feet. Looks away.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_81.t: Holy shit. You're really a ghoul.
It isn't a question. His voice goes up an octave.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_85.t: Rhombus swallows. Hard. He opens his mouth to speak, but no words come out.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_87.t: But I *want* you to hurt him. I mean, I don't want to see him get his limbs torn off, or anything--

557109403234316c1c0044d9_88.t: Racter purses his lips. Koschei scuttles back to his side.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_93.t: His voice goes up an octave, and he points a trembling finger at Is0bel.
She... ah... brought you along to... to beat the information out of me, didn't she?

557109403234316c1c0044d9_95.t: Rhombus swallows. Hard. He opens his mouth to speak, but no words come out. His eyes are locked on the drone that's slowly scuttling toward him.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_96.t: You're the muscle, I take it. You and your friend.
She brought you along to beat the information out of me, didn't she? Typical.

557109403234316c1c0044d9_97.t: You're the muscle, I take it. She brought you along to beat the information out of me, didn't she?
The guy is terrified, but he's doing his best to hide it. His best isn't very good.

5508afcf343061b0160031d5_1.t: She opens both eyes and points to the ground.
It enters on this floor, circles through this room, then flows back out. If we wanna sabotage Wuxing's qi, we should find a way to prevent it from circulating back into the atrium from here.

5508afcf343061b0160031d5_3.t: If we disrupt the flow of qi here, it will affect the atrium. Subtly, but enough to destabilize this building for weeks. Perhaps months.

5508afcf343061b0160031d5_4.t: You can feel the energy surging through this office.

5508afcf343061b0160031d5_5.t: It's within this room that all the building's qi coalesces before flowing back out into the rest of the structure.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_1.t: Can you believe this? Trapped up here. They're asking us to put our lives on hold - our *business* on hold.
His eye catches a corner of the pool, then locks on his own reflection. Without pause, he leans over the water and begins smoothing down his already tidy hair.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_2.t: Those idiots don't even know what they're looking for. They're calling it a "security situation," but when I pressed them for details? Nothing!

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_3.t: He straightens up and smooths out his suit.
I highly doubt that. They're imbeciles, plain and simple.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_5.t: And the worst part? I have top-level clearance for those elevators. I *should* be able to access them without question. But these imbeciles refuse to let me through - never mind the fact that I outrank them.
He raises a hand to adjust one of his cuff links.
They told me to wait over here, as if I were some... *nobody.*

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_6.t: Makes you wonder what poor life choices they must've made to wind up in those uniforms.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_9.t: The executive looks Gaichu up and down. His mouth opens, then closes. He shakes his head in a visible effort to dismiss his thoughts, then mutters a short response.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_11.t: The man seems to notice Racter for the first time. Looks him up and down.
I'm familiar with that Russian institution. Drones and aerospace, correct? I'm fairly certain that we bought a few engine designs from Grishin-Aviakor back in the late forties. Solid work.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_13.t: The man hardly gives Duncan a second look.

Well, obviously. But by that logic, these grunts should be doing whatever I tell them to. I'm more important to the company than them, *and* their commanding officers, and they're *refusing* to let me past. It's infuriating.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_18.t: I don't know about all of that, but the system has created a lot of inconvenience for me.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_20.t: The exec raises his eyebrows. A short burst of laughter follows.
Ahh, what a wonderfully lowbrow way of putting it. I'm in complete agreement.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_21.t: He straightens up and smooths out his suit.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_23.t: He straightens up and smooths out his suit. Shoots you a critical look.
*You* can wait here as long as you want. My time, however, is far too valuable to be wasted here. Every second that I remain on this floor, Fuchi Orbital Dynamics loses money.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_25.t: The exec is busily primping in a small, portable mirror. He glimpses you from the corner of his eye, and with a loud cough, clamps the compact shut.
Ahem. Hello. Any news? Time's wasting, and time is money.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_27.t: He regards you for a moment. Smiles to himself.
Yes. I'm sure that I'm quite extraordinary to you. What's a man of my age and talent doing here at Fuchi? Happy to indulge you, my friend.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_29.t: Jens tosses a derogatory glance toward the elevators.
And I make more in a week than those guards do in a *year.*

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_37.t: FOD's been in discussions with the Executive Council to build a spaceport in Hong Kong - a joint venture between Chinese corporations and multinationals like Fuchi. Things were going great - better than great - until *this.*
He motions toward the guards blocking the elevators.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_38.t: His eyes narrow, irising to pinpoints. A swift and mechanical response - they must be enhanced in some subtle way. He lowers his voice to a whisper.
Very perceptive of you. Now lower your voice, if you please. We wouldn't want to attract any undue attention.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_39.t: And I know a fellow shadowrunner. I'd marked you as soon as you walked into the room.
He jerks his head in Lily's direction.
An extraction job, I take it? Best of luck.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_41.t: My handle is Awiin. I'm here from London, looking for a missing friend. I tracked him here, to Ikon Tower, and then... nothing.
He jerks his head toward the elevator guard.
Look. We are both professionals, and we both need to get off of this floor. Our odds of that are better if our cover stories remain intact.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_42.t: I gave you my real identity as a professional courtesy. Yes, I'm a fellow runner; it's always a pleasure to meet a colleague. Now, if you please: drop this line of inquiry, address me only as Jens Rasmussen, and show me the deference that you would a real Fuchi VP.
He gives Lily a significant glance.
I won't blow your cover if you don't blow mine.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_45.t: What a charming niece you have, friend.
His voice regains the imperious bluster that it originally carried. He smooths the fabric of his coat with one hand as the elevator guard wearily returns her attention to him.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_46.t: What a charming niece you have, friend.
His voice regains the imperious bluster that it originally carried. He smooths the fabric of his coat with one hand.
...Now, shall we discuss something else? I am a *very important man,* after all.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_47.t: A missing friend. I've come here from London looking for him. I followed his trail here, to Ikon Tower, and then... nothing.
He jerks his head toward the elevator guard.
Look. We are both professionals, and we both need to get off of this floor. Our odds of that are better if our cover stories remain intact.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_49.t: He gives Lily a significant glance.
I won't blow your cover if you don't blow mine.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_51.t: It is fairly clear that we're both in the same line of work. Takes a runner to know a runner, after all.
He jerks his head in Lilian's direction.
An extraction job, I take it? Best of luck.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_52.t: I wouldn't dream of interfering with the work of a fellow shadowrunner. I'd marked you as such the moment you walked into the room.
He jerks his head in Lily's direction.
An extraction job, I take it? Best of luck.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_57.t: He rolls his eyes and resumes primping in his compact.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_59.t: Young woman, I am Jens Rasmussen - the *Vice President* of Shipping for Fuchi Orbital Dynamics.
Derision drips from his tongue as he addresses the woman in a slow, loud voice.
I am one of the highest ranking executives in this city, and *this* keycard gives me access to *those* elevators. I don't *care* what that explosion was - I'm leaving this floor.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_61.t: The executive twist his torso, turning to face you. His mouth is curled into a sneer.
Can you believe this? The nerve of these people!

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_64.t: A single security guard stands in front of the elevators. It looks like a mob has formed in front of her, lead by the Fuchi executive that you met earlier. The two stare daggers at each other. The exec acknowledges your approach with a curt nod.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_66.t: He rolls his eyes, huffing imperiously.
*Yes?* What on Earth do you want from me?
He steps away from the elevator guard. She turns away, muttering into her commlink.
I don't know you, do I? I don't believe that we've ever even spoken before.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_67.t: He points at the elevator guard. She wearily returns her attention to him.
*She,* on the other hand, is giving me a level of grief far beyond her station.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_69.t: After all, I can ensure that she's demoted once I return to Denmark.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_78.t: Jens flings his wrists into the air.
*Finally!* What a mess this has been. I'll make certain that your superiors hear about this! Now, excuse me... I have a meeting to attend.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_83.t: Rasmussen throws a smug look in your direction.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_84.t: The exec waves his hand dismissively.
At this point, you could be a drug-addled arsonist and I wouldn't care, just so long as I can *leave* this place.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_85.t: Jens looks back at the guard. Straightens his tie.
Now, my new friend and I have business to attend to. *Step aside.*

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_90.t: He nods at the security guard.
You should be thankful that you're among *friends.*

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_93.t: Jens flings his wrists into the air.
*Finally!* What a mess this has been. Now get out of my way!

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_95.t: The exec makes a show of checking his designer wristwatch. He sighs, drawing out the noise.
Can we get *on* with this? I have a meeting in thirty-five minutes. If I miss it, I'll make sure that *you* miss all your marks on your next performance report.

5583852530303198170031ca_6.t: I was informed that you're the best person to contact regarding the passcodes for the magical research center.

5595ddb430303184110076b4_1.t: Glowing bluish-white letters crystallize above the tangled network.

Document Cluster A201-WCP-003-Secure.11/Final WALLED CITY LOW INCOME HOUSING PROJECT FINANCIAL RECORDS AND REPORTING - FINAL _______________________________________ MATERIAL CLASS 11 - GRANTED ACCESS ONLY

5595ddb430303184110076b4_4.t: Most is a dense description of cost targets, metrics, financial analysis, and accounting methods. However, a short section of the final paragraph stands out.

...achieved 35% diversion of funds, while maintaining workmanship at generally acceptable levels. Severe downward cost pressures, large finance risk, and information control presented major challenges in executing a financial strategy for the WCLIHP.

5595ddb430303184110076b4_12.t: This is an enormous scrolling list of Walled City construction expenses. Next to each is a single word.

5595ddb430303184110076b4_14.t: An organizational hierarchy forms beside the system chart, listing upper management of the utilities component. At the top, you see a familiar name.
Edward Tsang, Vice President of Special Projects.

5595ddb430303184110076b4_15.t: Directly beneath him are two subordinates.
Cameron Yu, Construction Engineer, Excavation and Utilities.
Under Cameron Yu, a full organizational chart fans out dozens of names and positions.

5595ddb430303184110076b4_16.t: The name of the second subordinate is missing. In its place is a single word.
Under the second subordinate, a much larger organizational chart appears, with hundreds of nodes. In this second chart, however, the same word accompanies every node.

5595ddb430303184110076b4_17.t: Thin threads of yellow light spring from numerous nodes in the huge system chart and begin flowing through others. The yellow threads snake down below, bouncing through a series of nodes and changing colors, until they converge into a single brilliant point. An incandescent white word appears beside it.

55ce278b643661e81700140a_2.t: I was informed that you're the best person to contact regarding the passcodes for the magical research center.

Dear Ms. Lin-Jiao, Allow me to rephrase my earlier request: you send me the passcodes, and I'll review the magical research center's finances.

55ce278b643661e81700140a_8.t: >>RE: Datastore Passcodes Request >>*Vice President of Magical Research* >>to: Shan Lee
Attached are the passcodes for my department's core system terminal. You will access the system only under my direct supervision.

54b99b13306364e41b000280_0.t: The security guard stands in front of the patio, a bored expression on his face. He looks you up and down as you approach.

54b99b13306364e41b000280_1.t: His eyes land on Gaichû, and his face goes white. He recoils on horror.
What the hell?! Ghoul! Ghoul in the lounge!

54b99b13306364e41b000280_3.t: The guard stares at you, his mouth agape.
What the hell do you think you're doing, bringing a ghoul in here?!

54b99b13306364e41b000280_4.t: He leans in to inspect Gaichû.

54b99b13306364e41b000280_8.t: The guard looks at you expectantly.

54b99b13306364e41b000280_10.t: Hey, that sounds really good! You know, a lot of show business-types come around these parts. I'll do what I can to shop it around! "Ghoul School." It has a great ring to it!

54b99b13306364e41b000280_12.t: Well, I've kept you long enough. You head on in to the party!
He extends his hand for Gaichû to shake.
It was a real pleasure meeting you, sir.

54b99b13306364e41b000280_18.t: Hey, that sounds really good! You know, a lot of show business-types come around these parts. I'll do what I can to shop it around! "Chop-Chop Kitchen." It has a great ring to it!

54b99b13306364e41b000280_20.t: Hey, that sounds really good! You know, a lot of show business-types come around these parts. I'll do what I can to shop it around! "ShadowFORCE." It has a great ring to it!

54b99b13306364e41b000280_22.t: I won't call the police, I promise! I won't call anybody.
The guard's eyes dart from side to side. A bead of sweat traces its way down his cheek.
Go on in, be my guest! This job isn't worth getting turned into a monster for.

54b99b13306364e41b000280_23.t: The security guard stands in front of the patio, a bored expression on his face. He looks you up and down as you approach.

54b99b13306364e41b000280_25.t: He takes the invitation and scrutinizes it. He then looks at you, reluctance plain on his face.

54b99b13306364e41b000280_26.t: The security guard pauses. He leans forward and scans your face, his expression uncertain. After a moment, he steps back.
Yeah... Yeah, I remember you. Talking to Kevin Chiu. Terribly sorry. Won't happen again.

54eb9f4a3430617007000f3f_0.t: Stepping into the anterior hallway of the Shangri-La, you're confronted by a massive wall of troll - the restaurant's front door security. He speaks with surprising elocution.
Welcome to the Shangri-La. We strive to provide our patrons with an exquisite dining experience. If you have any questions, or require special accommodations, don't hesitate to speak with our staff.

54eb9f4a3430617007000f3f_2.t: He looks you up and down. Frowns.
Please keep in mind that any disturbances will be promptly deescalated by our security. We reserve the right to bar service to unruly patrons. Thank you, and enjoy your meal.

54eb9f4a3430617007000f3f_3.t: With a puff of his chest and shake of his shoulders, the troll assumes a more aggressive posture. His face remains a mask of neutrality despite the obvious irritation in his voice.
Wuxing prides itself on having world-class security personnel. You'd do well to remember that the best kind of security is often hidden from sight.

54eb9f4a3430617007000f3f_8.t: The troll ignores your threat, unphased, his eyes gliding across the faces of newly-arrived guests.
Believe me, you'd find the consequences of such an outburst most…unsavory. Behave yourself, and you'll get to indulge in the fine cuisine here at Shangri-La. Good evening.

54f1208b3430615c05005793_0.t: A guard steps forward and raises his hand.

54f1208b3430615c05005793_4.t: A corner of the guard's mouth cracks into a weak smile.
Sorry, but not even the mighty "please" and "thank you" will help you here.

54f1208b3430615c05005793_5.t: The guard lazily looks you over.
We have a hyper-responsive security team on duty. You could try, but you won't get far.

54f1208b3430615c05005793_7.t: Security's got the area closed down to contain a situation on the docks. No gawking allowed. This is the Wuxing Employee Patio, reserved for Wuxing customers and their guests. General public's not admitted.
He widens his stance to emphasize his authority.

54fe12ff343061c813005052_0.t: The guard watches you with suspicion as you approach.
You seem confused. Something I can help you with?

54fe12ff343061c813005052_1.t: Thank you for informing us. We'll file a maintenance request right away.

54fe12ff343061c813005052_2.t: She looks around nervously, then leans in.
Yes... Yes, you're right. Let's lower our voice, hm? No need to drag the captain into this.

54fe12ff343061c813005052_3.t: We can handle this quickly if you tell the maintenance worker. I'll let you in this once, but you can't tell anyone. Got it?

54fe12ff343061c813005052_4.t: The guard watches you with suspicion as you approach.
You seem confused. Something I can help you with?

54fe12ff343061c813005052_7.t: This doesn't give you access to our maintenance facilities. If you're with another Wuxing maintenance crew, your ID won't work here. Only in your jurisdiction. I can't let you through.

54fe12ff343061c813005052_8.t: It's odd that someone from research wants in, but it says you're cleared. Here, let me get the door for you.

54fe12ff343061c813005052_9.t: Take your time. I'll be here.

54fe12ff343061c813005052_11.t: The guard hastily scans your face.
I... I don't... please forgive me. Just let me see your ID, and I'll let you through.

54fe12ff343061c813005052_12.t: It's odd that someone from research wants in, but it says you're cleared. Here, let me get the door for you.

563a46753063643417003e71_1.t: The exasperated woman grabs her forehead in frustration.
You're telling me that I'm *trapped* on this floor, but you can't say *why*?

563a46753063643417003e71_2.t: She jabs a finger into the guard's armored chest.
You can't keep me here. I have a job, and I mean to keep it!

563a46753063643417003e71_4.t: It doesn't *have* to be personal to ruin my day! If I don't make my next shift...
She covers her mouth with her hands and exhales slowly.
My entire *career* is in jeopardy, don't you understand that?!

563a46753063643417003e71_6.t: The woman ducks to the side of the shotgun and whirls around. Her eyes land on Gaichu. She blinks. The fear and confusion drain from her face, and she lifts a hand to smack the guard's shoulder.

563a46753063643417003e71_8.t: I mean that he's not a ghoul! He's an *actor!*
The woman motions to Gaichu with an open hand.

Do you live under a rock, or something? The media trades have been buzzing about this upcoming show, "Ghoul School." It's supposed to have *incredible makeup,* and the character they keep describing looks just like this guy!

563a46753063643417003e71_9.t: According to the feeds, the character he's playing is a ghoul gym teacher. He eats the unruly children, and teaches the rest to love themselves. And he sings!
She gives Gaichu a playful wink.

563a46753063643417003e71_13.t: I mean that he's not a ghoul! He's an *actor!*
The woman motions to Gaichu with an open hand.

Do you live under a rock, or something? The media trades have been buzzing with news about this upcoming show, "Chop Chop Kitchen." It's supposed to have *incredible makeup,* and the character they keep describing looks just like this guy!

563a46753063643417003e71_14.t: According to the feeds, it's a competitive cooking show with a ghoul master chef who eats the losers!
She gives Gaichu a playful wink.

563a46753063643417003e71_17.t: I mean that he's not a ghoul! He's an *actor!*
The woman motions to Gaichu with an open hand.

Do you live under a rock, or something? The media trades have been buzzing about this upcoming show, "Shadow Force." It's supposed to have *incredible makeup,* and the character they keep describing looks just like this guy!

563a46753063643417003e71_18.t: According to the feeds, it's about a group of shadowrunners who travel through time to combat an unknown evil that threatens to destroy all of humanity.
She gives Gaichu a playful wink.
Isn't that right, Defender of the Earth?

563a46753063643417003e71_22.t: It doesn't *have* to be personal to ruin my day! If I don't make my next shift...
She covers her mouth with her hands and exhales slowly.
My entire *career* is in jeopardy, don't you understand that?!

563a46753063643417003e71_23.t: She turns to the nearest onlooker - you.
Can you believe this? We're stuck here! And this man refuses to let anyone leave. Something about needing permission because of an accident, or something. He won't even tell us any more than that!

563a46753063643417003e71_28.t: He *still* won't let me go to work. I've missed nearly half of my shift... I'm going to be the laughingstock of my department! If I lose my job because of you, I... I swear I'll...
Her temper gives way to a restrained sob. She catches herself, and buries it beneath an angry frown.

563a46753063643417003e71_41.t: The irate woman scoffs at you, tossing her head at the guard.
You think I haven't asked him that ten times already? He's a rock.

5525b2503063644415002055_0.t: This ork is busily snacking on a steamed bun. As you approach, he wipes off one of his hands and sticks it out toward you.
Hey, stranger! Nice to see you. Zippy Toetag at your service. How are you liking Whampoa Garden?

5525b2503063644415002055_1.t: Nice to meet you too. Listen, I know it's a little forward of me to just say hello and whatnot, but I'm interested in giving you a hand if I can.
Zippy gestures expansively at the squalid, neon-lit streets of Whampoa Garden.
This is my home - at least for the time being - and I'd like to stop these killings.

5525b2503063644415002055_3.t: So since I don't know you, and I can smell a shadowrunner a mile away, you've got to be the outsider they asked to stop the killings, right?
Zippy tilts his head back, obviously pleased at his amateur deduction.

5525b2503063644415002055_4.t: See? I have good eyes. A lot of good eyes, actually - if you're in the market for replacements. Only slightly used, and they only come from certified donors, I swear!
A hearty belly laugh erupts from the ork, and he slaps one of his thighs in elation.

5525b2503063644415002055_7.t: I did my residency back in the UCAS. Coulda become a real M.D., too, if things hadn't gone south for unrelated reasons.
Zippy opens his coat, revealing a cyberdeck. He pats it proudly.
I also deck a little, but I'm better at slicing skin than IC. Whampoa Garden seemed a good fit for me.

5525b2503063644415002055_10.t: I've lived a lot of places, done my fair share of shadowrunning. But this place is special. It's been a long time since I really felt like anywhere was a real home.
Zippy looks off past you, toward the Whampoa Garden streets. His expression grows wistful, like he's remembering something from a long time ago.
I'm sure I'll move again, but not for a while. I'm not done with this city or these people.

5525b2503063644415002055_14.t: I know. I always wanted to be a stand-up comedian, but all my jokes are terrible.

5525b2503063644415002055_15.t: Zippy holds up his hands, still chuckling.
Okay, okay, I get the message! No more bad jokes. I know I'm no comedian.

5525b2503063644415002055_18.t: There is a sharp intake of breath as Zippy shakes his head.
Pretty gruesome business. Gan died from a broken neck. Looked like someone had wrenched it around, and his arms and legs were cut off. Some skin flayed away, too.

5525b2503063644415002055_21.t: Damn. I liked Tong. The BTL business is unsavory, but the man had to eat. And his regular sims were great. Generally all-around nice guy. Friendly with everyone, never had anyone mad at him. There ain't no justice, let me tell ya.

5525b2503063644415002055_22.t: Well, dollars to donuts it's the same story. Teeth, claws, the works.
It's all so insane. Tong never hurt anybody.

5525b2503063644415002055_24.t: Well, they're an eclectic bunch, that's for sure. Where to start...

5525b2503063644415002055_26.t: Ip's the muscle, and has an encyclopedic knowledge of cyber and bioware. Definitely a good guy to have watching your back. Not too friendly, but you know how it is - you get a lot of cyber, people start wondering if you'll tear their arms off.
Zippy makes a chopping gesture with one hand.
He's got moves straight out of Blood Carnival 3: The Reckoning. Terrible movie, but great fight choreography!

5525b2503063644415002055_28.t: You already know about Tong. Ran sims, BTLs, skillchips. Gan used to be a city planner before he had a nervous breakdown and got involved in statistical analysis.
Zippy pauses to collect his thoughts.
Nakamura came from Fukuoka, and was interested in entertainment - trid mostly. Spent a lot of time analyzing subliminals in ads.

5525b2503063644415002055_30.t: Maybe a month ago, she just up and disappeared. I went to her shop one day, and she just wasn't open. Nobody's seen her since.

5525b2503063644415002055_57.t: Zippy turns the necklace over in his fingertips.
Yeah, that's hers all right. Where did you find it?

5525b2503063644415002055_58.t: Zippy takes a long, slow breath and exhales it loudly.

5525b2503063644415002055_59.t: Shaking his head, Zippy crosses his arms.
I guess she didn't disappear after all - at least not in the way I thought. If that's where you found it, she's got to be dead. So why would the killer not make a mess, like he did with the others?

5525b2503063644415002055_62.t: Zippy recoils, shuddering against the rain.
What kind of blade? Was it a knife? A sword? Maybe an axe?

5525b2503063644415002055_63.t: I don't like that at all.
This whole affair's got me on edge. People dying is nothing new, but the flayed skin... Ugh.

5525b2503063644415002055_64.t: After a moment, Zippy composes himself and looks back to you.

It sounds possible that whoever's been killing the elders is trying to muddy the waters with all of the blood and gore business. This is the crime of an intelligent, careful killer. Be careful. This investigation might take you somewhere very unpleasant.

56097ecf643661801100391d_1.t: Aah, you're here, very good. I am Senior Inspector Andy Lam, one of the leaders of this task force. Thank you for coming so promptly.
He extends a hand for you to shake.

56097ecf643661801100391d_3.t: He offers her a slight bow.
My apologies, Senior Inspector Qiu. And as for you... you must be wondering what you and your team are doing here.

56097ecf643661801100391d_9.t: From the device's tiny screen, a familiar face leers back at you. Lam holds it to your face for a few seconds, then pulls the device away.

56097ecf643661801100391d_10.t: You have history with this woman.
He phrases it as a statement, not a question.

56097ecf643661801100391d_18.t: Now. We understand that the prospect of working with a police task force could feel... foreign... to you. And I fully expect that you have questions you'd like to ask.
If there's anything pressing that you'd like to know, I'll do my best to answer.

56097ecf643661801100391d_27.t: He pauses for a moment before continuing.
...And in the interest of full disclosure, we are both technically Mitsuhama employees.

5609dc5c643661e00e007ff7_9.t: Lam acquiesces with a nod of his head.

She's right. What we need is tangible evidence of Krait's wrongdoing. Financial records, written communications, video feeds... that sort of thing. We know where that proof is being held, but we can't get to it without tipping our hand. That's where you come in.

5609dc5c643661e00e007ff7_47.t: Going in quiet is safer, but... go ahead, if it comes to that. Do what you have to do.

5609dc5c643661e00e007ff7_52.t: He shifts his gaze to you.

When you get to the Admin level, you'll be stepping into hostile territory. Krait and her people have more or less taken over the whole floor, and they won't think twice about putting a bullet in your head if they catch you there.

5609dc5c643661e00e007ff7_70.t: We'll forward the details to your mission computer. For now, let's keep moving forward... we've got more important things to do than go around in circles on this.

5609dc5c643661e00e007ff7_86.t: Nobody's threatening you. Senior Inspector Qiu was just being careful. As long as you don't try to screw us over, you've got nothing to worry about.

5609dc5c643661e00e007ff7_89.t: He throws Qiu a sidelong glance.
You wanna get us stared, Fiona?

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_1.t: She fires off a rapid-fire reproach in a thick Filipino accent.
Why are you staring like that? Didn't anybody teach you manners?
She plants her fists on her leather-clad hips and juts her chin out at you.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_2.t: She blinks, and her jaw unclenches.
It's all right. I can tell you're a newcomer. I just feel a little protective of my part of town.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_3.t: The ork looks you up and down. Finally, with a gruff nod, she introduces herself.

I'm Rosario Ignacio... or, to the members of my community, Cherry Pink. I specialize in transport missions and nanny services, and I occasionally shadowrun for walking-around money. I can also fry up a mean batch of lumpia, but you can't have any unless I like you.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_5.t: It's a bona fide Filipino nickname. Unless you want a mouth full of fist, you'll show it respect.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_7.t: She puts her hands on her hips.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_8.t: Her nose wrinkles, and she crosses herself.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_9.t: A corner of her mouth twitches upward. She gives you a sidelong glance.
Then you must earn it. Everybody wants to be my friend because everybody wants lumpia. Only a handful deserve it.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_10.t: Look around you - see these shadowrunners and dealers of contraband and Johnsons? There's a fair number who have kids, though they wouldn't shout it from the rooftops. It's usually too dangerous to keep family close. That's where I come in.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_11.t: The ork raises her chin proudly.

I've shuttled kids in and out of war zones. I've gone into hiding with little ones for months on end while their parents had to lie low. I've taught children good manners, hygiene, and the proper use of firearms for over twenty years.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_13.t: I show 'em how to use weapons *safely.*
She jabs a finger at you for emphasis.
What's dangerous is not explaining their world to them - the shadows, that is. These kids have to grow up fast. Weapons training is appropriate when you're living *in a smugglers' den.*

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_15.t: The ork crosses her arms tightly across her chest.
My charges always survive. End of story.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_16.t: Her features soften. The corners of her mouth twitch downward.
It's not a perfect world. But I do my best.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_17.t: She looks at you with pity.
You never had a good nanny. I can see it in your eyes....I can smell it, too.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_18.t: I *usually* nanny children, but you're a prime candidate for some remedial lessons in cleanliness. No offense.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_20.t: You lack a sense of humor.
The ork shakes her head and mutters something about shadowrunners nowadays.
Run along, now. I have no time to talk to humorless people.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_21.t: She juts her jaw out again.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_22.t: The ork raises her chin proudly.

Plenty. People will never stop having kids. The more dangerous the world gets, the more my services are necessary. I've taught children good manners, hygiene, and the proper use of firearms for over twenty years.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_23.t: I wish that there were more. If more people did the things that I do, fewer kids would suffer.
The ork raises her chin proudly.
I've taught children good manners, hygiene, and the proper use of firearms for over twenty years.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_24.t: Well, I used to take care of the children of a Wuxing bigwig, up in the Peak. Turned out she was siphoning corporate funds to support both a gambling addiction and an extravagant lifestyle.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_32.t: The country's caught up in a struggle between the Japanese and Huk resistance - nationalists and neo-anarchists backed by the dragon Masaru. I couldn't just waltz in and look for family. Besides, they could be barricaded in caves avoiding JIS forces, for all I know.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_33.t: Cherry Pink's nostrils flare, and she furrows her brow.
Well, no relatives have come looking for me, either. Besides, I've built my own family here in Hong Kong.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_35.t: Well, I haven't, personally. I just want to be *home* somewhere.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_36.t: I agree. Perhaps you may deserve lumpia after all. No promises, though.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_37.t: She snorts and lifts her chin high.

Filipinos are a resilient people. I think the Huk will triumph eventually. And if *I've* got a Filipino community around me, I'm good. Thanks for talking about it, anyway. Not many people care enough to bother.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_43.t: She shakes her head and mutters something about shadowrunners nowadays.
I just want to make friendly conversation, and people only ever want lumpia. It's shameful. No, you cannot have lumpia. You may only desire it from afar.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_46.t: She looks away suddenly, her eyes moistening.
I'm not sentimental about my old post, if that's what you mean.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_47.t: The ork takes a big breath and continues.

But on Sundays, my only day off, I used to go and sit with the other nannies and housekeepers. We'd bring pieces of cardboard so we could sit on the ground in the shade under bridges. We'd gossip. Eat lumpia and rice cake. Nothing special.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_51.t: She gives you an unwavering sidelong gaze.
You're a stranger on my turf. I expect people to be civil to one another.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_52.t: You're not too good at making friends or fitting in to new surroundings, are you?

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_53.t: Yes I do. A mouthy stranger.
She crosses her arms, widens her stance, and glares.
This is my neighborhood. I've got the right to check out any newcomers.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_54.t: I guess I came on a little strong. Sorry. There are so many new faces around that I get defensive.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_55.t: The ork raises her eyebrows at you and snorts.
I've been here a lot longer than you. Anyway, if you haven't noticed, this is a city of immigrants. Hong Kong belongs to us as much as to anyone else. It's my home.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_56.t: The chromed-up ork juts out her jaw.
Back again, eh? Will you be polite this time, I wonder? Or do you want another taste of my scorn?

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_57.t: What my "deal" is. That's what you want to know.
She rests her massive hands on her hips.
All right. I can tell you're a newcomer. I didn't mean to be standoffish before, I just feel a little protective of my part of town.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_58.t: Well... you're a stranger to me.
She rests her massive hands on her hips.
All right. I can tell you're a newcomer. I didn't mean to be standoffish before, I just feel a little protective of my part of town.

55924258303035cc1d003f3c_60.t: It's so hot out... so humid!
She fans herself with a cybernetic hand.
If you're back to talk, go on and talk. I'm listening.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_2.t: This game isn't over yet, you old fart! You just watch, I'll make a comeback so beautiful that it'll make you *weep.*
Gin stomps his foot for emphasis - or perhaps out of frustration.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_10.t: Gin reaches over the game board and whacks him.
God damn it, you badger! You really had me going there... I'd thought that we were done with those accursed dreams!

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_13.t: I said nothing of the sort! You're just old and deaf. Misheard me, that's all.
The old man blushes. A strange look for him. He seems to realize his reaction and coughs to cover it up, but the flush lingers.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_15.t: If by "dreams" you mean "exquisite visions of buxom geriatric women," then absolutely.
But beyond that, no. The only nightmare I've had recently was about my hair falling out.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_17.t: Gin scowls at Shyu, then turns his head toward Lau.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_20.t: Oh, come, now. Don't storm off in a huff.
Our conversation was only just getting started.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_21.t: Shyu and Gin exchange a look. They glance between you and Lau before Gin finally leans forward and speaks to you in a low voice.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_26.t: Gin's upper lip curls in annoyance as he watches them. He flicks his wrist dismissively.
Eeyaah, you can both kick it, for all I care.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_33.t: The edge in Gin's voice is gone.

We're lucky that our friend still joins us for Go. He could be out climbing mountains, or building his empire, or whatever you kids do these days. Instead, contents himself with providing us geezers his good company.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_35.t: I always knew that you were a cheat! At least Shyu and I can enjoy it when you're harassed by waiters at the Pancake Hut.
Gin smiles wickedly and looks at you.
Has to convince them he's eligible for the senior buffet every time.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_45.t: The other elder jabs a finger toward the first.
No, Shyu! We are going to *finish* this. And we can't blame those dreams for our endless game now that they're gone. No excuses, no interruptions!

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_53.t: The long-faced man gives you a cursory look, and continues to collect black stones.
Just a gawker, Shyu. It's your turn.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_55.t: Gin sighs, shoulders slumping forward. After a moment of scowling, he reluctantly speaks.
The two elders turn toward the younger man.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_59.t: Gin shakes his head and crosses his arms.

What makes you think we'd be comfortable sharing our dreams with a complete stranger? Seems like a good way to get ourselves in trouble... 'specially with what you kids can do with that Matrix you all love so much.
He turns his head. Pretends to spit.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_60.t: You can't come in here demanding this and that of us! Show some respect to your elders, young man. Just what makes you think we'd be comfortable telling a stranger about our dreams, anyway?

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_61.t: Gin leans in. Stares at you.
Can't say I'm comfortable with giving a stranger details about my dreams.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_69.t: Gin broods in his corner, arms crossed and jaw set.
I've changed my mind. I don't want to share my dream.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_71.t: Gin sticks out his lower lip.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_72.t: He holds his palm up toward you and looks at Shyu.
See? Straight from the horse's mouth! I don't *have* to.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_74.t: Gin's mouth opens and closes like a gasping fish. But as he looks at Lau, his body relaxes, and a sadness seems to take hold of his features.
All right... all right. But don't you go repeating any of this, you hear me?
He takes in a deep breath, and slowly releases it. Folds his hands in his lap.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_76.t: A change comes over him. His eyes brighten, and he continues energetically.

But then... *then* I dreamed of the Walled City. I stood before it, and its door opened to me, and when I passed inside, all of my guilt fell away. It was as though the city had absolved me, had washed away my guilt! And I remember feeling *happy.*

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_77.t: As his remembrance ends, so does his lightened mood. His face shrinks into a pout, and he stares grumpily at the ground.
There, I did it. You happy?

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_80.t: He mutters something about a lazy generation and no appreciation, and then he waves you away.
Let's change the subject. My ulcer's acting up.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_81.t: His jaw drops. He tuns his head to Lau, eyes wide.
Did you *hear* that? Such disrespect! Those sorts of ill manners don't deserve a response.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_88.t: Can we get back to the game now?

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_95.t: Gin glances up at you, then returns to the game.
Bah. Can't you see we're busy?

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_101.t: Gin tosses his arms into the air, nearly knocking over the playing board.
Damnit, Shyu! When you start a move, you *commit to it.* I swear, if you're doing this on purpose...

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_107.t: They've gotten a hell of a lot worse.
Look, let's not go into detail.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_108.t: Alright. But afterward, you have to let us finish our game.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_109.t: He nods approvingly, then glances at Lau and Shyu before continuing.
Our dreams have become, ahh... unpleasant, so to say. Darker. Violent.
He runs his hand along his tightly-cropped beard.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_111.t: You can see him shiver as he thinks about it.
Well, I said no details. So no details. That about sum it up, Shyu? Master Lau?

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_118.t: I'll hold you to it all the same.
He glances at Lau and Shyu before continuing.
Our dreams have become, ahh... unpleasant, so to say. Darker. Violent.
Runs his hand along his tightly-cropped beard.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_119.t: I can see right through that ennui of yours, young man. Trying to appear "cool" and "hip" - well, you don't need to play it up here. We're already unimpressed.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_121.t: He gives Lau a sideways look.
The deal is: I give you information, and you take yourself and that hipster charade of yours back to your friends.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_123.t: I don't make deals with terrorists! You best plunk your butt down, because it's that sort of attitude that'll get you a heaping spoonful of nothing from your seniors.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_126.t: Look who's back. Why don't you go egg a car or something and leave us alone?

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_131.t: Gin rubs his temples in an exaggerated display.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_136.t: A short laugh, like a single punch to the gut, bursts out of Gin.
There's no way this whelp has that sort of grace.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_146.t: I feel it as well, Master Lau. Different somehow.
Gin smashes his fist onto the Go board, disturbing the tiny stones.
That slum should've been bulldozed years ago!

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_153.t: Gin glances up at you, then returns to the game.
Bah. Can't you see we're busy?

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_155.t: Look who's back. Why don't you go egg a car or something and leave us alone?

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_159.t: Sooner rather than later. We're not aging in reverse, you know.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_1.t: Ahh, it's a monster! Kill it!
His hand immediately goes to the pistol on his hip, and he starts to rise to his feet.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_2.t: The sergeant pauses, pistol halfway drawn. His voice is a dubious monotone.
What the hell are you talking about?

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_4.t: I... I suppose that they can't, actually.
The HKPF sergeant hesitates, and slowly sinks back into his seat.
Next time, I'd advise you to think twice about waltzing into a police station looking like that.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_7.t: The HKPF sergeant hesitates, and slowly sinks back into his seat.
I... well... that doesn't sound like a *crime* as much as it does a civil matter. Not the sort of thing that I can help you with, anyway.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_8.t: The sergeant pauses, pistol halfway drawn. His voice is a dubious monotone.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_12.t: The desk sergeant glances between the two of you, and then shrugs.
That's pretty clever, I gotta say.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_14.t: What can I do for you, mister, uh...
His gaze shifts to the screen of his PDA. He reads the name that his scan pulled off of your badge.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_16.t: 'Fraid not. Shift change. Sheng's on a smoke break. So, you want something, or what?

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_17.t: And, uh... who was it that was supposed to meet you up there?

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_20.t: Oh. One of the lucky ones, then.
I thought we'd already released all of them...

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_26.t: The desk sergeant glowers at you. He opens his mouth to say something, thinks better of it, and shakes his head.
Just a minute. But in the future, I'd advise you to watch your tone, and to remember where you are.
He turns to his terminal, scowling.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_29.t: Good on you. You'll need to head up a level for that.
He sets his jaw and gives you an appreciative nod.
Y'know, some of the sadsacks here at the station have a problem with bounty hunters. "Scum that prey on scum," they call you. But me, I love you guys. You doing what you do makes our job easier.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_33.t: The desk sergeant crosses his arms over his chest.
I heard you the first time.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_35.t: If you two are finished, I'd like you to answer my question. *Why* do you want to talk to Mia?
What's the nature of your business on that level?

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_41.t: Uh... huh. Someone told you that, huh?
He turns to a terminal set into the desk at his side, and you hear the rattle of keystrokes. The display is tilted away from you - you can't see what's on the screen.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_43.t: Really. Okay, let's check the logs and see who screened you at the door. That should give us the name we're looking for.

...Says here that Zaho let you in, just about fifteen minutes ago. That sound right?

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_45.t: Zaho isn't here today. He's home sick, unloading his guts into a pit toilet.
His mouth twists into an ugly smile.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_46.t: His nose wrinkles. He looks from you, to the screen, back to you.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_47.t: The "man" who let you in here was Mok.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_48.t: *Mui* let you in here? And he said to meet you upstairs?

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_50.t: He lurches to his feet, his hand moving to his weapon. At the same time, he half-turns to shout a warning to the constables in the room.
