562fbdf96436617803006bc6_53.t: Tell you what. You stay right where you are, and I'll get whoever's working the front desk over here to clear this up.
We'll get this figured out in no time.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_54.t: He stares at you for a long moment, then turns back to his puzzle, shaking his head.
Go on, then. And don't bother me again.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_55.t: P.C. Cheung was wrong. There's nothing for you in admin. I'd suggest that you take your troubles back to security, before I call them here to escort you.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_57.t: So Mia told you that Mia could help you if you met her upstairs.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_59.t: No, hang on a second. You said that you were "supposed" to go to the top floor.
He turns to a terminal set into the desk at his side, and you hear the rattle of keystrokes. The display is tilted away from you - you can't see what's on the screen.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_60.t: No, hang on a second. You said that you were "supposed" to go to the top floor.
He turns to a terminal set into the desk at his side, and you hear the rattle of keystrokes. The display is tilted away from you - you can't see what's on the screen.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_61.t: You just asked me one, didn't you?
Look, this isn't a help desk. But you've already pulled me away from what I was doing, so go ahead and ask what you want.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_63.t: The only suspicious behavior I've seen around this station is what I'm seeing from you right now.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_64.t: The desk sergeant's cheeks begin to redden.
Who do you think you are, questioning a cop about what happens at the station?

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_65.t: The anger on his face drains away. Wariness replaces it.
A journalist. In here. Give me the name of the constable that let you in here. Do it right now.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_67.t: The anger that's been building in the desk sergeant comes boiling over. He slams palms down on the the desk in front of him.
Okay, smartass. I want the name of the constable who let you in here, and I want it *now!*

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_69.t: His frown deepens, and he jots something down on a sticky note.
P.C. Cheung made a mistake. I'm gonna have words with her about that. Now I'd suggest that you take your troubles back to security, before I call them here to escort you.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_72.t: Make it quick, and make it good.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_73.t: Who let you into this station, smartass? What was the name of the constable who let you through that door?

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_74.t: He stares at you, then shifts his gaze to the puzzle. Slowly, he reaches for his pencil and marks in the letters.
Thanks. Will there be anything else?

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_75.t: Really. Okay, let's check the logs and see who screened you at the door. That should give us the name we're looking for.
He turns to a terminal set into the desk at his side, and you hear the rattle of keystrokes. The display is tilted away from you - you can't see what's on the screen.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_76.t: ...Says here that Zaho let you in, just about fifteen minutes ago.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_77.t: He looks away, shaking his head slightly.
Lots of busy people here, you shouldn't be bothering 'em unless you've got a good reason. Go on, be about your business.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_78.t: What can I do for you, miss, uh...
His gaze shifts to the screen of his PDA. He reads the name that his scan pulled off of your badge.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_79.t: The desk sergeant frowns up from his puzzle.
Weber. I told you, I'm busy. What do you want now?

54c2c61f30636498210021b1_1.t: Pleased ta meetcha. Cheng was kind enough to arrange this little sit-down between us.
He extends a slablike hand for you to shake.
You can call me Dr. Shenyang.

54c2c61f30636498210021b1_15.t: Are you kidding? Underhanded is when I frame you for indecent acts on your last trip to Cambodia. Blackmail’s just business.

54c2c61f30636498210021b1_18.t: Good. I can't have Ma knowin' that I'm after him. In my business, everybody's got dirty tricks, but if you make it public you're using 'em?
Dr. Shenyang draws a finger across his throat.
That's it. My career's as dead as the People's Republic of China. Nobody'll work for or with me ever again. So don't embarrass me, hey?

54c2c61f30636498210021b1_23.t: He looks up at you, eagerness in his eyes.
So what, we got a deal? You gonna do it?

54c2c61f30636498210021b1_24.t: Good man! That's what I like to hear! When you're done, drop Cheng a line - I'll come meet you back here and I'll hand over the money.

54c2c61f30636498210021b1_29.t: Because it's gauche, and I can't have Ma knowing I'm after him. In my business, everybody's got dirty tricks, but if you make it public you're using 'em?
Dr. Shenyang draws a finger across his throat.
That's it. My career's as dead as the People's Republic of China. Nobody'll work for or with me ever again. So don't embarrass me, hey?

54c2c61f30636498210021b1_30.t: Oh, yeah... Cheng talked you up when I approached her with the job. Given what she told me, this job should be cake.

54c2c61f30636498210021b1_35.t: He offers you a chipped-toothed grin.

A lot. There's this one scene where two trolls gang up on an elf, and they... well, I don't wanna spoil it. But believe me, it is *glorious.* I'll send a copy over to Cheng so that ya can take a look.

54c2c61f30636498210021b1_41.t: What are ya, slow? I got nothin' else for ya until you get the dirt on Ma.
Dr. Shenyang makes a shooing gesture with his hands.
You got a job, now go do it.

54c2c61f30636498210021b1_42.t: Shenyang is busily puffing away on a foul-smelling cigar, his commlink in one hand. His eyes flash with anticipatory glee as he sees you.
Dr. Shenyang greets you in the name of all producers. How'd the shindig go?

54c2c61f30636498210021b1_46.t: Dr. Shenyang is about to speak, but his commlink begins to buzz. Irritated, he answers it and switches to speaker mode.
Yeah, who is it? Whaddya want?

54c2c61f30636498210021b1_48.t: Dr. Shenyang eyes the image of Neville Ma with deep suspicion.
You don't sound too mad, Nev. What's the catch here, anyway? You must have the next big thing lined up already...

54c2c61f30636498210021b1_53.t: As long as you chase stars like Wong, you will lose. Don't be afraid to think bigger, Doctor. Reach for the drama, not the dramatists.
With that, Neville Ma hangs up on Dr. Shenyang.

54c2c61f30636498210021b1_54.t: For the next thirty seconds, Dr. Shenyang releases a stream of violently imaginative invectives at his phone. Even Strangler Bao looks taken aback at the ferocity of Shenyang's anger and the inventiveness of the sexual positions that he describes. Finally, he takes a deep breath and composes himself.
That dirty little weasel... Insulting my creativity like that! The nerve.

54c2c61f30636498210021b1_60.t: Dr. Shenyang is tapping away at his commlink. He peers up over the edge of it as you approach, not bothering to lower it or look fully at you.
What, you want a medal or somethin'? I got work to do, kid. If I have any other jobs, I'll let ya know. Now let me get back to this deal, all right?

5600340a626632e015004432_1.t: You find the constable with his face buried in his PDA. He raises a single finger at the sound of your approach.
Gimmie a moment - just one last note.
His hand flies back to the device, tapping furiously at its tiny keypad. A few moments later, he hits the send button and breathes a sigh of relief.

5600340a626632e015004432_2.t: He gives his neck a quick, tired roll before turning his attention to you.

5600340a626632e015004432_3.t: Any number of people at any given time. My superior officers, security, HQ, my barber - I've got a lot of contacts, and a lot of inter-work information to sort through.
He glances at the PDA in his hand and shrugs.
Plus, I hate leaving unread mail in my inboxes.

5600340a626632e015004432_5.t: His normally stern face cracks to reveal an amused smirk.
Why not both? Either way, I'd still be busy.

5600340a626632e015004432_6.t: His normally stern face cracks to reveal an amused smirk.

5600340a626632e015004432_7.t: Now, are you just here to tease me, or do you actually need something?
He crosses his arms over his chest.

5600340a626632e015004432_11.t: I... Excuse me. I didn't mean to appear, ah... distracted.
He hesitates, nervously scratching his sun-tanned wrist, then continues.
It's just that someone around here is grilling fish, and I worked through my lunch break today. That's all. Really.

5600340a626632e015004432_12.t: He shuffles, straightening his stance, clearly waiting for you to change the subject.

5600340a626632e015004432_15.t: The constable's attention is locked onto his PDA's display. He inhales through his nose, then speaks.
Just because I'm standing here doesn't mean I'm not busy.

5600340a626632e015004432_16.t: He shrugs, his tattoos moving with the rise and fall of his shoulders.

5600340a626632e015004432_19.t: Hui fixes you with a hard stare, but almost immediately breaks his gaze when his PDA buzzes. After a series of quick taps on the device, he flips it off and slips it into a back pocket. He nods at you.

5600340a626632e015004432_20.t: He raises a hand to rub his chin.
...I mean. About time I got that right. What brings you to my corner?

5600340a626632e015004432_25.t: He motions to your feet, eyes still on his PDA.

5600340a626632e015004432_27.t: He shrugs, then switches his PDA's display screen off. He looks at you.
Just a little something I picked up on the streets. But I'm sure you're here with other business. Name it.

5600340a626632e015004432_28.t: He motions to your feet, eyes still on his PDA.

5600340a626632e015004432_29.t: If it does, then I must not be very popular. I have to rely on old habits to know when you're around.
He motions to your feet, his eyes still fixed on his PDA.

5600340a626632e015004432_35.t: His lip curls into a half-smile.
Yeah. Seems that way. So... there something I can do for you, runner?

5600340a626632e015004432_49.t: It's fine. It isn't like I was doing much anyway - things are pretty quiet right now.

5600340a626632e015004432_52.t: Never been a big fan of street names. They feel kind of immature, don't they? Like a kid playing make-believe.
You wanna go around pretending to be the Big-Time Criminal, you knock yourself out. To me, you're the name on the form.

5600340a626632e015004432_58.t: Uh, sure... okay. I paint. Oils, mostly landscapes. I've had a few showings in Victoria Harbour coffee shops. Could we change the subject, maybe? This is getting a little too personal for my tastes.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_7.t: Gin and Lau look nervously at him. Shyu continues.
Its was... It was terrible. Even worse than before. I was standing here, and the sun was beginning to set. It was red, like blood, and cast a rippling red reflection off of the water.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_9.t: I broke through the spell and turned back to the game, and... Gin had won!

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_12.t: Shyu looks at Gin in surprise.
I believe that's the first time you've ever called our friend here by their name, Gin. Don't tell me you're growing *fond* of them!

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_19.t: The other two look at him and nod their heads in agreement. Shyu speaks slowly.
We've been on this earth for a great many years now. Some have been good, some bad. But of all the things I'm most thankful for, it's your good company and our games of Go.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_24.t: Shyu can't contain himself. His breaths come out in rasps, and it looks as if he might inhale his beard from the intensity of his laughter.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_28.t: The force of Gin's statement only fuels Shyu's laughter. He speaks between breaths.
I'm sorry, Gin. I am. Sorry you're such an old pooter.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_29.t: His laugh subsides, and Shyu beams at you.
Tell me, how old do you think we are? Gin and I.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_36.t: In that ballpark, yes. But consider this: Master Lau is beyond his seventies.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_37.t: Seems like it, doesn't it? Well, consider this: Master Lau is even older than we are.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_39.t: Let a group of old men keep their secrets. Perhaps, after a dozen years and twice as many Go games, we will tell you. For now, know that he has earned our respect many times over, and leave it at that.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_44.t: One of the players, a round, cheerful man, notices you watching their game.
Why, hello young man. Something you need?
A broad smile brightens his cheeks.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_46.t: Shyu's cheeks flush red, and he looks a little embarrassed.
Please excuse me, but my friend here is being most insistent. Alright, Gin, it's your turn...

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_52.t: Ahh. You got me there, Gin.
He laughs - a pleasant, full sound. As it trails off, he spots you watching the game. A broad smile brightens his cheeks.
Why, hello there, young man. Something you need?

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_54.t: I could use a short break. How about you, Gin?

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_58.t: The three men exchange looks. Shyu turns to you.
What a coincidence. Wasn't but an hour ago that we were all discussing that very thing. We have been having bad dreams. Every one of us.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_62.t: Shyu waves his hand dismissively at Gin.

Don't mind him. He's always been a poor sport. But he raises a good point... some of our dreams contain information best left private - just as yours do, I'm sure. Having just met, how can we be sure that you'll respect our personal information?

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_66.t: There's a moment of silence before Shyu speaks up, the rosiness in his cheeks having drained away.

I, too, saw the long hall. My own experience was different, however. The hall was far off in the distance, and I was looking down on it from a strange angle... as though I were perched on a wall high above.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_67.t: He pauses and looks at you.

You know, young one... yes, you... you look very much like the person I saw walking that hall. They moved steadily down the path, walking at an even pace. And just behind them, a great and terrible shadow followed.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_68.t: Of course, I didn't have the best view up there on that wall, or... wherever I was. It could have been anyone. Perhaps this old mind of mine is seeing your face now, and misremembering the dream.
He lets out a chuckle, and a small flush of red returns to his cheeks. He looks over at Gin.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_83.t: Enough of that, Gin. Our young friend here asked in earnest for our advice. They deserve a proper response.
Shyu gives you a gentle smile.
Please forgive our friend. Bitter years have left him suspicious of others.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_85.t: Suddenly, his cheeks flush. He blinks twice, then looks away.
...But perhaps these are just the ramblings of an old man. Please forgive me.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_87.t: Shyu bows deeply, his eyes to the ground.
Thank you... I can only hope these dreams do lead to something beautiful. There's already enough deception in this world.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_96.t: Shyu shakes his head at his friend.
You never mind him. Is there something else you need?

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_100.t: He strokes his short beard thoughtfully.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_116.t: If Shyu heard Gin, he doesn't show it. He looks at you, and his eyes widen.
The shadows that follow you are getting closer.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_132.t: Shyu's eyes eagerly seek you out, and land on your face with silent relief. He gives you a shy smile.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_135.t: No, no. Nothing like that. It's related to our dreams, actually... Your timing is perfect.
He holds his beard thoughtfully, a mischievous look to him.
Or have you been lurking behind a buoy, waiting for the right moment to pop out and feed the dramatic tension of the moment?

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_137.t: You never know. Seems like something I might have tried in my youth.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_143.t: Gin and Shyu nod grimly to themselves. Shyu picks up the subject.

News of the Walled City often falls on ears deaf to its blights. Very few people from outside the city care about what happens behind its walls. But, as of late, it's become harder to ignore. People are starting to *see* the city.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_147.t: Shyu's voice is sad, but soothing.
You know well as I that such a thing isn't possible. People live there - *tens of thousands* of them. Where would they go?

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_148.t: I feel it, too. The change. But what we're changing into, I don't know. It's just like in my dream... We're sitting on the threshold of something great. Great, and terrible. If you can get to the bottom of this, there'll be more than just us Go players eager to thank you. But, please. Hurry.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_154.t: Shyu shakes his head at his friend.
You never mind him. Is there something else you need?

562fee69643661301100816a_1.t: You've gotta get me out of here. Please, please, *please* get me out of this cell.
He clutches the bars of his cell door, his knuckles whitening as his grip tightens.

562fee69643661301100816a_5.t: I was just making a little money - what's wrong with that?! I'll tell you what - NOTHING.
He pounds the wall of his cell with an open palm.
But the powers that be can't stand free enterprise, so they had their goon squad lock me away.

562fee69643661301100816a_7.t: Yeah - free for *them.* But if the little guy wants to get a taste, well...

...Look at what happens. They have you arrested for "crimes" that they're all guilty of a hundred times over, they seize your business, and they put you away.

562fee69643661301100816a_15.t: You do all that, and you get your money. And I get my freedom.
He flashes you a brilliant smile.

562fee69643661301100816a_16.t: Come on, please - you've gotta help me! If you don't, I'm as good as dead!
He slams the heel of his palm against the door.
I won't last ten minutes where they're sending me!

562fee69643661301100816a_20.t: Oh, come on, you can't be that naive. The Executive Council is pro-business as long as it's *their* business. But if the little guy wants to get a taste, well...
He rattles the bars of the door again.

562fee69643661301100816a_22.t: You're damn right it is. The Executive Council is pro-business as long as it's *their* business. But if the little guy wants to get a taste, well...
He rattles the bars of the door again.

562fee69643661301100816a_23.t: Oh, come on. You know better than that. If they've got me here, they've already made up their minds. I'm going to fucking prison!
I was just making a little money - what's wrong with that?! But the powers that be can't stand free enterprise, so they had their goon squad lock me away.

562fee69643661301100816a_24.t: This... this is all a misunderstanding! That's all it is, a stupid mistake! I didn't do anything wrong!

562fee69643661301100816a_28.t: Why would you... we had a deal!

562fee69643661301100816a_29.t: Go away! GET OUT OF HERE!

562fee69643661301100816a_31.t: He leans forward to inspect the data stick. His mouth curls into a frown.
You opened the evidence bag. I told you not to do that.

562fee69643661301100816a_32.t: If the cops opened that bag, then they might've copied the stick. And if they did that, it won't even matter if you destroy it.

562fee69643661301100816a_38.t: He watches as the stick burns.
...Well, at least they won't be able to use that against me any more. That's something, I guess. Now hold your PDA up to the bars, and I'll wire you the money from my account.

562fee69643661301100816a_41.t: I've never seen you before in my life.

562fee69643661301100816a_44.t: He lets out a heavy sigh, and his shoulders slump. He wipes his forehead with the back of his hand.
Man, what a relief. Thank you so much for doing that. Now hold your PDA up to the bars, and I'll wire you the money from my account.

562fee69643661301100816a_47.t: He slicks back his hair with a spidery hand.

562fee69643661301100816a_57.t: He leans forward to inspect the data stick.
...And the evidence bag is still sealed, too. Just liked I asked you.
A smile begins to creep across his face. He nods eagerly.

562fee69643661301100816a_59.t: We had a deal. A thousand nuyen. You agreed to those terms.

562fee69643661301100816a_65.t: I told you to leave me alone, you prick.
He slams the bars of his cell doors with the palms of his hands.
Get out of here before I call the guards.

55a59bbf306364900e0061a5_0.t: X-Floh nods in your direction as she sees you.
Hey there. Anything else I can do for you?

55a59bbf306364900e0061a5_1.t: A wiry elven woman leans over the bar, propped up on one elblow. She glances back at you over her shoulder as you approach, lifting a half-empty glass to her lips.
The selection here is total crap, but it's cheap. I hope you're not looking for quality, or you're gonna be really disappointed.

55a59bbf306364900e0061a5_2.t: The woman snorts, and dips her pinky into her drink to fish out a stray gnatt.

If you ask real nice, I think they've got some tonic water in the fridge. Aside from that, you're as likely to get drain cleaner as you are a splash of bitters. Even a simple Manhattan's probably beyond the means of this joint.

55a59bbf306364900e0061a5_3.t: Call me X-Floh, by the way.
X-Floh regards you calmly, her eyes calculating. After a few seconds, she nods.
Maybe we can help each other.

55a59bbf306364900e0061a5_5.t: I'm what the eggheads call a 'mystic adept' - kind of a physical adept magician. I throw spells, and I can kill with a touch. I'm rarer than unicorns, my friend.
X-Floh lifts her glass, draining the last of it.

55a59bbf306364900e0061a5_6.t: Suit yourself. But I guarantee you none of 'em have the same set of skills I do. Mages and adepts are rare enough, but how many people d'you know that can combine the two?
Holding up a balled fist, X-Floh grins widely.
Mystic adepts are rarer than unicorns, my friend.

55a59bbf306364900e0061a5_7.t: Hey, that's the spirit! With an attitude like that, you won't get disappointed very often.
She raps a knuckle against her glass.
I figure as long as it gets you drunk, it's good with me.

55a59bbf306364900e0061a5_8.t: X-Floh is crouched over the bar, nursing a glass of something brown and unfettered by niceties such as ice or garnish. As you approach, she lifts a hand in greeting.
Welcome to the party. Hope you're not in the mood for anything fancy. The best you can hope for in here is not to go blind.

55a59bbf306364900e0061a5_10.t: X-Floh stands up, reaching over the bar to pull out an unmarked bottle and a spare glass from the other side. She tops off her own drink, and pours one for you.
Bottoms up. After the stuff we've been doing, I think we could both use it.

55a59bbf306364900e0061a5_12.t: The elf cuts loose with a single, sharp laugh, and slaps a palm down on the bar.
You're a man after my own heart, that's for sure. I can drink to that attitude.

55a59bbf306364900e0061a5_15.t: X-Floh taps the base of her neck with two fingers.
That's because it's chipped. My only concession to cyberwear - when you move around as much as I do, linguasofts pay for themselves pretty quickly. They're not perfect, but they get the job done.

55a59bbf306364900e0061a5_17.t: I was running out of Guangdong for a while, but I was part of a team that lifted some SAM guidance software from Baihu Corporation. One of the team bit it during our escape, and Baihu got their military allies to track down the rest of the team.
X-Floh shrugs, taking a long drink from her glass.

55a59bbf306364900e0061a5_21.t: Running a hand through her hair, X-Floh leans back as she thinks.
Yeah, I got a couple. There was this one time I signed on with some mercs in Eastern Europe. They had a job to do near Krasnodarski Krai, lifting some Abkhazian military software.

55a59bbf306364900e0061a5_22.t: The plan was pretty simple - we'd HALO jump from a drone on the Russian side of the border, hike in toward the Abkhazian research facility, and blast our way in. The Abkhaz forces were supposed to be Matrix hotshots, but crap in ground combat. Simple, right?
The elf shakes her head, eyes rolling.

55a59bbf306364900e0061a5_27.t: X-Floh lifts her glass in salute.
See you. You know how to find me if you need me.

5602ee046266328c18001575_55.t: The mercenary's voice was incongruously soft. Her tone carried that old familiar detachment - X-Floh was a hired gun, here to do the job that she was paid for and nothing else. She wasn't your friend. Never would be. You didn't bring up the whiskey on her breath. Didn't see much point in it as long as she got the job done.

5604a7a9643661740e0013c2_40.t: Of course we're prisoners, they knocked us out and penned us like animals. They've even impounded that ridiculous boat that you live on.

5604a7a9643661740e0013c2_45.t: I don't get any of this. If these are legitimate police, why do they have us fenced up in an impound yard? And why did they gas us like that? That isn't standard procedure. What the hell have you gotten me into?

5604a7a9643661740e0013c2_50.t: I don't get any of this. If these are legitimate police, why do they have us fenced up in an impound yard? And why did they gas us like that? That isn't standard procedure. What the hell have you gotten me into?

5604a7a9643661740e0013c2_55.t: They finally let you out, huh? They must've been going at you for five or six hours in there.
Sorry I couldn't help, but I'm just a hired gun. You're the one they're interested in. It isn't my business to know why.

5604a7a9643661740e0013c2_57.t: There is a long, awkward pause.
So, uh... did they beat a confession out of you? I don't see any bruising...

560efedf643661880e003a1b_14.t: X-Floh leaned in to whisper into your ear.

I don't believe that they can see us through the smoke... but if we get too close, we'll be in the same boat as those poor fools. We should let the cops have these two and slip around. Give 'em a wide berth.

561444d664366150150022f8_40.t: Nobody should be as lucky as you. I don't know how this sort of thing keeps happening, but it's creepy.

56ac0de5626632681d00a572_9.t: If that's what you want. I'm only in this for the paycheck, calling the shots is your job.

56ac0de5626632681d00a572_14.t: Let's try to find a way around these assholes, huh?
She nodded toward the fresh corpses of the locals that the patrol had just gunned down.
If we get too much closer, they'll probably fire on us, just like they did to those poor idiots.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_5.t: The reporter wears a look of earnest concern like a coat of cheap deodorant.
And is that when you started running?

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_9.t: The reporter turns to the camera and pretends to check his notes.

There you have it, Sunny. Our third report of mass hallucinations and violence caused by an explosion in an illegal drug lab. And while reports remain sketchy, it appears that street crime is again on the rise.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_10.t: He half turns and sweeps his arm over the scene.

As you can see, it's still turmoil and confusion here as police and Executive Council peacekeepers respond to the emergency situation, attempting to maintain order. But in all this chaos, in the maze of poverty that *is* Kowloon Walled City, we may *never know* what really happened in there. Back to you in the studio, Sunny.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_12.t: After a moment, he sees you looking at him and turns to you with a scowl.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_13.t: He laughs sardonically and continues packing.

Everyone's regurgitating a line of corpcrap, pal. They may not know it, but they are. Everything you see, everything you've been taught, every official you "elect," all bought and paid for by the people pulling the strings.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_14.t: No, I don't believe that story.
He laughs sardonically and continues packing.

Am I wearing a t-shirt that says, "I'm a fool, feed me your line of corporate bullshit"? No. But in my experience, the rest of the world is. Everything you see, everything you've been taught, every official you "elect," all bought and paid for by the people pulling the strings.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_15.t: He laughs sardonically and continues packing.

Of course there was no illegal drug spill. Of course it's a line of corpcrap. Everything you see, everything you've been taught, every official you "elect." They're all bought and paid for by the people pulling the strings.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_17.t: No eye contact. He remains bent over his equipment case.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_18.t: The reporter finishes packing and stands up straight, brushing the wet grit of the street off his hands. As he does so, his eyes narrow. His gaze moves over your crew, flicking from face to face before eventually landing on you.
Looks like you just came from inside. So tell me... what did you really see in there?

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_19.t: Why am I not surprised? No one even remembers that she built this hole in the *first* place. Not that it would matter. Jo Tsang holds this town by the balls.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_20.t: It wouldn't surprise me if she was running some kind of test in the Walled City. It's like their little petri dish.
Wouldn't surprise me if the whole thing blew up in her face because of low safety standards, either.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_21.t: What, are you surprised I believe you? We live in the future - flying cars, dragons... why not a demon-goddess? Hell, a corp probably summoned her for some kind of fear experiment or something. That kind of shit happens all the time - the Walled City's like their little petri dish. They *love* using 'em like lab rats.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_22.t: You had to go into the Walled City to see that? It's 2056 - greed is the little engine that makes the world go 'round.
And I'm sure this has something to do with some corp experimenting on the people in the Walled City. It's like their little petri dish. They *love* using them like lab rats in there.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_23.t: A bad luck machine caused all of this?

Must've been a helluva lot of bad luck. And I'm sure this machine has something to do with some corp experimenting on the people in the Walled City. It's like their little petri dish - they use 'em like lab rats in there.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_25.t: Why would it? That's just the way it works. You think I give a shit about journalism? Do you think anyone does anymore?

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_27.t: He points at the trid screen mounted on the side of his news van.

And there's a prime example of an authority figure. Jo Tsang, rising star on the Executive Council. Just *look* at her spinning this to her own ends, talking about the humanitarian relief effort she's mounting. Y'know--

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_28.t: Your comm chirps. It's Kindly Cheng.
Go ahead and take your comm. I'm gonna go before this scene turns into some kind of riot. It usually does.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_42.t: He looks at you like you're from Mars.
Good luck. You look like just the type to do it.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_43.t: You're in the 2050s, pal. Not the 1950s.
He picks up his gear and turns to go.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_44.t: The reporter gives you a lopsided smile.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_45.t: I think it's *more* worth my while to keep my job. I start babbling about government or corporate cover-ups on camera and I'm finished here. And I know plenty of guys who got the strength of the righteous and then mysteriously disappeared.
He picks up his gear and turns to go.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_52.t: The reporter turns to the camera and pretends to check his notes.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_79.t: Don't be a fool. This place is swarming with cops and military. I would think twice before I started threatening people around here. These guys play for keeps.
He picks up his gear and turns to go.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_80.t: The reporter gives you a lopsided smile.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_81.t: I think it's *more* worth my while to keep my job. I start babbling about government or corporate cover-ups on camera and I'm finished here. And I know plenty of guys who got the strength of the righteous and then mysteriously disappeared.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_0.t: This ork woman is festooned with small circuits, tiny trid screens, and speakers. The trid screens display snippets of trid broadcasts from every corner of the world. The tiny speakers play counterpoint with a sussurus of voices speaking Sperethiel, Punjabi, Kazakh, and other languages. Her expression is haggard, and she fidgets with her fingers as you approach.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_1.t: Welcome! Welcome to the Whampoa, my friend! I am Elder Ng, and these are Elders Tang and Ip.
Ng gestures to the elf and human, who each incline their heads respectfully.
Thank you so much for answering our request for help. We had nowhere else to turn.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_7.t: Ng shakes her head sadly. She reaches out a hand toward Is0bel, who shrinks away from it.
She's dead. So are Gan and Nakamura. And Tong was killed just tonight. So much blood. You have to stop this from continuing.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_9.t: Ng lifts a hand, wiping away tears from her eyes.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_15.t: Some time early tonight. He'd locked up his shop, but Ip stopped by to ask him about some skillchips he had. The door was unlocked, and inside...
Ng lifts her hands helplessly. She opens her mouth, but no words come out.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_19.t: Not that I can think of. We keep to ourselves. We buy and sell technology. We're not mercenaries or criminals, we're merchants and deckers. And even if someone was cheated in a deal, this kind of response is unthinkable.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_27.t: Ng bows her head and spreads her arms wide.
Our ways may seem strange to you, but they have sustained us for many years. I do not expect you to understand.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_30.t: Ng gives Is0bel a look of deep sadness.
Just because you cannot see or touch a thing does not mean that it does not exist. Just because you do not believe in it does not mean it does not protect you from afar.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_40.t: We could probably drive off the Special Duties Unit as well, but the cost in blood would be too high.
Since they weren't hunting a Whampoan, we saw no reason to refuse their request.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_44.t: Why would we have? Her departure from the Whampoans isn't related to your investigation - it happened before the killings began. I'm certain she's just off sulking somewhere.
No doubt she'll come waltzing back next month, all full of attitude that life dared to go on without asking her permission.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_53.t: Magpie? I doubt that very highly. Whatever character flaws she might have had, she was a pacifist when it came to physical violence.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_59.t: Excellent! We can sleep soundly for once, without looking over our shoulders. We are in your debt.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_61.t: Ng's face pales. She grits her teeth, slowly enunciating her reply.
I'm certain the killer would have said anything to shift blame. Perhaps we can compensate you a little extra for your trouble?

5525b55b30636400170026dc_64.t: Ng's blinks several times, recoiling a little.
The killer is...alive? Why would you leave him alive?!

5525b55b30636400170026dc_65.t: Ng pinches the bridge of her nose with two fingers, sighing heavily.
Yes. That is *technically* true, but I had assumed our intention was clear! An armed killer stalked and slaughtered us. What else would I possibly want you to do?!

5525b55b30636400170026dc_66.t: Ng shakes her head in disbelief.
I will send some people to the stockroom to...handle...the situation. You have my thanks for your service. I will let it be known you are always welcome in Whampoa Garden.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_67.t: There is a long, silent moment as Ng looks at you with an expression caught somewhere between astonishment and horror.
No. I can't imagine any world in which I would want to subdue a ghoul for fun.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_68.t: Ng's face pales. She grits her teeth, slowly enunciating her reply.
I'm certain the killer would have said anything to shift blame. Perhaps we can compensate you a little extra for your trouble?

5525b55b30636400170026dc_70.t: As you approach, Elder Ng's eyes widen. Her mouth falls open, and the veins on her neck bulge.
What are you doing?! You've brought this... THING... into our home! Quick, kill it before it kills us!

5525b55b30636400170026dc_76.t: You believe this vermin? This creature that feasts on metahuman flesh, that kills and dismembers our tribesmen? You are a naive and foolish man, if that's the case.
Ng spits on the ground in front of Gaichu.
What proof do you have that Magpie is dead?

5525b55b30636400170026dc_103.t: Ng glares at Gaichu in stony silence before addressing Porter.
You cannot believe what these people say. They are not to be trusted. And even if this is true, he still killed Tong.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_124.t: Then you, too, must die. Ip!

54ff5820306364a01b004921_3.t: The ork's cyberdeck suddenly starts to spark and smoke. Yelping, he yanks the cord out of his head and drops the deck.
God dammit! You broke my concentration, you son of a...

54ff5820306364a01b004921_4.t: You guess? YOU GUESS?! I should wring your--
Bull starts to lunge forward toward you, but the woman next to him holds out a hand and he stops.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_11.t: You asshole! This isn't... You think that... ARGH!
The apoplectic ork reaches into his jacket, pulling a pistol halfway out before the woman standing next to him extends a hand. Reluctantly, he stops.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_12.t: Expensive, nothing! This deck's a custom build I put together myself! You think that I'm about to let you--
The apoplectic ork is cut off as the woman standing next to him holds out a hand.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_16.t: Based on the sound of it, Knight-Errant's having a hell of an off-day too.
Tentatively, Bull smiles. It's a rueful grin, offset by occasional glances of sadness at his deck's smoking carcass.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_26.t: Bull looks at the rest of his team. One by one, they nod their assent.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_32.t: Opti considers this for a long moment, and then tosses you the keycard. You toss him a certified credstick in return.
Sure. It's not doing us any good. Consider it a peace offering.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_46.t: Bull shakes his head and snorts crudely.
We can argue about this here, or we can try and get out of here before we get killed. If it makes you feel better, you hang onto it until we get out of harm's way.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_49.t: I didn't even know that was there. Shoulda done better legwork before we got here...
Bull reloads his pistol, snapping it back into the holster with a loud click.
We'll follow. More guns couldn't hurt, right? You lead the way, we'll be right behind you.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_62.t: Christ on a crutch, that was nasty...
The ork wipes sweat from his brow with the back of one hand.
Jarl, is that the last of them?

54ff5820306364a01b004921_67.t: The ork jumps and whirls around to face you.
Ahh! Sweet Jesus, Mary, and Joseph!

54ff5820306364a01b004921_68.t: Bull's hand is on his gun, but he doesn't draw it.
Who the hell are you? You're not security.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_70.t: Bull waves an RFID keycard at the door, but it only buzzes in denial. He glances at the keycard in irritation.
What the hell? This card doesn't work!

54ff5820306364a01b004921_75.t: Okay, okay... We'll have plenty of blame to place later. Right now, we have to get the hell out of here, nab that prototype, and clear out before they get more soldiers down to this level.
Bull tilts his neck to one side and then the other. Each time a rippling pop runs down his spine.
We've got a job to do, people, so let's get cracking.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_77.t: Turning around, the Ork decker regards you quizzically.
Son of a bitch. Guess you guys got here first. Tell you what, we'll come in there and we can talk this over.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_78.t: Bull waves an RFID keycard at the door, but it only buzzes in denial. He glances at the keycard in irritation, and then turns his eyes to you.
Uh... Hey. Would you mind letting us in? I'd rather not be trapped in here when Knight-Errant backup arrives.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_88.t: Bull looks mournfully at the keycard in his hand.
If wishes were fishes... Looks like we made the wrong move, heading up there first.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_92.t: You gesture at Bull, getting his attention.
Yeah? You made a decision yet? I'm getting kinda nervous out here.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_139.t: Here, catch! This keycard's worthless to me.
Bull tosses you the keycard he was attempting to use on the lab door.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_141.t: I know you're trying to be quiet over there, but I can totally hear you.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_142.t: Here, take the damn card. It's worthless to me. Just help us out of here in one piece.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_148.t: Knock yourself out, pal. The thing's worthless to me anyway. Just help us get out of here in one piece.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_150.t: Don't sass me, Opti. If I'd had my way, I'd have brought a second deck, too... Goddamn Sparky IC.
The Ork hefts his deck mournfully. Wisps of smoke trail out of it, and there is the distinct smell of ozone.

550b0530306364f8110000d8_50.t: To hell with that, Jarl. Next time, I'm bringing a goddamn assault cannon.
Bull glances at his revolver ruefully.
The old six-gun just doesn't cut it when you go up against Knight-Errant.

550b0530306364f8110000d8_67.t: Well, crap. I guess you have to do what you think is right.
Bull sighs, rubbing his hands over his face.
I guess there's nothing to do but try for another job.

550b0530306364f8110000d8_90.t: Bull takes the prototype from you, detaching the GPS tracker and handing it back to you.

Thanks again - I really mean it. This win means I'll probably be able to help out my friends back in Chicago. So what now? Do you want us to leave, or can we stick with you until we get to the MTR station?

5513336d306364cc040080ec_13.t: Wiping his brow with the back of one hand, Bull nods toward you.
You saved the hell out of my bacon tonight. Thank you - seriously. Not a lot of people'd stick their necks out for another runner like you did. I ain't gonna forget this, I promise you that.

5525b32a30636444150020a1_5.t: Well, one day I was out with my friend Suon, and her brothers were working on clearing some mines. Narong lobs an airfoil grenade into the underbrush and a few seconds later this goat comes bouncing over. Looks at the bush and says "Baaaaa?"
Porter mimes an explosion with his hands.
We had goat stew for dinner the next night. That poor damn goat.

5525b32a30636444150020a1_15.t: The Whampoan snorts, shrugging his shoulders.

That she was a bitch? That I've never met anyone as deliberately contrary as her? There's not much to say. Nobody wanted to hang out with Magpie. Even though she was the newest Elder, she acted high and mighty around everyone. She'd block deals and argue against Nakamura and Ng just because she could.

5525b32a30636444150020a1_18.t: In that case, take the damn key. You'll have to forgive me if I don't want to go down there with you.
Porter peels an antique yale-style key off of a key ring and hands it to you.
Knock yourself out, man. There's an entrance up by the Jade Mountain restaurant. Can't miss it.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_71.t: Porter hefts his pistol warily, but does not aim it at anyone.
Yeah, I have to say... This isn't a good idea. Why the hell is a ghoul in here, and why is it wearing armor?

5525b55b30636400170026dc_75.t: Porter nods at the Elders, looking back to you with a wary expression.
I'm interested to hear what kind of evidence you have to support this theory. As far as I can tell, this monster killed Tong and the others, and that makes him a threat that should be eliminated.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_79.t: Porter takes a moment to examine the necklace. He nods at you.
This is definitely Elder Magpie's. If you found it in the storm drain, that's suspicious... But hardly proof on its own.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_82.t: That's true. It's a guess. And even if it is her blood, that doesn't prove that the Elders hired this ghoul to kill her.
Porter gives Elder Ip a sidelong glance.
The Elders have always protected Whampoa Garden to the best of their ability.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_89.t: Porter regards the Elders for a long moment.
It still doesn't prove the Elders knew it was the ghoul.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_90.t: You point out the armor's markings to Porter, and he nods with some satisfaction.
This is from his armor, definitely. But how does it prove that the Elders knew of the ghoul's existence?

5525b55b30636400170026dc_94.t: Porter considers this for a moment, and then shakes his head.

That doesn't add up, Ng. Why would the police ask us for entry now? They've never been polite before, and they've always tried to force their way in here. It just doesn't ring true to me. Did you ask them to come? In order to hunt down this ghoul?

5525b55b30636400170026dc_96.t: That's true. Those murders were vicious and cruel.
Porter fixes you with a hard stare.
I don't see any way to explain that away. This ghoul is a monster for how he killed Tong and the others.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_104.t: Porter looks back at Ng, his expression flat and without emotion.
Maybe so, Ng. But a monster would not take Tong's suffering into account. He may be a killer, but he's not heartless.

5525b55b30636400170026dc_129.t: Porter reaches out to stop him. Ip reluctantly stops.
Ip, no. Don't make this any worse than it is.

569ed20a306364a81f0057b1_11.t: Why do you buy a fire extinguisher if you don't expect to be on fire? Just in case, man! I speak twelve languages, including Yoruba, Salish, and Latvian. Hell, I even speak a little Basque. Try and get use out of *that* one.

569ed20a306364a81f0057b1_13.t: Yeah, sure, but it's *boring.* You can't make wordplay with a chipped language, and you always sound a bit like a robot. I speak twelve languages, including including Yoruba, Salish, and Latvian. Hell, I even speak a little Basque. Try and get use out of *that* one.

569ed20a306364a81f0057b1_14.t: In a perfect world? Sure. But when it comes to languages, I'm kind of a quantity-over-quality guy. 569ed20a306364a81f0057b1_16.t: He takes a slice from the plate and pops it into his mouth, and a blissful expression washes over his face.

569ed20a306364a81f0057b1_22.t: All right, all right. I'll tell you, if you insist. It's like this, okay? I take field jobs sometimes. I don't like it, but with some gigs, you've just gotta be out there in person.

5633e0113063644416006cf6_9.t: Isane folds her arms with a smirk.

5633e0113063644416006cf6_42.t: Lily bends over and adjusts the buckle on her left shoe.

5633e0113063644416006cf6_43.t: Lily gives herself a once over, and rubs absently at a drop of blood that landed on the hem of her dress.

Oh, come on. Don't tell me I lost the EMS tranducer...

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_3.t: As she turns her attention back toward the table, she spares you a quick glance.

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_5.t: Ahh, crap. This is gonna take more work than I thought... Oh well. Another time.

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_6.t: The elf flicks a nail against the tag, grinning crookedly.

Some jackass at the public laundromat keeps trying to walk off with my stuff, so I've been writing my name on things. Makes me feel a bit like a kid, but I don't have to keep buying new armor, at least.

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_8.t: Nice to meet you. Glad to get your name - it's a lot nicer than 'that foreigner', which seems to be what most of the locals call you.

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_9.t: Thanks. I'd be doing this at home, but my neighbor's got some kind of heavy duty electrical work going on, and it's fritzing out the unshielded parts of this little guy. Had to vacate or risk frying his internals.
She leans over, peering closely at some particular part.

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_10.t: I'm trying to do drone surgery in a *bar*, with big oafs bumping into the table. I'm about as careful as I can be. Especially since I can't work at home - my neighbor's got some kind of heavy duty unshielded electical equipment playing havoc with my instruments.
She leans over, peering closely at some particular part.

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_11.t: Eos has the internal workings of a micro-drone spread out on the table in front of her. She pokes and prods at it with a multimeter, studiously marking the results on a nearby PDA. As you approach, she holds up a hand.

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_12.t: There is a sudden popping noise, and a thin wisp of smoke trails up from the drone's innards. Eos stands stock still, and then throws her hands up in the air in defeat.

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_13.t: Eh. I was trying out a new power cell. It wasn't rated for this size drone, but I figured it was worth a shot - I wanted to mount a tiny stun gun on the underside.

Stupid idea anyway. Next time, I'll use a hypodermic needle, fill it with Neurostun or something.

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_14.t: Eos is examining a disassembled micro-drone, the parts spread across the grimy table she's at. She excitedly waves you over.

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_15.t: My neighbor's doing some kind of work on an unshielded electrical system. It's screwing with my instruments - but that's not important!
She points at a battery on the table with her little finger.
*This* is a heartbreaking work of staggering genius, it is.

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_18.t: Eos beams proudly, and bangs a fist on the table.
Knew you'd like it! Now let's just turn this little baby on and--

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_19.t: There is a sudden popping noise and a bright flash. A thin wisp of smoke trails up from the drone's innards, which are now mostly slag. Eos just stares at it, mouth open.

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_20.t: With a defeated sigh, Eos sweeps the parts into a pile with her hands, depositing them into one of the many pockets on her jumpsuit.
Win some, lose some, I guess. So, what can I do for you?

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_21.t: You don't have any idea what you're talking about! It'll be fine! Here, let me turn it on to show you--

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_22.t: That battery's for a drone five times the size of this one. I've strapped a miniature stun gun to the underside. It can crawl up on an unsuspecting guard, and *pow*!
She claps her hands together with a loud crack.

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_24.t: A heartbreaking work of staggering genius, that's what!
She points at a battery on the table with her little finger, eyes wide with excitement.

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_26.t: I've always loved cars. Old ones, new ones, even the beaters that are falling apart and you have to hold together with bailing wire and chewing gum. I like drones too, but my first love was a 2034 Alfa-Romeo Ferinata. Drove that thing until the wheels fell off.
Eos grins crookedly, propping her chin up on one fist.

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_29.t: I started to make a name for myself. Took some jobs for the Comanche Mafia smuggling things in and out of the Pueblo Corporate Council.
Eos spreads her hands helplessly, grinning.

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_31.t: Honestly? It's my sunny disposition. I'm not even kidding.
The elf giggles to herself for a moment, head falling back as she stares toward the ceiling.

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_34.t: Eos gets a smile that spreads from ear to ear, and she leans over to whisper in a conspiratorial tone.

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_37.t: Eos grins at you, eyes twinkling with mischeif.
I knew I liked you for a reason. Revion was hanging out the side of the car the whole time, twin pistols blazing at the cops. Never had a drive like that before or since... But I keep looking.

55a6a50c306364f41b0073cb_40.t: Tapping a finger to her brow, Eos grins.
See you. Don't be afraid to call!

55afe788623666140a004ea9_1.t: When the woman sees you heading her way, her face hardens. You see her hand slip into her jacket pocket, but as she studies your faces, the fear in her eyes gives way to curiosity.

5588a592303031e827004f11_17.t: A calm, stern-faced man stands before you. He casually checks his watch as he addresses you, his voice tinged with boredom.

5588a592303031e827004f11_20.t: He snaps his fingers, and the guards behind him train their weapons on your position.
Before you decide anything, at least hear me out. You're in a position to come out of this with an edge against Tsang. That alone should be enough to pique your interest.

5588a592303031e827004f11_21.t: It just so happens that you've stumbled into the right place at the right time.

I imagine that doesn't happen often in your line of work.

5588a592303031e827004f11_24.t: The corners of his eyes crease as a flat smile stretches across his jaw.

A little trideo editing, and some creative violence on my guards' part, and we'll claim you assaulted us and entered the Matrix by force. Your invasion of our direct system will only bolster our strikes against Josephine.

5588a592303031e827004f11_26.t: Excellent. The information I need is in the datastore labeled "Foreign Accounts." Return those files to me, and you're welcome to whatever else you find in the Matrix.

5588d78e3030317c21007a70_0.t: You exit the core system. The vice president, watching nearby, visibly relaxes.
Hell, took you long enough. You got it all on one chip, right? Hand it here.

5588d78e3030317c21007a70_1.t: His face flushes with anger, and he rounds on you.
Listen here, you little shit. We made a deal. Runner or no, I expect you to keep your word. And if you refuse... well, I've got a team of men who'll paint you red with a snap of my fingers.

5588d78e3030317c21007a70_2.t: He nabs the chip from your outstretched hand.
Very good. You came through, as promised. A wise choice. I may have need of reliable people such as yourself in the future.

55bd05d330303158140060de_1.t: His expression shifts into a grimace. He peers at you with malice behind his eyes.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_1.t: A pale, assault-rifle toting human glares at you from inside the room. He looks ready to raise his assault rifle, but doesn't.
Hey, Opti... I think I figured out why the alarms were going off.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_14.t: Jarl spends a few moments scrutinizing you, when his face suddenly lights up.
Wait a minute... I recognize you. You're that runner, right? Your name's all over the shadows of Hong Kong. From what I hear, you've been doing a lot of work for Kindly Cheng.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_21.t: While he hasn't made out the words exchanged, the street samurai seems to have discerned that you're discussing something amongst yourselves, and tenses up.
Hope you're not planning something stupid over there...

54ff5820306364a01b004921_24.t: Jarl glances around at the rest of his team and then at you.
I don't want to interrupt, but we still have a big problem. We're both here for the laser. What now?

54ff5820306364a01b004921_38.t: Jarl cuts loose with a deep, emphatic laugh.
You know what? You're all right. I like your attitude.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_42.t: Jarl - or The Jarl, whichever - is nonplussed by Dizzy's antics.
It's a title, Dizzy. It deserves a 'the' before it.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_57.t: Yeah. I'm from all over - don't really stay in one place much. The other three are all from the Great Lakes area.
Ruefully, Jarl glances at the rest of his team.
This was supposed to be an easy trip overseas for us... Do this job, maybe two or three more, and fly home.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_58.t: Jarl puts one finger to his ear. He cocks his head, listening intently. After a moment, he lets out a string of half-uttered curses.
We're running out of time. We've got to move, and soon.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_63.t: Looks like it, Bull. My C-12 wrecked the stairwell, so we're safe from backup via that route.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_72.t: Despite the worry on the faces of his compatriots, the human named Jarl is unconcerned. He glances casually around the room, rifle slung.
Plans, first contact with the enemy... You know that hackneyed old phrase. Where there's ammo, there's a way out. So are you going to let us in or what?

54ff5820306364a01b004921_73.t: Despite the worry on the faces of his compatriots, the human named Jarl is unconcerned. He glances casually around the room, rifle slung.
Har har. Are you going to open the door for us, or what?

54ff5820306364a01b004921_76.t: The runner named Jarl seems to have noticed you, finally. He gestures in your direction.
Hey, Bull? Looks like we've got some company in the lab.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_86.t: Given the shitstorm we set off by going to the executive penthouses first, we probably have a few minutes. They'll be too busy trying to figure out what we were doing up there.
Jarl runs a hand through his hair and shrugs.
Hardingham and Taylor's apartments are pretty trashed, so they'll have to figure out what we're really after before they can respond again.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_132.t: He gestures toward you, body tensing up.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_134.t: Looks that way. I know this was too hot a job for only one fixer to pick up...
Jarl looks you over. He has the clinical expression of a technician evaluating an inanimate object.
Tell you what, we can talk this over in more detail inside.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_135.t: Jarl nods in appreciation once the other team is safely inside the lab.
Thanks a lot. We would have been cut apart out there. Never let yourself get backed into a corner, you know?

550b0530306364f8110000d8_25.t: I know you have a rep to protect... But this is eat or starve for me. It's not just about this job, it's about all the others I won't be able to get if I scrub this one. You do me a favor, I'll pay it back to you in spades. I can make it worth your while.

550b0530306364f8110000d8_51.t: Well, you wanna go down that road, I won't stop you. I might make fun of you for carting one around, though.
The Jarl turns to face you.
So now we just have to get to the central MTR station, yeah?

550b0530306364f8110000d8_53.t: You'll note I'm not a BTL junkie, though. And I like having all my limbs attached and in relatively good condition. I'm in it for the money, not the rush.
The Jarl turns to face you.
So now we just have to get to the central MTR station, yeah?

550b0530306364f8110000d8_54.t: The Jarl produces a handkerchief from his back pocket, wiping off his face.
So now we just have to get to the central MTR station, yeah?

550b0530306364f8110000d8_78.t: The Jarl takes the prototype from you, detaching the GPS tracker and handing it back to you.
Excellent. I'll make sure nobody hears about this deal - at least not on my end. So what now? Do you want us to leave, or can we stick with you until we get to the MTR station?

5513336d306364cc040080ec_1.t: The Jarl nods in appreciation, extending a hand to you.
Thanks for all the help tonight. I don't know if I'd have been able to get out of there without you. The world's better off with people like you in it.

5513336d306364cc040080ec_2.t: The usual. Make a handoff, get paid. Probably take a few more jobs out here. Got a lot of debts to pay off, you know? Nothing's free in this life.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_4.t: The thick troll pulls his mouth into a comic frown.
He makes a show of patting his pockets.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_9.t: The thick troll's voice is casual. Amused. He shakes his gun in Wu's face.
Little late for that, isn't it?

54bd886b323437742d0012df_10.t: The troll winks at you and gives his attention to Wu.
Maybe we got off on the wrong foot just now. Something we can do for you, officer?

54bd886b323437742d0012df_12.t: The troll nods his enormous head.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_15.t: The troll maintains amused eye contact, but his weapon stays trained on Wu.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_19.t: The troll's yellow-brown tusks shift into a smile again.
Kinda, yeah. The old man hired us to take you all on an excursion.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_23.t: The troll's yellow-brown tusks shift into a smile again.
He needed friends - the heavily armed kind. The old man hired us to take you all on a little excursion.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_32.t: The troll tosses Wu a sideways smile while staring down the barrel of his gun.
Sorry to burst your bubble, Lone Star. Guess you didn't know the old man as well as you thought, huh?

54bd886b323437742d0012df_36.t: The troll is wary but willing. He lifts the barrel of his gun. Slightly.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_37.t: You can tell the ork girl's had enough of the tension. She blurts it out.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_38.t: She waves a hand at the three of you.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_39.t: Two large rats, clinging to her hip and shoulder, poke their heads out of the folds of her clothing. They fix their beady eyes on you intently.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_40.t: The girl sneers, rolling her eyes.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_46.t: The troll flashes you a smile that's all tusks and no warmth.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_47.t: He looks at his team as he slowly starts to lower his weapon.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_48.t: Didn't make much sense to us either. But like I said, Nuyen's nuyen.
Okay, gang. I tell you what.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_49.t: It's a shithole. The worst slum in the eastern hemisphere. Trid clips and news stories can't do it justice.
Memories of growing up in the Redmond Barrens flash through your mind.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_50.t: The girl sighs. Rolls her eyes.
Yeah. This is Kowloon. But Kowloon *Walled* City is different. It's a shithole. The worst slum in the eastern hemisphere. Trid clips and news stories can't do it justice.
Memories of growing up in the Redmond Barrens flash through your mind.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_51.t: No one you wanna mess with.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_52.t: The ork girl chimes in. Her tone is light - she's trying to diffuse the situation.
We do odd jobs. For money.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_56.t: The ork girl chimes in. Her tone is light - she's trying to diffuse the situation.
We do odd jobs. For money.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_58.t: She gets a wry look in her eye. Her answer is flippant.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_59.t: The thick troll snorts a laugh.
That guy looks like he could use an enema. Something we can do for you, officer?

54bd886b323437742d0012df_60.t: Oh, I can see that! Your buddies are totally chill. Something we can do for you, officer?

54bd886b323437742d0012df_61.t: The troll notes your action with a nod.
Maybe we got off on the wrong foot just now. Something we can do for you, officer?
His mouth breaks into a patronizing grin.

5584860f303031201800028f_1.t: The maintenance manager's eyes are glued to a book in her hands. Without looking at you, she speaks.

5584860f303031201800028f_3.t: Those idiots... Just a second. I'll sort this out.
She whips out her PDA and pounds its keys.

5584860f303031201800028f_14.t: She flicks her finger toward the door and speaks quickly.
Outside. Around the corner. Don't leave this level unless you get a Tsang ID. Be a shame if you never made out of here... Get your credentials in order, and get out.

5584860f303031201800028f_15.t: Before you do anything around here, you've gotta change out of those ridiculous clothes. Hit the locker room and grab a uniform. Show some goddamn company pride.
She studies you again, clearly unimpressed.
Well, least they finally sent me *somebody*... here, the locker code.

5584860f303031201800028f_16.t: She looks confused and slightly annoyed by your gung ho attitude.

5584860f303031201800028f_18.t: She lets out a breath of relief.

About time I got some help down here. I've got a task to break you in fast. The autocoupler on the main terminal is out. Flow regulators need a restart. Engage its airflow mixers, then report back to me.

5584860f303031201800028f_20.t: She slams her hands on top of her desk.
What!? You signed off on the goddamn turbine engine? We can't do that! I'd need... I'd need *at least* a crew of four level-three techs to even do an evaluation of that thing!

5584860f303031201800028f_21.t: The anger drains from her. She closes her eyes and rubs her temple.
Shit, rookie. If you signed off on the order, we've got to do it, or this department is sunk.

5584b096303031601e00042f_0.t: A live video window is open in the middle of the terminal's screen. You see the face of the ever-tense maintenance manager glaring back at you.

5584b096303031601e00042f_3.t: The turbine hisses as the hydraulics pressurize.

Well, that's something. Indicator lights are still red, though. Sometimes the engine's seismoradar connection becomes misaligned, so try adjusting that. Shouldn't need to move it more than a couple centimeters.

5584b096303031601e00042f_4.t: Huh. Maybe the rotor speed needs to be recalibrated?

5584b096303031601e00042f_5.t: Something groans inside the turbine, and the base of the rotor begins to rumble.

Hey, that's it! Don't let the noise fool you - we've identified the problem. The rotor must've been recalibrated the opposite of what it needed. One more try, and it should fix the turbine. Give it a go!

5584b096303031601e00042f_6.t: The rumbling sounds of the machine steady, and its indicator lights turn green.
That's what I want to hear. Great work! I'll remember this come your next review.

5584b096303031601e00042f_10.t: A thunderous grating noise rumbles inside the machinery, followed by a loud explosion as a sheet of flame erupts from its top.

55a59ab130636420200089d6_0.t: Brent nods to you as you approach.
Hey again. Is there something else you wanted?

55a59ab130636420200089d6_1.t: Something I can help you with, friend?

The elf has a hard-set look to his face and the cold stare of someone accustomed to casual violence. The most striking thing about him, however, are the glowing golden tattoos that wrap from his forehead around toward the back of his head, just over one ear.

55a59ab130636420200089d6_2.t: It's not ink. Not really. It's a spell.
The elf's tone is flat and disinterested, but he fixes you with an intense stare.
Magical tattoo. The kind of thing you tend to pick up in the business.

55a59ab130636420200089d6_3.t: Fixing you with a dubious look, the elf cocks his head.
What the hell do you think, man? Look at you. We're obviously working the same angle here in Heoi.

55a59ab130636420200089d6_4.t: He doesn't extend a hand, but nods at you in greeting.
I'm a mage. One of the best in this hemisphere, in fact.

55a59ab130636420200089d6_5.t: The name's not attached to any SIN, so what does it matter?

55a59ab130636420200089d6_7.t: Yeah? What kind of look is that, anyway?
He leans back, knitting his fingers together on the table in front of him. His expression is dubious, but there's an interested glint in his eye.

55a59ab130636420200089d6_8.t: The elf nods a few times, and spreads his hands in acceptance.
I guess you have a point. We can usually tell our own. Body language, you know. Hyper-alertness, that look that says 'don't mess with me'.

55a59ab130636420200089d6_9.t: The elf casually extends one arm, turning it over so you can see his pale skin.

I'm no local. I speak the language, but that's just common courtesy when you're working in another country. You want to get chummy with Hong Kongers, go hang out at a night market. I'm only here for work.

55a59ab130636420200089d6_10.t: Brent lifts two fingers in greeting.
Hey. Good to see you're keeping safe. Got any jobs you need me for?

55a59ab130636420200089d6_19.t: What kind of bullshit question is that? Are you interviewing me for a job renting tuxedos, or something?
Brent snorts, and hunches over the table on one elbow.
Magic is about power. You can dress it up however you like, but that's the core of what it is.

55a59ab130636420200089d6_23.t: Brent lifts a hand, gesturing in the air as he speaks.

It's like this: we know we can graph the power output of magical energy. We can benchmark the intensity of spirits and astral energies. The entire basis of modern thaumaturgy is treating magic like any other science. We don't know how everything works, but the same is true for quantum physics.

55a59ab130636420200089d6_27.t: Brent shrugs, one corner of his mouth curling up in a wry smile.

When I started running, I was too young to know better. By the time it mattered, I was too good for anyone to do anything about it. Since I'm SINless, it's not like any of the megas can attach meaningful information to the name - it's just as meaningless as any street name, in terms of tracking me.

55a59ab130636420200089d6_30.t: Brent lifts two fingers, running them across his forehead toward the tip of his ear.
Got this done in Yokohama a few years back. An old crewmate's brother is a tattoo artist out there, specializing in quickened spells.

562144fc643661e80d004512_1.t: You're here. Right on time. Good.

I don't think I could've waited much longer.

562144fc643661e80d004512_2.t: Constable Cheung glances past you, spotting Gaichu. She pales, her lips drawing back into a tight line.
You brought a goddamned ghoul to a *police station*? What the hell is wrong with you!?

562144fc643661e80d004512_4.t: Cheung continues to eyeball you like you've sprouted a second head, the veins on her neck bulging. After a few tense moments, she exhales explosively.

562144fc643661e80d004512_9.t: She raises a finger to her lips, shushing you.
Don't use my name! In fact, just don't speak. I'm in a compromising enough position as it is.

562144fc643661e80d004512_10.t: She reaches into one of her voluminous pockets and fishes out an object. A slip of plastic, slick and shiny, connected to an alligator clip. The word VISITOR is emblazoned on the front.

562144fc643661e80d004512_21.t: She taps a code into the PDA on her wrist, and you hear a buzzing sound. The door pops open.

562144fc643661e80d004512_24.t: She pauses, her eyes flitting away.
...I know. Totally cornball, right? Like some cliche out of an old movie. But I guess that I'm doing it anyway. Go ahead and take that for what you will.

562144fc643661e80d004512_30.t: Nuh-uh. No way. I agreed to help this task force, but I am *not* going to put myself into a combat situation with the SDU.
She taps a code into the PDA on her wrist, and you hear a buzzing sound. The door pops open.

562144fc643661e80d004512_31.t: Yeah, well. It's a little late now, huh?

562144fc643661e80d004512_33.t: No drones, you've got nothing to worry about there.

562144fc643661e80d004512_41.t: Cheung throws her hands up in the air and rolls her eyes.
Fine. If you want to get killed, go talk to Sheng at the desk. Ask for Mia, in admin. If he's somehow gone blind and doesn't notice the cannibal, he'll give you access to the second floor.

54f6285b306364140400187c_8.t: At the sight of Gaichu, the receptionist recoils visibly, and his face grows ashen.
Dear god, is that... is that a GHOUL with you?!

54f6285b306364140400187c_10.t: The receptionist's eyes bulge in panic as he shrinks away from Racter.
Don't fret?! When you waltz in here with a goddamn cannibal in tow...!?

54f6285b306364140400187c_15.t: The receptionist recoils, eyes wild as he reaches for the panic button on his desk.
But he's not tied up! He's just... walking around! With you!

54f6285b306364140400187c_17.t: The receptionist seems dubious, and spends a few moments typing furiously at his computer. After a moment, his eyes go wide.

Well... It does say here that there's a standing bounty order on foreign corporate military personnel. I suppose I can let you in, then. You'll have to stay on the first floor until your Knight-Errant Intelligence Division contact comes to escort you in.

54f6285b306364140400187c_19.t: The receptionist's panic seems to have dulled his response time, though he begins reaching for the panic button on his desk.
But... Look at him! He's got all the signs! Look at his skin! Look at his eyes!

54f6285b306364140400187c_20.t: While visibly dubious, the secretary seems to calm down a bit.
I suppose... I suppose that makes sense. Who'd bring a ghoul into an Ares facility, anyway?

54f6285b306364140400187c_21.t: Well, he looks like he needs it! Go on into the lobby. You can contact your doctor from the public terminal.

Have a pleasant day. And... good luck with the treatment.

54f6285b306364140400187c_22.t: The receptionist slaps a panic button on his desk.
Security! Get security out here right now!

54f6285b306364140400187c_24.t: The receptionist stares at you, his eyes bulging in their sockets. He speaks slowly, in a strained tone.
What do you mean, 'so what'?! I can't let an infected cannibal go roaming around the building! He'll eat somebody!

54f6285b306364140400187c_29.t: The receptionist narrows his eyes at you.
That was... fast. And you don't really look like maintenance.

54f6285b306364140400187c_33.t: The receptionist raises an eyebrow very slowly.
You're just stringing business-related words together. You have no idea what those mean, do you?

54f6285b306364140400187c_34.t: Listen, I don't have time to entertain corporate wannabes. Come back later when you have some real business here, okay? Try and get in without a valid reason and I'll be forced to call security.

54f6285b306364140400187c_36.t: As you wish. Please, go right ahead. You'll have to stay on this floor until someone comes to escort you upstairs.

54f6285b306364140400187c_40.t: He looks unsure of himself. You can see his eyes flit to a conspicuous panic button mounted to the side of his desk, but he doesn't move.
You aren't on the guest li--

54f6285b306364140400187c_42.t: The secretary blinks a few times, his eyes glazed over by Racter's barrage of technobabble.
Oh, I... Uh... Yes, I see. That's... a lot of interesting information. What should I put down on the arrival sheet, then?

54f6285b306364140400187c_48.t: I warned you not to enter the building without signing in!
The receptionist slams his hand onto the panic button at his desk.
Security, we have intruders in the lobby!

54ff5820306364a01b004921_5.t: The woman is as tightly wound as a coiled snake who's about to strike. Her knuckles, knees, and boots are covered in blood.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_39.t: Y'know, there's a strange kind of logic to that attitude... If it works, it works.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_41.t: I'm Dizzy. The guy with the rifle is The Jarl.

Those two are Opti and Bull. Magic and tech support. So... You ever heard of us?

54ff5820306364a01b004921_64.t: Taking a long drink from the canteen, the woman breathes a deep sigh of relief before tossing it back to Jarl.

You, sir, need to practice your martial arts.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_69.t: Yeah, sure... But let's get inside first. Thats stairwell isn't the only way into this area.
