54ff5820306364a01b004921_74.t: Yeah, yeah... So you got lucky and we didn't.
Dizzy looks around the office. Her body's as tense as a coiled snake about to strike.
You think you could open this door for us, since Bull's keycard isn't working?

54ff5820306364a01b004921_130.t: At this point, all I want to do is get out of the line of fire.
C'mon, let us in. I'll keep these jokers from trying to grab the loot and run. The pay on this run isn't worth dying over.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_133.t: Dizzy bristles at his, her neck tensing. After a moment, she exhales a long breath, as if to calm herself.
I can respect that. But we're getting in that lab. Hit it, Bull.

550b0530306364f8110000d8_1.t: That was closer than I'd have liked. Whew!
Dizzy hunches over, hands on her knees as she pants excitedly. She straightens as she calms down.
Chalk that up on things I don't want to do again: get gassed while trying to escape from Knight-Errant.

550b0530306364f8110000d8_45.t: Opti, I don't think I've ever met anyone who's as much of an adrenaline junkie as you.
Dizzy shakes her head, disbelief plastered across her face. After a moment, she turns to you.
So what now? We just march through the basement into the Central MTR station?

550b0530306364f8110000d8_47.t: I'm with you there, Jarl. Any run you can walk away from is a good one, in my book.
Dizzy turns to face you, one fist on her hip.
So what now? We just march through the basement into the Central MTR station?

550b0530306364f8110000d8_91.t: Dizzy takes the prototype from you, detaching the GPS tracker and handing it back to you.

Awesome! We both get what we want out of this deal. It's rare for that to be able to happen, in the shadows. So what now? Do you want us to leave, or can we stick with you until we get to the MTR station?

5513336d306364cc040080ec_10.t: I don't know. More work out here, I guess? It's a big city, and there's a lot of call for shadowrunners these days. I always have bills to pay, so why not stay where the work is?

5525b2eb3063644415002093_3.t: Call me Demergo. I'm not one of the Whampoans, but I've been here long enough that they don't think twice about me being here.
Demergo drains the last of his soykaf in a large gulp.
So what've you found so far?

5525b2eb3063644415002093_5.t: Demergo tosses the soykaf cup toward the nearby garbage can. It bounces around the rim and and rolls inside.
When I hear about shit like this happening? I keep my ear to the ground. Old habits, you know.

5525b2eb3063644415002093_6.t: Elder Ng asked me to look into the killings after the first murder. She couldn't afford my fee, though, so I took a pass. It's not just the money, either.
Demergo shudders, folding his arms across his chest.
You see that stuff day after day, you pay a price. It eats away at you.

5525b2eb3063644415002093_11.t: Demergo nods, though his expression is still one of disgust and sickness.
Sure, if you're appraising antiques or trying to find where a lost cat went. But turning that on murder scenes is an invitation to horrors beyond imagining.

5525b2eb3063644415002093_13.t: Demergo's gotten himself a new cup of soykaf. He must run on that stuff. As you approach, he lifts it in salute.
Hey again. Any new developments you need my help with?

5525b2eb3063644415002093_15.t: I don't know much. Even though I've been here almost two years, I'm an outsider - they don't really accept anyone who's not as much of a tech-nut as they are. I'm just the crazy mage that lives down the road.
Demergo pauses and looks off into the distance as he collects his thoughts.

5525b2eb3063644415002093_20.t: Demergo lowers his voice, leaning in toward you so that only you can hear.

The funny thing is, the Hong Kong Police Force was out here a few weeks before that - maybe a month ago, tops. I know that doesn't seem like much, but the Whampoans don't usually let the police in here. That by itself is odd enough, but it gets even stranger.

5525b2eb3063644415002093_22.t: That's exactly the kind of question you should be asking - and that the Elders refused to answer when I asked.

Go take a look for yourself, if you want. Watch out, though. A gang's taken up residence in there, dealing drugs and BTLs. They call themselves the Red Spear. Not too violent, if you don't push them, but be careful what you say.

5525b2eb3063644415002093_23.t: That's not what I would have expected. With the amount of mess at each scene, the victims should have left behind a hell of a lot of fear and pain. Are you sure?

5525b2eb3063644415002093_24.t: Then it sounds like this wasn't a crime of passion. A planned attack would have that kind of resonance, in my experience. It's possible the gore show is to throw off anyone looking for the actual reason.
Demergo closes his eyes and starts to nod.
Yeah. If I wanted to kill someone and get away with it, I'd try and point the finger at some kind of monster.

5525b2eb3063644415002093_25.t: Demergo opens his eyes and smiles a thin, humorless grin.
Basic investigation 101: it's usually the simplest explanation, but don't discount other possibilities.

5525b2eb3063644415002093_29.t: That says to me that the attack is either a professional hitman or a practiced serial killer. It could be both, too. Assassins don't tend to be the most empathetic people.
Demergo considers this information for a moment before speaking again.
Was the attack a thrust or a cut?

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_0.t: The lab worker rounds on you, and hisses through his mask.
Get out of here! What if someone *sees us?* We've exchanged too many words already.
He waves his hands at you like someone trying to shoo away a stray cat.

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_1.t: The finger in your face makes it objectively clear that you're the "you" in question here.
I gave you that code. And instead of respecting our kinship, you betrayed me and let the monster out! SECURITYYY!

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_2.t: Uhh. Weren't you just...? Since when are you with Tsang maintenance?

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_3.t: He props his chin up with his hand.
You were with maintenance all along, but off the clock. Or, ah... your uniform was being laundered. Or you forgot it at home. You just got the job between now and our last conversation.

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_4.t: Of course, maybe you're not with maintenance at all. Maybe you're...
His hand drops and he gasps in surprise.
ACTORS! Oh man, oh man. Am I on camera right now? No, wait... no. This is Tsang - it's highly unlikely they'd let you set up video equipment in here.

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_5.t: In times like these, I always prefer to follow Occam's razor. In which case, you're probably an impostor. From the look of you before, maybe even a shadowrunner... Better not take any chances. SECURITYYY!

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_6.t: As you enter the room, a group of security guards turn toward you, revealing the lab worker at the center of their flock. He points at you.
there they are! they's the one who used their silver tongue to lull me into a state of trust. Then, when my defenses were down, they beat the containment code out of me!
He lets out an exaggerated sob, and the guards leap toward you.

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_7.t: You overhear a masked man in a lab coat mumbling to himself. After a moment, you realize that he's narrating his actions.

With a flash of his pen, he approves the incoming lab expenses. Science shall not suffer at the hands of miserly department heads. No, not on *his* watch. Such fiery acumen, such daring! He runs a hand through his luscious locks of stubble. The women swoon. The men swoon. He is metahumanity's last hope!

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_8.t: His shoulders stiffen in surprise. He makes a small sound, then looks at you. You can't tell if he's angry or embarrassed.
Hello! Hello, hello. Yes, ah... how long have you been there? Actually, never mind. You're here to... fix something?

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_9.t: The lab worker rolls his eyes.
The *air* quality? You've interrupted my research to talk about this janitorial drivel? Test it yourself! I'm working.

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_12.t: You... You can see all that?
His baffled expression quickly morphs into one of ecstatic delight.
No one else gets it, how crucial this project is. It's been so long since I've met anyone who understands my work here! I don't know where to begin...

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_17.t: He leans in, and his voice drops.

To be perfectly honest, I actually got the idea from a video game. "Magic of Evermore: The Awakening." It was incredibly popular a decade ago. Have you played it? No, that doesn't matter. You don't have to have played it. You already *get* it. We're kindred spirits, you and I. Just like Balgar the Great and his pox-faced friend, Pucok.

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_19.t: I don't want to brag, but there's no other scientist in intra-astral research who could accomplish what I've done. Built it from the ground up, myself. I named the sonar system BALS - Bridged Astral to Litodocument Sonar - after META's hero, Balgar.
He smiles wide, looking thoroughly pleased with himself.

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_21.t: A tear glistens in the corner of his eye. He turns his head to the side and shouts into his shoulder.

Damn you, Chad Brawnsen. You know, I started wearing this mask soon after I graduated - which I did early, I'm sure you've guessed - so people will think I'm sick and be friendlier. But even if they start out friendly, the second I open my mouth, they shrink away. I know it's my intellect... it intimidates people. I just wish they could see past that. See *me.* Like you do.

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_23.t: He cautiously glances over his shoulder.

I really shouldn't be doing this, but I like you. So if you promise me - *promise* me - that you won't tell anyone, I'll give you the code to our containment chamber. We hit the astral jackpot in there. It will blow your mind! But I'd be seriously breaking company ordinance, so...

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_24.t: He nods excitedly, mask bobbing up and down.
Just don't let anyone see you enter, all right?

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_30.t: He nods excitedly, mask bobbing up and down.
Just don't let anyone see you enter, all right?

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_31.t: His words are heavy with disdain.
If you'd kindly *leave* now, that'd be great. And, please, resist the urge to pester me again. I'm very busy.

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_32.t: You overhear a shaggy man in a lab coat mumbling to himself. After a moment, you realize he's narrating his actions.

With a flash of his pen, he approves the incoming lab expenses. Science shall not suffer at the hands of miserly department heads. No, not on *his* watch. Such fiery acumen, such daring! He runs a hand through his luscious locks of hair. The women swoon. The men swoon. He is metahumanity's last hope!

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_33.t: His shoulders stiffen in surprise. He makes a small sound, then looks at you. You can't tell if he's angry or embarrassed.
Hello! Hello, hello. Yes, ah... how long have you been there? Actually, never mind. Who are you? Why are you in my lab?

559f14f7303031181b0064f0_35.t: A smug pride replaces his former uneasiness.
My lab *is* rather formidable. But there's little here that someone like you could comprehend. Why don't you get back to doing... whatever it was you were doing? But outside my work space, please.

54dd4f1c653937e81300425c_2.t: Step back, Johnny. They know exactly where they are. Don't you, runner?

54dd4f1c653937e81300425c_3.t: He nods to his guards and steps forward. The harsh style of his greased-back hair glistens in the light. It looks slick, like tar, exaggerating his slimeball visage.
Let's skip the banter your kind so enjoys and move right onto business. Who sent you? Have the Red Dragons finally tired of my evasiveness?

54dd4f1c653937e81300425c_4.t: Or, perhaps... The Yellow Lotus. They've been trying to wrest Kowloon from our grasp for years.
He studies you from within the safe reach of his guards. Crosses his arms.
Well? Don't just stand there. Who sent you?

54dd4f1c653937e81300425c_5.t: I'll be damned if I let you take me to Woo.

54dd4f1c653937e81300425c_6.t: A raise of his hand, and the Talon steps forward.
Talon, this miscreant has distracted us from our meal. And now, I'm afraid, it's grown cold. We must repay them in kind. Let their corpse cool out back when you're done.

54dd4f1c653937e81300425c_8.t: I don't have time for this. It doesn't matter who sent you - they're about to be sorely disappointed.

54dd4f1c653937e81300425c_9.t: Rooster's face contorts into a frown.
Find yourself funny, do you? You're pathetic.

54dd4f1c653937e81300425c_10.t: I don't have time for your shitty joke and false bravado. It doesn't matter who sent you - they're about to be sorely disappointed.

54dd4f1c653937e81300425c_12.t: Sweat drips down Rooster's gaunt face. His eyes dart back and forth across the room, trying to size you up - assess the situation. Without support, he appears nothing more than a trapped rooster who, without his talons, is defenseless. In an effort to invoke a sense of authority, he raises his voice.
W-who are you? What do you want?

54dd4f1c653937e81300425c_13.t: Zhou, where is the Talon? Where is the rest of my security?
A second passes while his eyes scour the room, looking for someone - anyone - to come to his aid.
N-never you mind. Just get this street tramp out of here!

54dd4f1c653937e81300425c_19.t: Kidnap? Who do you think I am - some five-year-old in a park?

54dd4f1c653937e81300425c_20.t: How dare you speak to me as if I were a child!

54de7152653937a40b00028b_0.t: No, wait! Wait... Spare my life, I beg you. Please!
Drops of spit spatter from his mouth, his desperation obvious.

54de7152653937a40b00028b_5.t: Rooster's teeth are clattering inside his head. His frame shakes uncontrollably. The realization that he's at your mercy - with no one left to defend him - is setting in.

54de7152653937a40b00028b_6.t: Rooster stammers incoherently for a moment, but his words soon catch up with him.
I-It's just outside of this room. To the right. But... but it's locked.

54de7152653937a40b00028b_7.t: He blinks, confused, his shocked mind slowly processing your implication.
He reaches into his coat pocket and withdraws a small key.

54de7152653937a40b00028b_11.t: His mouth twists downward in disgust.
What a waste of our time. A bullet in his head, and we could march outta here easy. Unless this guy knows another way out, we're not going anywhere with him raving at our side.

54e2771c653937bc1600027f_1.t: The words start pouring out of Rooster's mouth so quickly that they blur together.
You free me, get me away from here, and I guarantee you'll be rewarded. Nuyen, favors, power... Whatever you want!

54e2771c653937bc1600027f_5.t: Rooster looks up at them, irate. His fury fizzles upon spotting the thugs' various and ample gutlery.
N-no! Why, I can... I can make you rich! I--

54e4ced9343061c011000f9d_1.t: At the sight of the Talon, you see Rooster's body relax. His voice cracks.
Talon! I'm over here! I've got weapons trained on me - can't move. Save me, and we'll get out of here. Take this incident straight to the top. They'll herald our survival, reward us--

54e4ced9343061c011000f9d_3.t: Rooster is trembling with fear... or anger. Or both. He raises a shaky finger and points it at the Talon.
You can't speak to me that way. You're beneath me! I'm still a Red Pole, and when the triad comes for me, I'll see you flayed for those words.

54e4ced9343061c011000f9d_5.t: Rooster's fake bravado melts away. Eyes wide, he looks desperately around him, trapped like a mouse in a snake's coil - enemies on each side. His voice is small, pleading.
Talon... *Johnny,* please. Please help me. It wasn't all bad, right? We had some good runs. You remember.

54e4ced9343061c011000f9d_6.t: I had no choice, Johnny! I was forced to surrender! But if you help me - get me out of here - I can see to it that you're rewarded. Even promoted! How would you like that?

54e4ced9343061c011000f9d_9.t: At the sight of the Talon, you see Rooster's body relax. His voice cracks.
Talon! I'm over here! I've got weapons trained on me - can't move. Save me, and we'll get out of here. Take this incident straight to the top. They'll herald our survival, reward us--

54e4ced9343061c011000f9d_11.t: Rooster is trembling with fear... or anger. Or both. He raises a shaky finger and points it at the Talon.
You can't speak to me that way. You're beneath me! I'm still a Red Pole, and when the triad comes for me, I'll see you flayed for those words.

54e4ced9343061c011000f9d_13.t: Rooster's fake bravado melts away. Eyes wide, he looks desperately around him, trapped like a mouse in a snake's coil - enemies on each side. His voice is small, pleading.
Talon... *Johnny,* please. Please help me. It wasn't all bad, right? We had some good runs. You remember.

54e4ced9343061c011000f9d_14.t: I had no choice, Johnny! I was forced to surrender! But if you help me - get me out of here - I can see to it that you're rewarded. Even promoted! How would you like that?

54ecfca8343061e01b00108f_1.t: Rooster flinches at your approach. When he speaks, he tries to sound confident, but his voice cracks.
The 289s are gonna jam you up for this.

54ecfca8343061e01b00108f_10.t: Rooster flinches at your approach. When he speaks, he tries to sound confident, but his voice cracks.
The 289s are gonna jam you up for this. And you just wait until the Talon recovers! You'll be dead by the end of the week.

552f062b323431e41b0069fa_4.t: He lifts his doughy face from the fire to squint at you.

552f062b323431e41b0069fa_14.t: He lifts his doughy face from the fire to squint at you.

552f062b323431e41b0069fa_26.t: Refresh my memory... didn't we just help peel a swarm of devil rats off of them a few seconds ago?

552f062b323431e41b0069fa_54.t: Patrick licks his lips and nods, wide-eyed.
As long as we have her to guide us, we'll pull through. Why don't you follow us? We'll take you to her.

552f062b323431e41b0069fa_63.t: This is our home. Our *nest.* We ain't gonna leave it, no matter what--

555cf6063234318c180000a7_1.t: Whoa, hold up. The damned anti-boarding system's gone haywire again.
He points at the crackling pool.
Look. It isn't draining the way it's supposed to.

555cf6063234318c180000a7_7.t: Well. It isn't *that* dangerous, all things considered.
He glances at you and Is0bel. Sizes you up.
You've got boots on, right? You could probably make it across without--

555e6218626632302300162c_3.t: The dwarf yanks his weapon from its holster with a trembling hand. His cheeks are flushed.
Stupid girl! You're not supposed to tell them! It'll tear us to shreds if it finds out!

555e6218626632302300162c_9.t: Her dwarf companion calls after you.
Hurry back! We need to get you downstairs to meet with Malvina.

555e6218626632302300162c_10.t: Her dwarf companion calls after you.
Hurry back! Cadmus doesn't have long, after all.

555e6218626632302300162c_11.t: Her dwarf companion calls after you.
Hurry back! We can't leave Malvina waiting...

561444d664366150150022f8_6.t: Racter's murderous drone scuttled forward with alarming speed, its chassis all hard edges and gleaming metal. It reared back, splaying its killing legs wide.

561444d664366150150022f8_12.t: A low voice growled out from beside the bar.
Fuck me sideways, Jimmy. Have I gotta do everything myself around here? What do I even keep you around for?

561444d664366150150022f8_18.t: Yeah? And, uh... what kind of business are we talking about?

561444d664366150150022f8_22.t: If the old lady wanted a war, she'd've sent her soldiers, not a hired hand.
He smiled at you, his gold-capped teeth glinting in the light.
Can't help but notice that she didn't do that.

561444d664366150150022f8_37.t: Pang's eyes burned. He growled at you through clenched teeth..

561444d664366150150022f8_42.t: Yeah, and wouldn't that be terrible? We certainly don't want any trouble with the police. 'Course, you'd be dead, so...
He spread his hands with a smile.

561444d664366150150022f8_43.t: What business is it of yours?
His mouth dips into a frown. He holds the expression for a few seconds, then shakes his head.
...Y'know what? I'm gonna go ahead and answer that for you. It's none of your fucking business. The rock belongs to me now, end of story.

561444d664366150150022f8_45.t: He began to chuckle. An ugly sound, thick with malice.
I don't think so, errand girl.

561444d664366150150022f8_47.t: He began to chuckle. An ugly sound, thick with malice.
I don't think so, errand boy.

561444d664366150150022f8_52.t: Kitchen's closed, sweetheart. Unless you wanna man the oven yourself, chicken's off the table... but if you come back for happy hour sometime, I'd be happy to hook you up.
He shifts his gaze to you.
Now, if you want the rock, I've gotta assume that you're on Cheng's payroll. That true?

561444d664366150150022f8_56.t: Kitchen's closed anyway, sweetheart. Come back for happy hour sometime, and I'll hook you up.
He shifts his gaze to you.
Now. If you're after the rock, I've gotta assume that you're on Cheng's payroll. That true?

56157f2a643661e00e0026a7_4.t: It's behind the bar. The rock... it's behind the damned bar.
His voice was leaden, his cheeks flushed.

56157f2a643661e00e0026a7_14.t: You killed the boss. Took our heavy out, too. All for a fucking rock.
Guess that makes me in charge. Now, what should I do with you...?

56157f2a643661e00e0026a7_18.t: I'm thinkin' that maybe I'll teach you a lesson instead. My boys need to see that I'm as strong as Eddie was.
He made a hand signal, and the remaining gangers flung themselves back into the fray.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_2.t: The man named Opti is draped in charms and magical fetishes - particularly raven feathers. He regards you for a moment, and shakes his head in disgust.
A damn near perfect operation, ruined because you had to charge in here, guns blazing. Christ. How's it coming, Bull?

54ff5820306364a01b004921_15.t: Opti looks you up and down, a crooked grin erupting across his face.
For such a big shot, you sure roll loud.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_18.t: Honestly, we very nearly screwed this job, too. We went up to the executive penthouses looking for access codes to the lab.
Opti produces a keycard and holds it up so you can see.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_19.t: Pulled this thing out of a locked box in Hardingham's penthouse. We were sure it would open the door to this lab.
Opti snorts and stares disgustedly at the card.
Nope. Had to cut through the lock to get in here.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_23.t: Opti considers this for a long moment, and then tosses you the keycard.
Sure. It's not doing us any good. Consider it a peace offering.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_59.t: Well, I guess there's nothing we can do about it now.
Opti quickly checks over his gear and weapons, reassuring himself they're all still in working order.
Can we get a move on? We're all gonna be up shit creek if we don't get out of here.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_60.t: We flew in from Seattle for this job. We're planning a few more before we head home, but this isn't exactly the best start to our little vacation...
Opti quickly checks over his gear and weapons, reassuring himself they're all still in working order.
Listen, I hate to bring down the mood, but we need to get the hell out of here.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_66.t: We had to go up there, Dizzy. Without the keycards to get into the lab, we're as good as dead. Bull could've hacked the door, but his deck got slagged after you threw that Rigger into him and broke his concentration.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_71.t: Opti shoots an angry look at bull's keycard.
Uh... Would it be too much to ask to have you open the door?

54ff5820306364a01b004921_80.t: Probably the same thing as you. That lab has a laser prototype, yeah? That's what we're here for. It just looks like you got to it first.
Opti glances over his shoulder nervously.
Do you think we could have this conversation inside?

550b0530306364f8110000d8_52.t: Opti twists his neck to one side, vertebrae popping loudly.
Are you kidding me, Jarl? That's the kind of job worth doing! Hell, junkies pay ten nuyen a chip to get that kind of rush!

550b0530306364f8110000d8_57.t: Yeah, yeah... I guess you have a point. But we'd only known them what, two weeks?
Opti turns and nods toward you.
What now? We make a run for the MTR station?

550b0530306364f8110000d8_92.t: Opti takes the prototype from you, detaching the GPS tracker and handing it back to you.
You, are a real stand up individual. So what now? Do you want us to leave, or can we stick with you until we get to the MTR station?

5513336d306364cc040080ec_5.t: Opti exhales a sigh of relief.
It's not every day you have a run go that sideways and live to tell about it. I don't think I'd have been able to get out of that jam without your help. Still... Beats the hell out of a desk job.

5513336d306364cc040080ec_7.t: Opti extends a hand to you.
Keep safe out there. This world's tough enough without losing more decent people, you hear me?

5525b4a530636400170026bd_0.t: This woman sports brilliantly-hued hair that shifts colors, scintillating with every passing moment. She is powerfully built - a natural athlete, or someone who works out a great deal. Notably, a large shotgun rests on an arcade cabinet next to her. She affords you a short glance as you approach. When she speaks, you note a peculiarly lyrical accent to her voice.

5525b4a530636400170026bd_2.t: Well, sure isn't it the reason they call me 'Rainbow'. Rainbow Smite.
She kicks one foot toward the shotgun laying against the cabinet.
That shooter's why there's also a 'Smite.'

5525b4a530636400170026bd_3.t: The woman's mouth twists up into a wry grin.
Aye, me neither. Managed to find this one near by accident. But I heard the music and thought 'Hold on there, Rainbow... You know that tune.'

5525b4a530636400170026bd_4.t: After a few moments, Rainbow's game is suddenly over - her tank obliterated by a barrage of air-to-ground missiles. She hisses her breath in through clenched teeth.
Piss on your ma! A right robbery, that was.

5525b4a530636400170026bd_5.t: Rainbow shakes her head again, and turns to face you fully.
You don't seem like them Whampoans. They're all a might touched, yeah? Maybe you are too, but you don't advertise it like they do. What's your story?

5525b4a530636400170026bd_15.t: Bang on! And I don't look a day over twenty five, do I?
Rainbow flexes one arm, grinning widely.
Aye. I'm Irish, not one of them Noggins. Pack of punters, the lot of 'em. Always going on about their ancestral lands and the glory of the past and all that bullshite.

5525b4a530636400170026bd_17.t: The skivers in the new Tir didn't pay for the land they took - they just had a pack of bullies with guns.
Rainbow scrunches her face up into a disgusted sneer, and mock spits at the ground.
I've always been sympathetic to the little guy in a fight. They offered to pay for my help, so I said yes, simple as that.

5525b4a530636400170026bd_22.t: I was out in Jakarta last year, hunting one of them Indonesian vampires... You ever heard of 'em? Call 'em "leyak," or "penanggalans," depending on who you ask. Their heads and guts come out of the body cavity, and they fly around like a giant messy pile of pasta.
Rainbow bares her teeth and pantomimes floating around.
Took me a solid two months to track her down, but I did.

5525b4a530636400170026bd_24.t: Been spending a lot of time down in the storm drains these days. I heard rumors of a 'rat king' down there - where a mess of devil rats get all tied together into some kind of big, awful, rat-beast. Haven't tracked it down yet... But I figure it's only a matter of time, yeah?
She pats the barrel of her shotgun affectionately.

5525b4a530636400170026bd_26.t: Aye, sure. Watch your step down there, though... The drains are a might bigger than people'd normally expect.

5525b4a530636400170026bd_28.t: Rainbow doesn't answer immediately. Instead, she stares at you like you're some kind of alien.
Oooookay. That's not the answer I expected. What do you mean, stacked like firewood? Can you describe the pile in more detail?

5589ee2a303031b009007f83_0.t: The woman eyes your uniform. She smiles.
Something I can do for you?

5589ee2a303031b009007f83_2.t: The woman's eyes search you, like she's trying to remember whether she's met you before.

5589ee2a303031b009007f83_3.t: Then I suppose you must be from Contracts & Acquisitions. When will your team stop pestering us?

5589ee2a303031b009007f83_6.t: She crosses her arms. Satisfaction radiates from her smile.
That's Sales for you. And if Acquisitions happens to approach you, just tell them you're with us.

5589ee2a303031b009007f83_7.t: Well, don't think for too long. Opportunities to join my team are highly coveted. This offer won't last forever.

5589ee2a303031b009007f83_11.t: Her mouth drops open, but after the initial surprise drains away, she looks doubtful.
What kind of issue are we talking about?

5589ee2a303031b009007f83_13.t: She stifles a gasp, and then glances nervously around the room.
I heard about that on the trid!

5589ee2a303031b009007f83_14.t: Everyone, this man has just informed me that there's a maintenance emergency in our area. I need all of you to drop what you're doing and leave so they can make their repairs.

5589ee2a303031b009007f83_15.t: She straightens up and immediately takes on an air of authority.
Our time *is* valuable, as befits a team of our importance. I appreciate the respect and high priority that we've been given. We'll return shortly, so you'd best be finished by the time we're back.

5589ee2a303031b009007f83_22.t: Her upper lip curls in anger.

You have no right to traipse in here and start making unfounded demands of me and my workers. Any and all repairs you need to make must be made after we've left at the end of the business day. If you have a problem with this, take it up with security.

5589ee2a303031b009007f83_24.t: Without a word to you, she raises her voice and addresses the office.
Everyone, out! A fire drill's about to hit, and I want us in our safety station before the other departments even hear the alarm. Let's move it!

5589ee2a303031b009007f83_25.t: She hesitates. Looks at her workers, then back at you.
Yes... Yes, I appreciate you telling us. It won't do to have our team be the last ones leaving the vicinity. We have a reputation to uphold. All right, everyone. No time to pack up! We need to go.

5589ee2a303031b009007f83_28.t: The woman glances at you from over her clipboard.
I've no time to take outside inquiries right now. Please come back another time.

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_10.t: The human went as white as a sheet, the blood draining from his cheeks.
I, uh... we're not afraid of you... I mean, why do you even care if we rob this store? What are you even doing here?

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_11.t: His eyes went wide, and he raised his hands in surrender.
Okay! Okay! I'm sorry! We don't want any trouble with you! Just, uh, tell me how to make this right, okay? Tell me what I can do.

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_12.t: The human went as white as a sheet, the blood draining from his cheeks. Looked like he recognized the name.
I, uh... we're not afraid of you... I mean, why does your boss even care if we rob this store? Yellow Lotus never come around these parts.

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_14.t: His eyes went wide, and he raised his hands in surrender.
Okay! Okay! I'm sorry! Just, uh, tell me how to make this right, okay? The Black Sun Boys don't want no trouble with the Yellow Lotus!

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_19.t: There, now. We good? If me an' Needlenose keep doing our thing, will you leave us in peace? We don't want trouble with no shadowrunner.

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_26.t: He cleared his throat and spat, then wiped his lips with a grimace.
All this ash in the air, it's fuckin' up my sinuses. Makes me wanna hurt somebody... maybe someone like you.

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_27.t: He opened his mouth to retort, but something in his expression changed as he looked you up and down. He swallowed, his Adam's apple bobbing up and down with the motion.
I, uh... we're not afraid of you... I mean, why do you even care whether we rob this store? What's it to you, anyway? Hurry up, Needlenose. Get that door open *now!*

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_28.t: The troll at the door panel grunted in response.
Don't rush me! I think I've almost--

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_30.t: His lip curled into an ugly sneer.
Yeah, well we don't *need* luck. Needlenose here is real good with electrical stuff. He'll have this thing open in no ti--

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_32.t: Ow! Goddamned piece of shit panel!
He rubbed his burnt and blackened fingertips, cursing like a sailor under his breath.
I told you not to rush me, man! Now my fingers are all burnt!

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_33.t: The human looter shook his head in disgust.
Good job, Needlenose. Really great work. We're gonna have a talk about this when we get back home. And as for you, stranger... I hope that the cops put a bullet in you. Go to hell.

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_34.t: What do I look like, a goddamned newsfax? Can't you see that we're *busy?*
He turns his head and spits. An enormous glob of phlegm spatters against the asphalt.

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_36.t: The human went as white as a sheet, the blood draining from his cheeks. Looked like he recognized the name.
I, uh... we're not afraid of you... I mean, why does your boss even care if we rob this store? Yellow Lotus never come around these parts.

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_38.t: Okay! Okay! Go easy! We don't want no trouble with the Yellow Lotus, and we've got no quarrel with you. Just tryin' to do our thing here, all right? You, ah... you got questions, maybe I can answer 'em. You know, as a show of good faith?

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_44.t: Yeah, I might. What'd finding out be worth to you?

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_45.t: He raised his hands in surrender.
All right, all right. Can't blame me for tryin' to make a few bucks, can you? I'll answer your question, but then you go. All right?

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_49.t: He reached for your credstick, an ugly smile spreading across his face. As he was about to curl his fingers around the plastic of the stick, he turned back to his companion.
Y'hear that, Needlenose? This night is payin' off for us already. I've got our beer money right here--

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_51.t: Ow! Goddamned piece of shit panel!
He rubbed his burnt and blackened fingertips, cursing like a sailor under his breath.
Be lucky if those cops around the corner didn't hear that--
He broke his sentence off, biting his tongue, his cheeks turning red with embarrassment.

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_52.t: The human looter stared at you for a long moment, then withdrew his hand. He shook his head in disgust.
Good job, Needlenose. Really great work.

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_54.t: Shut up. Just... shut your damned mouth. We're gonna have a talk about this when we get back home.
He turned back toward you, all full of righteous indignation. Not enough to pick a fight, though.
I hope that the cops put a bullet in you.

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_55.t: He swiped the credstick from your hand with a sneer.
Yeah. Yeah, we'll do that. The hell with this anyway. C'mon, Needlenose. This is a waste of time, let's get out of here.

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_56.t: He reached for your credstick, an ugly smile spreading across his face. Swiping it out of your hand, he tucked the stick away in a pocket.

Yeah, there are cops in the area. They're real trigger happy, too... lots of shooting, and not many questions. You'll find a whole pack of 'em just around the corner, led by one of those Special Duties assholes. My advice? You'll get out of this alley and indoors, and you'll do it fast.

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_94.t: I'm done talking to you, man. Walk away and let us get back to our business.
He waved you away with a calloused hand.

563e72903063645422006c84_1.t: The elf's eyes flutter open. She turns to you with a sad frown on her face.
Really thought I had it that time.

563e72903063645422006c84_2.t: The perfect aroma to accompany my new tea. I call it "An Existential Crisis While Stargazing at Dawn." Or "Peach Dew." I'm torn between the two.
I've nearly got it. The taste is there, but the smell...

563e72903063645422006c84_3.t: She sighs, and a lock of red hair tumbles onto her cheek.
Guess I'll have to brew another batch. But this time, maybe a hint more cinnamon.

563e72903063645422006c84_4.t: She shakes her head and looks at you. Her disappointment quickly transforms into a wide-eyed, beaming smile. She leans in close.
My name's Witchita Red, but you can just call me Red. What brings you to my tea shop?

563e72903063645422006c84_6.t: The elf gingerly sets the teacup on top of the counter.

563e72903063645422006c84_7.t: Her frowns quickly transforms into a wide-eyed, beaming smile. She leans in close.
My name's Witchita Red, but you can just call me Red. What brings you to my tea shop?

563e72903063645422006c84_9.t: Her smile broadens into a toothy grin. She raises her hands and points backward over her shoulders to the racks of tea cannisters behind her.
I've got you covered, buddy. Oh, have I ever.

563e72903063645422006c84_11.t: Oh! That reminds me - I just received, not two hours ago, a shipment of a super rare purple-green tea! You might have heard it referred to by its nickname, pureen tea. I know that it sounds strange--
Red pitches her voice and pretends to be talking as you as she wiggles her hands by her head.
"But, Red! Green tea can't be purple - it's *green*!"

563e72903063645422006c84_12.t: Well, you'd be *right!* But *this* green tea has been turned purple by the blessing of an Appalachian monk, which also imbues the tea with restorative powers. No joke! Pureen tea can boost the immune system by three-hundred percent! But the effect only lasts as long as it's in your system, and let's be real, it doesn't come cheap.

563e72903063645422006c84_13.t: If that sounds a little out of your price range, I've also got a broad selection of tea accessories, like this brand-spanking-new kettle cleaner!

She grabs a small device from behind the counter and raises it for your inspection. She gently depresses the contraption's handle with her fingertip, and the machine responds with a hushed whirring noise.
Would you like me to demonstrate?

563e72903063645422006c84_16.t: Not a problem! I've got a large selection of caffeine-free herbal teas, and many of my most popular teas have decaffeinated variants. Would you like me to show you?

563e72903063645422006c84_17.t: She slowly waves her hand as she watches you run away, a confused and somewhat rattled expression on her face.

563e72903063645422006c84_20.t: Her eyes dance over the ceiling as she thinks.
Well, there *is* the drone room. But only employees with the door code can get through it, so I'm afraid you'll just have to use the front door like everyone else.

563e72903063645422006c84_24.t: Red carefully pours herself a cup of the freshly brewed tea. Her eyes stay locked onto the steaming liquid.
You're absolutely right. But, unfortunately for you, I love my job, and I wouldn't jeopardize it for anything.

563e72903063645422006c84_25.t: Her eyes glance at the nuyen on the table. She mutters to herself.
Mm, I really *could* use a new steam infuser...really brings out the subtler flavor of herbs.

563e72903063645422006c84_26.t: She thrusts her arms into the air and stretches them above her head in an exaggerated yawn. As her arms drop, her hand lands on your proffered credstick, and she slides it slowly toward herself. With a quick tuck into her pocket, she swipes the stick and hands it back. She removes a pen from her jacket and busily scribbles something on the back of a tea sample packet. Red's mouth curls into a mischievous smile.
Drone room door - this oughta get you through it.

563e72903063645422006c84_28.t: You find Red at the back of the store with what looks like a new pot of tea.
Ahh, changed your mind? I've got *quite* the selection here. We can start at the beginning, if you'd like. Would an alphabetic breakdown work best for you, or would you prefer to go by type?

563e72903063645422006c84_29.t: Her lips pucker into a tiny pout, but she nods her head understandingly.
Oh. Okay. You'll come around, you know. So, what do you need?

563e72903063645422006c84_33.t: Red's eyes widen, and her face goes dead serious.
Contaminated? Oh, no, not here. I sell only the *finest,* *purest* teas available, and I personally inspect each load we're delivered. You can *count* on quality here.

563e72903063645422006c84_34.t: She chews on her lips as she considers your question.

5564c9c5323431a81f00212a_0.t: A tall, well-groomed man with jet black hair leans casually against his fruit stand. His hard features suggest that he hasn't smiled in a long time, but his demeanor isn't cold - only cautious. He appears less interested in watching his wares, and more interested in watching people.

5564c9c5323431a81f00212a_1.t: The man sees you approach and speaks, his inflection smooth and careful not at all like a fruit vendor's.
Fresh fruit. You here to buy?

5564c9c5323431a81f00212a_3.t: He studiously observes his surroundings before returning his gaze to you.
Name's Yang. But people 'round here call me "Steel Drake."

5564c9c5323431a81f00212a_7.t: He turns to his stand and, with a click, hits a hidden switch. What originally appeared to be a bin of apples now slowly slides up from the counter, revealing, just beneath the counter, a handful of guns, explosives, and other wicked gadgets.

5564c9c5323431a81f00212a_8.t: We waves his wrist toward the display.

5564c9c5323431a81f00212a_11.t: So, you're local. That's what people 'round these parts call me.
It's just a nickname. You know how that goes.

5564c9c5323431a81f00212a_13.t: What are you looking to buy? Soya, mycoprotien, kaf? I've got a whole cartload of products.

5564c9c5323431a81f00212a_14.t: I might be able to help you there. I've an extended stock for special customers like yourself, with only the finest and freshest intel... among other things.

5564c9c5323431a81f00212a_17.t: They didn't miss me. They probably just know better than to mess with one of their top earners.

55777381303031d015002cd7_0.t: The loading dock supervisor is glued to his monitor, his hand holding a sound piece up to his right ear. You hear a faint cheer come from the device, and he shoves his fist into the air in triumph.
With his eyes now off the screen, he spots you.

55777381303031d015002cd7_1.t: He pauses as he takes in your appearance.
You really the special courier Wuxing sent to deliver our load of enviro controller chips?

55777381303031d015002cd7_3.t: You see his eyes dart over the ID. He glances at you before sighing and pushing it away.
Checks out. Huh. Delivery standards have really dropped these days...

55777381303031d015002cd7_7.t: Yeah, I get it. Books, covers, don't judge them, whatever. Still, I need to see your credentials.

55777381303031d015002cd7_9.t: The dock supervisor glances up from his screen, eager not to miss any of his program.
What're you doing back here? Hurry and drop the load at security.

5589ef11303031b009007f94_0.t: The acquisitions manager looks at you eagerly.

5589ef11303031b009007f94_1.t: Yes, perfect. Thank you. As promised, here is the code.
A stroke of fear seems to freeze her.
I'm... not going to get into any trouble, right? You covered your trail?

5589ef11303031b009007f94_2.t: The woman before you looks like one very burnt-out suit.

5589ef11303031b009007f94_3.t: She chews on her lip, unsure. With a sigh, she speaks.
Yeah... why not? Doubt I'll ever see you again. I could use a good venting session.

5589ef11303031b009007f94_4.t: It's the stack ranking here. My team, Contracts & Acquisitions, is in the deep end, but *not* because we're slackers like Sales would have you believe.
The way she bites into the word "Sales" reeks of enmity.

5589ef11303031b009007f94_6.t: Come this quarterly review, whoever's last in the stack rankings will get demoted. Or worse - sent back to the training pool.

5589ef11303031b009007f94_8.t: She gives you a long look.

5589ef11303031b009007f94_12.t: She chews on her lip. Nods.
Okay. I'll do it. The Sales team's data for the station code - that's the deal.

5589ef11303031b009007f94_14.t: The elf in front of you is nothing short of exhausted. Her disheveled appearance and red-ringed eyes scream burnout. A tired, listless voice greets you.

5589ef11303031b009007f94_16.t: Her nose wrinkles, and lips purse. You can tell she doesn't trust you.
There's nothing you can do about it, anyway. Nothing anyone can do. Now, excuse me. I have to get back to work.

5589ef11303031b009007f94_17.t: She gives you a dubious glance.
I need to work. Please leave me alone.

5589ef11303031b009007f94_18.t: The manager's eyes light up at your approach.
Thank you again. If there's anything my team can do to help you in the future, you let me know.

55a59c51306364900e0061c3_1.t: This tall, well-build human has been watching you since the moment you entered Club 88. Surreptitiously, of course - glances over his shoulder and the like - but you're certain he's been watching you. He finally speaks as you draw near.
I hear you're the one to talk to about work in the shadows.

55a59c51306364900e0061c3_2.t: He nods in satisfaction, expression relaxing a bit.
Good. Because I'm looking for work here in Hong Kong, but I don't have my own team.

55a59c51306364900e0061c3_4.t: He shrugs and folds his arms across his chest.
I'm looking for work here in Hong Kong, but I don't have my own team. I asked around who the players are here in Heoi. They said to talk to you.

55a59c51306364900e0061c3_6.t: Ducé raises a hand to you as you approach.
Hey. Any chance we'll get any jobs running against Shiawase?

55a59c51306364900e0061c3_10.t: Because the sons of bitches killed my wife and stole my son.
Ducé's voice is hard and flat.
Anything to screw them over, I don't need to get paid for.

55a59c51306364900e0061c3_13.t: That's right. And it's not just blood, either. If a company wants to produce bioware on the cheap, they use SINless Type-O bodies as templates to grow the 'ware in. Human factories, basically.

55a59c51306364900e0061c3_14.t: Sick stuff, but it's not like we can complain to anybody. Officially, we don't even exist.
Shaking his head, Ducé looks away.
Let's talk about something else, okay?

55a59c51306364900e0061c3_16.t: Good. The bastards were in it for a cheap payday - just another body to add to their assembly line. You ever heard of a Type-O immune system?

55a59c51306364900e0061c3_18.t: Gesturing toward the rest of the bar, Ducé surveys the dingy interior with an uncritical eye.

Places like this exist all over the world - dives where cops won't come in except with riot gear. Where people like us gather around and try to figure out how to make our next nuyen. Find the right one, you can find any kind of work you want.

55a59c51306364900e0061c3_19.t: Nope. Originally, I was in IT. You know - tech support.

56218ac8306364fc18005d11_1.t: Hey, wait a second... We're going the wrong way!
El Ducé gestures wildly towards the tram windows.
Over there, on the left! That's where we're supposed to be headed!

56393981306364d40d007911_4.t: Owch! What the heck, man? That was my foot!
He follows his friend's eyeline to where you're standing. His eyebrows raise in surprise.

56393981306364d40d007911_5.t: Yeah, well, it's over. Without better software, there's no way I'm getting into Hilton's system.
The ork turns his back to the table and leans against it. He thinks for a moment, his mouth a frown.
It's too bad. From what I've heard, this'll be the biggest movie in *years.*

56393981306364d40d007911_6.t: Anyway! What do you want, eavesdropper? Did somethin' break?

56393981306364d40d007911_8.t: Chow rolls his eyes at his friend.
Oh, shut *up,* Keenan. Let them speak for themself. Go on, stranger. Tell us what you need.

56393981306364d40d007911_10.t: That's because I was adopted, you insensitive ass.
He runs a hand over his head, then gives it a good shake, as if trying to dismiss your comment.
I was born in Madrid, but I grew up in Mong Kok. Been here since I was three years old.

56393981306364d40d007911_11.t: He looks you in the eyes and gives you a small smirk.
Considering your own sloppy Cantonese, I'm sure you can tell that *my* Cantonese is on par with the natives. 'Cause I *grew up here.*

56393981306364d40d007911_12.t: Because I'm cooped up in this damn tower all day, helping the ultra-rich with the most basic of technical problems. Hence my colleague's earlier comment.
I don't get a lot of time off. But when I *do,* I need to blow off some serious steam. And who wouldn't wanna walk the red carpet with all of the stars?

56393981306364d40d007911_17.t: He gives his friend a wild look, lips drawn back in a snarl.
I saw them this time, you limpdick!

56393981306364d40d007911_19.t: Chow rubs his shin with a grimace. He glances at you.

56393981306364d40d007911_22.t: Sorry, dude. It's locked. Only myself and one other guy have the code.
He stands up straight - puffs out his chest a little. You can practically see the self-importance wafting off of him.
Can't give it to just *anyone,* y'know?

56393981306364d40d007911_27.t: Okay! I'll do it! Just don't go all knife-happy on us, all right?
He jots a number down on a scrap of paper and slides it to you.
There, are you happy? Now leave us alone!

56393981306364d40d007911_31.t: The ork's head jerks up at your words. He looks at the other two technicians, whose mouths hang open in surprise. They all exchange glances, and then nod simultaneously.
Oh, hell yes. You've got yourself a deal!
He jots a number down on a scrap of paper and slides it to you.

56393981306364d40d007911_32.t: You're pretty resourceful, aren't ya? But how can I be sure that *you* aren't the one that security's looking for?
He holds a straight face for a few seconds, then covers his mouth and laughs.

56393981306364d40d007911_33.t: Yeah... *right.* Not with that little girl of yours. You're more likely to piss off the PTA than a corporation like Fuchi. You've got yourself a deal!
He jots a number down on a scrap of paper and slides it to you.

56393981306364d40d007911_34.t: His chest deflates. It would seem that his confidence only extends as far as the door.
Ahh... no can do, chief. Rules are rules, and I don't wanna get chewed out by the boss.

56393981306364d40d007911_35.t: He screws up his face in concentration.
...I mean, I guess there's always the freight elevator.... but it's on a different security circuit.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_18.t: My evenings have been peaceful. No dreams of the past, the present, the abstract or macabre. Just dreams of Go.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_34.t: The facade works in my favor. Occasionally, these two forget I'm decades their elder, and they get cocky in our games. An unexpected, but welcome, side effect of my treatments.
Lau's dark eyes sparkle inside his otherwise unreadable face.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_47.t: The young man breaks eye contact with the game to glance your way.
This game is very important to us. You must leave us to concentrate on it.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_56.t: He looks to be in his early thirties, but it's difficult to tell. The skin on his face is smooth and supple, with only the suggestions of creases at the eye, mouth, and forehead. But small gestures - a blink, a flare of his nostrils - briefly reveal the lines of his face. The man's eyes rise from the Go board and study the elders. He nods.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_63.t: The younger man's voice cuts through the din like a razor, and the elders fall silent. He turns to you and he locks his eyes - dark orbs that burn with piercing scrutiny - onto yours. He nods, and returns his gaze to the Go board.
I believe you're trustworthy. Please, make yourself comfortable. I'll share my dream with you.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_64.t: He exhales slowly, then begins speaking.

I dreamed of a long, dingy hall. When faced with such a thing, one generally walks its length, so I lifted my right foot and placed it in front of me. But upon taking that first step, I found myself instead drawn down an alleyway to my left. It wasn't there before, this alley. It appeared only as began to walk.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_65.t: As I moved down the alley, I found myself surrounded by friends and loved ones. They all stood there, silently watching me with smiles on their faces. And as I passed each one of them, they fell to the ground in my wake, dropping like puppets with severed strings. Somehow, I knew that if I followed this road to the end, I could have everything I'd ever dreamed of. But I woke before I could reach it.
He folds his hands in his lap.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_73.t: Lau's voice is firm, but soft.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_106.t: Yes, we're all still having the dreams.
Lau's voice is calm, but troubled.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_117.t: Excuse us. Our friend is tired.
Lau clasps a hand on Shyu's shoulder, who doesn't seem to notice.
These dreams have taken a toll on all of us. Please come back some other time.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_125.t: I'm in agreement with Gin. You've gone too far. I suggest you take a moment to collect yourself.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_127.t: I, for one, enjoy our young friend's visits.
A hint of a smile touches Lau's lips as Gin grumbles to himself.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_134.t: An eerie mood sweeps over the three men, their previous energy replaced with uncomfortable stillness. The men's moods appear to shift between fear and sadness. A silent minute passes before Lau speaks.
I'm surprised that you haven't heard. These dreams have taken a local man's life.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_138.t: Their expressions sober, humor replaced with uncomfortable stillness. The men's moods appear to shift between fear and sadness. A silent minute passes before Lau speaks.
I'm surprised you haven't heard. These dreams have taken someone's life - a local man.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_139.t: Your kindness is appreciated. We are doing well enough, though we're struggling with this new reality the dreams have presented us.
It must have been something horrific to witness that man's death. To think, he likely had his whole life in front of him, and just like that... he's destroyed by his dreams.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_141.t: Lau's face remains a mask of placidity, but his his dark eyes betray his distress.
We'd all do well to remember that this world is filled with dangers. For many of us outside the Walled City, it can be easy to forget threats beyond sickness and age.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_142.t: Very little. Only that the man was raving about his dreams before he died.
Lau's face remains a mask of placidity, but his his dark eyes betray his distress.
We'd all do well to remember that this world is filled with dangers. For many of us outside the Walled City, it can be easy to forget threats beyond sickness and age.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_145.t: The attack was subtle. It infected us through our dreams, and now...
Lau raises his right hand. Opens and closes it. He turns it over and stares at the veins snaked beneath the skin's surface.

54dbe9e13030316c15006ca2_156.t: I, for one, enjoy our young friend's visits.
A hint of a smile touches Lau's lips as Gin grumbles to himself.

5525b2c6306364441500207f_0.t: The man in front of this stall is rooting through a box of music chips with the swift fingers of someone who knows what they're looking for. Behind him, a music player pumps out a constant stream of distorted, atonal music. The stall's proprietor is nowhere to be seen.

5525b2c6306364441500207f_3.t: The music afficionado waves his hand dismissively.

The show might have been great, but come on. Echelon Sixty's old news. They're already being mainstreamed - I saw a trid ad for the new Mitsubishi Ostro scooter, and they were using 'Main Volt Under-Bus' as the backing music. Made me sad, I tell you.

5525b2c6306364441500207f_5.t: See, your friend gets it! Real talent, that crew. Like nothing I've ever heard before!

5525b2c6306364441500207f_6.t: The decker inclines his head politely toward you.
Moe Jnebi, at your service. Decker, technologist, music fan. You're not from around here, are you? You don't hold yourself like a native.

5525b2c6306364441500207f_7.t: Moe runs a hand through his hair, shaking the collected rain off of it.
I thought so. What brings you out here?

5525b2c6306364441500207f_8.t: The decker pauses a moment, face falling.

Oh, that's a nasty business. I heard Tong died tonight. I don't know who or what they pissed off, but it seems like a really bad scene. I'm trying to keep my nose out of it. Still... I like this place. Is there anything I can do to help?

5525b2c6306364441500207f_12.t: We're a collection of freaks, geeks, nerds, and tech junkies. This place may look like a third-world bazaar, but believe me: we have stuff here that's cutting edge in almost every field.
Moe swells with pride, and plants both hands firmly on his hips.
Maybe it's not as advanced as the stuff the megas have in their development labs, but it's as advanced as anything you can find in a store.

5525b2c6306364441500207f_14.t: The Red Dragon made a play four years ago, but we chased 'em out.
Moe gestures with both hands as if shooing away a bothersome insect.
Scooted off back to Ho Man Tin, and they've never been back.

5525b2c6306364441500207f_23.t: Moe purses his lips, his gaze going distant for a moment.
Well, I guess upper management was worried that info would hurt their stock ratings by a fraction of a point or two. So they fired the guy. With a.308 round, right through the skull.

5525b2c6306364441500207f_27.t: Moe chuckles, running a hand through his hair.
Well, it wasn't so bad at the time. But in the long run, I think you're right. I'm not as comfortable out here on the edge, but I'm a lot happier.

54beba923063641c1f0019a1_0.t: The ragged waiter manages to straighten up as you approach.
Good evening. Welcome to the Veranda at the Repulse Bay. How may I be of service?

54beba923063641c1f0019a1_3.t: Sorry, I'm just a waiter. Seems you need someone from maintenance, but most of our janitors and handymen are off-site tonight.
Can't risk the beautiful people bumping into them. It might offend their delicate sensibilities.

54beba923063641c1f0019a1_5.t: Tell you what. Try talking to Chef Kang in the kitchen. He's got keys to just about every door in the place. On VIP request, he's expected to personally deliver their meal. It's a status thing. "Compliments of the chef," or whatever. Poor guy spends as much time delivering food as he does cooking it.

54beba923063641c1f0019a1_11.t: The waiter opens his mouth to reply, then pauses.
Just how do you know about that? When were *you* in Ma's apartment?

54beba923063641c1f0019a1_20.t: Between you and me, he's... difficult. Which is putting it lightly. Man runs the staff ragged.
He jerks his thumb in the direction of the Veranda.
That's his party out there. Richies like him love to display their wealth.

54beba923063641c1f0019a1_21.t: Like you wouldn't believe. He once made a handful of us walk all the way out to Shek O to fetch dumplings for his guests. It was pouring out, with winds blowing around fifty kilometers per hour. Practically a death march in that weather.

54beba923063641c1f0019a1_25.t: And that's just *one* of our experiences with him. Needless to say, no one here's a fan of the man. But we're paid to serve the tenants here, so that's what we do.

54beba923063641c1f0019a1_33.t: Not yet. I can't afford to miss any tips.
I went to Macau last week, and it wiped me out. Bad luck with the horses, you know?

54beba923063641c1f0019a1_34.t: The waiter stares at you, suspicious, but his desperation gets the better of him.

54beba923063641c1f0019a1_37.t: Five hundred nuyen and the code is yours.
He blurts it out without a second thought.

54beba923063641c1f0019a1_38.t: He whips out his PDA and glances at the bank balance. A satisfied grin lights up his face.
Oh, and if you get caught, I didn't tell you anything.

54beba923063641c1f0019a1_39.t: Have fun with Ma's things. If some were to go missing, maybe wind up at a pawn shop, I doubt they'd be missed.

54beba923063641c1f0019a1_40.t: "Nuyen to spare" my ass. That makes you a liar *and* a cheapskate.
After a moment, he holds out his hand.
But... I'm willing to work with this. Every bit helps.

54beba923063641c1f0019a1_42.t: Something in the waiter's face softens as he looks at you.
Aaw, hell. If you're really that bad off, you need the money more than I do. The code is 1635. If you get caught, we never talked. Got it? Now, if you'll excuse me, I have work to do.

54beba923063641c1f0019a1_46.t: His brow furrows, but he looks sheepish.

560032c4626632e015004414_0.t: Hey! I got news about that friend of mine... you know, the one who was under the weather.
He gives you a wolfish grin.
He's been stabilized, thanks to you. Looks like he's gonna pull through.

560032c4626632e015004414_1.t: You should be. I haven't forgotten about our deal.
He kicks one of the duraplast crates with the side of his foot.
You want something from my special stock, you just say the word. I'll get you all set up.

560032c4626632e015004414_2.t: What a coincidence. I do, too.
He kicks one of the duraplast crates with the side of his foot.
You want something from my special stock, you just say the word. I'll get you all set up.

560032c4626632e015004414_3.t: Yeah, it is. Fair's fair, after all.
He kicks one of the duraplast crates with the side of his foot.
You want something from my special stock, you just say the word. I'll get you all set up.

560032c4626632e015004414_5.t: So, you're the new talent. The shadowrunner.
He rubs his nose with a sleeve.
The brass told me that you've got clearance to buy here, so your money's welcome. If you need supplies, I can outfit you for a reasonable price.

560032c4626632e015004414_10.t: He leans forward and lowers his voice.
...Of course, it's possible that an item or two could go missing, or that the numbers on my inventory sheet could be wrong. And the odds of that happening might increase if you did me a favor.

560032c4626632e015004414_14.t: His finger dances over the touchscreen of his PDA. The screen flashes twice in response.
Just forwarded you the pertinent details. You'll find them on your mission computer. Please, don't drag your feet on this. My friend really is in terrible trouble, and he won't hold out much longer.

560032c4626632e015004414_26.t: Got assigned to it, the same way that most of the constables here were. Believe it or not, this wasn't a volunteer assignment for most of us. When the Executive Council hands you an edict, you follow it.

560032c4626632e015004414_27.t: Don't really think there's all that much to say. Been on the force for twenty two years now, and I've been loaning out gear for most of it. Got a wife back home, a couple of kids. That's about it, really.
His sentence trails into an awkward silence.

54bd5af6323437fc31001310_4.t: Looks like we're on a stroll through Smuggler Central.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_8.t: Carter backs him up. She whips her arms forward, fists blazing with magical fire. Her eyes sweep the scene, darting from one figure to another.
All right, everyone just be cool!

54bd886b323437742d0012df_14.t: Carter sees it too. Keeps her voice neutral.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_34.t: Carter remains completely still. Her voice is level. Moderate. She's done this before.
Let's all stay focused. No need for this to get ugly.

54bd886b323437742d0012df_35.t: She speaks quietly to Wu, who listens without turning, his focus squarely on the troll.
We need to find Raymond. These are the people who can help us do that.

54c2cde7323437f02400396c_1.t: She examines the control panel for a moment, then throws Wu a backward glance.
Looks like there's another way off the docks on the other side of this gate. I think that I can bypass the lock.

54c2cde7323437f02400396c_3.t: Carter steps aside to let you get at the panel.

550af5e63234375c2f0000a2_2.t: No, it won’t. They use this kind of concertina on prison walls back in the UCAS. Goes through bone like a propeller through sea foam.

550af5e63234375c2f0000a2_4.t: Even if you could, it'd snap back on you. Ever seen a high-tension wire whip through a human body? You don't want to.

550af5e63234375c2f0000a2_7.t: It'll take me a few minutes, though.

553570a7623666f4120001e5_35.t: The woman standing beside him breaks in.
We should get going, Duncan. Head back to the meeting point in case your dad shows up.

553570a7623666f4120001e5_37.t: The woman taps her chest with an armored finger.
Carter, Seattle Lone Star. I'm Duncan's partner.

553570a7623666f4120001e5_38.t: I let him carry my coffee for me sometimes... write my parking tickets... that kinda thing.

553570a7623666f4120001e5_39.t: The corner of her mouth flicks upward in amusement.

553570a7623666f4120001e5_40.t: Unfortunately, yes - although partner's not the right word for it, exactly. I like "superior officer" better.
I let him carry my coffee for me... write my parking tickets... that kinda thing.

553570a7623666f4120001e5_41.t: The woman taps her chest with an armored finger.

553570a7623666f4120001e5_52.t: The sooner we find him, the sooner you all can have a big happy family reunion over dinner.
And the sooner I can find a place to get a drink around here.

55b26cb5313663a436009ae2_4.t: Who knows. It's Hong Kong. Not exactly sure how things work around here.

54bd87f83063648803001820_1.t: Good evening. I'm sorry, I don't believe we've been formally introduced.
I'm Neville Ma, owner of Yellow Springs Studios.

54bd87f83063648803001820_2.t: He gestures at the array of beautiful people mingling on the dance floor.
Appearances are important in Hong Kong. If you look weak, that's how you'll be treated. Make a show of prosperity, and it will follow.

54bd87f83063648803001820_3.t: I blame the newsfeeds for that. They made the accident seem much more serious than it was. My car was destroyed, true, but I emerged largely unscathed.

54bd87f83063648803001820_4.t: I wouldn't say that at all. Perhaps it's true in Europe or the Americas, but here in Hong Kong, image is a matter of life and death.

54bd87f83063648803001820_10.t: It seems as though that's all anyone wants to hear about these days. To be honest, I'm getting rather tired of repeating the story. Suffice it to say, a delivery truck - a drone - experienced a glitch and ran a red light. If it hadn't hit me, it would have hit someone else.

54bd87f83063648803001820_13.t: The smile falls from his face.
How do you know about that?

54bd87f83063648803001820_15.t: His tone changes - suddenly, he's all business.
I shall see to it immediately.

54bd87f83063648803001820_16.t: He stares at you for a long moment, then gives you a slight nod. He fishes the fob out of his pocket and hands it to you.
Thank you. Any other time, I'd handle this myself, but I cannot be seen fleeing my own party.

54bd87f83063648803001820_31.t: He dismisses your comment with a wave.
It's nothing. A drop in the bucket compared to what we pay for sets and costumes. Period dramas are costly to produce, but they're what the people want to watch.

54fa23f8343061c016004e45_1.t: He glances behind him, and, assured by something, turns back toward you with a smile.
I'm Charles. Haven't seen your face before. Are you new here?

54fa23f8343061c016004e45_3.t: Oh, right! I learned about this in training. Please, tell me - how can I help?

54fa23f8343061c016004e45_5.t: Charles runs a hand through his hair.

None that I've noticed. Security's normally airtight. Even the emergency response system is on point. I've been told they have some really high-end fire and blast systems. Doubt you'll find any security risks here.

54fa23f8343061c016004e45_8.t: Isn't it wrong to make a false report like that?

54fa23f8343061c016004e45_9.t: Yes, you're right. Of course you are - you're the security consultant!
I'm... I'm sure everything will be fine. You'll see.

54fa23f8343061c016004e45_10.t: Isn't it wrong to make a false report like that?

54fa23f8343061c016004e45_11.t: Yes, you're right. Of course you are - you're the security consultant!
I'm... I'm sure everything will be fine. You'll see.

54fa23f8343061c016004e45_12.t: He gives you a timid nod.

54fa23f8343061c016004e45_13.t: I can't do that! I'll be fired on the spot! Or *worse*... no, I can't help you. I'm sorry.

54fa23f8343061c016004e45_14.t: His face pales. He swallows hard.
Yes, you're right. I should have known better. I'm... I'm sure everything will be fine.

54fa23f8343061c016004e45_15.t: His jaw drops, but he quickly recovers and lets out a chuckle.
We're all just trying to get by somehow, right?

54fa23f8343061c016004e45_16.t: It takes Charles a moment to realize that you're serious. An array of emotions flashes across his face, from humor all the way to fear. He starts to panic and steps back, eyes searching the locker room for help. His mouth opens and closes, wordless. He looks something like a fish caught on land.

54fa23f8343061c016004e45_18.t: Charles gives you a small nod and gingerly relinquishes his Wuxing ID.

54fa23f8343061c016004e45_19.t: I know how that goes. I'm pretty new here myself. Still learning the ropes.

54fa23f8343061c016004e45_20.t: Yeah. The upper floors are off limits. *Highly* restricted.
He glances around. Lowers his voice.
Not sure what's happening up there, but only company execs, security, and maintenance have access.

54fa23f8343061c016004e45_27.t: You're back. What else do you need?

54fa40af343061441b004e60_0.t: Charles rushes up to the front desk.
A fire has broken out in the locker room!

5502210f3430614818003b63_0.t: Charles rushes up to the guards, feigning a loss of breath.
...Sir! I've received a warning... about a break-in on floor twenty-seven. My coworker's up there... says he's in danger!

552c2a54306364300300505f_0.t: The woman hefts her weapon, tossing a nod toward the gangers behind her.
This lot's our turf. If you're lookin' for a fix, you're welcome to trade. If you're not here for business, clear the hell out. You try and wander around on our turf, we're gonna have to air you out.

552c2a54306364300300505f_4.t: man, are you deaf? What the hell did I tell you? No, you goddamn well can't wander around our turf.
The ganger shakes her head, mumbling something about idiots with no memory.

552c2a54306364300300505f_5.t: The ganger shifts nervously, glancing around at her allies.
Okay, I'm listening. What kind of business are you on?

561444d664366150150022f8_1.t: Bar's closed, on orders of the boss.
He pointed at the door that you walked in through.
Step on out of here. Now.

561444d664366150150022f8_2.t: His hand lowered to the knife at his belt.
And you come here and tell me that? You got a death wish, or what?

561444d664366150150022f8_9.t: He looked back over his shoulder, toward the bar.

561444d664366150150022f8_10.t: He shook his head, defiance in his eyes.
No way. You don't talk to Combat Pang. You talk to *me.* And I'm about out of patience with--

561444d664366150150022f8_11.t: He took a moment to size you up - really *examine* you - and the bravado disappeared.
He looked back over his shoulder, toward the bar.

561444d664366150150022f8_62.t: You've got no business telling any Black Sun Boy what to do, asshole.
His hand lowered to the knife at his belt.
Now, I'm gonna give you a choice. Get movin', or start bleedin'.

561444d664366150150022f8_63.t: He shook his head, defiance in his eyes.
No, I don't, and I don't care. In here, you're *nothing* unless Combat Pang says different.
His hand lowered to the knife at his belt.
Now, I'm gonna give you a choice. Get movin', or start bleedin'.

569ecd67306364a81f00521a_1.t: The woman nods her head at you, taking a drag on her cigarette.
You must be the runner from Heoi. Heard about that dust-up at the Tigers' Den - glad to see that you got out alive.

569ecd67306364a81f00521a_3.t: Us old runners, we gotta stick together, you know? You stand by your crew, they stand by you, and you can't count on anyone else.
She purses her lips, looking around with an annoyed expression.
If my crew would ever freaking get here... why am I always the first one on the scene, anyway? I wanna get out of the shadows and onto a beach!

569ecd67306364a81f00521a_4.t: Nah. First time here - I'm just waiting for the rest of my crew to show up.
The woman looks you up and down.
You look exactly like I thought you would. Heard some stories about that business with Josephine Tsang. You can call me Triple Alpha, by the way.

569ecd67306364a81f00521a_5.t: Call me Triple Alpha. You're kinda famous in Hong Kong, you know - all that stuff with Tsang Mechanical Services and the Walled City.
Alpha shrugs, extending a palm in a placating fashion.
I'm not spying, I promise. I'm just plugged in to the community.

569ecd67306364a81f00521a_7.t: Alpha lets out a rough cackle.

man, you don't know the half of it. You tally up the runs I've been on, I think I might have seen more stuff than even FastJack. I might not be as old as him, but I think that I take jobs a lot more regularly than he does.

569ecd67306364a81f00521a_8.t: I've been to Los Angeles and Seattle, Hong Kong and Berlin, and everywhere in between. If it's a weapon, I've fired it. If there's a corporation, I've run both for and against it.
The woman gestures languidly with her cigarette as she speaks.
It's never dull, I tell you that.

569ecd67306364a81f00521a_9.t: We've got a rigger, a mage, and an adept. I guess there's also our jack-of-all trades, too - she's not really very good at anything, but she's got a lot of heart. Totally unafraid to march alongside us, even if all she can manage is to plink away with that crappy pistol of hers. And then there's me. You can guess what I do.
Alpha gestures to the cyberdeck and the assault rifle slung across her back.

569ecd67306364a81f00521a_12.t: Alpha shakes her head and stares off into the distance.
Nah, I don't think so. I've been running the shadows for my whole life. It's all I've ever done - I don't know how to do anything else. This is more of a vacation. A break, if you will.

569ecd67306364a81f00521a_13.t: I figure we take a break - hit some beaches, take in the sights that are off the beaten path, and come back when we're rested. You keep doing the same thing forever, you're bound to burn out.
Alpha glances sidelong at you and gives a little grin.

569ecd67306364a81f00521a_14.t: I don't wanna brag, but I'm kinda rolling in money, yeah.

569ecd67306364a81f00521a_16.t: Pretty sure we'll be back, though. And when we are...
She makes a low whistle, and grins widely.
Better watch out, because we'll be top of the line.

569ecd67306364a81f00521a_19.t: Aside from hit a nice beach somewhere? I dunno, there's plenty of interesting stuff to see around the world.
She takes a drag on her cigarette and ponders the question.

569ecd67306364a81f00521a_23.t: I like your style. I kinda feel like we've seen the same kinda runs. So watch your back, okay?
She takes a long drag on her cigarette, and flicks the butt away before lighting a new one.

569ecd67306364a81f00521a_24.t: Maybe they will - but scarier things have tried and failed, so I'm feeling pretty okay with my one vice.

569ecd67306364a81f00521a_25.t: She touches two fingers to her brow in a wry salute.
Good luck. I think we might run into each other again - after I've gotten upgraded and taken a bit of a break. Looking forward to it when it happens!

569ecd67306364a81f00521a_27.t: Triple Alpha takes a long drag on her cigarette and exhales a plume of smoke out of the corner of her mouth.
Hey again. What else can I do for you?

54d3f03730303128060031df_1.t: His voice is coarse, like steel wool on your eardrums.
Come and get it, because now's your last chance! Everything is on sale - won't find prices like these anywhere else, my friend.

54d3f03730303128060031df_3.t: No, not I. Not here. Plan's to leave these waters soon as I wrangle up enough supplies to do so.
He leans toward you, voice lower, breath surprisingly free of alcohol.
There are things happening here... locals call 'em dreams. I call 'em *evil.* And I don't want no more of that.

54d3f03730303128060031df_7.t: His over-large smile turns into a deep frown.
That's a right shame, my friend. I suggest you try and find yourself a way out. Nothing good can come of these... "dreams." They'll get inside of you. Scar you.

54d3f03730303128060031df_9.t: He holds up a hand, face stern.

No, no. Do I look like a psychic to you? I've got no readings or meanings or any of that nonsense. Just a feeling in my gut. And it's saying to get out! Get out now. And if you know what's good for you, you'll leave these parts too.

54d3f03730303128060031df_10.t: He shrugs, the motion nonchalant and uncaring.
I take what I can get. Sometimes I find things, sometimes I take things, and sometimes I play the game. Invest a little. Make a little something more in return.

54d3f03730303128060031df_11.t: He crosses his arms and looks you over.
And why do you ask? You judging a perfectly respectable businessman such as myself in these harsh times? You've never been in this business. I can see it. You've no right to fault me.

54d3f03730303128060031df_12.t: That's right. And I get by. Thankfully, I've got no family to feed - just some crewmates - so I keep myself afloat. Even make a little extra to enjoy life's finer things, like traveling.

54d3f03730303128060031df_14.t: Tough, sure. Can't stay in any one place for too long, gotta float under the radar, and business partners come and go... but I get to see the world.
He looks out to sea, and his briny beard outlines a wide smile.
Heading to Macau next. Can't wait to get out of here.

54d3f03730303128060031df_17.t: Aye, that'd help a man out.
Go ahead and have a look. Take your time.

54d3f03730303128060031df_19.t: His nostrils flare, and he leans back in surprise.
Wouldn't have pinned you for a runner. Guess I can still be surprised by people. You look pretty green, though. Take my advice, and don't get too caught up in it. Hold on to yourself.

54d3f03730303128060031df_20.t: You do what you have to.

54d3f03730303128060031df_21.t: He slaps his stomach and lets out a laugh.

We gotta have standards. Anyway, I'm nearly stocked up here. Just a few more trades, and a few more sales, and I'll be on my way to Macau. This is your last chance to buy any of my wares before I set sail, so have a look. Take your time.

54d3f03730303128060031df_23.t: There you go, judging me and mine again. While I might keep my business dealings open, I don't *kill* people. Heck, I've never even raised my voice to a stray dog!

54d3f03730303128060031df_24.t: His nostrils flare, and he leans back, looking you over. A burst of laughter cracks through his surprise.
If you're a runner, you're greener than a spring tree!
